To ask the Minister of Finance when he will answer AQW 41727/22-27.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the reasons for the rise in driving test cancellations at Larne test centre, including any action her Department is taking to address this; (ii) the number of cancellations by the Driver and Vehicle Agency, in the last month; and (iii) whether any assessment has been made of the impact of these cancellations on drivers and instructors.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any steps she has taken, since taking office, to progress the delivery of the York Street Interchange project.
To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the amount spent by her Department on the Hillview and Flax Street gate, in North Belfast.

To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on grid connectivity.
To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the funding benchmarking exercise for the Northern Ireland Hospice.
To ask the Minister for the Economy whether any assessment has been made of her Department's performance on the issue of energy.

To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when her Department became aware of the proposed 450 job losses at Ulster University.

To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 29444/22-27, to detail a breakdown of the £329,732.
answer_text: The Ulster Council of the GAA (UCGAA) submitted an expenditure forecast of £707,705 in March 2025, of which £329,732 was ultimately not eligible for grant award by the Department under the terms of the Funding Agreement with the UCGAA.The amounts not reimbursed by the Department via grant award are broken down as follows:Administrative and site costs: £58,362Integrated Consultant Team (ICT) costs: £253,568Public relations, media and community engagement costs: £18,130Project team costs: £78Following further final minor reconciliation adjustments within the claim, there remained a net total of £329,732 that was not reimbursed by the Department.All expenditure claimed is assessed against the terms of the Funding Agreement. The Department reserves the right, at its absolute discretion, to determine what constitutes eligible expenditure under that Agreement. These controls are in place to protect the public purse and to ensure that all expenditure reimbursed aligns directly with the strategic objectives of the project.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any steps his Department is taking to reduce the backlog of Ophthalmology Regional Cataract Service outpatient assessments.

To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any work his Department is doing with the Housing Executive (NIHE) to ensure adaptions recommended by health professionals can be approved for NIHE tenants with non-mobility related conditions, such as severe epilepsy, which are not considered within occupational therapy home assessments.
To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department will consider the ten-step plan recommendations provided by representatives of the UKCVFamily.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail any steps his Department is taking to ensure carers can pre-book respite care home placements sufficiently in advance.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the policy intent of Section 18 of the Road Traffic (Northern Ireland) Order 1995.
To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the total spend of the £45 million allocated to the Local Economic Partnerships, broken down by local council area.

To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the rational for attending events organised by the Aisling Awards in the United States of America contrary to official advice; (ii) the total expenditure by her Department on travel and attendance at these events; and (iii) any payments made by her Department to the Aisling Awards, since January 2022.

To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department is assessing Baroness Hallett’s Module 4 Report within the Covid Inquiry, published on 16 April 2026, particularly in relation to recommendations on support for those who experienced adverse reactions from COVID-19 vaccination.
answer_text: I welcome the publication of the Module 4 Report by the UK Covid-19 Inquiry.  My Department is currently reviewing the Report and will consider all recommendations, including those concerning individuals adversely affected by a COVID-19 vaccination.I note that the Report acknowledges that, while the COVID-19 vaccination programme was “an extraordinary feat”, “there must be appropriate financial support for those rare cases of people suffering side effects” and has recommended reform of the existing financial support arrangements provided by the Vaccine Damage Payment Scheme (VDPS).The VDPS scheme is administered by the Business Services Authority, which is part of NHS England, on behalf of the whole of the United Kingdom and therefore any reform to the VDPS will be a matter for UK Government Health Ministers.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 39561/22-27, to provide an update.

To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 39560/22-27, to detail how his Department plans for need, given that the Housing Executive do not hold information on demand for this service, nor on the number of applications versus allocations in the last year,
To ask the Minister of Health whether primary schools receive visits from dental professionals.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 42284/22-27, given the developer has stated that they will not be progressing with the adoption of the development, to detail any advice that can be given to how adoption can now be achieved.
answer_text: As advised in my answer to AQW 42284/22-27, a number of matters need to be checked and evidenced for this structure to achieve technical approval, all of which have been communicated to the developer. Responsibility for the construction of roads and associated infrastructure to an adoptable standard at this development remains with the developer. The developer has advised my Department that they are unable to achieve Technical Approval of the bridge. However, my Department remains willing to engage with the relevant parties should they wish to proceed with the technical approval process.In circumstances where a developer fails to deliver a street to an adoptable standard the Department cannot adopt and has no recourse other than to advise that the street will remain private.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant AQW 43436/22-27, to detail (i) any work being undertaking by her Department to strengthen legislation on e-scooters; and (ii) whether any assessment has been made of how the issue can be addressed currently.
answer_text: It is currently illegal to use e-scooters on public roads and public spaces here. Anyone using an e-scooter illegally may be subject to enforcement action by the PSNI.The PSNI has a number of powers to seize retain and dispose of vehicles where they are being driven carelessly or inconsiderately and causing or likely to cause alarm, distress or annoyance to members of the public.However, in recognition of ongoing concerns regarding the illegal use of e-scooters, my officials have been monitoring the progress of e-scooter trials in Britain, as well as the impact of legislative changes in the south. When analysis on this subject is available, my officials will provide advice based on this evidence, and I will consider if any legislative change should be brought forward here.Road safety continues to be of paramount importance to me, and I am committed to working proactively, along with key partners in the Road Safety Strategic Forum, to address a range of issues which negatively impact on road safety, including illegal e-scooter usage. I hope this will provide you with some reassurance.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any plans his Department has to increase the number of pupil places available at any existing or new Specialist Provision Social Communication units in primary schools in the North Belfast constituency.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has advised that:In planning for future demand, the EA are currently progressing 2 additional Specialist Provision Social Communication classrooms in the North Belfast Constituency. EA continue to engage with schools across Northern Ireland to develop specialist provision classes to meet the needs of our children and young people with SEN, within their local community.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 37866/22-27, for an update on the lands.
To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of Specialist Provision Social Communication units in primary schools in the North Belfast Constituency, in each of the last five years.
answer_text: There are currently 20 Specialist Provision Social Communication classes operational across 10 schools within the North Belfast constituency.The number of additional Specialist Provision Social Communication classes established in each of the last five academic years are:2020 - 4 classes2021 - 2 classes2022 - 2 classes2023 - 4 classes2024 - 4 classes2025 - 4 classes

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 42852/22-27, whether a date for works to commence at Currie Primary School has been agreed.
answer_text: A start date for works to commence at Currie Primary School has not yet been agreed as a final budget for 2026/27 has not been agreed.Upon receipt of the Minor Works budget allocation, the requisite works at the school will be assessed by the Education Authority alongside other competing priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of appointable candidates, provided to her by officials, in advance of her decision on the appointments to the board of Invest NI.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail when the age for screening for bowel cancer will be reduced from 60 to 50, in order to bring Northern Ireland in line with the rest of UK.
answer_text: Work to expand the bowel screening programme down to 50 years of age, in line with a UK National Screening Committee recommendation and the rest of the UK, is ongoing but must be viewed within the context of wider financial and capacity challenges within the supporting services. Regardless of age, anyone experiencing any symptoms of concern should contact their GP.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 42480/22-27, whether approval has now been given for Rathcoole Primary School.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has advised that the business case addendum for the traffic management/car parking scheme at Rathcoole Primary School was approved on 19 March 2026. An ‘Invitation to Award’ letter was issued to the successful tenderer on 26 March and the EA is now collating the required information from the successful tenderer in order to award the contract.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when he will appoint the panel for boxing in Northern Ireland.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether any consideration has been given to making Hillview Avenue, Newtownabbey, a one-way street.
To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any steps his Department is taking to ensure arts venues are sensitive to the needs of those with epilepsy when hosting theatre productions.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the usage of Trasnslink's Late Night Services pilot.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether any assessment has been made of the effectiveness of the Northern Ireland Road Authority and Utility Committees.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 42284/22-27, to detail what that would constitute technical approval.
answer_text: Technical approval is my Department’s process for checking and agreeing the design of a highway structure (for example, a bridge, culvert or retaining wall) that is over, under or next to departmental land. The process is set out in the Design Manual for Roads and Bridges (DMRB) document CG300.In simple terms, my Department will only grant technical approval once it is satisfied that the structure has been designed to the required recognised standards and meets the Department’s policies and guidance.Technical approval is not something that can normally be applied after a structure has been built, because it is intended to be in place before construction starts. Where a structure has already been constructed, the Department would need evidence to confirm that the required standards and checks were achieved during design and construction. Building without technical approval therefore carries a risk that compliance cannot be demonstrated.There remains a number of matters that need to be checked and evidenced for this structure to achieve technical approval. These have been communicated to the applicant, and my Department remains willing to engage with the relevant parties should they wish to proceed with the technical approval process.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 38712/22-27, to provide an update.
answer_text: There is no further update beyond the information provided in respect of AQW 38712/22-27.Issue of the tender for the traffic management works at King’s Park Primary School is subject to available capital funding in the 2026/27 financial year. There are many competing demands on the Department’s constrained capital budget. I will continue to make the case for increased and sustained investment in our schools’ estate.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether his Department will work with the Education Authority to secure additional full-time nursery places for oversubscribed units within the Newtownabbey area.
answer_text: The Education Authority has advised that sufficient funded pre-school education places are available in the Newtownabbey area to meet current demand for the 2026/27 academic year and additional provision is not required. The purpose of my Department’s Pre-School Education Programme (PSEP) is to provide a funded pre-school education place for every target age child whose parents want it.  It is not the intention of the PSEP to provide every child with a funded place in their parents’ first preference setting, nor is it possible to do so.  The majority of children do, however, receive an offer of a funded place in their first preference setting with 92.43% of applicants receiving an offer in their first preference setting in Stage One of the 2026/27 admissions process.Settings that have transitioned to the standardised full-time session length, including those that are oversubscribed, will not be given preferential treatment in the allocation of funded pre-school places by the EA. This means that if the EA increase PAN allocations in an area due to the level of overall demand, they will do so irrespective of the session length offered by individual settings.Increasing the PAN of one setting above the overall level of need would inevitably lead to a reduction in enrolment for another setting. The EA’s allocation process is therefore intended to treat all settings equitably, maintaining stability in the sector and ensuring that all target aged children can access a funded pre-school education place.The EA keeps PAN allocations under review throughout the admissions process and may make adjustments to settings’ allocations where they are needed to reflect the overall level of demand in local areas.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the vesting and redevelopment of Fortwilliam Parade, Belfast.
answer_text: The Housing Executive is in the process of appointing a demolition consultant for demolition of the site at Fortwilliam Parade. The demolition of the properties is scheduled for an Autumn 2026 start. A housing association has not yet been nominated however the plan remains to develop social housing on the site.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether her Department has considered any steps to provide stronger regulation of management companies, responsible for the upkeep of developments, including in relation to management fee increases.
answer_text: My Department published a scoping report on the issue of shared property management in October 2025, which considered regulation of those who manage shared property as well as service and management fees.I am keen to develop legislation for consideration in the early part of the next mandate. I expect the resources to be available shortly to allow a small team to be set up to undertake further work on this important area. Engagement with stakeholders and public consultation will form an integral part of that work when the team is in place.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the Hopefield Avenue and Shore Road, Newtownabbey, controlled crossing assessment.
answer_text: An assessment of the case for a controlled pedestrian crossing at the junction of Hopefield Avenue and Shore Road, Newtownabbey, was completed on 1 April 2026. The outcome of that assessment indicates that current pedestrian crossing demand and traffic flows at this location fall below the thresholds required for the Department to consider the provision of a controlled crossing.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether her Department is planning to emulate Clause 8 of the Crime and Policing Bill, as currently amended, to strengthen e-scooter seizure powers, similar to that in England and Wales.
answer_text: I am aware of the proposals within the Crime and Policing Bill in relation to vehicle seizure powers, as well as the UK Government’s recent consultation proposals to amend secondary legislation to increase statutory charges, and reduce the disposal timeframes, for seized vehicles.Under the devolution settlement, the Department for Infrastructure has lead policy responsibility for road safety matters, but my Department will consider the need to make equivalent changes to the vehicle seizure regime in Northern Ireland, in consultation with the Police Service for Northern Ireland and DfI officials.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any contingency nursing plans or alternative provision options that can be offered to ensure Special School Summer Schemes can proceed.
answer_text: Appropriate nursing support has been provided to support summer schemes in Special Schools for many years, with a number of different models of delivery operating depending on the identified need. Earlier this year my officials engaged with the Department of Education (DE) and Education Authority (EA) to explore appropriate nursing cover options for the summer schemes to be hosted in special schools this year. Those options would have provided an enhanced level of support which included additional onsite training for classroom assistants and also direct telephone access to a registered Community Children’s Nurse familiar with that cohort of children during the school/scheme day as required. In the EA’s view a nurse is required on each SEN summer scheme site to provide oversight and healthcare interventions for children with complex needs in those settings and they proceeded to announce the cancellation of the 2026 SEN summer schemes on 25 March 2026.Recognising the importance of these Summer Schemes to the children attending them and their families, I have tasked the Chief Nursing Officer to meet with the five regional HSC Trust nursing leaders to develop plans to meet the request of a registered nurse on each site to ensure the summer schemes can progress this year. My officials have also asked EA to provide information in relation to the safety concerns raised by them so that a full risk assessment can be undertaken so we can continue to work with DE, EA and the HSC Trusts to agree a plan to mitigate any risks.  Whilst extremely challenging, my Department is committed to making every effort to source the identified additional nursing cover, involving appropriate staff with the right skills and knowledge including the appropriate children’s safeguarding training.   Some key data has been requested from EA to enable the HSC to fulfil this commitment and we await receipt.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail how her Department will respond to the Public Accounts Committee report on Road Openings by Utilities, highlighting the long-term impact of utility works on Northern Ireland’s road conditions.
answer_text: My Department welcomed the NIAO report and the opportunity to explore the issues more fully at the PAC hearing. While there has been significant progress in this area in recent years, there remains work to do. My Department is committed to making further improvements and addressing the concerns raised going forward.Following publication of the report on 26th March the Department for Finance commissioned draft responses to the PAC recommendations. My officials will work with their counterparts in DoF to provide the required information.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether his Department can seek alternative commitments for nursing provision at special school summer schemes, if an offer from Department of Health to the Education Authority is not forthcoming.
answer_text: I refer the Member to the answer to AQW 43502/22-27 published in the Weekly Answer Booklet for week ending 3rd April 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee Scheme.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether any consideration has been given to the resurfacing of Ashgrove road, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: Officials are currently considering the 2026/27 programme of works, details of which will be included in my Department’s 2026/27 annual report to Council. Programmes cannot be finalised until such times as budgets confirmed.My officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with the Department’s Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 42765/22-27, whether the paper was due to be discussed at the last Executive meeting, but was absent from the agenda, as she was out of the country and not available to attend the meeting.
answer_text: As I was out of the country on Ministerial business on 12 March the Minister of Finance was presenting papers on my behalf. Indeed the Finance Minister presented another Department for the Economy paper on my behalf, which was on the agenda that day. Had the REPG paper been allowed on to the agenda, the Finance Minister was ready to also present that. However the REPG paper was not allowed on to the agenda. Thankfully it has now been approved by the Executive and I am grateful for the Member's support in that regard.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) to detail the estimated waiting time to access Paediatric Occupational Therapy in the Northern Health and Social Care Trust from the date of referral; and (ii) for an update on any efforts to reduce waiting lists for such.
answer_text: The most up-to-date Allied Health Profession (AHP) waiting time information indicates that, as at 28 February 2026, the number of children aged 0–17 years waiting for their first appointment with Paediatric Occupational Therapy in the Northern Health and Social Care Trust is as follows:HSC TrustTotal Waiting Waiting greater than 13 WeeksNorthern1123911The Department of Health and the Health and Social Care Trusts, including the Northern Trust, continue to implement a range of regional and local measures aimed at reducing waiting times across outpatient and AHP services. Current actions include:Deployment of the regional My Waiting Times NI platform, which supports clearer and more transparent monitoring of average waiting times across all Trusts. Monthly data updates help inform operational planning and service responsiveness.Ongoing data validation and optimisation through the ‘encompass’ electronic patient record system, improving service management, appointment scheduling and clinical prioritisation as Trusts transition fully to digital records.Clinical prioritisation of referrals, ensuring that children with the most urgent clinical need are assessed and treated first, while routine referrals are managed in line with available capacity and regional guidance.Expansion of the AHP workforce pipeline, including the Minister’s announcement on 14 May 2025 of a record 505 AHP preregistration training places for the 2025/26 academic year. This included 93 commissioned Occupational Therapy places, with further commissioning discussions underway for 2026/27.These measures are part of a wider programme of work aimed at improving efficiency, strengthening data quality, and supporting sustained reductions in waiting times for Paediatric Occupational Therapy and other AHP services.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 42664/22-27, to detail the number of years Carollhill Road, Newtownabbey has been listed within her Department's three-year resurfacing programme, prior to her Department's forthcoming 2026-27 Annual Report.
answer_text: As outlined in my response to AQW 42664/22-27, Carolhill Road, Newtownabbey was previously identified as a potential resurfacing scheme in 2022 and included in the 3-5 year resurfacing programme..  In the interim, officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with my Department’s Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when the reinstatement of road markings at Sandyknowes Roundabout will commence.
answer_text: Lining defects which have been identified from the most recent inspection of the Sandyknowes Roundabout have been instructed to the contractor for remedial action.  Any safety related defects will be repaired as soon as possible with the remainder of defect repairs undertaken in the summer months when the weather is more favourable.Officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with my Department’s limited service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail any discussions she has had with the PSNI to undertake a targeted campaign to seize e-scooters being used on the road network.
answer_text: The operational detection and investigation of crimes involving e-scooters, including how resources are deployed, is an operational matter for the Chief Constable, who is accountable to the Northern Ireland Policing Board.  I am committed to respecting the operational independence of the Chief Constable and the role of the NI Policing Board.The regulation of e-scooters, including the assessment of current legislation and enforcement powers, is primarily a matter for the Department for Infrastructure. My Department is content to work with the Department for Infrastructure to confirm that if any new offences and penalties are proposed, they are proportionate and consistent with existing law.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail when his Housing Amendment Bill will be submitted to the Executive.
answer_text: It is my expectation that the Housing (Amendment) Bill will be submitted to the Executive in May 2026, subject to ongoing engagement with the Office of the Legislative Counsel and key stakeholders as well as Executive priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail a breakdown of the spend of the additional funding awarded by the Executive to her Department for roads maintenance, in the 2025-26 financial year, broken down by constituency.
answer_text: Each division has been made an allocation based on assessed need and capacity to deliver.  The breakdown of allocation is provided in the table below:DivisionAllocation (£m)Northern8Southern15Eastern6Western11Total40

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail a start date for the Glengormley Public Realm Scheme.
answer_text: The current programme is for construction of the Glengormley Public Realm Scheme to commence on site in late 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any assessment made of how the BBC became aware of her proposals to the Executive to distribute the £81 million of UK Government funding for energy support, before Members of the Northern Ireland Assembly.
answer_text: The proposal to reduce electricity bills was contained within an Executive paper circulated on Thursday 19th March.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 41456/22-27, to detail when the works on the cost estimate will be completed.
answer_text: I refer the Member to AQW 41456 / 22-27. The works are progressing as resources permit.At present officials are seeking costs from utilities for service work/relocations. This can take some time and is largely outside the control of the Department.When costs are received and an overall estimated scheme cost is established, a decision will be taken on whether to progress to the approval’s procedure and detailed design.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail when works on the playground at Currie Primary school, Belfast, will recommence.
answer_text: Due to budget pressures and competing priorities, it was not possible to complete the planned outdoor playground works at Currie Primary School before the end of the financial year 2025/26. However, the Education Authority intends to take the scheme forward for delivery in the 2026/27 financial year, dependent on budget availability and other priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on work to ensure the outcomes of the Infected Blood Inquiry are implemented in Northern Ireland, including the payment of compensation.
To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she has made any assessment of the BBC being a tenant of the Belfast Stories, Belfast Region City Deal project.
answer_text: In the first instance, a decision relating to the proposal will be a matter for Belfast City Council.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail any further consideration she has given to Harper's Law, following the introduction of her Criminal Justice (Sentencing etc) Bill (NIA 26/22-27).
answer_text: This issue has been included for consideration within the Review of Sentencing Policy, which was launched on 14 January 2026. My Department is currently conducting research into the policy issues within scope of the Review, with the aim of developing legislative proposals for the next Assembly mandate. Public consultation on proposals arising from the Review is scheduled for 2027.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the rational for her policy change, as outlined by her officials at the Committee for the Economy meeting held on 18 March 2026, that she now longer requires the support of all higher education providers to progress the implementation of the Review of Higher Education Funding.
answer_text: All feedback received on the draft Terms of Reference for the wider review of Higher Education funding is being considered before next steps are determined. I remain committed to working in partnership with the local Higher Education Institutions, students and unions to find a sustainable way forward for higher education funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any evidence that substantiates her claim at the meeting of the Committee for the Economy, held on 16 March 2026, that the DUP were blocking the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee scheme at the Executive.
answer_text: The Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee Scheme was circulated to the Executive Committee for comment on 2 February 2026, along with a request to have it tabled at the next Executive meeting. Three Executive meetings have since taken place, with this paper remaining absent from the agenda despite my understanding that the First Minister agreed its inclusion on the agenda.I have received supportive comments from the Ministers of Finance, Health, Justice, Infrastructure, and Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs.I have not received responses from the Ministers of Education or Communities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the current number of staff in her Department's Energy Division; and (ii) how that number compares with January 2022.
answer_text: My Department monitors staff resourcing levels centrally with records held dating back to June 2022.Currently Energy Group has 125.15 FTE (Full Time Equivalent) staff in post, similar to a staffing level of 124.5 FTE in June 2022.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department will approve the extension of the pedestrianisation scheme at Hill Street, Belfast.
answer_text: I was pleased to announce the pedestrianisation of Hill Street from 4 December 2025. It is a thriving area in the Cathedral Quarter of Belfast as more and more visitors come to enjoy the hospitality on offer. Importantly this scheme will facilitate a vibrant social space that prioritises people by making the streets safer for pedestrians.  By contributing to a more pleasant and enjoyable environment it will help maximise the street’s potential and enhance the nighttime and weekend economy. The current experimental Order was introduced as a six month pilot which currently extends to 3rd June 2026. The scheme does permit access for certain vehicles at prescribed times of the day, such as loading and unloading exemptions between 6am and 12pm.Exemptions are also in place for Blue Badge holders to ensure accessibility to amenities in the area.I am pleased to note that since its introduction, survey results indicate an almost 80 percent reduction in cars using Hill Street. At present I see no reason why the experimental scheme cannot be extended for a further six months during which time, officials will carry out engagement with key stakeholders to determine if amendments are required before introducing permanent legislation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, in light of his support for the Armed Forces, to detail any work his Department can do to secure the creation of a Defence Technical Excellence College in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My Department actively engages with the Ministry of Defence and with veterans’ organisations on a range of issues impacting on the Armed Forces and relating to the delivery of benefits, housing and employability support.The matter of securing the creation of a Defence Technical College in Northern Ireland cuts across the responsibilities of a number of Departments across the Executive.I have tasked officials within the Department to explore with the Ministry of Defence the potential for expansion of the Defence Technical Excellence Colleges to Northern Ireland and to help inform discussion with other relevant Departments.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the number of years Carolhill Road, Newtownabbey, has been on her Department's three-year resurfacing programme; and (ii) when the work will start.
answer_text: Details of the resurfacing schemes planned for the 2026/27 financial year will be included in my Department’s Annual Report to Antrim and Newtownabbey Borough Council.In the interim, officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with my Department’s Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any discussions her Department has undertaken to secure the creation of a Defence Technical Excellence College in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My officials continue to engage with counterparts in the British Government in relation to the proposed Defence Growth Deal. The British Government have not published details of the specific focus or projects the Defence Growth deal will address at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail how her Department (i) plans to address the changes to the way support for energy-intensive industries is paid for by the UK Government; and (ii) intends to compensate for this.
answer_text: The Energy Intensive Industries (EII) Compensation Scheme provides compensation to EIIs to alleviate the costs incurred due to the NI Renewables Obligation levy.The Department for Business and Trade (DBT) ended funding for the EII Compensation Scheme in December 2025. Therefore, the British Government no longer provides financial support for EIIs in the North. Funding for the EII Compensation Scheme will be considered as part of the budget process. DBT will continue to administer the scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether her Department has engaged with businesses that may be impacted should it be unable to provide the £7.5 million needed to fund support for energy-intensive industries, in the 2026-27 financial year.
answer_text: The budget allocation process for the 2026/2027 financial year has not concluded. Once the budget is agreed there will be a further opportunity to engage with and consider funding to support energy intensive industries for the indirect costs of the NI Renewables Obligation.	I recently met with local energy intensive industries to discuss energy costs and the importance of maintaining business competitiveness in this region.  My officials will continue to engage with industry and key stakeholders to progress work in this area.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether any assessment has been made of the Executive Office funded organisation, Relatives for Justice, being subjected to an investigation by the Charity Commission.
answer_text: The Executive Office has policy responsibility for victims and survivors issues, it is not involved in the awarding of funding to victims and survivors organisations or individuals. The Charity Commission is an Arm’s Length Body of the Department for Communities and has operational independence as the statutory regulator of the charity sector here.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department has carried out any assessment of media reports that the Charity Commission have been investigating Relatives for Justice for a three-year period, without concluding their report.
answer_text: The Charity Commission for Northern Ireland (CCNI) has operational independence as the statutory regulator of the charity sector in Northern Ireland. While my Department sponsors CCNI, it has no direct role in the delivery of the CCNI’s statutory functions.  CCNI has provided the necessary assurance that they are taking necessary action under its authority as statutory regulator.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she will publish all letters held by her Department from UK Government departments, since the Autumn Statement in December 2025, pertaining to the reduction in electricity bills here in Northern Ireland funded by HM Government.
answer_text: Correspondence between Ministers and officials, including exchanges with other administrations, is not routinely published, as it is important that Ministers and departments are able to engage in frank and candid discussions on policy and funding matters.I do not intend to publish these, however, the Department has been transparent about the substance and outcomes of this engagement, including the measures agreed to support electricity consumers here, and I will continue to keep the Assembly informed as appropriate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when she received a copy of the letter dated 4 December 2025 from HM Treasury to the Minister for Finance, confirming Northern Ireland would benefit from funding to reduce energy bills.
answer_text: I understand that the Department of Finance did not receive a letter from the Treasury on this issue on 4 December.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether he will publish the letter received from HM Treasury on 4 December 2025 regarding funding to support households in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: No letter regarding funding to support households here was received from the Treasury on 4 December.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 40556/22-27, to provide an update.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has advised that the business case addendum for the parking scheme at Rathcoole Primary School has been completed and is going through the EA’s internal approval process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, given rising fossil fuel costs and the need for Northern Ireland to strengthen its energy independence, to detail any (i) issues currently delaying or affecting the progress of the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee legislation, including any steps her Department is taking to address these issues; and (ii) additional resources that are required to support departmental officials in progressing the legislation.
answer_text: The Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee (REPG) scheme policy intent was agreed by the Executive on 26 March 2026. 		My Department aims to introduce the required primary legislation for the REPG to the Assembly before summer recess 2026. Once the Primary Bill is in place, subordinate legislation will be taken forward to set out the detailed arrangements.		My Department, including Energy Group, continuously reviews how to best use the resources available and is focussed on strong leadership, and ensuring the right people with the right skills are delivering priority policies, including the REPG.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether her Department’s legal advice to the proposal to reduce electricity prices for residents living near new renewable generation installations has now been received.
answer_text: As with all policy development, my Department takes legal advice as appropriate at various stages. Legal advice is subject to legal professional privilege, and it would not be appropriate to comment on its status or content.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any representations her Department made to the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, regarding the potential economic impact of the proposed extension of the Greenhouse Gas Emissions Trading Scheme (Amendment) (Extension to Maritime Activities) Order 2026, to the local economy.
answer_text: My Department engages on an ongoing basis with the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (DAERA) on matters relating to the UK Emissions Trading Scheme (ETS), including its development and implementation.DAERA is the lead Department for the UK ETS, including policy responsibility for the extension of the Scheme to maritime activities. As such, formal consideration of the policy design and its impacts, including economic impacts, is led by DAERA in conjunction with the UK ETS Authority.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) whether her Department undertook any assessment of the potential impact of the draft Greenhouse Gas Emissions Trading Scheme (Amendment) (Extension to Maritime Activities) Order 2026 proposal, on Northern Ireland’s (a) competitiveness; (b) supply chains; and (c) cost of living, given the region’s reliance on sea freight connectivity with Great Britain; and (ii) any engagement that took place between her Department and relevant industry stakeholders, prior to the Order being laid.
answer_text: My Department engages on an ongoing basis with the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (DAERA) on matters relating to the UK Emissions Trading Scheme (ETS), including its development and implementation.		DAERA is the lead Department for the UK ETS, including policy responsibility for the extension of the Scheme to maritime activities. As such, formal consideration of the policy design and its impacts, including economic impacts, is led by DAERA in conjunction with the UK ETS Authority.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 39875/22-27, to provide an update.
answer_text: The Education Authority Asset Management Team has drafted the business case for Seaview Primary School, however, the business case can not be finalised until a site valuation report is received from Land and Property Services (LPS). It is anticipated that the business case will be lodged with the Department for consideration by the end of April 2026.Should the business case be approved by the Department, the preferred option will be progressed in design.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 41457/22-27, whether Northern Ireland received a Barnett Award for this scheme.
answer_text: The North has not received any Barnett Consequential for the Warm Homes Discount Scheme. The scheme has never applied here.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQO 3055/22-27, to provide an update.
answer_text: I thank Mr Brett for his continued interest in the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice. I wish to acknowledge the valuable and compassionate role the Hospice plays in providing vital care for babies, children, and young people with lifelimiting and lifethreatening conditions. I also recognise the extremely challenging financial environment in which hospices and other charitable organisations are currently operating.In the 2025/26 financial year, my Department has allocated £1.8 million to support the delivery of a range of services for children and their families. These services include overnight short breaks, family support, hospiceathome provision, bereavement support for wider family networks, and specialist palliative care.My Department is undertaking a benchmarking exercise to review the level of funding provided to the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice for commissioned services. This work is expected to be completed and shared with the Hospice by the end of this financial year. A meeting between Departmental officials and representatives of the Hospice will be arranged in the coming days to facilitate discussion on service data, activity levels, costings, and comparative benchmarking.You will also be aware of the recent Health Committee inquiry into access to palliative care services in Northern Ireland and the recommendations arising from that review. I am currently considering those recommendations. However, you will appreciate that the Department of Health continues to face significant financial pressures and competing priorities at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 40772/22-27, whether this street is included within the report.
answer_text: As stated in my responses to AQW 40884/22-27 and AQW 40772/22-27, details of all resurfacing schemes planned for the 2025/26 financial year were provided in my Department’s annual report to Council. This report can be found at: DfI Roads Council Report Antrim & Newtownabbey 2025. Further schemes that are programmed as a result of additional in-year funding are announced in advance of delivery.Officials are currently considering the 2026/27 programme of works, details of which will be included in my Department’s 2026/27 annual report to Council. Programmes cannot be finalised until such times as budgets confirmed.In the meantime, my officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with the Department’s Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 40557/22-27, given Translink's assessment that the bridge is safe, to detail the reason why her Department does not view this as a case for adoption purposes.
answer_text: Loughshore Manor is a private development accessed via a bridge over the railway line which was constructed in consultation with Translink in 2008.  In 2015, planning consent was granted for a revised scheme which required the adoption of the road and footway over the already constructed bridge.At the time of construction, the bridge was not being considered for adoption and therefore technical approval was not required.  Since the revised planning consent the developer has been working with the department to gain the necessary compliance requirements for the bridge to allow the adoption of the associated road and footways.The developer has advised that they are unable to achieve design compliance for the bridge. As design compliance is a fundamental requirement of the adoption process, the Department is therefore unable to undertake any works at this development to bring it to an adoptable standard.Translink’s assessment that it is safe from a rail perspective does not constitute technical approval.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 40884/22-27, whether Ballyhenry Avenue features in the annual report.
answer_text: As stated in my responses to AQW 40884/22-27 and AQW 40772/22-27, details of all resurfacing schemes planned for the 2025/26 financial year were provided in my Department’s annual report to Council. This report can be found at: DfI Roads Council Report Antrim & Newtownabbey 2025. Further schemes that are programmed as a result of additional in-year funding are announced in advance of delivery.Officials are currently considering the 2026/27 programme of works, details of which will be included in my Department’s 2026/27 annual report to Council. Programmes cannot be finalised until such times as budgets confirmed.In the meantime, my officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with the Department’s Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 39876/22-27, to detail the information requested.
answer_text: As stated in my response to AQW 39876/22-27, details of all resurfacing schemes planned for the 2025/26 financial year were provided in my Department’s annual report to Council.  This report can be found at DfI Roads Eastern Division- 2025 Annual Report to BCC November 2025. Further schemes that are programmed as a result of additional in-year funding are announced in advance of delivery.Officials are currently considering the 2026/27 programme of works, details of which will be included in my Department’s 2026/27 annual report to Council. Programmes cannot be finalised until such times as budgets confirmed.In the meantime, my officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with the Department’s Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the reopening of the steps at Deacon Street, Belfast.
answer_text: The pedestrian steps at Deacon Street, Belfast, were closed in November 2024 due to safety concerns. My officials have completed a feasibility assessment and identified a repair solution involving the resetting of the existing granite slabs and the installation of a new bespoke handrail.These works are programmed for the 2026/27 financial year, subject to the availability of funding. Until these works are completed, an alternative pedestrian route remains available via Gainsborough Drive, North Queen Street and Deacon Street.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on his engagement with the hospitality industry in relation to Reval 2026.

To ask the Minister for the Economy whether her Department will engage with Student Finance Northern Ireland, in relation to the £6,500 tuition fee cap, which is restrictive to prospective post-graduate students applying to courses which exceed this cost.
answer_text: On Friday 6th March I announced a 54% increase in the Postgraduate Tuition Fee Loan to £10,000 following the outcome of a review and public consultation. My officials liaised with the Student Loans Company and the Education Authority—who collectively administer Higher Education student support to students from here under the banner "Student Finance NI”—throughout the review and consultation period, and the new loan amount will be available to post graduate taught students for the forthcoming academic year (2026/27). This rise closes the funding gap and future-proofs it for the next three academic years, at which time it will be subject to a further review.The various options considered in the consultation ensure that the maximum loan value is set at a level reflective of standard postgraduate fees. My officials will continue to monitor local postgraduate fee levels and take the necessary legislative steps to cap fees in the future if deemed required.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether her Department would consider a mechanism by which the Student Finance NI loan cap is indexed to course fees, to prevent the gaps in disparity between the maximum loan available and overall cost of specific courses becoming unreasonably significant.
answer_text: On Friday 6th March I announced a 54% increase in the Postgraduate Tuition Fee Loan to £10,000 following the outcome of a review and public consultation. My officials liaised with the Student Loans Company and the Education Authority—who collectively administer Higher Education student support to students from here under the banner "Student Finance NI”—throughout the review and consultation period, and the new loan amount will be available to post graduate taught students for the forthcoming academic year (2026/27). This rise closes the funding gap and future-proofs it for the next three academic years, at which time it will be subject to a further review.The various options considered in the consultation ensure that the maximum loan value is set at a level reflective of standard postgraduate fees. My officials will continue to monitor local postgraduate fee levels and take the necessary legislative steps to cap fees in the future if deemed required.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail when the last rates payment was received by Land and Property Services from Green Cross Art and Bookshop Limited.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department will consider resurfacing Hillview Park, Glengormley, in the 2026-27 resurfacing programme.
answer_text: Details of the resurfacing schemes planned for the 2026/27 financial year will be published in my Department’s annual report to Council.My officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with the Department’s Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any discussions she has had with HM Government regarding student finance interest payment levels for Northern Ireland students studying in mainland GB.
answer_text: Students from here receive their student loans under the same terms regardless of where they study. The interest rate is set at the lower of RPI or the Bank of England base rate plus one percent, and equals the lowest interest rate applied to any student loans in other regions. While only students from here continue to receive loans under this plan, other nations have historic borrowers to which these terms and conditions apply. It is therefore not possible to unilaterally make changes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether her route development policy will be published by 31 March 2026.
answer_text: My Department has been encouraged by the ongoing engagement of key stakeholders in both the aviation and tourism sectors in the development of the policy consultation document.  This engagement has supported a more comprehensive policy development process.Consequently, the public consultation on the aviation policy was launched on 27 March 2026 and will be open for eight weeks, closing on 22 May 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the reason why she does not have trilingual provision in her Departmental correspondence.
To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the vacant homes pilot schemes.
answer_text: My Department is currently in the early stages of planning a limited pilot exercise aimed at gathering information on issues related to vacancy in a mix of manageable sample areas, the details of how this will be achieved are still being determined. The purpose is to obtain a robust evidence base to inform any future policy decisions related to vacant properties. It is important to note that this is primarily a limited fact-finding pilot exercise that will not provide any new Departmental grant funding to bring vacant properties back into use, and no area will be disadvantaged by not participating in this pilot.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 36398/22-27, to provide an update.
answer_text: My officials have confirmed that the Treasury will provide funding for the North, ensuring that domestic electricity customers here receive comparable benefit in respect of their contribution to the Northern Ireland Renewables Obligation (NIRO).Work is ongoing with key partners to identify the most appropriate delivery mechanism.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on planning application SPD/2024/0076/F Comber Section 54 Application for Removal of Condition 10 of Planning Approval X/2009/0858/O.
answer_text: The application remains under consideration, and work is progressing on a planning report and recommendation.  A decision will be taken as soon as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the introduction of revised anti-social behaviour rules for social housing tenants.
answer_text: My Housing (Amendment) Bill will include provisions relating to injunctions against unacceptable behaviour, changes to grounds for possession and changes to eligibility for social housing and Full Duty homelessness status where serious unacceptable behaviour has occurred.It is my expectation that the Bill will be Introduced in May 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any discussions his Department has had with Home Office accommodation providers, to release void properties back into the housing market.
answer_text: I engaged with representatives from Mears in September 2025 to discuss local housing issues and the scale of their operations and expressed concern about he level of void properties in their portfolio.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 39159/22-27, to detail when the cost estimate will be completed.
answer_text: I can advise that the cost estimate for the scheme is ongoing including preliminary estimates from utility providers for service alterations. The junction layout is also to be modelled to advise designers of the working capacity of a revised layout.Subject to a successful outcome, the proposal will be considered for progression to detailed design, dependent on the availability of funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 40555/22-27, to detail the reason why she publishes other HMRC data, but will not publish the information requested in this Assembly Question.
answer_text: As per my answer to AQW 40555/22-27, HMRC is responsible for the publication of this data.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 40774/22-27, to change the timeframe to the last three months.
answer_text: Since international trade falls within my Ministerial remit, geopolitical issues are discussed regularly across a wide range of meetings and engagements. To identify and extract references from minutes over the last three months would not be feasible, as this would require a disproportionate use of staff resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the Warm Home Discount Scheme for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The Department for the Economy is not considering the introduction of a Warm Home Discount Scheme in the North. While similar schemes operate elsewhere, the smaller and distinct energy market here means an equivalent approach would be disproportionately costly and would increase bills for many consumers here.My Department has responsibility for energy efficiency policy to reduce household energy use and bills, supported by the Northern Ireland Sustainable Energy Programme (NISEP), which focuses on improving energy efficiency in lower income homes.Officials maintain regular engagement with the Department for Communities (DfC), which has lead responsibility for fuel poverty and has recently announced the Warm Healthy Homes strategy to further support low income households and improve energy efficiency.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on the progress of the Dilapidation Bill.
answer_text: The Dilapidation Bill is currently in Committee Stage, which has been extended to 15 May 2026 to allow the AERA Committee adequate time to scrutinise the Bill. 		Oral evidence sessions concluded on 22 January 2026 and the Committee has now finished its deliberations. Formal clause by clause scrutiny is scheduled to begin on 12 March 2026.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail all resurfacing schemes that will be on-site in the North Belfast constituency, before 31 March 2026.
answer_text: Carriageway resurfacing has recently been completed on Hanna Street, and works are ongoing on Somerton Road. Planned works on Canning Street, Alexander Gardens, Fortwilliam Parade, Somerton Close, Rosslea Way and Beechwood Avenue are also being progressed through the 7.85m Winter Recovery Roads Fund within this financial year. Details of all resurfacing schemes planned for the 2025/26 financial year are provided in my Department’s annual report to Council.My Department will continue to undertake routine inspections and actionable defects will be prioritised for repair in accordance with the Limited Service Policy in so far as resources permit.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any support available to Motability Car users appealing decisions by Motability Operations Ltd.
answer_text: My Department does not hold this information. The Department has no role in the administration of the Motability scheme or decisions taken by Motability Operations. Motability Operations can be contacted by calling:Motability Customer Services: 0300 456 4566, or by post at:Motability Operations22 Bishopsgate6th FloorLondonEC2N 4BQ

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when handrails will be (i) replaced; and (ii) installed, within Rathfern, Newtownabbey, at (a) Altnacreeve Park; (b) Lacken Gardens; (c) Ardranney Drive; and (d) Tulleevin Drive.
answer_text: I refer the Member to my answer to AQW 39469 / 22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when the online fault report, W261403301 for Jellicoe Drive, will be responded to.
answer_text: I would refer the Member to the online track a fault facility provided via the NI Direct website Track progress of a reported road fault or issue | nidirect.  Officials provide updates to the fault reported at each stage of the process.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail his Department's position on implementation of the principles of John’s Campaign, including (i) the visitation rights of family carers of patients living with dementia and Alzheimer’s in hospitals and other care settings; and (ii) their recognition from staff within those settings as essential partners in care of such patients.
answer_text: (i). My Department supports the principles of John’s Campaign and recognises the invaluable role that carers play as care partners. This is particularly pertinent for supporting people living with dementia when they are receiving treatment or care in an unfamiliar environment such as a hospital or care home. John’s Campaign advocates for flexible, needs -led access for family carers of people living with dementia, emphasising open visiting and recognition of carers as essential partners in care.  Family carers are recognised as experts in the person they support, providing continuity and reassurance for the person living with dementia. I support the principle that people living with dementia should be accompanied by those closest to them and that family carers should be welcomed as partners in care as this will reduce distress and support better outcomes for people living with dementia.(ii). My Department has facilitated the implementation of John’s Campaign in many of our hospital settings. This has included the provision of staff awareness training on the principles of John’s campaign and supporting open access visiting for family carers as well as carer involvement as core partners in establishing daily routines and contributing to assessment, care planning and decisionmaking. I acknowledge that further work can be done to ensure this initiative is sustained and extended to be  more widely available across our hospitals and within care home settings.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (i) for an update on proposed reforms for mandatory CCTV in abattoirs; and (ii) for his assessment of the UK independent Animal Welfare Committee October 2025 recommendation for the phasing out of high concentration CO2  stunning for pigs to be replaced by other methods.
answer_text: The Mandatory Use of Closed Circuit Television in Slaughterhouses (Northern Ireland) Regulations 2026 was considered by the Executive in January and I aim to complete the remaining steps to lay and make the legislation as soon as possible.I am aware of the recommendations made by the Animal Welfare Committee around the use of high concentration Co2 for stunning pigs. Ministers in the Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs have committed to consulting on a UK wide ban and there has been preliminary engagement with local industry here. I am open to Northern Ireland’s inclusion as part of that exercise in due course.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, following her meeting with the Commissioner for Ulster Scots and British Identity, to detail when her Department will produce an Ulster Scots policy.
answer_text: Following a productive introductory meeting with the Commissioner for Ulster Scots and Ulster British Tradition, my officials continue to engage with the Commissioner’s Office to discuss, among other matters, how my Department and its partner organisations can assist in the development and implementation of the UlsterScots Strategy, and explore the development of an Ulster Scots policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when bollards will be installed at Abotts Cross, Newtownabbey, to stop vehicles driving on the footpaths.
answer_text: Road safety is a high priority for my Department and we are committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and address the needs of all road users.I can confirm that bollards are planned for installation at this location before the end of March 2026.In the interim, concerns regarding driver behaviour should be reported to PSNI via their non-emergency number, 101.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when the work relating to W241261092, will be completed.
answer_text: I would refer the Member to the online track a fault facility provided via the NI Direct website Track progress of a reported road fault or issue | nidirect.  Officials provide updates to the fault reported at each stage of the process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any plans her Department has to resurface the roundabout at the top of the Prince Charles Way, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: Details of all resurfacing schemes planned for the 2025/26 financial year are provided in my Department’s annual report to Council.In the meantime, my Department will continue to undertake routine inspections and actionable defects will be prioritised for repair in accordance with the Limited Service Policy in so far as resources permit.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any cost incurred, as a result of the agreement to expand Ireland's Hidden Heartland into Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I have provided £300,000 of additional support to Tourism NI from my Departments 2025/26 allocation for a targeted marketing campaign following signing of the licencing agreement to include Fermanagh in the Ireland’s Hidden Heartlands brand. The campaign launched on 16 February and will run until 31 March.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) for her assessment of the condition of the road surface at Ballyhenry Avenue, Glengormley; and (ii) to detail any plans her Department has to improve it.
answer_text: Details of all resurfacing schemes planned for the 2025/26 financial year are provided in my Department’s annual report to Council.In the meantime, my Department will continue to undertake routine inspections and actionable defects will be prioritised for repair in accordance with the Limited Service Policy in so far as resources permit.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any plans her Department has to resurface Hillview Avenue West, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: Details of all resurfacing schemes planned for the 2025/26 financial year are provided in my Department’s annual report to Council.In the meantime, my Department will continue to undertake routine inspections and actionable defects will be prioritised for repair in accordance with the Limited Service Policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the sale of the land owned at Abbeycentre, Newtownabbey, by the Northern Ireland Transport Holding Company.
answer_text: Translink have deemed the Abbey Trading Centre site surplus to operational requirements, and as such are taking steps to dispose of it. As per Civil Service policy the site will initially be offered to other government bodies. If there is no interest, then the site will be placed for sale on the open market.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on land purchased by her Department to deliver Belfast Rapid Transport Phase 2 to North Belfast.
answer_text: Now that the Outline Business Case has been approved, my officials will engage with their counterparts in Antrim and Newtownabbey Borough Council with regard to the acquisition of the land.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 39262/22-27, to publish the number of times it has been recorded in minutes, as requested.
answer_text: Due to the sheer volume of minutes held, I am not in a position to provide a figure. As stipulated in my first answer, I meet with many overseas businesses and prospective investors, and geopolitical issues are often discussed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail how her Department can improve road safety for cars exiting Sandyholme Park, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: PSNI collision data have not recorded any personal injury collisions at this junction in the past 3 years.  On the basis of this information, this location is not identified as a priority for additional measures at this time; however, it will continue to be kept under review as further data and information become available.Concerns regarding driver behaviour can also be reported to the PSNI using their non-emergency 101 number.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the drafting of the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee Scheme.
answer_text: The Final Scheme Design for the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee (REPG) scheme was published by my Department in September 2025. I have also brought forward a paper to the Executive to agree the final policy.		Once Executive approval is secured, my department aims to introduce the required primary legislation for the REPG to the Assembly before summer recess 2026. Once the Primary Bill is in place, subordinate legislation will be taken forward to set out the detailed arrangements.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when the Higher Education Financial Needs Assessment will be published.
answer_text: A final copy of the Financial Needs Assessment was recently provided to me. I have met with officials to discuss the next steps, which will include engaging with the local Higher Education Institutions as key stakeholders to the Assessment. Further details regarding publication of the report findings will be available in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 39674/22-27, (i) for her Department's estimate of the cost to bring the development up to an adoptable standard; and (ii) whether any of the bond will be used to surface the roads.
answer_text: The developer has advised that they are unable to achieve design compliance for the bridge. As design compliance is a fundamental requirement of the adoption process, the Department is therefore unable to undertake any works at this development to bring it to an adoptable standard.The surety held for the development is only released to the Department once all works have been completed and the site has been adopted. The Department cannot selectively undertake individual elements of work using the bond; if works were to commence, the Department would be required to take all necessary steps to achieve adoption, including securing design compliance for the bridge and for the costs of all such works.A highlevel assessment of costs indicates that the works required to bring the site to an adoptable standard would significantly exceed the value of the bond held.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when the yellow boxes at the junction of (i) the Ballysillan Road and Crumlin Road; and (ii) the Ligoniel Road and Crumlin Road, Belfast, will be repainted.
answer_text: Remedial works to refresh both yellow boxes at this location have been instructed following a recent inspection and it is anticipated that these works will be completed over the next two weeks, subject to favourable conditions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 36213/22-27; and AQW 38714/22-27, to detail any action he will take in light of funding provided by his Department to the organisation.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 39158/22-27, to detail a timeline of when resources will be available for the survey at Hopefield Avenue, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: The survey for a pedestrian crossing on the Shore Road close to Hopefield Avenue will take place within the next 4 weeks. I have asked officials to update you once the assessment has been undertaken.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 38937/22-27, to detail (i) whether Translink's approval was a legal requirement; (ii) Translink's assessment of the safety of the bridge, in light of her Department's view that technical approval cannot be achieved; and (iii) any proposed actions her Department will now take to ensure technical approval.
answer_text: There was no legal requirement for the developer to obtain approval from Translink for building over the railway. 	Translink’s assessment is that the bridge is safe from a rail perspective.  The developer has advised that they are unable to achieve design compliance for the bridge. As design compliance is a fundamental requirement of the adoption process, the Department is therefore unable to undertake any works at this development to bring it to an adoptable standard.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 39673/22-27, to detail whether she will publish the figures as requested.
answer_text: HMRC is responsible for the publication of these figures.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 38821/22 27, to define, as soon as possible.
answer_text: A Good Jobs Charter public consultation is planned for launch in April 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 37495/22-27, to detail when the business case addendum for the Rathcoole Primary School parking scheme will be completed.
answer_text: The Education Authority has advised that the business case addendum is currently being prepared and is expected to be complete before the end of February 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when the Utility Regulator (Support for Decarbonisation Preparation) Bill will be introduced to the Assembly.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any plans her Department has to address the safety concerns at the junction of North Derby and Shore Road, Belfast.
answer_text: I can advise that officials are not aware of a safety issue at the junction of York Road/North Derby Street and Castleton Avenue and have no plans to change the current junction layout.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the timeframe for the next steps for the Rates Revaluation process.
answer_text: As I set out in my statement to the Assembly on 2 February 2026, I decided to stop Reval2026 for a full examination of the issues. I will take time to consider evidence before deciding next steps. My full statement is available at:Finance Minister Oral Statement - Reval 2026 | Department of Finance

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the reason why energy insecurity has risen since she took office.
answer_text: Security of supply is a key priority for my Department, who work closely with the Utility Regulator and the wider energy industry to review and monitor security of supply and ensure resilience of the system.In 2025, due to unprecedented damage at Ballylumford power station following storm Darragh, and unforeseen technical issues at Coolkeeragh power station, it was necessary to implement mitigations to ensure security of supply.These mitigations were successful in alleviating the security of supply concerns identified by SONI, the electricity transmission operator, over the winter months of October to December 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the reason why the consumption of renewable electricity has fallen since she took office.
answer_text: The reduction in the renewable share of electricity consumption reflects technical limits on how much renewable electricity the system can safely accommodate at any given time.A key factor is continued high levels of dispatch down. The System Operator’s Draft Dispatch Down Action Plan confirms that system stability requirements, operational security rules and existing network limitations routinely prevent the system from utilising all the renewable generation that is available.Furthermore, the System Operator’s Transmission Development Plan identifies the need for reinforcement and investment to move renewable electricity from where it is generated to where it is needed. Until this infrastructure is delivered, renewable output will continue to be constrained.My Department does not hold all of the levers required to address these system wide issues. Grid investment, system operator actions, planning processes and cross jurisdictional infrastructure programmes all play essential roles.My Department’s work is focused on working collectively to deliver the transition in a way that ensures value for money for consumers.
