question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail when further detail on his proposals regarding boxing in Northern Ireland will be advanced.
answer_text: I will be making an announcement in relation to the Terms of Reference and membership of the oversight panel in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any planned works her Department has to improve the road condition of the Ballyclare Road, Glengormley.
answer_text: I can advise that a section of the Ballyclare Road, from the Antrim Road junction to the Glencairn Drive junction, will be resurfaced through the delivery of the local council’s Environmental Improvement scheme, which is anticipated to commence in July 2026.  My officials have also identified a further resurfacing works from the Glencairn Drive junction to the mini roundabout at Jubilee Way, which will be carried out on completion of the scheme.Officials will also continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with my Department’s current Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on her work to ensure Northern Ireland benefits from the work of Great British Energy.
answer_text: Officials from my Department engage regularly with the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero (DESNZ) and with representatives from Great British Energy (GBE), following the publication of their Local Power Plan. It is important to note that GBE operates independently, with its own governance and strategic direction, however officials continue to engage to maximise benefit from the Local Power Plan and its other strategic priorities.I accepted the offer of £1.66 million investment from GBE last year which has supported renewable energy and battery storage projects in our Further Education colleges. This funding will enable them to generate clean, more affordable energy and reduce their reliance on fossil fuels.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on her work on Carer's Leave.
answer_text: I intend to introduce an entitlement to Carer’s leave in my forthcoming Employment Rights Bill. This will provide a new right for employees with caring responsibilities to take up to one week of unpaid carer’s leave every 12 months to care for a family member or dependant with a long-term care need.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on the introduction of the Sentencing Bill.
answer_text: Executive approval for the Introduction of the Sentencing Bill was granted on 12 February 2026. The Bill will be introduced as soon as Assembly pre-introduction formalities, which are underway, have been completed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) whether her Department is aware of pavement parking at the junction of Antrim Road, Hightown Road and Ballyclare Road; and (ii) to detail any action her Department can take to address this.
answer_text: My Department is aware of reports of pavement parking at the junction of Antrim Road, Hightown Road and Ballyclare Road, Glengormley. Traffic Attendants undertake a programmed mobile patrol in this area, and patrol arrangements are kept under regular review to ensure they remain appropriate.In areas with Double Yellow Lines restrictions, Penalty Charge Notices (PCNs) are issued to vehicles parked in contravention. These restrictions apply to both the carriageway and the footway. Where vehicles are observed parking to the rear of the footpath, these areas are unadopted private land and unenforceable by the Department.Following the public consultation on options to tackle inconsiderate pavement parking and publication of the findings report, I have decided to proceed with introducing a ban on parking a vehicle wholly on the footway, parking across dropped kerbs and double parking. This approach represents the most practical and effective solution at this stage, taking account of feasibility, enforcement considerations, and legislative vires. It is my aim to introduce these changes in the latter part of 2026, subject to the legislative process. These measures are another positive step to help further address inconsiderate pavement parking across the North, making our streets safer and more accessible, particularly for disabled people, older citizens and those with prams.Additionally, vehicles parked causing an obstruction on the road or footway are a matter for the PSNI.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the School Improvement Programme for Belfast Royal Academy. [R]
answer_text: The business case for the Belfast Royal Academy School Enhancement Programme (SEP) project was approved in January 2026.  The project is currently progressing through RIBA Stage 2 (Scheme Design).Funding is available to progress SEP projects through the design stages to pre-tender. A decision to release projects to tender for a contractor will be taken on a case-by-case basis, dependent on budget availability at that time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (I) the total amount spent by the Local Economic Partnerships; to date; and (ii) the breakdown of spend.
answer_text: All 11 Councils have been incurring expenditure on Local Economic Partnerships as part of their work to date, some councils have submitted claims for payment. Details of amounts spent will be available once these claims are approved.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any steps his Department can take under its remit for the community and voluntary sector, to assist the aims of the UK Strategic Defence Review 2025 in Northern Ireland, to increase Cadet Forces by 30 per cent, by 2030.
answer_text: Funding is provided to seven organisations through the Volunteering Infrastructure Support Programme and I am aware that Volunteer Now, our largest strategic volunteer partner, have had engagement with the Army Cadets regarding advice for recruiting volunteers and how their volunteers could be recognised.		Whilst Cadet Forces do not fall within the remit of my department, I recognise the value of these organisations and the contribution they make to our young people and wider society. I also support the UK Strategic Defence Review 2025 to increase Cadet Forces by 30 per cent, by 2030.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any road safety projects programmed for North Belfast in the (i) 2025-26 financial year; and (ii) 2026-27 financial year.
answer_text: Details of the planned works programmes are included in my Department’s Annual Report to Council.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the progress of delivering a new build for Seaview Primary School.
answer_text: The Education Authority Asset Management Team is finalising the business case for Seaview Primary School.  It is anticipated that the business case will be lodged with the Department for consideration by the end of February 2026. Should the business case be approved by the Department, the preferred option will be progressed in design.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the projects that will be supported by the Enhanced Economic Investment Zone.
answer_text: The £150 million Enhanced Investment Zone programme has been approved by the Executive.  British Government approval is anticipated to follow shortly. The programme identifies a number of thematic areas for spend such as Skills, Infrastructure, Research & Development and Business Support. These are designed to support productivity improvements in the Advanced Manufacturing sector with specific focus on high potential clusters of photonics and biotechnology. A number of significant projects have been identified for potential funding and discussions are continuing with key private sector partners and others to bring these forward for approval.  No individual projects have been approved to date.		This approach ensures interventions are shaped with industry and academia and aligned with areas of genuine competitive strength. The programme is designed to be flexible so that funding can be reprioritised, with projects added or removed, in order to respond quickly to new and emerging market opportunities.		Once individual business cases are completed and approved, we will provide further detail on specific projects.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to provide details of her forthcoming trip to the United States of America.
answer_text: The itinerary for my visit to the United States of America is still being developed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on her Department's interconnection policy.
answer_text: Research has been completed to support the development of interconnection policy, and its findings are informing the development of the policy in this area. As previously committed, I plan to outline the policy by the end of 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the reason why her Department has not published a cluster strategy; (ii) the amount spent during the development process; and (iii) when the strategy will be published.
answer_text: My Department has published seven sectoral action plans across which clustering is a common theme, and where actions were agreed with delivery partners to drive collaboration and networking activity within each sector.Complementary work to support cluster development is being undertaken through programmes such as PEACEPLUS, Shared Island Fund, Local Innovation Partnership Fund and the Enhanced Investment Zone.During 2024 the department worked closely with Invest NI to co-develop, fund and launch an £11m industry-led Cluster Acceleration Programme to develop priority clusters over the next 10 years – that Programme is live and making good progress.In the context of the shortened mandate I remain focussed on action.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail how the UK Local Innovation Partnership Funds will benefit Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The Local Innovation Partnerships Fund (LIPF) is a £500 million UK Research & Innovation (UKRI) led programme designed to accelerate innovationled regional growth through locally led partnerships between government, academia and industry. At least £30 million has been confirmed to support innovation clusters in the Belfast-Derry Innovation Corridor. The five-year funding (2026-2031) will support the development and scaling of highpotential innovation clusters.Following an Expression of Interest process, two priority clusters have been selected by a new LIPF Innovation Panel: Data Enabled Translation for Connected Health (DETCH) and, Digital-Twin Infrastructure for Green Innovation In Transport Systems. (DIGITS). These clusters were chosen for their strong regional strengths and significant potential for economic impact.Within these clusters funding will support project portfolios that strengthen our research, innovation and commercialisation capability; attract private investment; and deliver innovationled growth and good jobs across the North. Although delivery is corridorfocused, benefits will extend more widely through supply chain and collaboration opportunities. Clusters located outside the Belfast-Derry Corridor can also apply directly to UKRI for up to £20 million per place through a separate competitive strand of the Fund.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail her Department's planned resurfacing schemes, in North Belfast, in the 2026-27 financial year.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment due to years of underfunding, and this has created significant challenges in our ability to maintain the road network in a safe condition.While there are many roads that would benefit from investment, due to limited budget resources, resurfacing work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need for intervention.Details of the resurfacing schemes planned for the 2026/27 financial year will be published in my Department’s annual report to Council.On 2nd December, I was pleased to launch the new Road Maintenance Strategy which is now open for public consultation and in support of the new approach I have also commissioned a full digital road survey of all roads in the North.The digital road survey and the analysis of the data it produces, is an investment in the future of our road network enabling a better understanding of the condition of that network and therefore inform strategic decision making going forward.Whilst the strategy and survey will provide greater detail to inform strategic decision making, this will also continue to be supported by the reporting of potholes and surface defects via the online portal and maintenance staff will continue to inspect and make safe defects in accordance with our policy to ensure the safety of the travelling public.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the amount held in bond by her Department, for Loughshore Manor, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: The developer bonded Loughshore Manor in four parts of varying sizes. The total amount of bond held by the department under the Private Streets (NI) Order 1980 is £231,400.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the (i) number of; and (ii) value of, compensation payments, made by her Department for damaged cars as a result of potholes in North Belfast, since January 2022.
answer_text: The management information my Department holds in relation to roads related public liability claims can be found online at: https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/road-asset-maintenance-directorate-management-information-roads-related-claims-period-20202021-20242025.Table 4.2 provides details of concluded claims in which compensation was paid over the last five full financial years, broken down by Section Office and claim type.  Table 7 provides details of claim expenditure for the last five full financial years broken down by Section Office, claim type and expenditure type. Please note Section Offices broadly align with Council areas.  North Belfast is primarily covered by Belfast City Council. However, some peripheral northern areas may fall under the jurisdiction of Antrim and Newtownabbey Borough Council.  These council areas are covered by two Section Offices, namely Belfast North and Antrim and Newtownabbey.Claim figures have been extracted from DfI's Claims Management System, whereas the financial data has been extracted from the NICS accounting system, Account NI. Unfortunately, as the NICS Accounting System does not record the cause of incidents that lead to compensation payments, it is not possible to disaggregate the amount of compensation paid out by defect type.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the percentage of growth in exports in (i) Northern Ireland; and (ii) Great Britain, in the last year.
answer_text: The requested statistics can be found on this website Regional trade data table.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any consideration her Department has given to road signage at Cedar Lodge School, Landsdowne Park, Belfast.
answer_text: Regrettably, when assessed against these competing priorities, Cedar Lodge School did not score highly enough for inclusion in a works programme. However, a recent site survey has identified that there are no standard ‘School’ signs in the vicinity of Cedar Lodge School. My officials will therefore arrange for signs to erected at the earliest opportunity.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance, pursuant to AQW 37497/22-27, for an update on any discussions with HM Treasury.
answer_text: Treasury has now confirmed that it considers 75% of NI Renewables Obligation (NIRO) costs as a comparable offer to 75% of the Renewables Obligation (RO) in Britian. £81m Resource AME has been set aside for from 2026-27 to 2028-29. I understand that DfE officials are working on a business case which it will submit to Treasury, detailing the design and delivery assumptions around the scheme here.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail when Abbots Court Supported Living Scheme in Newtownabbey will be extended, in light of unprecedented demand for supported living in that area.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Northern Ireland Housing Executive.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“Abbotts Court is a service delivered by the Northern Health & Social Care Trust and referrals are made through Health & Social services.At this time, we are not aware of any requests or plans for the expansion of Abbots Court Supported Living Scheme in Newtownabbey. We also do not hold information on demand for this service, nor on the number of applications versus allocations in the last year.”

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 37578/22-27, to detail the target date for the installation of the signs.
answer_text: The earliest estimated date for the installation of the missing directional signs at the Cloughfern roundabout is April 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any consideration her Department has given to (i) a controlled crossing point; and (ii) school safety road signs, at St Therese of Lisieux Primary School, Belfast.
answer_text: Road safety is a high priority for my Department, and we are committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and to address the needs of all road users. We are acutely aware that children and young people are amongst the most vulnerable groups using our roads, particularly around schools.I have asked my officials to add this location to the list of assessments for controlled pedestrian crossings which will be undertaken as resources permit.You will also be aware that the Department is currently taking forward a third tranche of part time 20mph zones at 40 schools across the North which are listed on the Department’s website.  This tranche focuses on rural schools that are located on roads where the national speed limit applies and will bring the total number delivered to 273 schools. I am also undertaking a consultation on speed limits, seeking views on the wider use of permanent 20mph speed limits, the lowering of national speed limit on rural single and dual carriageways, and alignment of HGV speed limits with other regions. This consultation will help inform on the use of additional tranches of part time speed limits at schools and permanent 20mph speed limits on roads in and around schools.I have also asked officials to undertake an assessment of the current school signage to ensure it remains appropriate at this location.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on her Department's work to (i) replace and repair unsafe or damaged handrails; and (ii) provide additional handrails at steps, in Rathfern Estate, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: Whilst there are no plans to provide additional handrails, they do form part of the highway infrastructure within this area and are subject to routine cyclical inspections.  As part of this process, any defects found to meet the intervention levels of the current policy are instructed for repair.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of permits granted by her Department to (i) private developers; (ii) utilities companies; and (iii) public bodies, for works requiring excavation or breaking of the road surface on the Shore Road, between its junction with Station Road, Newtownabbey, and Skegoneill Avenue, Belfast, since 2021.
answer_text: Since 2021, my Department permitted one Article 3(4c) road bond to be taken out by a developer to develop a retail unit and one Article 78 consent for a geo-technical company to complete bore holes on the Shore Road, between its junction with Station Road, Newtownabbey, and Skegoneill Avenue, Belfast. Utilities, who have a statutory right to place and maintain their apparatus in the road in order to supply services, completed 521 pieces of work on this section of the Shore Road in the same period. No permits were given to other public bodies to complete works requiring excavation or breaking of the road surface on this section of the Shore Road.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) for her assessment of the current condition of road markings at Sandknowes Roundabout, Newtownabbey; and (ii) to detail any planned works her Department has to upgrade the markings.
answer_text: Road markings serve a very important function in conveying information and requirements about the road network and there is no doubt that poorly maintained road markings can have a negative impact on road safety. With this in mind, my officials undertake regular routine maintenance inspections of all roads during which all defects, including defective signs and road markings, are noted. Road markings are not replaced within a routine/specific time frame, rather when approximately 30-40% of the marked area is ineffective or worn away. Where the condition of road markings falls below the required standards, they are renewed as soon as possible within the available funding limits. Repairs are prioritised to concentrate on regulatory road markings and those longitudinal lines necessary for continued road safety.The last maintenance inspection of the Sandyknowes Roundabout was on 30th January 2026 when a number of road marking defects were identified, which have been instructed to our contractor for remedial action in line with current policy.Officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with my Department’s limited service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 38291/22-27, whether her Department will consider further action, if the assurances for a completion of the roads and footways is not forthcoming.
answer_text: Pursuant to AQW 38291/22-27, my officials met with the developer on Wednesday 4th February 2026 to discuss a programme of works that would achieve a resolution for residents, and to ensure that the developer fulfils all its obligations to allow the adoption of Mount Gilbert and again emphasised the importance of completing these works as quickly as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department is considering resurfacing schemes for any section of the Shore Road, both Newtownabbey and Belfast, within the North Belfast constituency.
answer_text: While there are many roads that would benefit from investment, due to limited budget resources, resurfacing work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need for intervention.  The resurfacing of the Shore Road will be kept under consideration should further funding become available.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 38384//22-27, whether the response will be published before 31 March 2026.
answer_text: Following my response to AQW 38384/22-27, the “Using Biofuels to Transition Away from Fossil Fuels for Heating - Departmental Response Report” was published on the Department’s website on 27th January 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 38293/22-27, to detail her economic assessment.
answer_text: I provided my assessment in response to AQW 38293/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 38933/22-27, in light of the remarks of her Permanent Secretary that this must go before the Executive, to detail why she has not confirmed that position.
answer_text: I have confirmed the position, and I am happy to do so again that I will make the decision on whether Executive Approval is required as the work progresses and the content and scope of the policy is more developed.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on delivering a resolution to the ongoing industrial action by the Criminal Bar Association.
To ask the Minister of Health for an update on his engagement with the Northern Ireland Children's Hospice to achieve 50 per cent government funding.
answer_text: I thank Mr Brett for his continued interest in the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice, I recognise the valuable role the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice plays in providing vital care for babies, children and young people with life limiting and life-threatening conditions. I also recognise the extremely challenging financial context within which hospices and other charities are operating at present.In the 2024/25 financial year my Department provided £1.75 million direct funding to the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice. This will be uplifted to over £1.8 million in 2025/26, to deliver a range of services to children and their families. The services funded include overnight short breaks, family support, hospice at home, bereavement support for the family network and palliative care support.As I have previously advised the member in September 2025, my Department has been engaging with the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice to understand the costs incurred in delivering the vital services that they provide.My Department will now be undertaking a benchmarking exercise to consider the level of funding provided to the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice for services commissioned and it is expected that this will be completed and shared with the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice by the end of this financial year. You will also be aware of the recent Health Committee inquiry in relation to access to Palliative Care Services within Northern Ireland, and the recommendations that have followed. I am at present considering these recommendations, however you will appreciate that the Department of Health faces a number of significant financial challenges and competing priorities at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 38489/22-27, whether the publication launch will be before 2026 summer recess.
answer_text: As noted in AQW 38489 and AQW 38934, the consultation on biomethane network connection costs and gas quality is planned for later in 2026.	Departmental officials are also working in collaboration with the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (DAERA) and other key stakeholders through the Inter Departmental Biomethane Group to help develop biomethane policy. Timelines for publication or launch will be confirmed once this work progresses further.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of overseas businesses and prospective investors she has met with, not including Cantor Fitzgerald, in which a discussion on geopolitical issues is recorded in the minutes of the meeting, since 7 October 2023.
answer_text: I meet with many overseas businesses and prospective investors, and geopolitical issues are often discussed.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the number of convicted offenders who have been deported, as per section 32 of the UK Borders Act 2007, since 2020,
answer_text: The deportation of convicted offenders is a matter for the Home Office Foreign National Offenders Returns Command (FNORC). As the Northern Ireland Prison Service is not informed of FNORC decisions to deport, it does not hold the requested data. You may wish to direct your request to FNORC.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the adoption of the Glen Corr Development, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: Road adoption by my Department is governed by The Private Streets (NI) Order 1980 (The Order) and roads, including those within private developments, will only be considered for adoption if brought to standard through the Private Streets Determination process or by owners/frontages in accordance with Article 9 of the Order.Glen Corr is an ongoing development and requires further works to be carried out by the developer to bring the area up to an adoptable standard.On 25 September 2025 I announced the introduction of new Private Streets Enforcement Guidance for staff.  This guidance will greatly reduce the risk of developments, constructed from January 2024, remaining unadopted. My officials continue to monitor the progress of all housing developments and engage with developers to ensure timely adoption through the normal Private Streets process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) whether she anticipates that her proposals for Graduated Driver Licencing will result in lower car insurance prices for young drivers; and (ii) to detail any engagement she has had with the insurance sector on this.
answer_text: (i) The primary purpose of Graduated Driver Licensing (GDL) is to improve road safety for young and novice drivers. Evidence from other jurisdictions, such as New Zealand, Canada and several Australian states, indicates that GDL models can lead to meaningful and sustained reductions in collisions involving this age group. Should similar benefits be realised here, a corresponding reduction in insurance claims may follow. Any impact on insurance premiums will ultimately depend on insurers’ assessment of evidence demonstrating lower risk and reduced claims costs over time.(ii) I have engaged directly with representatives of the insurance industry sector on the potential impact of GDL. This included a meeting in June of last year with the Association of British Insurers (ABI), where GDL was considered alongside broader factors affecting motor insurance costs. Further engagement took place through correspondence with the Motor Insurance Taskforce, of which the ABI is a member. That correspondence reaffirmed the issues discussed at a meeting I attended with the Minister of Finance and the then British Government’s Minister for the Future of Roads, Lilian Greenwood MP. Agenda items included discussions around local concerns about insurance affordability. I also reiterated that my Department’s focus remains on measures within its remit to help reduce the underlying risk that insurers price against, including the implementation of GDL.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) when the last inspection of the condition of Carolhill Road, Newtownabbey was carried out by her Department; (ii) any planned works; and (iii) her Department's assessment of its current condition.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government and this has created significant challenges in our ability to maintain the road network in a safe condition.  I can confirm that the most recent highway inspection at this location was carried out on 4 September 2025.  The inspection identified 3 surface defects for repair, in accordance with my Department’s current policy.While there are many roads that would benefit from investment, due to limited budget resources, resurfacing work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need for intervention.  The resurfacing of Carolhill Road will be kept under consideration should further funding become available.On 2nd December, I was pleased to launch the new Road Maintenance Strategy which is now open for public consultation and in support of the new approach I have also commissioned a full digital road survey of all roads in the North.The digital road survey and the analysis of the data it produces, is an investment in the future of our road network enabling a better understanding of the condition of that network and therefore inform strategic decision making going forward.Whilst the strategy and survey will provide greater detail to inform strategic decision making, this will also continue to be supported by the reporting of potholes and surface defects via the online portal and maintenance staff will continue to inspect and make safe defects in accordance with our policy to ensure the safety of the travelling public.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 30805/22-27, whether he will publish his response to the consultation process.
answer_text: As I outlined in the recent Assembly debate, I am committed to doing everything possible to improve paediatric pathology services, including exploring the potential development of an allisland service and the use of lessinvasive postmortem approaches. Scoping work in both these areas has commenced. My conversations with my counterpart in the Republic of Ireland, while positive, have confirmed they do not currently have the capacity to undertake additional post-mortems from Northern Ireland. Their immediate priority is to build the necessary capacity that could support an all-island approach in the future through the training and retention of staff with appropriate specialist expertise. It is important to acknowledge that this is in the context of a global shortage of paediatric pathologists and is likely to take several years, and progress will depend on factors beyond our direct control. My Department is also exploring the feasibility of less invasive post-mortem techniques being safely and appropriately adopted in Northern Ireland which may provide clinical answers for some families and mitigate the need for transportation in the future, however these approaches remain at a relatively early stage of development internationally. My Department will continue to engage constructively with colleagues in the Republic of Ireland and in England to explore every possible option for improving these services for families at this most difficult time. However, it is not possible to be definitive on timescales at this stage as progress will depend on external workforce and system-wide factors.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 37761/22-27, to detail when this work will be completed.
answer_text: Departmental Officials have discussed residential parking provision within Rathcoole with the NIHE. The Housing Executive have agreed to contact you directly regarding this and will keep me updated on progress.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the upgrade to the junction of Shore Road and Fortwilliam at Mount Vernon, Belfast.
answer_text: As outlined in my answer to AQW 35624/22-27, the cost estimate is still to be undertaken.Subject to a successful outcome, the proposal will be considered for progression to detailed design, dependent on the availability of funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the road safety pedestrian crossing survey at Hopefield Avenue and Shore Road, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: With regard to the crossing at Hopefield Avenue and Shore Road, Newtownabbey, an assessment of this location has not yet been undertaken.  The location is currently on the list of assessments to be completed and will be undertaken as resources permit.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 37574/22-27, to detail those funding methods.
answer_text: As previously outlined in my answer to AQW 37574/22-27 I have asked my officials to consider possible alternative funding methods which could facilitate the scheme progression. As this work is at a very early stage and will take several months to complete, I cannot comment further on the detail at present.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on her work with the Commissioner for the Ulster Scots and the Ulster British tradition.
answer_text: I am looking forward to working with the Commissioner for the Ulster Scots and am meeting Mr Reynolds in the next short while.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department will follow NHS England in approving and funding the use of abiraterone.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) when the last road condition inspection took place for the Whitewell Road, North Belfast, (ii) the estimated date of the next routine inspection at this location; and (iii) whether this road is currently being considered for future resurfacing schemes.
answer_text: The Whitewell Road was last inspected on 14 January 2026, 23 carriageway defects and 2 footway defects were recorded for repair. An additional ad hoc inspection was completed on 19 January 2026 with two blocked gullies identified for cleaning and a carriageway defect picked up for repair. A further routine inspection is programmed to be completed before 14 February 2026.  Details of schemes being taking forward will be included in my Department’s Annual Report to Council.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any assessment that has been undertaken of future rail provision in North Belfast, in particular why no new rail halts are identified in the Shaping the Future plan, for areas such as Giant’s Park and Loughside, despite its designation for major regeneration and its proximity to an existing rail corridor.
answer_text: The All Island Strategic Rail Review included a recommendation that consideration be given to new stations in the Belfast and Derry city regions. As set out in the Project Prioritisation Strategy, new stations will be progressed as “business as usual”, subject to the necessary approvals and funding. This means that Translink will consider the need for additional stations through their usual network planning process.Translink currently has no plans to develop a new station at Giant Park in the short to medium term. Its modelling of the wider network has indicated that provision of any further railway halts on the Larne /Antrim line between Whiteabbey and York Street could only be considered following capacity increases, either through the introduction of battery-electric trains or infrastructure enhancements.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for her assessment of whether Translink’s Shaping the Future rail plan reflects adopted Local Development Plans (LDP), given that the Antrim and Newtownabbey Borough Council LDP 2030 identifies a future railway halt at Merville Garden Village, at the Abbey Centre, yet it does not appear in the published strategy.
answer_text: The reference to a Merville halt in the Antrim and Newtownabbey Borough Council Plan Strategy document and its omission from the Shaping the Future rail review reflects the differences in purpose and remit between these documents. The Plan Strategy is the statutory first part of the Local Development Plan prepared by the Council under the Planning Act (NI) 2011. Its purpose is to set the longterm spatial planning framework for the whole council area through establishing a vision, strategic objectives, spatial growth strategy, strategic policies and detailed management policies that guide development decisions. The Council has identified the Merville halt in its Plan Strategy because it remains a valid local policy aspiration for the Council. While the Plan Strategy may identify infrastructure such as new railway halts, all proposals requiring public expenditure remain subject to economic appraisal and resource availability. The Shaping the Future is based on a series of feasibility studies and is intended to support strategic rail investment planning and therefore prioritises major systemwide enhancements such as electrification, intercity journeytime improvements, reinstated lines, and airport connectivity. The proposal for a halt at Merville did not fall within any of the areas which were the subject of the feasibility studies.  However, it is recognised that wider network capacity constraints would require that any such proposals to be considered after route electrification and capacity enhancement on the Antrim / Larne line.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) when Translink gave approval to the developer of Loughshore Manor, Newtownabbey, to build a bridge over its railway line; and (ii) any action Translink can take against developers who develop over its railways lines without permission.
answer_text: Antrim and Newtownabbey Borough Council is the relevant planning authority for the planning application associated with this development and any concerns about the planning application should be raised directly with the council. Prior to the granting of the Planning Permission for the development at Loughshore Manor in 2007, Translink were consulted on this development work. It did not object to the proposals including the construction of a new replacement bridge.  If necessary, Translink have several processes in place to protect the safety of the railway network during any development works.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when she will lay the regulations extending Parental Bereavement Leave and Pay.
answer_text: The policy positions and regulations for statutory paid leave in the event of miscarriage have been shared with the Committee for scrutiny. These will be laid in accordance with Assembly procedures once the Committee completes its considerations. The amending regulations regarding Day One rights will proceed through the confirmatory process, while those relating to miscarriage will follow the draft affirmative procedure, requiring debate and formal approval before enactment. Implementation of the Day One rights provisions is contingent upon the approval of the miscarriage-related amendments.I trust that the Assembly will support the timely passage of these regulations without delay, ensuring that this crucial commitment to families is fully honoured and protections are available from 6 April 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) to detail the current rate of renewable energy consumption in Northern Ireland; and (ii) for her assessment of the likelihood of meeting the legal obligations by 2030.
answer_text: For the 12-month period October 2024 to September 2025, 44.2% of total metered electricity consumption in the north came from metered renewable sources. Delivering additional renewable electricity generation is complex, demanding a careful balance between security of supply, affordability, and legislative compliance. It requires joined-up working, across government and with our delivery partners. My Department does not hold all the levers, so collaboration is essential to unlock progress. Grid capacity remains a constraint, so new and upgraded transmission infrastructure is essential. My Department continues to work closely with the System Operator, and other partners, on the measures needed to accommodate more renewable generation, including actions set out in their Dispatch Down Plan and Operational Policy Roadmap.I also welcome the revised Strategic Planning Policy Statement on Renewable and Low Carbon Energy that offers a more supportive planning framework for renewable deployment.My Department’s work is focused on working collectively to deliver the transition in a way that ensures value for money for consumers.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether the biomethane policy will go out for consultation in March 2026.
answer_text: As noted in AQW 38489, a public consultation on the treatment of connection costs and gas quality standards is planned in 2026.	The Inter Departmental Biomethane Group has been reestablished in collaboration with the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (DAERA) and other key stakeholders to develop biomethane policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she will bring the proposed Ethical Investment Framework to the Executive for approval, given the remarks made by her Permanent Secretary at the meeting of the Committee for the Economy on 21 January 2026.
answer_text: As I have confirmed in previous responses, I will make the decision on whether Executive Approval is required once the content and scope of the policy is more developed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the reason why she has not laid regulations on safe leave for victims of domestic abuse.
answer_text: Progress on the implementation of Safe Leave for victims of domestic abuse was impacted by the lack of Executive following the passing of the bill at the end of the previous mandate. As I have indicated on a number of occasions, I am fully committed to introducing the legislation to operationalise safe leave during this mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when the Good Work Charter will go out for public consultation.
answer_text: A Good Jobs Charter public consultation will be launched as soon as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when the drafting of the Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill will be completed.
answer_text: The drafting of the Employment Rights Bill will be completed as soon as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when her Department's policies on (i) interconnection; and (ii) long-term energy storage, will be published.
answer_text: Research has been completed to support the development of interconnection and storage policy, and its findings are informing the development of the policy in this area. As previously committed, I plan to outline policy in these areas by the end of 2026.
