question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, in light of an oversubscription for places, for an update on any action he can take to provide further nursery places at Newtownabbey Methodist Mission Pre-School, based at Abbott's Cross Primary School.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) is responsible for managing the pre-school admissions process and ensuring sufficient places are available to accommodate all target age children whose parents want a place. My Department plays no role in this process. A managed allocation of funded places is essential to ensuring the stability of the Pre-School Education Programme. The EA wrote to pre-school settings on 4th November 2025 to notify them of their provisional Pupil Admission Number for the 2026/27 academic year and explained that these allocations may be subject to change.   The 2026/27 pre-school admissions process is currently ongoing, with Stage One applications having closed on 21st January.  Parents will be advised of the outcome of Stage One applications on Monday 23rd March. The EA has advised that there are currently sufficient funded places in the Newtownabbey area to meet demand but will monitor this and will adjust PAN allocations where necessary to ensure sufficient pre-school provision is available.    In each of the last twelve years, at least 99.8% of children whose parents stayed with the process to the end received an offer of a funded pre-school education place in a setting of their preference by the end of stage two of the process. It is, however, important to remember that it not possible, nor is it the aim of the programme, to accommodate every child in their parents’ first preference setting. Parents are encouraged to consider all pre-school provision in their local area, list a number of preferences and stay with the process to the end to maximise the opportunity for their child to be offered a funded pre-school place.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 37496/22-27, to detail when this will go out to public procurement.
answer_text: As noted in my response to AQW 37496/22-27, the Education Authority (EA) is responsible for the traffic management scheme at King’s Park Primary School, Newtownabbey. The EA has confirmed that the project is ready to issue to tender. However, issue of the tender is subject to available capital funding in the 2026/27 financial year. There are many competing demands on the Department’s constrained capital budget. I will continue to make the case for increased and sustained investment in our schools’ estate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 37868/22-27, to detail any engagement planned with the US administration, during this trip.
answer_text: The itinerary for my US visit in March has not been finalised.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on her work to secure Northern Ireland's position as a pilot VAT cut scheme, to support our hospitality sector.
answer_text: I have consistently advocated for a fairer tax regime for our local businesses, allowing them to compete on an all-island basis. I recently met with Hospitality Ulster and the NI Food To Go Association along with the Finance Minister to discuss their proposal for a pilot scheme for a reduced VAT regime here given the VAT differential with the south. We have committed to seek a meeting with Treasury to discuss this proposal. 		I am aware of the increasing pressures on hospitality businesses due to the latest rates increases, changes to employer’s national insurance contributions and the national minimum wage. These pressures come on the back of challenging years during the pandemic for the sector.		My Department will continue to monitor the impact that these issues are having on the local economy. My Department will work alongside our partner organisations to support businesses to navigate the various challenges.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 36213/22-27, for his assessment of the comments by the PSNI surrounding the lack of co-operation by a publicly funded group in their investigation into the criminal damage at Belfast City Hall on Saturday 19 October 2024.
answer_text: I condemn the lack of cooperation by the publicly funded bodies in the PSNI’s investigation into the criminal damage inflicted on the portrait of the former DUP Lord Mayor at Belfast City Hall on 19 October 2024. This failure to provide essential information, such as an attendee list, undermines justice, equality, and respect for heritage in shared public spaces.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 37495/22-27, to detail when this assessment will be completed.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) is responsible for the traffic management scheme at Rathcoole Primary School, Newtownabbey. The EA has advised that the tender report was received on 23 January 2026. The EA will now progress a business case addendum for the project.Award of the contract will be dependent on approval of the business case addendum and will be subject to budget availability against competing priorities.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on their work to secure a reduced VAT pilot scheme, to support the hospitality sector.
answer_text: We raised this issue directly with the Prime Minister and the Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister We remain fully supportive of the introduction of a reduced VAT pilot scheme aimed at supporting the hospitality sector and recognise the significant pressures facing the sector. We will continue to work with the Economy Minister to advocate on this issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, following a request from Belfast Model School for Girls on 16 January 2026, whether the Education Authority will provide a school crossing patrol guard.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has advised me that it has not received a request from Belfast Model School for Girls for a School Crossing Patrol (SCP).The EA contacted the school, which confirmed that some parents have recently made enquiries about the provision of a School Crossing Patrol and the school is currently considering this request.The school has been informed of the required process for submitting an application for an SCP should it wish to proceed.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any differences in the level of fees paid by his Department to dentists carrying out NHS work, compared to Scotland.
answer_text: In November 2023 Scotland introduced dental payment reform, restructuring their Statement of Dental Remuneration (list of dental fees) by grouping linked treatments together under a single item code. As a result, we can no longer carry out a like for like comparison of dental fees with Scotland.While a direct comparison cannot be made, I understand that the changes made in Scotland came along with significant additional investment and as a result the fess for treatment provided by Health Service dentists in Scotland are, in general, higher than the fees in Northern Ireland.I have previously stated that Dental reform is one of my Department’s priorities. The General Dental Service Cost-of-Service review, which is currently underway, will play a crucial role in informing the next steps towards developing a dental service which better meets the needs of both patients and the profession.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail when the Relief of Pain Clinic at Belfast City Hospital will reopen.
answer_text: The Belfast Health and Social Care Trust Chronic Pain Service relocated to the Donard Building in Knockbracken Healthcare Park in November 2024 on a permanent basis.The Chronic Pain Service was remodelled in 2020 to the Centre for Pain Rehabilitation to deliver an evidence-based, interdisciplinary model for people living with persistent pain.The Donard Building in Knockbracken was, therefore, renovated to meet the needs of our patients requiring pain rehabilitation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 36482/22-27, to detail the (i) nature; (ii) scope; and (iii) any proposals, contained within those discussions.
answer_text: Engagement to date has been limited and largely introductory in nature. We expect further details to provided by the British Government on the nature and scope of its Defence Growth Deal.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 37575/22-27, to define appropriate time.
answer_text: By the appropriate time I mean once the content and scope of the policy is more developed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 37765/22-27, to detail (i) any reason for the delay; (ii) his assessment of the impact of this delay on residents; (iii) a date for the demolition to start.
answer_text: This issue is partly an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:(i) any reason for the delay“The demolition had been delayed due to the need for additional preparatory work to finalise the initial brief and scope of works prior to finalising tender documentation”.(iii) a date for the demolition to start.“Subject to the successful completion of procurement processes, demolition is expected to commence during the 2026/27 financial year, as indicated previously. Based on comparable procurements, we are currently aiming for an autumn start”.(ii) his assessment of the impact of this delay on residentsI appreciate the delay may result in continued visual blight and necessitate ongoing site management. I have been advised by NIHE that they remain mindful of these concerns and will continue to monitor and maintain the area appropriately while the procurement process progresses.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 32828/22-27, to detail any updates her Department has (i) sought; and (ii) received on this issue.
answer_text: This matter was raised with Ulster University which provided assurance that it would be investigated and properly considered. The university has subsequently confirmed to officials that the investigation has been completed. Ulster University also noted in a public statement it has a “…commitment to maintain a safe, inclusive and welcoming environment for all.” I share this commitment and believe it is vital to achieving the target for the growth required at the Magee campus.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 35183/22-27, to detail a further breakdown of the (i) £110,753.00; and (ii) £19,775.94, spent.
answer_text: Due to the volume of information in the answer, I have arranged for it to be deposited in the Assembly Library.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the publication of a biomethane strategy.
answer_text: A public consultation on the treatment of connection costs and gas quality standards is planned in 2026. This consultation will propose policy positions that could facilitate the wider use of biomethane across the gas network.		The Inter Departmental Biomethane Group has been reestablished in collaboration with the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (DAERA) and other key stakeholders to develop biomethane policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when her Department's Business Plan 2025-26 Progress Report, will be published.
answer_text: A progress report on my Department's 2025-26 Business Plan will be published shortly after the end of the 2025-26 financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 35717/22-27, for an update on the publication.
answer_text: My Department published a Call for Evidence regarding the potential role of liquid biofuels, including BioLPG, which will help to inform policy development in this area. The Government response to this is in the final stages of review and will be published in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 30944/22-27, to detail why the target for the publication of the consultation on an aviation strategy was not met.
answer_text: The Department postponed publishing the aviation strategy consultation from last year due to urgent aviation related priorities, notably advancing the Legislative Consent Motion required by Westminster’s publication of the Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) Bill.Officials continued to engage stakeholders on key topics including route development, Air Passenger Duty, tourism, sustainability, and passenger services. A sub-group of the Tourism Partnership Board was also established to advise on policy proposals. Public consultation on the aviation policy is expected to begin in March.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 32606/22-27, to provide an update on the Net Zero Accelerator Fund.
answer_text: Invest NI commissioned an independent market needs analysis to assess the need for a Net Zero Accelerator Fund. The completed analysis has been shared with the Economy Committee. 		Work is now ongoing to design a call for applications to identify projects within the Low Carbon/Net Zero Sector that are currently constrained by access to finance.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the work her officials were undertaking to understand the impacts of the proposed airlink between Dublin Airport and City of Derry Airport, on climate change targets.
answer_text: The Irish Government are funding and leading the work to reestablish flights between Dublin Airport and City of Derry Airport.My officials are engaged with their Dublin counterparts and await further developments on work to establish service demands and specifications. Both the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs and the Department for Transport in London oversee policy mechanisms which support the decarbonisation of the aviation sector here.  My officials will work with them to ensure economic priorities are balanced with climate responsibilities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the earmarked funding awarded by the Minister of Finance to Northern Ireland Water, in terms of stalling economic growth.
answer_text: The Department for Infrastructure is responsible for the maintenance, development and planning of critical infrastructure in the north, including sponsorship of NI Water.  The Executive’s Programme for Government recognises that historic underfunding of the water and wastewater infrastructure has been a persistent challenge in the north, constraining economic growth. It will take sustained investment over many years to address decades of underinvestment by the British Government in that regard.  I therefore welcome the Finance Minister’s proposed draft budget 2026-29/30, which identifies wastewater as a strategic priority and includes earmarked funding to improve water infrastructure.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the amount of additional funding from the Proposed Draft Budget 2026-29/30 that he has allocated to the Northern Ireland Environment Agency to improve their response times to planning applications.
answer_text: The Minister of Finance has proposed in his Draft Budget consultation a Resource DEL allocation for DAERA across the next three years.  I am currently working through these proposed budget allocations with my officials on how this funding could be allocated internally across DAERA. This includes the Northern Ireland Environment Agency and the funding required to take forward its planning function.  This is part of an iterative process which has not concluded yet.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 23520/22-27, for an update on any progress made on the adoption of the Mount Gilbert development.
answer_text: Mount Gilbert is a private site under development by Platinum Assets NI Ltd. Platinum Assets NI Ltd has appointed Homesure Property Management Limited as its agent to ensure that the development is finished to a standard where it can be adopted by my Department. Homesure Property Management Limited has agreed to complete the outstanding works, including the provision of all necessary certifications.Whilst notification of certification and adoption of sewers by NI Water has been received by my Department, there remain outstanding issues in relation to incomplete surfacing to carriageways and footways. My Department has advised Homesure Property Management Limited accordingly and has engaged with them throughout to achieve completion of these outstanding issues.   Despite recent attempts by officials to establish a timeframe for completion, Homesure Property Management Limited have been unable to provide this information to the Department.  My officials will continue to engage with Homesure Property Management Limited to achieve a resolution for residents, and to ensure that the developer fulfils all its obligations to allow the adoption of Mount Gilbert.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health,  pursuant to AQW 20424/22-27, for an update on any work undertaken to address the issues raised.
answer_text: Pursuant to AQW 20424/22-27 of January 2025, Belfast Health and Social Care Trust continue to maintain oversight of performance management of individual teams.  As part of the service development plan for the Statutory Homecare Service, there is currently a robust action plan, monthly assurance processes and key performance indicators in place for this service area.In addition to controls in 2025, the Trust has established a Home Care Oversight Group co-chaired by the Director and Co-Director of Adult Community and Older Peoples’ Services to monitor Statutory Home Care Services.  Membership of this group includes service area representatives, Human Resources representatives as well as broad representation from the relevant Trade Union bodies.Figure 1 shows the number of cases per month held by the Home Care Service at North & West.Fig 1.Month endCaseload (no. of service users)January 25533February 25567March 25542April 25537May 25528June 25512July 25496August 25490September 25501October 25510November 25494December 25489This shows a continued reduction in service users due to a reduction in the staff working in the service and demand for services in the areas where staff have availability.  This means that whilst we are providing less hours, the service is doing so with a reduced workforce.The service continues to work on building workforce capacity through regular recruitment exercises and involvement within the Trust’s Social Care Workforce Steering Group.The service’s digital system, Care Line Live, shows that between October 2025 and November 2025, 8 staff left the North and West Team (5 to retirement, 1 to ill-health retirement and 2 to resignation).

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for her assessment of the role the planning system will play in ensuring that her Department meets its climate change targets.
answer_text: I recognise that the planning system can play a positive role in supporting the wider efforts of the Executive in reducing emissions and decarbonising energy use across the region in the long-term public interest. Furthering sustainable development (including mitigating and adapting to climate change) is at the heart of the planning system and policy contained in my department’s Strategic Planning Policy Statement (SPPS). The provisions of the SPPS apply to the whole of the North and must be taken into account in the formulation of Local Development Plans (LDPs) and are material to all decisions on individual planning applications and appeals. To ensure that the SPPS remains up-to-date to inform plan-making and decision-taking, my department published revised regional strategic planning policy on renewable and low carbon energy on 11 December 2025. The revised policy  reflects the changed context brought about through the Executive’s Energy Strategy and the Climate Change Act. I also recognise that we share a collective responsibility to make the planning system work and in tackling climate change. Council planning authorities in particular play a key enabling role in the delivery of sustainable development and in setting local policy for the future development of their areas through local development plans. Whilst responsibility for local planning policies, rests with local government, my department continues to work closely with all councils as part of Plan Strategy and Local Policies Plan preparation to ensure that climate change is recognised and that local solutions to any challenges are suitably addressed. As regards to decision-taking, under the two-tier plan led system all planning applications, including those for determination by my department, will be assessed on a case-by-case basis against the provisions of the SPPS, LDPs, guidance, the views of statutory consultees (including DfI Roads and DfI Rivers), and all other material considerations. The relevance and weight of material considerations is ultimately a matter of planning judgement for the relevant planning authority.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 32251/22-27, to detail any direct engagement she has had with Bank of America, as requested in my previous question.
answer_text: My officials in Invest NI are engaging directly with Bank of America to deliver the investment announced.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 33177/22-27, whether they are requesting this is now referred to Ministers.
answer_text: Our officials are continuing to work with colleagues in the Department for Communities to deliver existing policy ambitions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 37670/22-27, to detail how he will ensure parity of funding between North and West Belfast.
answer_text: My Department allocates funding to programmes in North and West Belfast based on assessed need and the purpose of each initiative.   The funding provided is as set out in the response to AQW 37670/22-27.As outlined in that response, both areas receive significant funding.  Funding levels vary depending on the size of the programme, who it is intended to support, and the outcomes it aims to deliver.  Full Service funding for North and West Belfast is allocated on an equal basis.In addition to the funding outlined, the RAISE Programme will support the attainment and aspirations of children and young people in North Belfast and West Belfast by delivering against the objectives set out in the bespoke North Belfast Strategic Area Plan.  Up to mid-2027, £796,544 is available to deliver against the objectives set out in North Belfast Strategic Area Plan and £469,660 is available to deliver against the objectives set out in the West Belfast Strategic Area Plan.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) to detail the amount of funding she bid for during the budget process, earmarked for NI Water; and (ii) for her assessment of the amount awarded by the Minister of Finance, in the Proposed Draft Budget 2026-29/30.
answer_text: My Department submitted bids for all resource and capital requirements that were identified by NI Water in response to the Budget 2026-29/30 Information Gathering Exercise. The identified resource requirements for NI Water, which were in excess of the Department’s 2025-26 opening position, were submitted as bids, totalling £39.2m, £52.7m and £61.1m for 2026-27, 2027-28 and 2028-29 respectively.For capital the total bids submitted in relation to NI Water, which reflected its total identified requirements, were £431m, £490m, £550m and £650m for 2026-27, 2027-28, 2028-29 and 2029-30 respectively.The proposed allocations for my Department would go some way towards addressing the level of investment required for our infrastructure and for maintaining service delivery, and I welcome the proposed earmarked allocations for NI Water. However, difficult decisions would be required in prioritising services and capital schemes for delivery.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 37499/22-27, in light of the fact that that Newtownabbey Methodist Mission Pre-School was substantially oversubscribed on 1st preferences, compared with St James's PS Nursery Unit, which has been undersubscribed on 1st preferences for two of the last three years, whether places could be transferred to Newtownabbey Methodist Mission Pre-School.
answer_text: It is a matter for the Education Authority (EA) to secure sufficient pre-school education provision in local areas to meet overall demand. It is not, however, possible for the EA to reallocate funded pre-school places in the area as suggested.  As St James's PS Nursery Unit is a statutory pre-school provider, any proposed change to its approved admissions and enrolment numbers must be requested via the statutory Development Proposal process.  Information on this process is available here.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any further consideration she has given to the appointment of additional intendent commissioners, to the Planning Appeals Commission.
answer_text: The Planning Appeals Commission (PAC) is an independent body and does not fall within the remit of my Department.  Decisions relating to the appointment of additional commissioners to the PAC are a matter for the PAC and its sponsor body, the Northern Ireland Courts & Tribunal Service (NICTS), and I have no role in those appointments.The Member may instead be referring to the separate work my Department is progressing to put in place appointed persons under existing powers in the Planning Act (Northern Ireland) 2011.  This will allow the Department to appoint independent inspectors to carry out Hear and Report functions in appropriate cases as an alternative to the PAC.  This work is wholly distinct from the PAC’s staffing or resourcing arrangements.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for her assessment as to why planning applications across Northern Ireland have fallen so starkly.
answer_text: The downward trend in the number of planning applications received is not unique to the North but is mirroring what is also occurring across other jurisdictions where rising costs and economic barriers have been highlighted as some of the key reasons for the declining number of applications.  My Department continues to work with all stakeholders to make improvements to ensure the planning system is as efficient and effective as possible to support applicants and developers alike.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the reason why the York Street Interchange has not been earmarked for funding under his Proposed Draft Budget 2026-29/30.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the reason why the York Street Interchange project has not been earmarked for funding, under the Proposed Draft Budget 2026-29/30.
answer_text: The York Street Interchange is not designated as a Flagship project by the Executive, nor is it currently funded through City and Growth Deals; therefore, no specific funding has been earmarked for it under the Proposed Draft Budget 2026-29/30. The Draft Budget 2026-29/30 general capital allocations proposed for my Department fall short of its requirements and therefore this would require difficult decisions in prioritising capital schemes for delivery.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when full planning coverage of Local Development Plans will be completed, by all local councils.
answer_text: All councils are currently preparing new Local Development Plans (LDPs) to provide the spatial framework for development for their areas over a 15-year period. Nine out of the eleven councils have published development plan documents, six of which, have adopted Plan Strategies and are working towards their Local Policies Plan publication. Council timetables indicate that the remaining two councils are due to publish draft Plan Strategy documents this year. The timing of Local Development Plan publication is primarily a matter for each council to decide in line with its priorities and the resources available to it.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail her Departments spend to date, under funding from the Public Sector Transformation Board, to improve the planning service.
answer_text: As of the end of December 2025, my Department has spent approximately £152k of the Public Sector Transformation funding to progress arrangements for appointing persons to undertake ‘Hear and Report’ work as an alternative to the Planning Appeals Commission.  This is part of a wider body of work that I am committed to, in collaboration with councils, to identify measures to improve the effectiveness and efficiency of the overall planning process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when she will respond to the recommendations submitted by the Interim Regional Planning Commission.
answer_text: The Interim Regional Planning Commission concluded its work programme and submitted its report to me in November 2025.  The report entitled: ‘The Next Decade: A Framework for Change in the NI Planning System’, summarises the work undertaken by the Commission, as part of the agreed work programme since February 2023.  It sets out a 10-year vision for planning in the North and includes eight recommendations to support that vision.I am currently considering the Commission’s report and its findings and recommendations; and will respond in due course.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether they both intend to represent the Executive at any engagements, in the United States of America, around the period of St. Patrick's Day 2026.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any planned improvements to the road condition at the junction of the Hightown Road and Antrim Road, Glengormley.
answer_text: Resurfacing of the Hightown Road and Antrim Road junction, Glengormley will form part of Antrim and Newtownabbey Borough Council’s proposed Environmental Improvement Scheme. The scheme includes footway and carriageway resurfacing and is anticipated for delivery within 2027.In the interim, my officials have instructed surface patching works at this location and it is envisaged that they will be completed this financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the proposed spend, under earmarked funding for transformation in the Proposed Draft Budget 2026-29/30, published by the Minister for Finance.
answer_text: The earmarked funding for transformation, as set out in the Draft Budget 2026-29/30 for my Department, is in respect of the funding it has been awarded from the Public Sector Transformation Fund. My Department has secured £18m from the Public Sector Transformation Fund over four years to deliver two transformation projects. This includes £15m to develop and deliver a pilot project that will use nature-based solutions to help transform our urban drainage systems, and £3m to exercise its existing powers under the Planning Act (NI) 2011 to appoint persons to carry out its Hear and Report as an alternative to the Planning Appeals Commission.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the differences in the level of fees paid by the his Department compared to Scotland for dentists carrying out NHS work.
answer_text: In November 2023 Scotland introduced dental payment reform, restructuring their Statement of Dental remuneration (list of dental fees) by grouping linked treatments together under a single item code. As a result, we can no longer carry out a like for like comparison of dental fees with Scotland.While a direct comparison cannot be made I understand that the changes made in Scotland came along with significant additional investment and as a result the fess for treatment provided by Health Service dentists in Scotland are, in general, higher than the fees in Northern Ireland.I have previously stated that Dental reform is one of my Department’s priorities. The General Dental Service Cost-of-Service review, which is currently underway, will play a crucial role in informing the next steps towards developing a dental service which better meets the needs of both patients and the profession.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the General Dental Services cost of service review.
answer_text: The Cost-of-Service review for General Dental Services, led by Professor Ciaran O’Neill is underway. Professor O’Neill is currently analysing data and engaging with Departmental officials, the British Dental Association, and other stakeholder groups to inform the development of evidence-based recommendations. The timeframe for completion of the review remains the end of March 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she will be travelling to the United States of America for any engagements, around the period of St Patrick's Day 2026, in her capacity as an Executive Minister.
answer_text: I plan to travel to the US in March to promote trade and investment, tourism and our screen industry.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 35079/22-27, to detail when land at (i) Mountcollyer Close; and (ii) Mileriver Street, will be included in the programme for transfer to a Housing Association.
answer_text: This is an operational matter for the Housing Executive.	I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:	“The Housing Executive owns the land at both Mountcollyer Close and Mileriver Street in North Belfast. The Housing Executive’s annual assessment of undeveloped land is underway. As part of this process, both sites will be considered internally in Quarter 4 of 2025/26 for their potential suitability for transfer to a Housing Association.Any decision will be taken in the context of a range of factors, including assessed housing need in the area, the Housing Executive’s revitalisation plans, and wider external considerations such as the availability of necessary infrastructure”.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department would support any small-scale initiatives to increase residential parking provision, in particularly heavily congested streets, within Rathcoole, Belfast.
answer_text: While I appreciate that the current layout within Rathcoole, Belfast may not provide the availability of convenient parking for all residents; it is an issue that is faced by many estates throughout the North. Additional parking within developments is assessed in accordance with the Department’s Policy and Guidance RSPPG E053 Car Parking Facilities in Existing Social Housing EstatesIn accordance with this policy, the Department does not provide parking for amenity reasons and without any significant safety or traffic progression concerns impacting on the wider road, Rathcoole as a residential estate, would not meet the criteria outlined above for a scheme to provide additional parking to be taken forward.Where 50% of the housing associated with any parking request belongs to the Housing Executive, it is responsible for determining if such a scheme should be considered and for the associated funding.  My officials are not involved in this assessment process, however any alteration to the road layout would need the Department’s approval.Where less than 50% of the housing belongs to the Housing Executive, confirmation of 50% funding is required before the Department would consider the matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the four primary schools involved in the RAISE programme's Odyssey Trust project.
answer_text: The Odyssey Trust is the lead organisation for the ‘Story Crafters - A Gamefied Approach to Literacy at Key Stage 2’ project in North Belfast.  This project has been identified to advance to the next stage of the RAISE Locality Led Projects process. The next step involves agreeing the terms of a Letter of Offer which will include confirmation of the involvement of all organisations, including schools, identified as collaborating partners in project proposals.The four primary schools detailed in the project proposal submitted by the Odyssey Trust are:-Holy Cross Boys Primary SchoolHoly Cross Girls Primary SchoolCurrie Primary SchoolMalvern Primary School

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, in light of a recent media report, whether she intends to review the current policy relating to the temporary release of prisoners over the Christmas period.
To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 35078/22-27, to provide an update on the tendering and demolition, in light of the November 2025 timeframe mentioned in his answer.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“Preparatory work for the procurement exercises for the demolition of properties vested at 53–55 Fortwilliam Parade has commenced. We plan to tender for a consultant in the coming weeks to develop the demolition tender documentation. Once the consultant is appointed and the documentation is finalised, we will proceed to tender for a contractor to carry out the demolition works. Subject to successful procurement processes, demolition is expected to take place during the 2026/27 financial year.”

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) when the Effective Use of Resources criteria for skin removal plastic surgery is due to be reviewed or updated; and (ii) whether his Department has made any assessment of whether this criteria is more restrictive than in other NHS regions in the UK.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail the current waiting time for routine non-cosmetic plastic surgery appointments for arms and legs in the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: The current average waiting time for routine non-cosmetic plastic surgery outpatient appointments and treatments at Belfast Trust is available at:  My Waiting Times NI - DOH/HSCNI Strategic Planning and Performance Group (SPPG)Plastic Surgery is recorded under the South Eastern HSC Trust which manages all referrals to the specialty. Unfortunately waiting times are only available as red flag, urgent or routine and not individual procedures within a specialty.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department would support any small-scale initiatives by the Housing Executive, to increase residential parking provision in particularly heavily congested streets, within Rathcoole.
answer_text: I am acutely aware of the benefits that small-scale interventions like this can make and I understand the constraints with parking in Rathcoole. This is an operational matter for the Housing Executive and I have therefore asked my officials to explore this with them in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) why she has not progressed the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee scheme within the timeframe she set out; and (ii) her assessment of the impact this will have upon confidence in her Department.
answer_text: Delivering additional renewable electricity generation is complex, demanding a careful balance between security of supply, affordability, and legislative compliance.My Department’s priority is to ensure that Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee auctions take place at the right time, securing the best technologies and capacity to meet our targets at a fair price for consumers. 	I am confident that we are building the right foundations to incentivise sufficient electricity generation whilst ensuring the scheme delivers the best outcomes for energy consumers and that local communities benefit from the transition to net zero.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the amount of funding his Department, or its arm's-length bodies, has allocated to (i) the West Belfast Partnership; (ii) EastSide Partnership; (iii) Forward South Partnership, in this financial year.
answer_text: My Department provides funding to a number of organisations to deliver locality-based education programmes.  Funding provided by my department in 2025/26 financial year is set out below. West Belfast Partnership – Sharing the Learning Programme £180kGreater Shankill Partnership – West Belfast Community Project £170kFull Service Community Network – West Belfast £366kFull Service Extended Schools – North Belfast £366kMonkstown Boxing Club £131k The Department of Justice provides funding as part of the Executive Programme on Paramilitarism and Organised Crime (EPPOC) for the WRAP Programme.  Both EastSide Learning and Forward South Partnership are delivery partners.  Eastside Learning £170kForward South Partnership  £252kGreater Shantallow Area Partnership £67kThrive – Monkstown and Rathcoole £114k

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 36816/22-27, to detail when the schedule of works will be available to address the flooding on the Mill Road.
answer_text: Further to my reply to AQW 36816/22-27, I can confirm that officials from my Department and NI Water are continuing to work to identify the most appropriate solution to the flooding on Mill Road. This is a complex issue and it is important that all potential solutions are fully investigated to ensure the most appropriate option is selected for delivery.   The design and planning work, as well as statutory approvals, must be progressed before a confirmed programme can be provided. I have asked my officials to provide you with the work programme for the preferred solution once it has been developed.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the reason why the North Belfast Principals Forum was not supported under the RAISE programme.
answer_text: While representatives of schools involved in the North Belfast Principals Forum have been engaged in the RAISE Programme, and some are members of the North Belfast RAISE Locality Reference Group, no project proposal was received from the North Belfast Principals Forum in the first call for Locality Led Projects.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the schools involved in the YEHA project, as part of the RAISE programme.
answer_text: At the request of the Locality Reference Group, the RAISE North Belfast locality has been separated into two sub-localities within the overall North Belfast locality – Greater Shankill and North Belfast.  Both Greater Shankill and North Belfast identified ‘Youth Work approaches to tackle non-attendance’ as an objective within their part of the overall North Belfast Strategic Area Plan.  The Youth Education Health Advice (YEHA) is the lead organisation for The Reconnect Project: A Youth Work Approach to Raising Attendance, Aspirations and Achievement for Young People in North Belfast.The schools detailed in the project proposal submitted by the Youth Education Health Advice (YEHA) organisation to the call for Locality Led Projects are:-Mercy CollegeBlessed Trinity CollegeA complementary project will address the North Belfast (Greater Shankill) ‘Youth Work approaches to tackle non-attendance’ objective. The Connections Project will be delivered by Streetbeat Youth Project in collaboration with 11 schools and 2 youth organisations. The schools detailed in the project proposal are:- i)	Blackmountain Primary Schoolii)	Belfast Boys Model Schooliii)	Harmony Primary Schooliv)        Hazelwood Integrated Collegev)       Glenwood Primary Schoolvi)      Springfield Primary Schoolvii)     Malvern Primary Schoolviii)    Springhill Primary Schoolix)      Belfast Model School for Girlsx)       Edenbrooke Primary Schoolxi)      Forth River Primary SchoolBoth projects have been identified to advance to the next stage of the RAISE Locality Led Projects process. The next step involves agreeing the terms of a Letter of Offer which will include confirmation of the involvement of all organisations, including schools, identified as collaborating partners in project proposals.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail how North Belfast ranks out of the 18 constituencies in terms of overall reduction in NHS dentistry places, since 2022.
answer_text: Over the period 31st March 2022 to 30th September 2025, the actual change in registration numbers for North Belfast (-20,028) ranks as the 6th largest reduction of the 18 Assembly Areas. All Assembly Areas have seen a reduction in total registrations over this time period.Over the same period the percentage change in registration numbers for North Belfast (-27.9%) is the 7th largest reduction among the 18 Assembly Areas.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 30805/22-27, for his assessment of the proposal from the Minister for the Economy.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) any discussions her Department has had with ASDA regarding the upgrading of the Cloughfern Roundabout, as per planning requirements; (ii) when this work will commence; (iii) why no action will be taken by her Department in the interim, to protect pedestrians; and (iv) when replacement signage will be installed.
answer_text: My officials are engaging with the developer’s design team to confirm the proposed design of the upgraded junction. The designer is aware of the department’s requirements to ensure that the improved layout for the junction is safe for all road users, including pedestrians and cyclists.The design of the junction improvement is at an advanced stage and the timing of any works is at the discretion of the developer but must conform with the conditions set out in the planning consent. The planning conditions also dictate that this junction upgrade is completed prior to the store opening.In the interim, officials continue to monitor the operation of the existing roundabout which currently facilitates pedestrian crossing points on all four legs  Given the planned future upgrade of this roundabout as part of the ASDA development, my department’s limited resources would be better directed to other priority interventions deemed most in need and consequently there are no plans for any further interim improvements at this time.  However, my officials are currently progressing the design of replacement signage at this roundabout and these signs are planned for installation once they are received from our supplier.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 36628/22-27, whether she will present the Ethical Investment Framework to the Executive for approval.
answer_text: I will decide on the need for Executive referral at the appropriate time, in line with the Ministerial Code.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the York Street Interchange scheme.
answer_text: In October 2022 my predecessor published a Placemaking and Active Travel Review report and asked officials to take forward further work to refine the options within it.Officials have completed this work and have engaged with key stakeholders on the York Street Interchange project, to gather their assessment of the report.Officials have presented the findings of this work to me and I am taking the time to consider the next steps for this key infrastructure project.As the scheme is not funded through City and Growth deals and is not identified as an Executive Flagship project, I have asked my officials to consider possible alternative funding methods which could facilitate the scheme progression. This work will take several months to complete.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 36398/22-27, for an update on these assessments.
answer_text: The Chanceller’s November Budget announced £150 reduction in domestic electricity bills in Britain. 		This is made up of, firstly, not renewing the Energy Company Obligation. This does not apply to consumers here as it is a cost that they have never paid.		In addition to this, Treasury will fund, from April, 75% of the domestic share of the Renewables Obligation. Electricity consumers here do pay towards the Renewables Obligation in their bills. Finance officials are engaging with Treasury to agree the level of Treasury support that will flow through to electricity consumers here.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 35429/22-27, to provide an update.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) is responsible for the traffic management scheme at Rathcoole Primary School, Newtownabbey. The EA has advised that the tender for the appointment of a contractor to deliver the project was returned on 10 December 2025. The appointed design team are currently assessing the tender returns and completing a tender report for review by the EA. Award of the contract will be dependent on approval of a business case addendum and will be subject to budget availability against competing priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 34984/22-27, to provide an update.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) is responsible for the traffic management scheme at King’s Park Primary School, Newtownabbey. The EA has confirmed that the design team has provided the tender package for the construction contract. However, delivery of the scheme is subject to available capital funding within an extremely constrained budget and many competing priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on discussions with HM Treasury in relation to retaining funds for Northern Ireland as a result of benefit fraud detection.

To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 36907/22-27, to detail (i) the number of places currently allocated to those schools which were not oversubscribed; (ii) in how many of the last three years have they been undersubscribed with first preference applications; and (iii) the number of occasions in the last three years that those schools which are oversubscribed this year (a) have previously been; and (b) by how many applications.
