question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 34463/22-27, for an update on the assessment.
answer_text: I can confirm this location is awaiting assessment which needs to be completed during evening hours. Officials will complete this assessment at the earliest opportunity when weather conditions become more favourable.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail any considerations she has given to updating the Police and Criminal Evidence (Northern Ireland) Order 1989, to ensure custody images of those convicted for sexual offences against children, are released to the public.
answer_text: The Department intends to take forward work to consider the need for policy and amendments to PACE 1989 on the use and retention of custody images.The Police Service of Northern Ireland already has decision making arrangements in place regarding the release of custody images of offenders. These arrangements provide for the release of offenders sentenced to a custody term of 12 months or more if appropriateness and legitimate policing purpose can be justified. Where a custodial sentence of less than 12 months is determined by the Court, there is still provision for a custody image to be released.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 35721/22-27, to detail any advice (i) sought; and (ii) received, on this issue.
answer_text: As I stated in my previous response, I seek and receive advice from my officials, including my Permanent Secretary, on all matters within my remit.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail how the £400,000, announced by her Department for promotion of air routes, will be used.
answer_text: Tourism Ireland will use the allocated £400,000 to work with all local airports on route development for 2026 and invite airlines to propose co-operative overseas activities in early 2026 to boost demand for important inbound routes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 35838/22-27, given this is not a significant active travel scheme, to detail whether this work be undertaken.
answer_text: My Department is not currently considering proposals in this immediate area. Whilst the areas of land in question may offer some opportunity to improve active travel infrastructure locally this is not sufficiently significant to develop as a scheme in isolation, as the limited resources of my Department need to be directed to the development of schemes that can provide maximum benefit to the community.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail each of the Belfast Region City Deal projects, which make up the total £6.5 million capital surrender, in the 2025 December Monitoring Round.
answer_text: The £6.5m capital easement set out in the Executive Paper related solely to the Executive’s contribution to the Tourism & Regeneration pillar of the Belfast Region City Deal.  A breakdown of the easement is set out below.Project Easement (£’000)Gobbins Phase 2529Gateway to the Mournes682Belfast Stories3,368Destination Royal Hillsborough1,595i4c Cleantech Centre295Total6,469As this funding is ringfenced, it will be made available at a later date.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of (i) adults; and (ii) under-18's, who are registered with NHS dental care, in North Belfast, in each of the last three financial years.
answer_text: The number of child registrations (aged under 18) and adult registrations (18+ years) for health service dentistry of persons living in North Belfast Assembly Area in each of the last three financial years is shown below in Table 1. Table 1: Child, Adult and Total Registrations for Health Service Dentistry in the North Belfast Assembly Area by Financial YearFinancial YearChild RegistrationsAdult RegistrationsTotal Registrations2022-23117,16857,06374,2312023-24117,24855,03872,2862024-25115,77042,25658,0261 At 31st March of the financial year

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail whether his Department will require dental practices to offer a minimum level of fully funded places as part of public funding provided to them, in a bid to address the declining number of registrations.
answer_text: I am fully aware of the concerns regarding the decline in dental registrations and the importance of ensuring access to dental care. Dentists are independent, selfemployed practitioners and the Department cannot compel individual dentists to take on patients or mandate a minimum number of fully funded places. Instead, the Department works to incentivise participation in publicly funded schemes, improve contract arrangements, and expand capacity where possible. Efforts are focused on making Health Service dental provision attractive and sustainable for practices, thereby encouraging more dentists to provide health service dentistry. My Department remains committed to exploring solutions that improve access.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail how her Department is working with Great British Energy to ensure that Northern Ireland receives its fair share of investment.
answer_text: Officials from my Department have engaged regularly throughout 2025 with the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero (DESNZ) and with representatives from Great British Energy (GBE), aiming to support alignment between their clean energy mission and the Executive’s energy ambitions, recognising the North’s distinct energy landscape.In March 2025, I accepted GBE funding for the current financial year from DESNZ Minister Shanks. A total of £1.66 million has been allocated to deliver renewable energy infrastructure across several Regional Further Education Colleges. This investment will result in lower emissions, improved energy efficiency, and reduced operating costs for these campuses in the years ahead.My Department continues to liaise with GBE and DESNZ through regular meetings to maximise opportunities for future investment in renewables.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement her Department has had with Liquid Gas UK on the role of liquefied petroleum gas and biopropane, in achieving net zero targets.
answer_text: A series of meetings, both bi-lateral and as part of a roundtable stakeholder event, have been undertaken by my Department with Liquid Gas UK in relation to the development of policies which aid in the achievement of net zero targets.  More recently, Liquid Gas UK submitted a return to the Call for Evidence on Using Biofuels to Transition Away from Fossil Fuels for Heating issued by my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any engagement her officials had with Great British Energy, prior to the launch of its Strategic Plan.
answer_text: Officials from my Department have engaged regularly throughout 2025 with the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero (DESNZ) and with representatives from Great British Energy (GBE).It is important to note that GBE operates independently, with its own governance and strategic direction. My officials’ engagement with DESNZ and GBE during the development of the Statement of Strategic Priorities and the subsequent GBE Strategic Plan, published on 4th December, emphasised the importance of aligning with our clean energy ambitions and recognising the North’s distinct energy landscape.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any steps her Department is taking to protect consumer choice in low-carbon heating options.
answer_text: The Path to Net Zero Energy Strategy aims to move away from the imported fossil fuels that put us in the position of a “price-taker”.  One of the key objectives of the strategy is to ensure that all consumers, business and domestic, have access to essential and affordable energy, including low carbon heating options. The focus is now on priorities for delivery to ensure that we maximise the benefits for our society and economy as soon as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her Department's assessment of Great British Energy's Strategic Plan.
answer_text: Great British Energy (GBE) operates independently, with its own governance and strategic direction, however, there is ongoing constructive engagement between my Department, the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero (DESNZ), and representatives from GBE.		GBE’s Strategic Plan acknowledges the North’s distinct energy landscape, including the devolved competence. My officials will continue to work with DESNZ and GBE to encourage tailored interventions here to support clean energy technologies, growth of local and community energy, and the development of clean energy supply chains.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the rationale for her recent visits to the United States of America and China, if they are not determined to be trade missions.
answer_text: My recent visits to the US and China were designed to build relationships, strengthen partnerships, promote international trade and investment, promote our tourism offering and strengths in further and higher education, and to showcase the north’s economic and creative strengths.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 34985/22-27, to detail whether he will request a review of the allocation of the 569 funded pre-school funded among providers, to reflect the demand of the area, as determined by oversubscription, and total number of first preference choice for each setting.
answer_text: The purpose of my Department’s Pre-School Education Programme (PSEP) is to provide a funded pre-school education place for every target age child whose parents want it.  It is not the intention of the PSEP to provide every child with a funded place in their parents’ first preference setting, nor is it possible to do so.  The majority of children do, however, receive an offer of a funded place in their first preference setting with 89% of applicants receiving an offer in their first preference setting in Stage One of the 2025/26 admissions process.My Department plays no role in making operational decisions about the funding of pre-school education places in local areas. This is a matter for the EA with the EA Pre-School Education Group (EA PEG) having responsibility for securing non-statutory pre-school education provision in local areas and ensuring sufficient pre-school provision is available in all areas to accommodate every target aged child whose parents want it while also balancing the relevant duties in relation to making best use of public funds. The EA PEG continually reviews pre-school provision levels and may adjust Pupil Admission Numbers (PANs) where it is appropriate to do so.  If the PAN for any setting in the Newtownabbey area is adjusted, the EA will contact the setting directly. I am, however, content that based on the data previously provided sufficient pre-school provision is available in the area to meet current demand. At close of Stage One of the 2025/26 admissions process the EA has advised that 434 children received an offer of a funded pre-school education place in their first preference setting; with a further 84 receiving an offer of a funded place in a lower preference setting.  Any child that did not receive an offer of a funded place at close of Stage One was invited to submit further preferences in Stage Two of the admissions process, at the end of which 99.8% of applicants overall that remained with the process to the end received an offer of a funded pre-school education place.The table below sets out which settings were oversubscribed with first preference applications in Stage One and those that were not within a two mile radius of Newtownabbey Methodist Mission Pre-School PG.School NameOversubscribed with First Preference Applications         (Y/N)Ashgrove Nursery School (full-time)YAshgrove Nursery School (part-time)NGlengormley Integrated PS PlaygroupYHazelwood Integrated PS Nursery UnitYHollybank Pre-School Playgroup, MonkstownNLowwood PS Nursery SchoolNNewtownabbey Methodist Mission Pre-School PGYRathcoole PS Nursery UnitNStepping Stones Pre-School PlaygroupNSt Bernard’s PS Nursery Unit, NewtownabbeyNSt Bernard’s Pre-School PG, NewtownabbeyNSt James’s PS Nursery Unit, NewtownabbeyNSt Mary’s Nursery School, NewtownabbeyYSt Mary’s on the Hill Playgroup, NewtownabbeyYWhitehouse PS Nursery UnitY

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 34985/22-27, to detail (i) the number of the 546 places that were allocated as a first preference; (ii) the number of lower preferences each provider accepted; (iii) how each place awarded corelated with the preference expressed; (iv) which providers were oversubscribed for first preferences; and (v) which were not.
answer_text: The purpose of my Department’s Pre-School Education Programme (PSEP) is to provide a funded pre-school education place for every target age child whose parents want it.  It is not the intention of the PSEP to provide every child with a funded place in their parents’ first preference setting, nor is it possible to do so.  The majority of children do, however, receive an offer of a funded place in their first preference setting with 89% of applicants receiving an offer in their first preference setting in Stage One of the 2025/26 admissions process.My Department plays no role in making operational decisions about the funding of pre-school education places in local areas. This is a matter for the EA with the EA Pre-School Education Group (EA PEG) having responsibility for securing non-statutory pre-school education provision in local areas and ensuring sufficient pre-school provision is available in all areas to accommodate every target aged child whose parents want it while also balancing the relevant duties in relation to making best use of public funds. The EA PEG continually reviews pre-school provision levels and may adjust Pupil Admission Numbers (PANs) where it is appropriate to do so.  If the PAN for any setting in the Newtownabbey area is adjusted, the EA will contact the setting directly. I am, however, content that based on the data previously provided sufficient pre-school provision is available in the area to meet current demand. At close of Stage One of the 2025/26 admissions process the EA has advised that 434 children received an offer of a funded pre-school education place in their first preference setting; with a further 84 receiving an offer of a funded place in a lower preference setting.  Any child that did not receive an offer of a funded place at close of Stage One was invited to submit further preferences in Stage Two of the admissions process, at the end of which 99.8% of applicants overall that remained with the process to the end received an offer of a funded pre-school education place.The table below sets out which settings were oversubscribed with first preference applications in Stage One and those that were not within a two mile radius of Newtownabbey Methodist Mission Pre-School PG.School NameOversubscribed with First Preference Applications         (Y/N)Ashgrove Nursery School (full-time)YAshgrove Nursery School (part-time)NGlengormley Integrated PS PlaygroupYHazelwood Integrated PS Nursery UnitYHollybank Pre-School Playgroup, MonkstownNLowwood PS Nursery SchoolNNewtownabbey Methodist Mission Pre-School PGYRathcoole PS Nursery UnitNStepping Stones Pre-School PlaygroupNSt Bernard’s PS Nursery Unit, NewtownabbeyNSt Bernard’s Pre-School PG, NewtownabbeyNSt James’s PS Nursery Unit, NewtownabbeyNSt Mary’s Nursery School, NewtownabbeyYSt Mary’s on the Hill Playgroup, NewtownabbeyYWhitehouse PS Nursery UnitY

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, following her announcement of a new licensing agreement, which will see the Ireland’s Hidden Heartlands brand used in County Fermanagh, to (i) detail the cost of the agreement; (ii) detail the length of the agreement; (iii) to define eligible businesses; and (iv) publish the agreement.
answer_text: There is no cost associated with the actual licensing agreement between Fáilte Ireland and Tourism Northern Ireland. The Minister has allocated £300k of funding for a marketing campaign for County Fermanagh to support the announcement. The agreement is for an initial period of five years. On the expiry of the fifth year following the date of the agreement and every five years thereafter, either party can give six months prior notice in writing of their withdrawal from this agreement. If no such notice is communicated, the agreement automatically rolls on for another five-year period.Eligible businesses need to:Have a listing on discovernorthernireland.com.  Have an address in County Fermanagh. Accept the terms and conditions and the Individual user agreement.The licencing agreement will not be published because it is commercially sensitive.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail why (i) AQW 34580/22-27; (ii) AQW 34715/22-27; (iii) AQW 35310/22-27; (iv) AQW 35721/22-27; (v) AQW 31513/22-27; (vi) AQW 32251/22-27; and (vii) AQW 32608/22-27, all remain unanswered.
answer_text: The following AQWs were responded to on:AQW 34580 – responded 18 December 2025AQW 34715 – responded 15 December 2025AQW 35310 – responded 22 December 2025AQW 35721 – responded 10 December 2025AQW 31513 – responded 18 December2025AQW 32251 – responded 13 January 2026AQW 32608 – responded 18 December 2025

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she will request that SONI publishes an update on the Key Performance Indicators, identified in the Utility Regulator’s recently published SONI Cost and Performance Report October 2020 to September 2024.
answer_text: The Utility Regulator’s SONI Cost and Performance Report for October 2020 to September 2024 highlights that SONI has not yet fully developed or implemented certain Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), and that further progress in this area would deliver benefits for consumers. I support the Utility Regulator’s recommendation that SONI should update its KPI performance in its future business plan publications.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer (i) AQW 34461/22-27; (ii) AQW 34128/22-27; (iii) AQW 30805/22-27; (iv) AQW 30554/22-27; and (v) AQW 30444/22-27.
To ask the Minister for the Economy for her Department’s assessment of (i) the performance of SONI; and (ii) its leadership structures, particularly in relation to the delivery of new infrastructure.
answer_text: As system operator, SONI’s responsibility is to plan and operate the electricity transmission system.  The delivery of new transmission infrastructure is an important enabler of power decarbonisation and so, delays in new infrastructure is disappointing, I have established the Grid Development Monitoring Group with senior representatives from SONI, NIE Networks, the Utility Regulator, which is charged with accelerating power decarbonisation including delivering more renewable electricity onto the system.SONI’s licence contains conditions regarding the leadership and governance structures of the organisation.  The Utility Regulator’s independent role is to establish and monitor these licence conditions to ensure that they operate effectively for the purpose of protecting electricity consumers.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, given the critical role electricity infrastructure plays in attracting and maintaining investment in Northern Ireland, to detail any steps her Department is taking to improve SONI’s timely delivery of key projects.
answer_text: My Department has established a Grid Development Monitoring Group with SONI, NIE Networks and the Utility Regulator to ensure that essential upgrades to our electricity network are delivered. This group provides a structured forum for oversight and collaboration on key projects.		A key objective is to accelerate grid development and strengthen the timely delivery of critical infrastructure, which is vital for supporting investment and economic growth.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the economic impact of SONI not delivering electricity transmission infrastructure projects on time.
answer_text: The Department recognises the importance in delivering new transmission infrastructure to reduce costs to electricity consumers and support economic growth. Therefore, I established the Grid Development Monitoring Group with SONI, NIE Networks and the Utility Regulator with objectives including the acceleration the delivery of grid infrastructure.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her Department’s assessment of the impact on the 2030 renewable electricity consumption targets of SONI’s ongoing delays in developing transmission infrastructure.
answer_text: Delivering the 80% renewable electricity by 2030 requires a range of issues to be addressed, including new electricity grid capacity and storage, the delivery of advanced technology in operating the grid as well as additional renewable electricity capacity.It follows that delays in delivering grid capacity, as outlined in the Utility Regulator’s report, is not helpful in working towards the 2030 target.I have established the Grid Development Monitoring Group with senior representatives from SONI, NIE Networks, the Utility Regulator, which is charged with accelerating the power decarbonisation including delivering more renewable electricity onto the system.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the recently agreed schedule of works by NI Water to resolve the flooding of the Mill Road and Shore Road, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: NI Water has identified the preferred short-term solution to help to resolve the recurring flooding at this location.  The proposed solution is to reline a large sewer that passes beneath the Shore Road and carries storm water from a combined sewer overflow to the sea.  To facilitate this work, NI Water will have to temporarily divert any storm flows, that may occur during the period of the weeklong sewer lining works, into a nearby culverted watercourse.However, before this temporary storm overflow diversion can be put in place, it will be necessary to repair a structural defect that has been found in the nearby culverted watercourse.  My officials are working to determine the extent and scale of the culvert repairs and also mitigate and manage any potential traffic disruption.Officials from my Department and NI Water have appointed design teams and a single preferred contractor has been identified to provide early contractor input to both schemes.  Design and planning work, as well as statutory approvals must be progressed, before a confirmed programme can be provided.Until this flooding issue is resolved, NI Water, along with officials from my Department and the PSNI have developed a Wet Weather Protocol, to manage any likely instances of flooding at this location.  This protocol has been developed and agreed by all parties and has been shared with Emergency Planning Teams.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 35718/22-27, whether she will publish the initial equality screening.
answer_text: My Ethical Investment Framework is still in development and, in line with established practice, internal screening documents are not published while policy is being formulated.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of projects, referenced in SONI’s Draft Transmission Development Plan Northern Ireland 2025 – 2034, that have been delayed, when compared with previously published timelines.
answer_text: The relevant detail is in Figure 4.4 on page 20 and on page 21 of the Utility Regulator’s report at the following link:2025-12-02 SONI Mid-Term C&PR.pdf

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any actions her Department is taking to address the fact that, according to the Utility Regulator’s SONI Mid-Term Cost and Performance Report 2020 - 2024, SONI is significantly behind where it had expected to be in delivering large transmission infrastructure projects.
answer_text: The delays in delivering new infrastructure, which is an important enabler of power decarbonisation, outlined in the Utility Regulator’s report, are disappointing.To help address this, I have established the Grid Development Monitoring Group with senior representatives from SONI, NIE Networks, the Utility Regulator, which is charged with accelerating power decarbonisation including delivering more renewable electricity onto the system.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail why AQW 35721/22-27 has not been answered, in line with timeframes set down in Standing Orders.
answer_text: My response to AQW 35721/22-27 has now been issued.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the finding in the Utility Regulator’s SONI Mid-Term Cost and Performance Report 2020 - 2024, that project slippage and non-progression within SONI, may not be in consumer interest.
answer_text: The SONI Mid-Term Cost and Performance Report for 2020 to 2024 provides the Utility Regulator’s independent assessment of SONI’s performance during the first four years of their current price control.  The cost and performance report noted that while SONI is performing well in several areas there is scope for improvement in project delivery. Delivering new transmission infrastructure will enable more renewable electricity onto the system, reducing prices for electricity consumers. I have established the Grid Development Monitoring Group with the Utility Regulator, SONI and NIE Networks to ensure that, collectively, we do all that we can to accelerate the required network delivery.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether the Review into the Energy Strategy will be published before the December 2025 recess period.
answer_text: As committed in the Energy Strategy – The Path to Net Zero Energy, a Mid-Term Review of the Energy Strategy will be published by the end of 2025. This is likely to be after recess commences on the 13th December.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of her comments in the Northern Ireland Assembly on 1 December 2025, in relation to her proposed Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill, to provide a date for its introduction.
answer_text: The Bill will be introduced to the Assembly once it is approved by the Executive.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when her Ethical Investment Framework will be presented to the Executive.
answer_text: I announced my intention to develop an Ethical Investment Framework as part of my Written Statement on 16 October 2025. This work is in development.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on all actions highlighted in her Department's 2024-25 Business Plan.
answer_text: An end year progress report against my Department's 2024-25 Business Plan is published on the Departmental website: Business Plan End Year Progress Report 2024-25.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the development of the business case for a new build for Seaview Primary School and Nursery Unit.
answer_text: The Education Authority Asset Management Team has assigned resources to complete the Business Case, which is now anticipated to be lodged with the Department by the end of December 2025.  Once approved by the Department, the project will progress to the Concept Design stage (RIBA Stage 2).

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether a GP can refuse to prescribe Armour Thyroid to a patient having been recommended for such by a Health and Social Care Trust endocrinologist.
answer_text: The GP would need to assess the current evidence and consider the clinical appropriateness of prescribing Armour Thyroid, considering its composition, licensing status, and the recommendations from NICE and the British Thyroid Association, which are expanded on below. If the GP is not satisfied, they can decline to prescribe. This applies regardless of whether the recommendation has been made by a Health and Social Care consultant, as GPs remain responsible for ensuring that any prescription aligns with their professional obligations, competence, regulatory guidance, does not breach the terms of their contract or indemnity cover, and is in keeping with the General Medical Council (GMC) guidance on Good Medical Practice.Armour Thyroid contains both thyroxine (T4) and tri-iodothyronine (T3) extracted from the thyroid gland of pigs known as desiccated pig thyroid extract (DTE). DTE, also referred to as natural thyroid extract, is an unlicensed medication in the UK and the current NICE recommendation is that it should not be offered. There is not enough evidence that it offers benefits over levothyroxine, and the long-term adverse effects are uncertain. The British Thyroid Association state that the routine use of DTE is not recommended, advising that prescribing should be reserved for use by endocrinologists prescribing in individual patients (under exceptional circumstances).

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on efforts to reduce waiting times for adult ADHD assessments within the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: There is currently no commissioned service for Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) in Northern Ireland. ADHD provision has evolved across Trusts in response to population demand and expertise within HSC teams. There is some provision available across the Health and Social Care system, but there are inconsistencies and lengthy waiting lists across Trusts. Although there is no commissioned Adult ADHD service, Belfast Health and Social Care Trust does provide some level of provision. Belfast Health and Social Care Trust reconfigured a small resource (0.5 Consultant Psychiatry) from within adult teams to process these referrals. Current waiting list is in excess of 3500, waiting times for an ADHD assessment based on current provision is 8 years from the point of referral.The National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) estimates that 3-4% of the adults in the UK have ADHD.The Department commissioned a needs assessment on ADHD in early February 2025, the findings of which will help inform options for a commissioned ADHD service across Trusts, subject to future budget availability.  The needs assessment, which covers both adults and children, will aim to determine the level of demand for a commissioned service, as well as recommendations for what such a service might look like, including potential implementation and staffing costs. The ADHD needs assessment report was received by the Department at the end of November 2025 and is currently being considered by officials, with a view to identifying next steps and arranging publication thereafter.In line with the usual process for commissioning of services, any decision to commission adult ADHD services in the future will be based on an assessment of the level of demand for services, and in the context of future budget availability.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any discussions she had with the Ministry of Defence, regarding Northern Ireland's allocation under the Local Defence Growth Deals.
answer_text: My officials have had initial discussions with British Government officials in relation to its Defence Growth Deal, in the context of the wider Industrial Strategy.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail how the Public Sector Transformation Board will support the Executive's AI Strategy.
answer_text: The primary role of the Public Sector Transformation Board (PSTB) is to advise and make independent recommendations on how to utilise funding which has been ring fenced for the purpose of transformation to improve public services outcomes.Transformation projects will align with the Executive’s AI strategy to deliver measurable improvements in cost, efficiency, and citizen experience.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the digital transformation element of the Public Sector Transformation Board.
answer_text: A Public Sector Transformation Board has been established to advise and make independent recommendations on how to utilise transformation funding to improve public services. A second call for transformation proposals has recently concluded, and the Board is currently finalising its advice and recommendations for Ministerial agreement. This will include advice on how to drive digital transformation across the NICS and wider public services. The Board recognises the central role that digital technology can play in transforming how services are delivered.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to outline any assessment that has been made of the effectiveness of the North Belfast Strategic Good Relations Programme.

To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of the Chancellor's announcement on lowering energy bills across the United Kingdom in the Autumn Budget 2025, to detail (i) when this will be applied in Northern Ireland; and (ii) any barriers that exist to its introduction, alongside the rest of the United Kingdom.
answer_text: Officials are assessing the schemes and costs associated with this Budget intervention with the aim of ensuring that energy consumers in the North benefit appropriately.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when the terms and conditions of the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee scheme will be published.
answer_text: Delivering additional renewable electricity generation is complex, demanding a careful balance between security of supply, affordability, and legislative compliance.My officials are prioritising the Primary Bill needed to make the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee scheme operational. Publication of the Terms and Conditions, now expected later in 2026, will follow the introduction of the Bill to the Assembly and once all necessary arrangements are in place.I am confident that we are building the right foundations to incentivise sufficient electricity generation whilst ensuring the scheme delivers the best outcomes for energy consumers and that local communities benefit from the transition to net zero.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any work the Forest Service has undertaken for their land to site onshore renewable energy.
answer_text: I recognise the potential for the use of the Department’s forests to contribute to renewable energy generation, and associated targets contained within the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022. This year Forest Service undertook a market engagement exercise which identified several potential candidate forests for wind energy development. Forest Service, in line with its statutory obligations under the Forestry Act (Northern Ireland) 2010, is assessing the compatibility of renewable energy projects at these forests with its sustainable forestry management duties. Sustainable forest management includes provision for current and future timber production. Following the significant storm damage in 2025, revision of harvesting plans is ongoing to ensure sustainability. This work will also help determine the most suitable areas for windfarm development and inform the Business Case processes and subsequent offer of a competitive procurement opportunity in 2026.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when the first auction for the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee scheme will take place.
answer_text: Delivering additional renewable electricity generation is complex, demanding a careful balance between security of supply, affordability, and legislative compliance.My officials are prioritising the Primary Bill needed to make the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee scheme operational. Once the primary Bill is in place, subordinate legislation will be taken forward to set out the detailed arrangements.These powers will enable us to run auctions at the right time, securing the best technologies and capacity to meet our targets at a fair price for consumers. The timeline for the first auction will be confirmed alongside the consultation on the Terms and Conditions, which is expected later in 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee legislation.
answer_text: My Department is targeting to introduce the required primary legislation for the REPG to the Assembly before summer recess 2026. Once the Primary Bill is in place, subordinate legislation will be taken forward to set out the detailed arrangements.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the Enhanced Investment Zone.
answer_text: Subject to the Executive’s approval, the intention is to seek final approval from the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government early in 2026 with the aim of Programme spend to commence in this financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department has a policy on providing funds to an organisation which has been found not to have fully co-operated with a police investigation
answer_text: All funding decisions are governed by the principles set out in Managing Public Money Northern Ireland.  My Department must ensure that public funds are used lawfully and properly, that the Accounting Officer safeguards against undue risk and reputational damage, and that funding agreements include conditions requiring compliance with all relevant laws and standards of propriety.Where concerns arise about legality, propriety, or reputation of an organisation, my Department will review the funding arrangement.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether their Department has a policy on providing funds to an organisation which has been found not to have fully co-operated with a police investigation
answer_text: As part of the overall governance and control framework, the Departmental Fraud Prevention Policy and Response Plan details responsibilities for the prevention of fraud, and the procedures to be followed in the event of a fraud being detected or suspected.  This document relates to fraud and loss both within the Department and its Arm’s Length Bodies.The Departmental Fraud Investigation Oversight Group oversees the investigation of fraud related raising concerns allegations, allegations of financial irregularity, relevant PSNI action and options for action if funds have been obtained fraudulently.  A summary of the work of the Group is provided in the published Annual Report and Accounts within the Accountability Report.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 35185/22-27, to detail how providing an assessment with a decision already made, interferes with democratic decision making.
answer_text: The decision is for the elected members of the Council and not for me.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) any time spent, to date, by staff on developing her Department's Irish language policy; (ii) the number of requests received by her Department from the public, since she took office, for Irish language services; (iii) when the draft policy will be available for scrutiny.
answer_text: (i)As outlined in previous responses, the work on the Draft Irish Language Policy is ancillary to other work and separate time records are not kept by staff. However, it is estimated that the total of staff time occupied in developing the draft policy would be relatively minimal (less than 5%) in terms of meetings, drafting and circulating for comment.(ii)Accessibility of Irish Language services is frequently raised with me by stakeholders and is a provision that I am keen to promote across my Department and Partner Organisations(iii)A consultation on the draft policy will issue in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the sale of land owned by Translink at the Abbey Centre, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: The Abbey Trading Centre site has been deemed by Translink to be surplus to operational requirements. They are now preparing the property for future disposal, commencing with the D1 process shortly and proceeding to open market disposal if necessary.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the amount of funding awarded by his Department to Glór na Móna in (i) 2024-2025; and (ii) 2025-2026.
To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on Project Gigabit.
answer_text: The procurement process for Project Gigabit is now approaching its conclusion. I am informed that the contract is expected to be awarded in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the amount of corporation tax paid by registered companies in Northern Ireland, in each of the last three financial years.
answer_text: The Office for National Statistics (ONS) Country and Regional Public Sector Finances publication estimates the North’s Corporation Tax receipts to be £936 million in 2020/21, £1,203 million in 2021/22, and £1,334 million in 2022/23, the most recent time period for which statistics are available. Estimates are based on an apportionment of a company's employees across Wales, Scotland, England and the North.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the projected cost, in the first year, of lowering the corporation tax rate in Northern Ireland to 12.5 per cent.
answer_text: To be undertaken effectively, a formal assessment of the cost of reducing Corporation Tax would need to be done in conjunction with Treasury and HMRC, who hold the relevant data. This was the case with previous estimates of the cost of Corporation Tax devolution. The last estimates provided in August 2017, projected a net cost of around £240m per annum – this was the difference between the reduction Treasury would apply to the block grant at the point of devolution, and the Corporation Tax receipts the Executive would expect to receive at the reduced 12.5% rate.  Since then, the Corporation Tax rate has increased from 19% to 25%, a 6-percentage point increase, and local Corporation Tax revenues are estimated to have risen from £822 million in 2017/18 to £1.3 billion in 2022/23.  As a result, the net cost of reducing Corporation Tax locally to either 12.5%, or the 15% global minimum rate on large multinational companies, can be expected to be significantly higher than previous estimates, costing hundreds of millions of pounds more, on an annual basis. This is also at a time when the benefits are likely to be less certain given changes in the global economy and geopolitical context. My department continues to engage with Treasury to progress work on fiscal devolution and to inform future Executive decisions in this regard.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) to detail why some patients have difficulty obtaining a prescription for ADHD medication from their GP, despite receiving a private diagnosis from a qualified and regulated UK-based service; and (ii) for his assessment of whether increasing shared care access for such patients with a private diagnosis would assist in reducing the Health and Social Care Trust waiting lists for ADHD assessments.
answer_text: (i) Patients choosing to procure healthcare privately are effectively stepping outside of the health and social care (HSC) system.  In these circumstances, there is no obligation on GPs to provide pre and/or post treatment support.The legacy Health and Social Care Board issued correspondence on a number of occasions to private providers and general practice, most recently in June 2021, regarding requests for HSC prescriptions following private consultation.  The purpose of this correspondence was to remind both sectors of their clinical responsibilities in relation to prescribing of medications, including Red-Amber list drugs, to patients under their care. This guidance still applies and has been supplemented in September 2024 by guidance issued by the Strategic Planning and Performance Group  ‘Private (non-HSC) Prescribing Requests Guidance for Primary Care Prescribers’ in September 2024 - Microsoft Word - SPPG guidance - Request to prescribe following private consultations September 2024 FVThe guidance provides a reminder that the legal responsibility for prescribing lies with the prescriber who signs the prescription, and it is the responsibility of the individual prescriber to prescribe within their own level of competence and scope of practice.Medication recommended for Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) would be considered a Red-Amber list drug requiring a shared care agreement.  Decisions on whether or not to enter into a shared care arrangement are based on the clinical judgement of the GP taking account of the availability of specialist care and a written shared care agreement setting out the roles of the specialist Consultant, the GP and the patient.When a GP considers that a privately recommended treatment is clinically appropriate; is content that the treatment falls within their clinical remit; and that if necessary appropriate shared care arrangements have been agreed with the private provider and the patient/carers, then they may issue an HSC prescription.  However, in doing so, the GP is accepting clinical responsibility for this decision.(ii) Currently, there is no specifically commissioned ADHD service for adults in Northern Ireland.  This means that patients choosing private care should expect to cover all associated costs, including tests, procedures, and ongoing access to medications. Drugs prescribed to treat ADHD are controlled and it is advised that they are prescribed initially by a Consultant. Anyone requesting a prescription through the HSC, will have to be physically assessed by the HSC prescribing clinician to assess their medical need. Drug treatment for children, young people and adults with ADHD should always form part of a comprehensive treatment plan that includes psychological, behavioural and educational advice and interventions (or occupational needs in the case of adults). If prescribing under shared care agreements it should be clear where responsibility for prescribing lies.With no commissioned

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any discussions he has had with the Department for Culture, Media and Sport for the continuation of the grassroots football investment fund.
answer_text: I have engaged with the Department for Culture, Media and Sport on the continuation of grassroots football investment, during meetings with the UK Sports Minister, Stephanie Peacock, in October 2024 and again in April 2025 alongside Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State Fleur Anderson.These discussions focused on sustaining the UK Multi-Sport Grassroots Facilities Programme and the need for further investment in football facilities.I remain committed to securing additional funding to meet the full demand.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when the review into Class C vehicles under the Taxi Act (Northern Ireland) 2008, will be complete.
answer_text: Officials are planning on providing me with an options paper, on the review of Class C taxi licensing, before the end of the year and any proposed changes to legislation, regulations or policy will be subject to a public consultation in early 2026.It is anticipated the review will be complete by Spring 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any concerns she has been made aware of regarding the use of Class C vehicles under the Taxi Act (Northern Ireland) 2008.
answer_text: I am aware that the taxi industry, in particular the wedding and funeral car sector, have been seeking a review of Class C licensing for a number of years. On 25 March 2025, I announced a phased review of taxi policy and legislation commencing with a review of Class C Taxi Licensing.As part of this review, I have had the opportunity to listen to stakeholder concerns regarding the use of Class C vehicles under the Taxi Act and my Officials and I are scheduled to hold further meetings in the coming weeks. Stakeholders have also been sent a survey to gather additional information on the operation of Class C vehicle and driver licensing.Officials are planning on providing me with an options paper, for improving the operation of Class C taxi licensing in the North, before the end of the year, and any proposed changes to legislation, regulations or policy will be subject to a public consultation in early 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether Buttermilk Loney, North Belfast, qualifies for grit box provision.
answer_text: My Department commits significant resources to maintaining approximately 5,600 salt bins that are provided for use by the public, on a self-help basis on public roads which do not qualify for inclusion on the gritting schedule, providing the necessary criteria are met.Residents can make an application to the Department to request the installation of a salt bin in their area however, provision of new salt boxes is not automatic and may be refused if the location in question is not on the publicly maintained road network; the gradient of the road in question is less than 5%; or if reasonable alternative routes are available.When considering requests for the provision of a salt bin, my officials carry out an evaluation using a criteria based system which takes into account various factors including topography, traffic volume, road gradient/geometry, alternative routes as well as other factors such as residential usage and community facilities including schools, hospitals and care homes for the elderly.A salt bin has previously been provided by my Department at the section of Buttermilk Loney which forms part of the adopted road network. There is an additional section of the Buttermilk Loney development which remains unadopted, and the developer would be responsible for any salt bin provision in this section.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for his assessment of the Law Society of Northern Ireland's rejection of plans to lower the standard of proof, applied by the Solicitors Disciplinary Tribunal for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I understand that, through the Law Society, the Solicitors Disciplinary Tribunal proposed, amongst other changes, amending the standard of proof applied by the Tribunal and it was solicitor members of the Society themselves who opposed it at a Special General Meeting on 23 October. It is a matter for the Tribunal to agree its rules, and I welcome its commitment to considering all responses it receives to the proposed changes.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to outline any further consideration she has given to including Harper's Law in her forthcoming Sentencing Bill.
To ask the Minister for the Economy when the Domestic Abuse Safe Leave regulations will be published.
answer_text: I am fully committed to introducing the legislation to operationalise Safe leave during this mandate.  The workplace can provide a safe space and staying in employment can help victims and survivors of domestic abuse to retain their financial independence.  However, they may also need time off work to deal with issues connected to that domestic abuse.  People in this situation should continue to be paid, so that they do not suffer financially.  My Department consulted on proposals to operationalise the Safe Leave Act last year. I hope to publish the findings as soon as possible.  As well as introducing regulations, my Department will issue supporting guidance for employers and workers to help them navigate this new right. Developing the guidance will entail engaging with key stakeholders to ensure it provides proper support to workers and employers so that this important right can be accessed by those who need it. I hope that access to paid safe leave from work will help victims and survivors of domestic abuse to access vital support.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail when the school crossing patrol at Harmony Primary School, Belfast, will be reinstated.
answer_text: My department is responsible for the Home to School Transport Policy and the Education Authority (EA) for the delivery of school transport which includes the operation of the School Crossing Patrol (SCP) Scheme. Where an SCP post becomes vacant, the EA undertakes site assessments to ascertain if the location still meets the criteria for the operation of an SCP. The EA informs me that it has undertaken three site assessments at Harmony Primary School and it determined that it does not meet the minimum threshold criteria and subsequently there are no plans to reinstate the school crossing patrol. The school was advised of this outcome on 19 November 2025, however if the Board of Governors is not satisfied with the decision then they may request that it is reviewed by the EA.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the trade levels between Northern Ireland and Israel, over the last five years.
answer_text: The requested statistics can be found on this website Regional trade data table.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any actions her Department is taking forward from the recommendations of the Matrix report, AI and the Future Work in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: When I launched the Matrix report ‘AI and the Future of Work in Northern Ireland’ on 26 September, I highlighted AI's significant potential to drive my Economic Vision for the north. Indeed, AI is already delivering real benefits for local businesses—boosting productivity, creating high-quality jobs, and supporting sectors like energy in the green transition. In October my officials established a new AI and Digital Technologies (AIDTech) team to consider the Panel’s recommendations in detail and to develop action plans that will help ensure the north is well positioned to capitalise on the opportunities presented by AI.To support this and ensure actions are co-designed, my officials are in the process of establishing an AI Advisory Panel, which will bring together expertise from across the sector, academia and government, to consider the Matrix recommendations and develop delivery plans as appropriate. I am confident that these actions will help drive innovation, support our workforce, and secure a prosperous future for our economy.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission for an update on work to improve security measures for MLAs and staff in Parliament Buildings.
answer_text: The Assembly Commission does not discuss the detail of security arrangements within Parliament Buildings in public, however, I can confirm that the Assembly Commission keeps security under regular review and the PSNI have carried out three reviews of security at Parliament Buildings in 2011, 2015 and 2022.  PSNI also provided a further Vulnerabilities Assessment in 2025.On 19 November 2025, a number of recommendations were discussed at a meeting of the Assembly Commission, and it was agreed that Assembly officials will now undertake further scoping to bring back advice for further consideration.In the coming months, the Assembly Commission will commence the installation of a partial electronic access control (EAC) system which will enable the creation of a designated public or visitor zone within Parliament Buildings.  Only persons who are in possession of an Assembly ID pass will be able to pass from that public or visitor zone into other restricted areas of the building.  The installation of this EAC system will be a significant improvement to the personal security and safety of Members and other building users.  Members will receive information on this in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she will be attending the annual members' reception of Aerospace, Defence, Security and Space NI, in Parliament Buildings, on Thursday 4 December 2025.
answer_text: I have not received an invite to this event.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on an improved parking scheme at Hillview Avenue, Cloughfern.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government and limited budget resources mean we must prioritise our interventions to those locations deemed most in need. Regrettably, when assessed against these competing priorities, the proposal for Hillview Avenue did not score highly enough for inclusion in the 2025/26 works programme. Details of the schemes planned for the 2025/26 financial year are included in my Department’s Annual Report to Council.

answer_text: The Department undertook an initial equality screening prior to the publication of my Written Ministerial Statement on 16 October. The screening concluded no adverse impact and therefore, no Equality Impact Assessment was required.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail any support provided through her Department, to assist organisations supporting male victims of domestic abuse.
answer_text: I recognise the need for support to be provided to male victims of domestic abuse.		The seven year Domestic and Sexual Abuse Strategy (2024–2031), which is jointly led by my Department and the Department of Health, recognises that domestic and sexual abuse can affect any individual, regardless of age, ethnicity, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation or any form of disability. 		The purpose of the Strategy’s Support and Provision Pillar is to ensure that victims are supported, feel safe regardless of diversity of need and are guided into services quickly.  A 24 Hour Domestic and Sexual Abuse Helpline is open to all victims.  In addition my Department also funds a domestic and sexual abuse advocacy service which is delivered by ASSIST NI and which is available to qualifying victims who can receive tailored help and support, specific to their needs regardless of gender.My Department provides core grant funding of £2 million to Victim Support NI which enables them to provide a range of support services to all victims and witnesses of crime, including male victims of domestic abuse. Through this funding, back-office costs are met together with service delivery costs for their range of services, providing victims and witnesses with emotional support, information and advice; assistance with claiming Criminal Injuries Compensation; support attending court; and signposting to other specialist services. 	Funding was also provided to the Commissioner Designate for Victims of Crime Office to carry out a mapping exercise of service provision across Northern Ireland for victims of domestic and sexual violence.  The first phase of this exercise on services available to male adult and child victims in Northern Ireland was published in August 2025. The report has been helpful in highlighting several gaps including a need to ensure greater inclusivity within existing support services. 		Funding has been allocated to the Men’s Advisory Project through both the Victims of Crime Fund and the Domestic and Sexual Abuse Strategy’s Small Grant Scheme to support outreach, counselling, and awareness-raising. My Department recognises the importance of ensuring that support is inclusive of all victims, including men and boys and will continue to work with other partner Departments to meet the needs of victims. Through the DSA Strategy, work is already under way to promote a gender-inclusive and trauma-informed approach to service delivery.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any advice she (i) sought; and (ii) received, from her Permanent Secretary regarding Israel, in advance of her Written Ministerial Statement on the Invest NI Review of Investments on 16 October 2025.
answer_text: I seek and receive advice from my officials, including my Permanent Secretary, on all matters within my remit.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any ongoing work his Department is currently undertaking to tackle rates of suicide amongst men.
answer_text: There are a wide variety of programmes funded under the Protect Life 2 Strategy that target men and services are tailored to men where possible.The Public Health Agency (PHA) commission regional and locality based suicide prevention services and programmes which are open and accessible to all sections of the community, including men. These would include Lifeline, the recently re-commissioned Self Harm Intervention Programme which is available on a regional basis, Flourish! Churches programme, training, Psychological First Aid and Stress Control classes, and The Rainbow Project which provides support for the mental and emotional well-being of lesbian, gay and bisexual people in Northern Ireland.Health and Social Care Trusts also provide MANifest men’s mental health training across the region. A wide range of programmes are also funded on a short term basis in local areas that have a focus on men including resilience building, mental health promotion through sport, men’s sheds, allotment and horticulture programmes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when a policy decision on liquid gas will be made.
answer_text: Earlier this year, following a period of stakeholder engagement, my Department carried out call for evidence to assess the role liquid biofuels, including BioLPG, could play in moving households and businesses away from fossil fuels for heating. This work is progressing through the final stages of analysis and review, with a report presenting the findings of the call for evidence due to be published in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on progressing the works to improve road safety to the junction of Shore Road and the M2 at Mount Vernon, Belfast.
answer_text: As previously advised, a cost estimate for the scheme is being undertaken.  The cost estimate was expected in October 2025, however due to resource pressures and other priority work, the estimate will not be considered until the new year.Subject to a successful outcome, the proposal will be considered for progression to detailed design, dependent on the availability of funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail (i)the number of occasions during the reduced-staffing period at NI Water pumping stations between 2020 - 2022, due to Covid-19 that any Emergency Relief Overflows (EROs) were triggered automatically, resulting in untreated wastewater entering waterways; (ii) how the volume of those ERO discharges compares with the company’s modelled annual spill figure of 16-20 million m³ of untreated or partially treated wastewater; and (iii) whether these ERO events were included in the assessment of nutrient inputs contributing to
answer_text: My Department does not collect or hold the information requested. I would advise you to contact NI Water for this information.My Department has not made a comparison with the modelled spill frequency. Again, I would advise you to contact NI Water for this information.A catchment-based approach was used in the New Harmonica Project, with Source Load Apportionment Modelling (SLAM) applied to available data for the reference year 2021.  The project’s objective was to model flows of nutrients (nitrogen and phosphorus) within the whole Neagh-Bann catchment system. ,.  This modelling approach was not designed to explicitly capture or quantify specific events in both time and space. As outlined in the Lough Neagh Action Plan, commissioned data gathering and modelling will build on the evidence base to enhance understanding at spatial and temporal scales, adding greater detail to the existing modelling of nutrient inputs.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail how the Education Authority's youth services, based in North Belfast, including the Greater Shankill area, are supporting and catering for children and young people with special educational needs, in the provision they offer.
answer_text: The Education Authority’s Youth Service (EAYS) has advised that it supports children and young people with special educational needs and disabilities (SEND) through inclusive youth work provision across the statutory, community and voluntary sectors.The EAYS has confirmed that a youth provision ‘Programme of Offer’, specific to SEND, is provided in the following areas of North Belfast including Greater Shankill:Glencairn Youth Centre ‘SENsational Hour’: Age 7-12 years, Monday from 5.30pm - 6.30pm, staffed by Youth Service. Fortwilliam Youth Centre ‘Reaching Beyond Programme’: Age 14-18 years, Tuesday from 5.00pm - 7.00pm, jointly staffed by Youth Service and Cedar Lodge School.Mountcollyer Youth Centre ‘SENsational Hour’:  Age 9-12 years, Tuesday from 6.00pm - 7.00pm, jointly staffed by Youth Service and Cedar Lodge School.Hammer Youth Centre ‘SENsational Hour’:  Age 12-15 years, Wednesday from 600pm - 7.00pm, jointly staffed by Youth Service and Cedar Lodge School.Mountcollyer Youth Centre ‘SENsational Hour’: Age 9-12 years, Thursday from 6.00pm – 7.00pm, staffed by Youth Service.Life skills development programme in Cedar Lodge School:  Year’s 11 and 12, Wednesday and Thursday from 9.00am – 12.00pm, staffed by Youth Service.Centre Based Youth Work for Young People with SEND in Belfast (including North and East Belfast) – 3 nights opening, with local area based youth funding awarded to Kids Together in partnership with SOLAS for children and young people aged 9-18 years.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for her Department's assessment of (i) the 12 week trial period permitting taxis to use bus lanes in 2017; and (ii) whether extending this type of scheme could assist in reducing congestion in Belfast.
answer_text: The assessment of the 12 week trial undertaken in 2017 can be found on my department’s website using the following link; Class A Taxis in Bus Lanes Trial 2017 - Data Review Technical Report | Department for Infrastructure.  This Report concluded that the trial was not long enough to provide robust data to obtain meaningful statistics for consideration.  A similar type of trial aimed at reducing congestion in Belfast is currently ongoing, having started in December 2024, to permit all classes of taxis to use bus lanes on Bruce Street, Dublin Road, Great Victoria Street and Shaftesbury Square. Following a recent consultation, these arrangements will be continuing to gather robust and meaningful data. This trial is continuing to be monitored to determine whether extending this type of scheme could assist in reducing congestion in Belfast.  Full details can be found on my Department’s website Experimental Traffic Control Scheme (Taxis in Bus Lanes - Belfast City Centre) 2025 Direction Order.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department is considering the resurfacing of Hallidays Road, Belfast.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government.While there are many roads that would benefit from investment, due to limited budget resources, resurfacing work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need for intervention.Details of the resurfacing schemes planned for the 2025/26 financial year are included in my Department’s Annual Report to Council.Officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with my Department’s Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the delivery of the proposed Traffic Management Scheme at Rathcoole Primary School, Newtownabbey, following the anticipated opening of the project for tender applications in October 2025.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has advised that there was a small delay with issue of the Expression of Interest for six contractors for the traffic management scheme at Rathcoole Primary School. The tenders were issued on 12 November 2025, to be returned by 3 December 2025.Award of the contract will be dependent on approval of a business case addendum and will be subject to budget availability against competing priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 34465/22-27, whether she considers her visit to the United States of America, from 13 November 2025, as a trade mission.
answer_text: I did not lead a delegation of businesses from the north to the USA and therefore my recent trip to Boston cannot be described as a trade mission.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department is considering the resurfacing of (i) Knockane Way; and (ii) Knockenagh Walk, within Rathfern Estate, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government.While there are many roads that would benefit from investment, due to limited budget resources, resurfacing work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need for intervention.Details of the resurfacing schemes planned for the 2025/26 financial year are included in my Department’s Annual Report to Council.Officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with my Department’s Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether (i) her Department carried out an Equality Impact Assessment, under Section 75 of the Northern Ireland Act 1998, on its 2024-25 Business Plan, (ii) an Equality Impact Assessment was specifically carried out at any time, into her decision to develop a departmental Irish language policy; and (iii) her decision to develop an Irish language policy, without an equivalent for Ulster Scots, was identified at any point as an equality issue.
answer_text: My Department did not undertake an equality screening of the overall business plan as the various components will have been screened in their own right.  My Department’s development of a Draft Irish Language Policy has been screened in accordance with section 75 of the Northern Ireland Act 1998 and that exercise did not identify any differential impacts. 	I look forward to working with both the Irish Language and the Ulster Scots Language Commissioners now that they are in post.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 31964/22-27, to detail when the next review of published patient information for the Northern Ireland Cervical Screening Programme is anticipated to take place.
answer_text: Published patient information for the Northern Ireland Cervical Screening Programme is reviewed annually by the Public Health Agency (PHA), with the next review and reprint due to take place in the summer of 2026. In the interim, the Cervical Screening page on NI Direct has been updated to reflect that the Northern Ireland Cervical Screening Programme does not screen for other cancers of the reproductive system, such as ovary, womb, vulval or vaginal cancer https://www.nidirect.gov.uk/articles/cervical-screening Screening programmes seek to screen healthy individuals, anyone with any symptoms of concerns should not hesitate to contact their GP.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) whether his Department is engaging with staff impacted by the proposed staff car parking pass changes to be implemented in January 2026 at the Royal Victoria Hospital; and (ii) to detail what consideration is being given to improving car parking at and near the hospital site for staff, including at the derelict Broadway tower blocks site.
answer_text: (i) The Belfast Health and Social Care (HSC) Trust have confirmed that the criteria-based parking permit application process for the Royal Victoria Hospital (RVH) has been paused and all staff who have previously held a car parking permit will retain their permit.The Trust will now consider the feasibility of increasing the number of available permits, the potential to provide alternative transport solutions, and their ability to reduce demand at the RVH site. (ii) The Department of Health’s regional Hospital Parking Charges working group meets regularly with regional HSC Trust representatives, including the Belfast HSC Trust, to discuss any car parking policy issues. The Belfast HSC Trust undertook a scoping exercise for the addition of a modular style car park on the RVH site. Trust staff met with Departmental officials in October 2024 to discuss delivery options and potential for capital funding.In advance of developing more detailed proposals for modular style parking, the Trust have decided it would be prudent to engage with the Planning Service to determine the likelihood of achieving the required planning approval.The development of options to alleviate car parking issues at RVH are restricted by the severe financial challenges facing the Department. The demolition of the derelict Broadway tower blocks site and redevelopment of the site cannot proceed until the necessary funds are secured.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the pay settlement for further education lecturers.
answer_text: Pay settlements for further education lecturers are a matter for further education colleges, as employers. I am advised by Further Education colleges that the most recent pay settlement for 2024/25 and 2025/26 has been implemented as agreed, with associated conditions of the pay award progressing in line with the timelines agreed with the recognised Trade Unions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any improvements that can be made to signage and lighting at the pedestrian crossing at Abotts Cross Primary School, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: Road safety is a high priority for my Department, and we are committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and to address the needs of all road users. We are acutely aware that children and young people are amongst the most vulnerable groups using our roads, particularly around schools.However, my Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government and limited budget resources mean we must prioritise our interventions to those locations deemed most in need. I understand that this location already has a controlled crossing in place with appropriate lighting and signage and the school already benefits from a 20mph safety zone.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 30678/22-27, why she will not provide her assessment of the decision.
answer_text: It is not for ministers to interfere with the democratic decision making of local representatives.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any discussions he has had regarding upgrading Windsor Park.

answer_text: The Dispatch Down Working Group, established by SONI and comprising representatives from SONI, the Utility Regulator, and my Department, first met on 9 September 2024 and has continued to meet fortnightly since then.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any road safety improvements that can be made to the pedestrian crossing at Cloughfern Roundabout, Newtownabbey, including additional lighting and signage.
answer_text: Road safety is a high priority for my Department, and we are committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and to address the needs of all road users.However, my Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government and limited budget resources mean we must prioritise our interventions to those locations deemed most in need.Planning approval for a new ASDA store on the Doagh Road requires the upgrade of Cloughfern Roundabout.  My officials are engaging with the developer to confirm the design of their proposals to upgrade the roundabout which will improve the layout for the benefit and safety of all road users, including pedestrians and cyclists.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the (i) demolition; and (ii) development, of vested (a) properties; and (b) land, at Fortwilliam Parade, Belfast.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“Demolition of Vested PropertiesThe properties vested at Fortwilliam Parade are currently in the tendering process for demolition. The brief and scope of works have been issued to two consultants for pricing, with quotes expected by the end of November. Tender documents are being prepared to enable an accelerated start on site, with demolition anticipated during the 2026/27 financial year. Development of Vested Properties and Land At this stage, no housing association has been appointed to redevelop the site or the associated vested lands. However, a housing association is expected to be appointed in Quarter 4 of the 2025/26 financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail how Kings Park Primary School could be granted nursery provision.
answer_text: For any significant change to a school, such as the establishment of a statutory nursery class, legislation currently requires the publication and approval of a statutory Development Proposal (DP).  There is no alternative mechanism for a statutory nursery class to be established outside the DP process. Should Kings Park Primary School wish to consider this they are advised to contact their managing authority, the Education Authority (EA), in the first instance.  Further guidance on the DP process can also be found here.An alternative to creating new statutory provision, may be the establishment of non-statutory pre-school provision at the school. If Kings Park Primary School wish to consider this option, I would advise that they engage with the EA Pre-School Team who can be contacted at: EAPSEP@eani.org.uk

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail when works on a new parking and drop-off area at King's Park Primary School, Newtownabbey will commence.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has confirmed that planning approval for the traffic management project at King’s Park Primary School was granted on 30 May 2025. The design team is currently finalising the tender documentation for the works. Upon receipt of this information, the EA has advised that officers will assess the budget position against competing priorities and consider whether there is sufficient resource available to proceed with the tendering process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether blue and green infrastructure funding can be utilised to create a pathway through derelict land at top of Burnthill Road and Carnmoney Road, Newtownabbey, to improve walking and cycling provision in the area.
answer_text: The Blue Green Infrastructure Fund ended in 2023 and therefore cannot be utilised for this proposal.
