Future investment in significant active travel infrastructure improvements in the area will be prioritised in line with the emerging Active Travel Delivery Plan.Public consultation on the draft plan concluded earlier this year and officials are currently finalising the Active Travel Delivery Plan for my consideration. Once published, it will provide a clear plan for investment in active travel in our larger towns and cities for the next ten years and beyond.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the rational for Newtownabbey Methodist Mission Pre School receiving 10 funded pre-school places, despite having 35 first preference applications last year.
answer_text: The Education Authority Pre-School Education Group (EA PEG) has responsibility for securing non-statutory pre-school education provision in local areas and for ensuring that sufficient pre-school provision is available in all areas to accommodate every target aged child whose parents want it. The EA wrote to all non-statutory pre-school settings on 7 February 2025 to set out in detail the rationale used for determining PAN allocations for the 2025/26 academic year.  A copy of this letter was previously placed in the Assembly library in response to AQW 24601/22-27.In practice the EA will only increase places in funded settings to meet an overall shortfall of provision in an area. Within a two-mile radius of Newtownabbey Methodist Mission Pre-School PG a total of 569 funded pre-school places were available for the 2025/26 academic year to accommodate the 546 first preference applications received during the 2025/26 pre-school admissions process.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 33293/22-27, for his assessment of the increasing number of applicants on the Band 5 regional waiting list for Speech and Language Therapy careers whilst the number of university places for this qualification remains at current levels.
To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she received Executive approval for the content of her Written Ministerial Statement on the Invest NI Review of Investments on 16 October 2025.
To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the cost of the additional run hours approved, at Kilroot Power Station, since September 2025.
answer_text: Kilroot Power Station’s generation costs are recovered through the normal market mechanisms. All electricity generators earn revenue by trading through the Single Electricity Market (SEM).  This mechanism is for all generation required to meet demand, regardless if it provided by Kilroot, Coolkeeragh, Ballylumford or from renewables and imports.  All activity within the SEM is carefully monitored by the Utility Regulator to ensure that generators are adhering to market rules.  We are working with UR who will be responsible for reporting on this matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, in light of the security of supply issues which resulted in the Executive having to approve additional run hours at Kilroot Power Station, whether he will consider repealing climate change targets.
answer_text: I refer to Dr Archibald’s statement to this Assembly on 6 November 2025 which set out detail of the exceptional challenges to security of supply and mitigating action that has been taken. 		I will not be considering repealing the 2030, 2040 or 2050 emissions reduction targets.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any security of supply issues that have arisen since 1 November 2025.
answer_text: I refer to my statement of 6 November 2025, providing an update on the security of electricity supply for the North. Department for the Economy: Update on Security of Electricity Supply

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the remaining resurfacing schemes in North Belfast in the 2025-26 financial year.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government.While there are many roads that would benefit from investment, due to limited budget resources, resurfacing work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need for intervention.Details of the schemes to be completed in the 2025/26 financial year will be provided in my Department’s annual report to Council.  This is scheduled for publication on 26 November 2025.Officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with my Department’s Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the security of supply issues which resulted in the Northern Ireland Executive approving additional run hours at Kilroot Power Station.
answer_text: I refer to my statement of 6 November 2025, providing an update on the security of electricity supply for the North. Department for the Economy: Update on Security of Electricity Supply

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail when the Communities in Transition Programme will be extended to other parts of North Belfast.
answer_text: In North Belfast, Communities in Transition (CIT) operates across two core areas of New Lodge and Ardoyne. CIT has also supported work in Tigers Bay in the past through an Area of Influence approach, which received £27,000 in funding.The future of and how it looks will be under consideration in due course.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail when the review of the North Belfast Strategic Good Relations Programme will be completed.
answer_text: A review of North Belfast Strategic Good Relations Programme was completed within the wider review of the T:BUC Strategy. We are actively working with officials regarding the review’s recommendations as part of the development of a refreshed approach to Good Relations.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any discussions he is having with Executive colleagues, regarding the lowering of Corporation Tax.
answer_text: At the Executive meeting on 6 November, I presented a paper setting out my approach to progressing discussions with the Treasury on a full Fiscal Framework. As part of that, my paper highlighted the range of taxes that could be suitable for devolution locally, as per the independent Fiscal Commission’s recommendations. I will engage further with Executive colleagues as my department’s work towards a full Fiscal Framework progresses on specific taxes and how they could be utilised if devolved.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when the drafting of her Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill will be completed.
answer_text: The drafting of the Employment Rights Bill will be completed as soon as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any further work he has undertaken to introduce a new phase of the Building Successful Communities initiative.
answer_text: The Building Successful Communities (BSC) Post Programme Review report has been finalised, and this will help inform any future interventions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the amount spent, to date, on legal proceedings over Irish language signage at Belfast Grand Central Station.
answer_text: My Department has not received all external legal costs to detail the amount spent, to date, on legal proceedings over Irish language signage at Belfast Grand Central Station, therefore we are not in a position to release the amount spent at this stage.I have stated previously that I remain committed to dialogue and avoiding the unnecessary use of public funds in litigation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, further to her Written Ministerial Statement on 16 October 2025 on Invest NI Review of Investments, to define the borders of Palestine, as referenced in her statement.
answer_text: The borders of Palestine, as recognised by the United Nations and supported by the International Court of Justice (ICJ), are based on the pre-1967 lines and include the West Bank, East Jerusalem, and the Gaza Strip.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when her Ethical Investment Framework, based on the United Nations Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights, will be published.
answer_text: This work is in development and there will be an opportunity for consultation in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when the review of the Energy Strategy will be published.
answer_text: The forthcoming Mid-Term Review of the Energy Strategy will set out key milestones through to 2030, ensuring transparency and accountability as we progress the delivery of the Energy Strategy. 		My Department aims to publish the Review by the end of 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail (i) any work her Department is undertaking to ensure enforcement of existing laws on the sale of fireworks; (ii) whether further restrictions for use of such would be considered, which would give greater protections to pets and areas of wildlife; and (iii) whether further regulations would be considered to prioritise the use of silent and low-noise fireworks in larger scale displays at public events.
answer_text: The existing fireworks licensing system aids partners, including PSNI, to identify unlicensed and illegal fireworks use.  My department provides PSNI with details of current registered retailers and stores, and officials will provide relevant information to support test purchases, statements and court attendance if required.Fireworks retailers are required to keep a record of fireworks sales, and submit the record to my Department annually.  Records are checked against licences issued, and any discrepancies are investigated and details shared with the PSNI.  My department may also consider revocation of a retailer’s registration to store fireworks if they fail to comply with the law.A fireworks licence includes conditions on use, which can include restrictions on types of fireworks that can be used depending on the type of event and location, and will only be issued if it is deemed safe to do so.  Additional conditions can be added to any licence as appropriate, and may include informing neighbours in advance.  Normally a licence will not permit fireworks displays to continue after 11pm.In my annual messaging, I remind people to be considerate of vulnerable people and animals and that low noise fireworks are available to purchase. The power to limit the decibel level of fireworks remains a reserved matter; however, information on firework safety and the availability of low noise fireworks is available all year round on the government services webpage nidirect.I have no current plans to review the existing fireworks licensing arrangements, however in recognition of the public concern around the misuse of fireworks, I have instructed my officials to examine penalties and enforcement concerning the illegal use or sale or fireworks in the pending review of sentencing issues.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the location of any planned trade missions she will attend in the remainder of 2025.
answer_text: I do not have any trade missions planned for the remainder of 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an for an update on her consultation on Developer Contributions for Wastewater Infrastructure.
answer_text: On 21 March I launched a consultation on developer contributions for wastewater infrastructure. The consultation ran for 14 weeks and sought views on options for a possible pathway to introduce developer contributions – either on a voluntary basis or in the form of a compulsory levy – which may help to fund wastewater upgrades to unlock the capacity of our sewerage network. 293 responses to the consultation were received.  These responses are currently being considered with a view to publishing a Consultation Responses Report in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any consideration her Department has given to a new pedestrian crossing at Loughside pitches, North Belfast.
answer_text: Road safety is a high priority for my Department, and we are committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and to address the needs of all road users. We are acutely aware that children and young people are amongst the most vulnerable groups using our roads, particularly around schools.However, my Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government and limited budget resources mean we must prioritise our interventions to those locations deemed most in need.As an assessment has not been undertaken previously, I have asked my officials to determine the need and priority of a crossing facility at this location. This assessment should be completed in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when Carolhill road, Newtownabbey, will be resurfaced.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government.While there are many roads that would benefit from investment, due to limited budget resources, resurfacing work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need for intervention.Details of the resurfacing schemes planned for the 2025/26 financial year are included in my Department’s Annual Report to Council.Officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with my Department’s Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 33402/22/27, when CAR-T cell therapy treatment will be available in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The delivery of CAR-T locally is dependent on the capital programme for the new haematology ward proceeding.  I have approved funding for the build subject to final approvals from Departmental officials.CAR-T services in the new ward will require service development funding to increase the capacity and this will be taken forward by the Department of Health and the Trust in advance of the completion of the ward as per established commissioning processes and subject to available funding at the time.In line with current timelines for completion of the build and attainment of relevant accreditation, Trust colleagues have advised that a CAR-T Cell Therapy service could be developed in Northern Ireland by 2030-31.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 33501/22-27, in recognition of its importance to the Economy, when she will meet with the ADS Group Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I have not received a request to meet with the ADS.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 33289/22-27, whether she will now instruct her officials to keep such records.
answer_text: My Department carries out regular inspections of all public roads and footways to ensure that essential response maintenance is identified and completed as necessary.  During these inspections, defaced signage is categorised as Traffic Sign Graffiti (TSG) on my Department’s Maintenance system.  This category does not provide the facility to indicate a description of the type of graffiti, and any mechanism to do so would involve significant changes to the current computerised system which would have an impact on the already limited resources available for the essential maintenance of our road network.In cases where signage presents a clear safety hazard, its removal will be prioritised. Other defective, damaged or missing signs, as well as those needing to be cleaned, are categorised with priority given to mandatory and warning signs. Cleaning of other signs is carried out where resources permit.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on his work to improve animal welfare.
answer_text: My Animal Welfare Pathway contains ten key reforms to improve animal welfare across four broad areas; companion animals; wild animals; farmed animals; and enforcement. A number of these reforms are already underway including recent public consultations on the introduction of Lucys Law and mandatory CCTV in abattoirs. Further, an expert advisory group to review dog breeding regulations here has been established and work is ongoing.My officials have commenced policy development work on the remaining reforms, and I hope to launch a public consultation in the months ahead.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the amount of funding allocated by his Department to dental practices, in the financial years (i) 2021-22; (ii) 2022-23; (iii) 2023-24; (iv) 2024-25; and (v) the projected figure for the end of 2025-26.
answer_text: The amount of funding allocated to dental practices is outlined in the table below:Expenditure £m2021/20222022/20232023/20242024/20252025/2026 est.133.9123.7112.6123.8121.6The funding to dental practices is based on supplier payment files as provided to the Strategic Planning and Performance  Group (SPPG) by BSO as part of their Annual Assurance Statements, as well as other payments processed directly by SPPG. The totals for each year exclude SPPG’s payments to HSC Pension Service in respect of employer superannuation costs for dentists, as those payments are not made to dental practices.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for her assessment of the role of restorative justice practitioners in the justice system.
To ask the Minister of Justice, following the judgment on 23 October 2025, for her assessment of the decision by the Public Prosecution Service to proceed with a case against Soldier F.
answer_text: The Public Prosecution Service is a non-ministerial government department in its own right and not therefore within my remit as Justice Minister.The decision on whether to prosecute cases is a matter solely for the independent Public Prosecution Service (PPS). It is an important tenet of our justice system that the work of the PPS remains independent of political interference. Infringing upon that for any case would set a dangerous precedent, with grave consequences for the separation of powers and checks and balances necessary for a fair and democratic justice system. Therefore, as Justice Minister, I cannot comment on the handling of prosecutions in individual cases.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of staff employed (i) full-time; and (ii) part-time, in her Department's Energy Team.
answer_text: At 24th October 2025 there are 115 full time staff (115 FTE) and 20 part time staff (14.29 FTE) employed in the Department’s Energy Group.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, following the adoption of Napoleon's Gate, North Belfast, whether grit boxes will now be installed.
answer_text: As an assessment for the provision of a grit box at this location has not been previously undertaken. I have asked my officials to complete an assessment for Napoleon’s Gate, Belfast in the coming days and provide you with an update on its completion.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he is considering reviewing the legal targets set in the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 29110/22-27, to detail whether a marching bands uniform grant could be introduced using resource budget of (i) the Arts Council; or (ii) any other cultural body, in conjunction with his Department.
answer_text: It would be possible in terms of vires to introduce such a scheme; but the needs of very many uniformed organisations would need to be considered when scoping any schemes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 33176/22-27, for her current assessment on the viability of this proposed airlink meeting her Department's climate change targets.
answer_text: As stated in my response to AQW 33176/22-27, my officials will continue to engage with relevant partners to understand the impacts and viability of the air service as this work progresses.  Where appropriate, this will include working with the relevant bodies, including the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs and the Department for Transport in London, who oversee policy mechanisms which support the decarbonisation of the aviation sector here.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 33595/22-27, when she anticipates the Department's aviation policy development will be completed.
answer_text: My officials intend to launch a public consultation on the matter in the coming months.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any steps (i) he has taken within the current financial year to support dental practices and their service provision; and (ii) his Department will take to address the declining availability of NHS-funded dentist places for patients.
answer_text: (i)	In June this year I announced £7m investment for 2025/26 aimed at supporting dental practices while longer-term reform is developed. The investment will fund continuation of the Enhanced Child Examination Scheme, a 30% enhancement on fees paid to dentists for Health Service fillings, extractions and root canal treatments, and a further £1.6m allocation to support dental practitioners who continue to provide Health Service dental care.  In addition, a further £2.5million has been made available for General Dental Services to help offset the impact of increased National Insurance contributions on General Dental Services. These measures reflect my Department’s ongoing commitment to sustaining dental provision, despite significant financial pressures. (ii)	I acknowledge the challenges facing many patients, particularly those not registered with a dental practice, in accessing dental care when they need it. General Dental Practitioners (GDPs) are self-employed, independent contractors who are free to choose the amount of Health Service (HS) care they provide. However, my Department has prioritised improving access to dental care through targeted investments, including the Dental Access Scheme (DAS). This scheme allows unregistered patients with an urgent or pressing (but non-urgent) dental need to receive treatment. While DAS does not cover ongoing care, it ensures that those in greatest need are not left without dental support. Longer term, my Department’s Health and Social Care NI three-year plan includes a commitment to develop an action plan and programme of work for the reform of General Dental Services by April 2027. I am clear that in common with other services, General Dental Services will require sustained effort to ensure sustainability over the longer term.  That is why I commissioned a cost-of-service review which will help inform the future direction of General Dental Services. The ability to implement transformative change however is significantly constrained by the Department’s £6 million budget deficit, which limits the scope for transformative change.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she will consider the relaxation of bus lane restrictions for taxis, during the Christmas period in Belfast City.
answer_text: In December last year my Department permitted all classes of taxis to use bus lanes on Bruce Street, Dublin Road, Great Victoria Street and Shaftesbury Square to ease congestion issues in the run up to Christmas last year. Following the recent consultation, I can advise that those arrangements will continue.  Full details can be found on my Department’s website at : Experimental Traffic Control Scheme (Taxis in Bus Lanes - Belfast City Centre) 2025 Direction OrderI fully understand the frustrations caused by traffic congestion and have recently announced a package of measures aimed at increasing bus reliability and reducing congestion during this year’s Christmas period. Extended hours of operation have been introduced on bus lanes along several of the key arterial routes to the south of the city, which were the worst affected with traffic congestion in the run up to Christmas last year.  The existing bus lanes on these routes will now operate during both morning and evening peak times.I have also announced that the road works embargo will be in place from 10 November after taking into consideration the level of congestion experienced last year, and the current closure of Durham Street. I welcome the contribution of Translink to accelerate their work programme with the aim of reopening Durham Street by the end of November 2025 which contribute to easing congestion in that part of the city. We can all play a part in reducing congestion by thinking about when to travel and how. Avoid peak times if you can and think about leaving the car at home and use public transport and park and ride facilities, if they are available to you.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the Audit Office report into her Department's delivery of the Northern Ireland Energy Strategy.
answer_text: I acknowledge the Audit Office’s report on the approach and implementation of the NI Energy Strategy and will endeavour to swiftly implement its recommendations.	My Department’s ongoing Mid-Term Review of the Energy Strategy will highlight the significant progress achieved to date, and set out delivery milestones up to 2030, to maximise our capacity to realise these targets. I remain committed to the delivery of a net zero energy system powered by local renewables, smart infrastructure, and shaped by the people who live and work here.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the reasons why her Department has failed to make progress in delivering targets, set out in her Energy Strategy.
answer_text: It is regrettable that for two years no Minister was in place to drive forward the Energy Strategy. Since taking on the Economy portfolio in February 2024, former Minister Murphy and I have led the Department to effectively deliver key commitments within the Executive’s Energy Strategy.	My Department’s ongoing Mid-Term Review of the Energy Strategy will highlight the significant progress achieved to date, and set out delivery milestones up to 2030, to maximise our capacity to realise these targets. I remain committed to the delivery of a net zero energy system powered by local renewables, smart infrastructure, and shaped by the people who live and work here.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for her assessment of the constraint caused by Dargan Bridge, Belfast, to future increased rail capacity.
answer_text: The All-Island Strategic Rail Review sets out a long-term vision for enhancing rail connectivity and capacity across the island, including recommendations to improve infrastructure in key urban areas such as Belfast. The Review acknowledges that physical constraints, such as those posed by existing structures like Dargan Bridge, may impact future capacity. I can confirm that there is currently no specific project being taken forward to upgrade or modify Dargan Bridge.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail when he will respond to the Equality Commission's review into Ulster boxing.
answer_text: AnswerI made a statement on this subject to the Assembly on 11 November 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities weather Sport NI will be held accountable for the issues raised by the Equality Commission's review into Ulster boxing.
answer_text: I welcome all efforts to ensure that boxing is inclusive of all communities in Northern Ireland and that discrimination is not tolerated.  The Equality Commission’s document, “An Equality Review of the Governance, Practices and Procedures Applicable to Ulster Boxing, September 2025” does not find fault with Sport NI. The Review invites my Department, Sport NI and others to take action to address the issues. I will be confirming the next steps in this process shortly.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment on the effectiveness of her Department's implementation of the Energy Strategy.
answer_text: Since taking on the Economy portfolio in February 2024, former Minister Murphy and I have led the Department to effectively deliver key commitments within the Executive’s Energy Strategy.	My Department’s ongoing Mid-Term Review of the Energy Strategy will highlight the significant progress achieved to date, and set out delivery milestones up to 2030, to maximise our capacity to realise these targets. 	I remain committed to the delivery of a net zero energy system powered by local renewables, smart infrastructure, and shaped by the people who live and work here.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the amount of funding his Department has provided to marching bands, since he took office.
answer_text: My Department invested capital funding of £823,000 in 2024/25 and £1,100,000 in 2025/26 for the Musical Instruments Programme.  The Arts Council has confirmed it allocated £593,083.00 in 2024/25 and £819,405.00 in 2025/26 to the Musical Instruments for Bands strand of the Programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of occasions the Energy Strategy oversight group has met, since January 2024.
answer_text: The Energy Strategy Oversight Group collaborates across the NICS to monitor and support the strategic delivery of the Executive’s Energy Strategy - Path to Net Zero Energy. Since January 2024, the group has convened five times and will meet again this year in December.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the start date for the pilot of late-night public transport.
answer_text: The extension of late-night public transport services was agreed at the Executive Meeting on 16 October 2025.  Translink have indicated that they hope to commence the late-night services pilot on 28 November 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when she will make an Oral Statement on her remarks regarding Northern Ireland's involvement in the UK and Israeli trade talks.
answer_text: I have outlined my position clearly in my written statement of 16 October.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the legal action against the Department for Infrastructure regarding Grand Central Station.
answer_text: On 27 June 2025, I was granted leave by the Court to intervene in an existing action by a third party against the Minister for Infrastructure in relation to her failure to refer her decision to install Irish language signage in Grand Central Station to the Executive. As this action is ongoing, it would be inappropriate for me to comment any further.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on the Strategic Review of the rating policy.
answer_text: ANSWERMy Department has now concluded the 2025/26 Strategic Reviews on Non-Domestic Vacant Rating  and Small Business Rate Relief. This extensive work was largely completed over summer recess and within the stated deadlines, and follows the work completed in 2024/25 on the Maximum Capital Value and the Early Payment Discount.I am now evaluating the Review outcomes and will be laying out the next steps in the coming weeks. These will include the steps required to implement any agreed changes for April 2026. Final decisions on rating policy, both in terms of additional support and how that can be funded through realignment of current rate supports, are decisions that need to be made collectively by the Executive.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on investment plans for Belfast Royal Academy.
answer_text: Belfast Royal Academy was announced in May 2020 to advance in design under the second call to the School Enhancement Programme (SEP).An Integrated Consultancy Team (ICT) has been appointed to take the SEP project at the school through the design and construction phases.  The project is currently at RIBA Stage 2 (Scheme Design) and the SEP proposal is to construct a three-storey extension east of the Bruce Building.  My Department is also currently supporting the school as it explores the possibility of securing the adjacent church buildings and site.Funding is available to progress SEP projects through the design stages to pre-tender.  A decision to release projects to tender for a contractor will be taken on a case-by-case basis, dependent on budget availability at that time.  I will continue to make the case for additional funding for education through the Executive.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any consideration been given to including lower North Belfast in phase 2 of Urban Villages.
answer_text: The Urban Villages programme has delivered significant improvements in community relations, regeneration, and local pride, and we are committed to building on this success by rolling out the initiative to other areas.We are actively identifying areas that would benefit from the support and investment provided by the Urban Villages programme and will provide Members with a comprehensive update once the details have been finalised.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for her assessment of the Criminal Justice Inspection report into its inspection of airport and harbour policing in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I note the findings of the report by Criminal Justice Inspection. The report identified a number of areas of concern, including in relation to the security planning framework at Belfast International Airport, and the lack of clarity on roles and responsibilities in relation to policing of high-risk crime types. However, it also commented positively on the focus of Belfast Harbour Police on neighbourhood policing. The report made a number of recommendations to address the issues identified, including those to be taken forward by Belfast International Airport Constabulary and Belfast Harbour Police, and those to be taken forward by those bodies working with the Police Service of Northern Ireland. Belfast International Airport Constabulary is a private police force, employed and controlled by the airport operator. Belfast Harbour Police is accountable to the Harbour Commissioners. The report recommended that the Department of Justice should take steps to prepare for the required legislative change to extend the jurisdiction of Belfast International Airport Constabulary and Belfast Harbour Police in the next Northern Ireland Assembly mandate’s legislative programme. This will require work to identifying and agreeing responsibility for progressing such legislation and will be subject to the Department’s resources and other legislative priorities in the next mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any work she is undertaking to attract international sporting events to Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Hosting The Open Championship at Portrush this year has shown our ability to host global events, and we look forward to its return. I recently confirmed that the north will participate in the bid to host the FIFA Women’s World Cup in 2035, championing women’s sport and our region internationally. I fully support the hosting of major events and international sporting events in the region, given the significant economic, media and social benefits events of a global scale can generate. In January this year, I launched the Tourism Vision and Action Plan which includes an action to identify a long-term programme of events that will support delivery of the Vision’s objectives. As part of this work, Tourism NI will consider internationally mobile, global events which the north could bid to host in the future – events that have potential to deliver significant economic benefits. Affordability will also be a key consideration to inform decisions to bid.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any discussions she has had with officials regarding the removal of unauthorised party political posters on her Department's land.
answer_text: Article 87 of The Roads (Northern Ireland) Order 1993. gives my Department discretionary powers to either seek to have the advertisement removed, or to remove it themselves if considered necessary. Given my Department’s ongoing resource limitations, priority is given to the removal of advertising where there is a clear road safety risk.When assessing the risks associated with posters, my Department evaluates various factors related to road safety, including potential obstructions to vehicles and pedestrians or impairment of sightlines. My officials undertake this operational work on behalf of the department.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the introduction of an air route development fund.
answer_text: The introduction of a route development fund is being explored as part of the Department’s aviation policy development.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail when the Sentencing Bill will be introduced to the Assembly.
answer_text: It is my intention that the Sentencing Bill will be introduced to the Assembly before the end of 2025, subject to Executive approval.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any work he is undertaking to attract international sporting events to Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The Department for the Economy, through its arm’s length body Tourism NI, is responsible for attracting and supporting the delivery of major events, including international sports events, to Northern Ireland.As Minister for Sport, I will continue to engage with sports governing bodies and organisations which are seeking to attract international sporting events, while exploring how my Department can best secure and support these efforts. My primary focus is to help in creating lasting legacies by enhancing participation outcomes, as outlined in my Department’s ‘Active Living’, Sport and Physical Activity Strategy for Northern Ireland. 	I have written to the Minister for the Economy asking her to bring a paper to the Executive in this regard.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she has issued any instruction to (i) Invest NI; and (ii) departmental officials, limiting support to the aerospace and defence sector in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: As part of my response to the Invest NI Review of Investments report I have instructed my officials to work with Invest NI to prepare additional measures, building on existing requirements, to ensure procedures for processing financial support are fully future-proofed and comply with the highest human rights standards.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the importance of the defence sector to the Northern Ireland economy.
answer_text: According to the ADS Group’s 2024 Northern Ireland Facts & Figures bulletin, up to 1,000 people are directly employed in the industry.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the economic impact of the aerospace and defence sector in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: According to ADS Facts and Figures Report 2024, the North’s aerospace sector comprises over 100 companies, employing approximately 9,000 people and generating £2.2 billion in annual revenues.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when she last met with ADS Group Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I have not met ADS Group NI.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail his Department's work in North Belfast, since taking office.
answer_text: Since I came into office in February 2024, my Department has delivered the following for the people of North Belfast:£3.02 million to various Voluntary and Community organisations£283,087 for Areas at Risk£5.88 million for Neighbourhood Renewal programmes£79,500 for Cost of Living Supports£52,198 for Small Pockets of Deprivation£190,000 for Test and Learn£1.06 million for Neighbourhood Renewal Capital Fund£600,000 for ABC Trust Health and Leisure Community Centre Project£150,000 for York Street Underpass – TransLink Revitalisation Project£500,000 for Ballysillan Playing Fields ProjectUnder the Football Ground Business Case Development Fund - £24,250 to Cliftonville Football Club and £22,000 to Crusaders Football Club£5,000 each to Gaelic Sports and Boxing under the Creating Opportunities FundSupported the network for 6 public librariesMake the Call - 1,466 people supported access to additional benefits of £2.91m£935,600 for 3,991 Winter Fuel claimants in 2024/25£2.3 million for Labour Market Partnership – Belfast and £792,000 for Antrim and Newtownabbey6 job fairs£24,500 for Black Box, Belfast£8.75m under the PEACEPLUS Programme towards Reconnected Belfast.Supporting People providers within the Belfast City Council area received £23.29 million in 2023/24 and £24.22 million in 2024/2560 Co-Ownership completions12 schemes started, creating 202 new social homes248 home improvement grants issued for disabled facilities 379 homes supported through the Affordable Warmth Scheme, with 638 measures installed£17.30m spent on NIHE maintenance of 3,857 dwellingsNIHE completed a new build project at Sunningdale Gardens in North Belfast.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to outline when CAR-T cell therapy treatment will be available.
answer_text: Patients from Northern Ireland have access to CAR-T treatments as per circular “HSC(SQSD) 12-22 – NICE Technology Appraisals – Process for Endorsement, Implementation, Monitoring and Assurance in NI” which supports the timely introduction of effective new medicines.If CAR-T treatments are approved by NICE then patients in NI will be granted access to them on an equitable basis with other nations through the arrangements in place for referral to trusted centres in the wider NHS.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 32372/22-27, given that policy has not transferred, to clarify her comments in the Assembly on 29 September 2025, stating that transfer of powers did take place.
answer_text: As I stated in the Assembly on 29 September 2025 and in my previous response on 12 October 2025, the respective Ministers agreed on 10 June 2024 that aviation policy should transfer to my Department. This ensures a clear and coherent approach across the sector, including airports and air connectivity. No transfer of powers was necessary following receipt of legal advice.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline the work her Department is undertaking to improve the Northern Ireland dispatch down rate.
answer_text: Officials are collaborating with the System Operator for Northern Ireland (SONI), NIE Networks, and the Utility Regulator to address Dispatch Down constraints through both policy development and ongoing engagement. My Department has set up a grid development monitoring group with SONI, NIE Networks and the Utility Regulator, with the aim of ensuring that key grid developments progress in a timely manner and in alignment with the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 targets.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any mechanisms within her Department to monitor attacks upon Welcome to Northern Ireland road signs at our international borders.
answer_text: Signage is inspected and maintained as part of the routine road inspection process.  Damaged, defaced or defective signs are reported through this cyclical process and where such reports impact on public safety, the removal and replacement of the signage is prioritised.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 30446/22-27, to outline why no such commitment was made.
answer_text: As previously indicated, there was no commitment made in the 2024-25 DfE Business Plan to develop an Ulster Scots policy.This position will be reconsidered after the appointment of an Ulster Scots Language commissioners.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to outline the work her Department in undertaking to ensure resolving child contact arrangements between parents through the courts, is actioned without delay.
answer_text: The listing and management of individual cases in the family courts is a matter for the independent judiciary. However, the Children (Northern Ireland) Order 1995, in Article 3(2), provides that, in any proceedings in which any question with respect to the upbringing of a child arises, the court must have regard to the general principle that any delay in determining the question is likely to prejudice the welfare of the child. This is often referred to as the ‘no delay principle’. More generally, in regard to work being undertaken by my Department, my Enabling Access to Justice Programme includes policy projects that will consider the circumstances in which legal aid can best be used to enhance access to justice and support positive outcomes for families through the family courts, and the circumstances in which alternative support mechanisms may be more appropriate, in particular through consultation processes relating to the means and merits tests for legal aid.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to outline how his Department will provide sufficient career opportunities for graduate Speech and Language Therapists, given (i) the recent increase in funded University places for this degree thereby increasing the pool of qualified individuals; and (ii) that local private hospitals do not employ Band 5 Speech and Language Therapists.
answer_text: Workforce issues and sustaining the HSC workforce across Health and Social Care is one of my main priorities. This year, my Department commissioned 505 Allied Health Professional (AHPs) pre-registration training places — the highest ever, which included a total of 56 pre-registration Speech & Language Therapy training places, representing an increase of 100% over the level from the 2024/25 (28).Recruitment within the HSC is the responsibility of the Health and Social Care Trusts, as the employers. Speech and Language Therapists (SLT) vacancies are filled from the Band 5 Regional SLT List, a process which is open to all Speech and Language Therapists including newly qualified SLTs and those SLTs who wish to be interviewed for the band 5 Regional List. The regional recruitment process runs twice yearly to ensure Trusts have applicants on the list to draw from.The AHP workforce subcommittee is progressing with implementation of the completed AHP workforce reviews, including speech and language therapy. Areas of focus include advanced practice, retention and clinical academic career pathways. Neither I, my Department or the HSC Trusts have any authority over the employment practices of private hospitals.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the amount spent by her Department, repairing road signs that include the name, Londonderry, over the last five years.
answer_text: My Department does not keep specific records of how traffic signs have been damaged.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to outline the work her Department is undertaking to ensure that, as a result of legal aid support, resolving child contact arrangements between parents are not frustrated.
answer_text: I do not underestimate the stress and worry that child contact cases can cause to parents.  My Department’s Enabling Access to Justice reform programme is committed to minimising the acrimony, stress and trauma for those who come into contact with the justice system by ensuring the right range of supports and services are available at the right time and disputes are resolved at the earliest possible opportunity.  This includes increasing awareness of alternative dispute resolution and examining ways in which parents can come to agreement about aspects of care for their children without resorting to legal redress.  	Recognising that there will still be child contact cases that require court decisions, my Department is currently analysing a range of reform options to ensure that legal aid supports access to justice and provides value for money.  Among these options are a number aimed at better supporting the timely and constructive resolution of Children Order proceedings through the legal aid system.  In particular, this includes measures to discourage the unnecessary initiation or prolongation of disputes over child contact, and to reinforce the principle that legal aid should enable—not hinder—child-focused outcomes. Frequent and adversarial litigation can destabilise children’s lives, particularly in high-conflict cases. The reforms aim to ensure that legal aid is used to facilitate resolution, promote stability, and prioritise the best interests of the child.  I intend to invite submissions of views and evidence in relation to these matters before the end of this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the limit that exists on the use of legal aid in resolving child contact arrangements between parents.
answer_text: Legal Aid is available to resolve child contact arrangements between parents to apply to a court for a contact order under the Children (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 or to defend such proceedings. Subject to each application satisfying the financial eligibility and legal merits test there is no limit to the number of times an individual can apply for or be granted legal aid for these proceedings.There is a provision for a waiver of the usual financial eligibility requirements where the applicant is a victim of abusive behaviour and is responding to proceedings under Article 8 of the Children (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 which include proceedings to decide contact arrangements. This waiver ensures that vexatious individuals who seek to abuse their former partner through the justice system cannot do so to the financial detriment of their ex partner.As part of the Enabling Access to Justice Delivery Plan, I will shortly consult on the use of alternative dispute resolution to ensure disputes are resolved with as little acrimony as possible using appropriate mechanisms. This will include looking at ways to resolve family disputes. Further details will be provided to the Assembly through the Committee for Justice in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline the actions her Department has taken in October 2025, to ensure the security of energy supply.
answer_text: Security of supply is a key priority for my Department, and we work closely with the System Operator (SONI), the Utility Regulator (UR) and the wider energy industry to review and monitor security of supply and ensure the resilience of the electricity system. I refer to my statement of 1 October 2025, providing an update on the electricity supply for the North. Department for the Economy - UPDATE ON ELECTRICITY SUPPLY

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether the introduction of the airlink between Dublin Airport and City of Derry Airport will help meet climate change targets.
answer_text: My officials will continue to engage with relevant partners to understand the impacts of the air link as this work progresses.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail when approval of the test and learn programme in North Belfast was given.
answer_text: Collaboration Test and Learn is an NICS wide place-based initiative aimed at exploring better and more collaborative ways of working across government and with the Community and Voluntary Sector (CVS). It recognises the need to operate more efficiently and effectively by reducing duplication and silo working to ensure maximum impact is made with our constrained resources.It focuses on how Departments work operationally together to deliver existing policy ambitions. It does not seek to change existing policies or priorities.NICS Board took the decision on inclusion of North Belfast. It was not referred to Ministers at the time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the amount of funding her Department has allocated to support the reinstatement of the airlink between Dublin Airport and City of Derry Airport.
answer_text: My Department has committed up to £12 million to support City of Derry Airport, ensuring its long-term sustainability and strengthening the North West’s competitiveness in trade, inward investment, and tourism. This funding enables the airport to plan for future growth and enhances its ability to attract new routes.		The Irish Government decision to progress the Derry–Dublin air service is welcomed. Cost for the delivery of this service can only be determined following a procurement process which will be undertaken by Department of Transport in Dublin.  Discussions between the Department for the Economy and the Irish Government are ongoing about the service including funding.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail when the North Belfast Strategic Good Relations Programme will be put out to open call.
answer_text: Currently there are no plans for an open call for applications to the North Belfast Strategic Good Relations Programme. The future of this programme is currently under consideration.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she plans to introduce a levy on international student fees paid to higher education institutions in Northern Ireland, similar to the levy proposed in England in the UK Government's Immigration White Paper and confirmed on 29 September 2025 by the Secretary of State for Education.
answer_text: No.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the £60 million bid made as part of the Spending Review for Skills.
answer_text: The Department for the Economy submitted a £64 million Resource DEL bid in the Spending Review to address increased demand and delivery costs for statutory skills programmes, including support for Special Educational Needs (SEN). This includes funding to replace the non-recurrent £15 million allocation received in 2025/26, which is essential to sustain entry-level programmes and meet statutory obligations under the Youth Training Guarantee.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline her future vision for the Northern Ireland Skills Council.
answer_text: I welcome the vital and valued contribution the Northern Ireland Skills Council (Skills Council) has made to our skills landscape.My Department has commenced a review of the Skills Council, and this work is currently underway.The review will assess the Skills Council’s current role and effectiveness, focusing on strategic alignment, governance, and stakeholder engagement. It will also explore how the Skills Council can better support delivery of my Economic Vision and help shape a responsive, inclusive, and future-focused skills system across the North.I look forward to the outcomes of the review and to identifying opportunities to further strengthen the Skills Council’s role and impact.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when the Skills Action Plan will be published.
answer_text: Please see previous response issued in AQW 32496 22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on her Cluster Policy.
answer_text: My Department continues to advance its agenda to support cluster development as part of our broader innovation and economic strategy. A key element of this is improving how we map and understand networks and clusters. To that end, we are building on the work of the British Government’s Department for Science, Innovation and Technology to develop an enhanced mapping tool that will help identify opportunities for North-South and East-West network and cluster development. It will also inform future policy and support innovation and export growth.Importantly, last October my predecessor, Conor Murphy, launched the £11 million Cluster Acceleration Programme – a 10-year funding commitment to support industry-led clusters that drive innovation, productivity and inclusive growth across the North. In parallel, we are partnering with the Department of Enterprise, Trade and Employment and InterTradeIreland to promote all-island cluster development. This includes joint initiatives such as the Shared Island Enterprise Scheme and the Synergy Programme, which foster cross-border collaboration in areas like fintech, cyber security, and advanced manufacturing.These efforts were recently highlighted at the TCI Global Conference in Dublin, where the collaborative all-island approach to clustering received international recognition.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the adoption of Napoleons Gate, Belfast.
answer_text: I can confirm that the development at Napoleons Gate, Belfast was formally adopted into the public road network on 27 February 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the works to progress the Belfast Rapid Transport Phase 2 in North Belfast.
answer_text: On the 25th February 2025 I was pleased to announce the next steps for the Belfast Rapid Transit 2 project which will see implementation of this innovative service across the North and South of the city, bringing the benefits already established by the Glider service to communities living along this route.My announcement represented an initial £48 million investment in our public transport infrastructure and is one of the projects being taken forward by my department as part of the Belfast Region City Deal. Funding of £35m has been allocated from our Deal partners, with an additional £13 million from my Department to deliver the initial phase which includes a park and ride on O'Neill Road.My officials are finalising the Outline Business Case in line with these delivery options.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline the investment plans at the Whiteabbey Train Station and park and ride facility.
answer_text: Recent works improvements at the Whiteabbey station have included an upgrade to passenger signage. The station will also be reviewed for further improvement works under future platform re-surfacing programmes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the purchase of land by her Department to provide a park and ride facility at the O'Neill Road, Glengormley.
answer_text: On the 25th February 2025 I was pleased to announce the next steps for the Belfast Rapid Transit 2 project which will see implementation of this innovative service across the North and South of the city, bringing the benefits already established by the Glider service to communities living along this route.My announcement represented an initial £48 million investment in our public transport infrastructure and is one of the projects being taken forward by my department as part of the Belfast Region City Deal. Funding of £35m has been allocated from our Deal partners, with an additional £13 million to deliver the initial phase which includes a park and ride on the O'Neill Road.My officials are finalising the Outline Business Case (OBC) in line with these delivery options. Once the OBC is approved, my officials will engage with their counterparts in Antrim and Newtownabbey Borough Council with regard to the acquisition of the land.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail any discussions her Department has had with the British and Irish Governments, in advance of the publication of a joint framework on dealing with the issue of legacy.
answer_text: My officials received an update on the proposed direction of travel from Northern Ireland Office (NIO) officials on 7 May 2025.  I received a general briefing on the proposed joint framework document from NIO officials on 11 September 2025. There have been no engagements between my Department and the Irish Government on this issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline the steps her Department will now take, following First Gazette Notice being issued by Companies House, to strike off from the register the development company responsible for the outstanding works in Willowbrook, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: As the Member has been previously advised in my answers to AQW 29954/22-27 and AQW 28325/22-27, my department is undertaking enforcement action at this development through Article 11 of the Private Streets Order. The department’s appointed contractor is now on site.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 31262/22-27, to detail when the business case will be completed.
answer_text: The Education Authority is currently arranging a structural engineer’s report on the modular nursery unit at St Bernard’s Primary School. This survey and indicative costs will be required to inform a business case so, at this stage, I cannot provide an indicative timeline for the completion of the business case. My officials will continue to keep the school informed of progress.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on her Permanent Secretary's meeting with Ulster University on Friday 3 October 2025, in relation to student safety issues, raised by the Irish News.
answer_text: This meeting was not in relation to student safety issues; however, this topic was raised. Ulster University provided assurances that the matter remains under investigation and is being properly considered.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 31709/22-27, to detail when her Department adopted the policy that only those matters, identified in the All-Island Strategic Rail Review, would be considered by her Department.
answer_text: I wish to clarify my previous response regarding the Dargan Bridge. It was not identified in the All-Island Strategic Rail Review, nor is it currently under consideration by my Department for double tracking at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the importance of delivering a grid connection policy.
answer_text: My Department consulted on options for transitioning to a more socialised grid connection charging policy in January this year and my decision will be published later this year.A more equitable connection charging system will unlock investment opportunities for the North.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to outline how her Department will seek to give safety reassurances to the Jewish community in Northern Ireland following the October 2025 terrorist attack at a synagogue in Manchester and the levels of antisemitism across the UK.
answer_text: I condemn the recent attack on Heaton Park Synagogue in Manchester in the strongest possible terms. Targeting worshippers at a synagogue is a horrific crime and particularly heinous on Yom Kippur, the holiest day in the Jewish calendar. Anti-Semitism must never be tolerated. Attacks on places of worship cause real harm to members and fear in the wider community.The PSNI have advised that their local and district teams intend to reach out to the NI Jewish community to provide reassurance after the Manchester attack. There are also Home Office schemes, available in Northern Ireland, to protect synagogues.The Jewish Community Protective Security Grant is managed on behalf of the Home Office by the Community Security Trust (CST) to protect synagogues, Jewish educational establishments and community organisations.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when a policy decision on increased Socialisation of Connection Costs in the Electricity Distribution Network will be made.
answer_text: My Department consulted on options for transitioning to a more socialised grid connection charging policy in January this year. 		My decision will be published later this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the importance of the North South Interconnector.
answer_text: The North South Interconnector is key to reducing consumer costs, strengthening security of supply, and increasing renewables.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the importance of delivering a long duration energy storage policy.
answer_text: I recognise the important role that long duration energy storage will play in decarbonising the electricity grid, in particular its ability to utilise excess renewable electricity and export power back to the grid at times of high demand.The Department has commissioned research on interconnection and energy storage here. This research will include analysis on the effect of energy storage on the cost to local consumers, security of supply and decarbonisation, and help inform policy development on both short and long duration energy storage. My Department aims to complete policy development in 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on Project Gigabit.
answer_text: Project Gigabit is still in the procurement phase as my officials, along with Building Digital UK (BDUK), are assessing new commercial rollout plans that became available over the summer. That assessment is due to complete shortly, allowing the procurement to be completed and a contract to be awarded, subject to all necessary approvals and value for money assessments being given.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the Net Zero Accelerator Fund.
answer_text: Invest NI commissioned an independent market needs analysis to assess the need for a fund to assist projects that could make a significant contribution towards reducing greenhouse gas emissions in the North of Ireland. This analysis is now complete and Invest NI, the Department for the Economy and the Strategic Investment Board are working together to consider the recommendations.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when the strategic planning policy on renewable and low carbon energy will be published.
answer_text: I intend to publish an update to the Strategic Planning Policy Statement, to include revised policy on the topic of renewable and low carbon energy, in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) to provide the link to the UK Government press release, which includes comment from her or her Department, welcoming the jobs announcement by Bank of America, as stated by her Permanent Secretary at a meeting of the Committee for the Economy on 1 October 2025; and (ii) to detail the date she issued that statement, for inclusion in the press release.
answer_text: The Department of Business and Trade (DBT) issued a regional press release on Thursday 18th September which contained a quote from myself.  DBT have informed the Department that they did not place these regionally tailored press releases on its website, instead opting to post a single overarching press release.Input for the press release was provided to DBT on Wednesday 17th September.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline what is included in her Good Work Charter.
answer_text: As part of my wider Good Jobs agenda, my Department is working with the Labour Relations Agency (LRA) to develop a Good Work Charter.  I will shortly launch a Good Jobs Charter consultation; it will seek views on what should be included in a Charter and how it can best be used to lever an increase in the number of Good Jobs in our economy. A Charter co-design group, which will include Employer and Trade Union representatives and subject matter experts, will design a Good Jobs Charter based upon the outcome of this consultation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of people residing in the North Belfast constituency that have completed a Skill Up programme.
answer_text: Since its inception  in 2021 1,629 individuals  residing in the North Belfast constituency have completed a Skill Up course

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when he will answer AQW 30102/22/27.
answer_text: AQW 30102/22-27 was answered on 8 September 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when the skills action plan will be published.
answer_text: I am launching the Skills Action Plan on 16th October 2025 at the NI Chamber Workforce Summit. I have asked the Chamber to invite Committee members and I hope to see you there.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether the legislation in relation to the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee scheme will be introduced in advance of the Christmas recess period.
answer_text: As detailed in my previous response to AQW 31963/22-27, my Department is targeting to introduce the required primary legislation for the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee (REPG) to the Assembly before the end of this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on her Department's joint work with the Department of Education, on skills.
answer_text: My Department continues to work with the Department of Education across a broad range of skills-related areas. This includes joint efforts on the Early Learning and Childcare (ELC) strategy, with a particular focus on workforce needs, and the development of the Digital Skills Action Plan in collaboration with industry to create alternative pathways into the digital sector.Officials are also engaging regularly on improving support for children and young people with Special Educational Needs (SEN), including a joint bid to the Transformation Fund aimed at enhancing transitions and pathways.The Department of Education has consulted with us on its new bursary scheme to support initial teacher education in high-demand areas. Collaboration is also ongoing across the 14–19 landscape, with thematic groups progressing work on Careers, Curriculum and Qualifications, and Post-16 education.Together, our Departments have engaged extensively with stakeholders to inform the joint three-year Careers Action Plan, published in June 2025.As you are aware, I have committed to establishing a Cross Departmental Ministerial Group with the Minister of Education. The first meeting is scheduled for 24 November and will provide a strategic platform to advance shared priorities and improve outcomes for young people.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the consultation she carried out before publishing her new enforcement guidance to tackle unadopted private developments.
answer_text: My officials consulted with industry representatives, the Construction Employers Federation and the National House Builders Association.Long-term unadopted developments primarily consists of sites where the cost of works to bring the development to an adoptable standard exceeds the funds held in the bond and the developer is no longer trading. The new Enforcement Guidance provides a clear 2-year timeframe following occupation of a development for enforcement action to be carried out. In addition, the complementary amendment to the bond release thresholds allows my Department to retain a greater proportion of the bond which can be utilised to employ external resources to complete the enforcement action should it be necessary.This new approach will allow us to proceed with enforcement of those cases where sufficient bond value is currently retained by my Department.  However, this source of funding is simply not available for long term unadopted sites where the bonds values are now insufficient cover the cost of improvements works either due to construction price inflation or due to prior release of bonds in accordance with the previous release thresholds.Unfortunately, my Department is not currently funded to cover the shortfall between the bond value and the cost of works to bring a long term unadopted development to an adoptable standard.  Unless an alternative funding source is identified which bridges this funding gap, my Department cannot compete the works and adopt the development.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline how her new enforcement guidance to tackle unadopted private developments, will help residents currently living in unadopted private developments.
answer_text: My officials consulted with industry representatives, the Construction Employers Federation and the National House Builders Association.Long-term unadopted developments primarily consists of sites where the cost of works to bring the development to an adoptable standard exceeds the funds held in the bond and the developer is no longer trading. The new Enforcement Guidance provides a clear 2-year timeframe following occupation of a development for enforcement action to be carried out. In addition, the complementary amendment to the bond release thresholds allows my Department to retain a greater proportion of the bond which can be utilised to employ external resources to complete the enforcement action should it be necessary.This new approach will allow us to proceed with enforcement of those cases where sufficient bond value is currently retained by my Department.  However, this source of funding is simply not available for long term unadopted sites where the bonds values are now insufficient cover the cost of improvements works either due to construction price inflation or due to prior release of bonds in accordance with the previous release thresholds.Unfortunately, my Department is not currently funded to cover the shortfall between the bond value and the cost of works to bring a long term unadopted development to an adoptable standard.  Unless an alternative funding source is identified which bridges this funding gap, my Department cannot compete the works and adopt the development.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the date when (i) the airport policy responsibilities transferred from the Department of Infrastructure to her Department; and (II) the transfer was approved by the Executive.
answer_text: On 10 June 2024 it was agreed by the respective Ministers that aviation policy should sit with my department. This would make it much clearer for stakeholders in the aviation industry which department had policy responsibility for the aviation sector.  DfI retains its existing statutory responsibilities which are set out in the Airports (NI) Order 1994. Legal advice also confirmed my Department had the requisite powers to provide funding to Derry City and Strabane District Council to ensure the long-term sustainability of the City of Derry airport.  Therefore, no formal transfer of powers in relation to airports was required.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 25187/22-27, to detail the works carried out to stop the repeat flooding at this site.
answer_text: As set out in my response to AQW 31405 22-27, through its investigations NI Water has identified several sewer defects and misconnections to the sewer network upstream and is currently determining what maintenance funding can be allocated from within the current 2025/26 budget to address these.  NI Water is also developing options to provide increased hydraulic capacity to the sewer network downstream of this location that will reduce the severity of flooding.  A decision on the way ahead is expected by the end of November 2025.In addition to this, the majority of the £15m of funding that my Department secured through the Public Transformation Fund for a retrofit programme of Sustainable Drainage System (SuDS) pilot projects will be invested in the sewerage network in this area.  Whilst these are pilot projects, each completed SuDS intervention will help reduce the intensity of the flow in the sewers, reducing the severity of out of sewer flooding and pollution from combined sewer overflows.I have asked my officials to make contact with your office, to provide you with more detail on the ongoing investigations, issues identified and to keep you informed going forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline the rationale for why her new enforcement guidance, to tackle unadopted private developments, did not include retrospective powers for developments already completed.
answer_text: My officials consulted with industry representatives, the Construction Employers Federation and the National House Builders Association.Long-term unadopted developments primarily consists of sites where the cost of works to bring the development to an adoptable standard exceeds the funds held in the bond and the developer is no longer trading. The new Enforcement Guidance provides a clear 2-year timeframe following occupation of a development for enforcement action to be carried out. In addition, the complementary amendment to the bond release thresholds allows my Department to retain a greater proportion of the bond which can be utilised to employ external resources to complete the enforcement action should it be necessary.This new approach will allow us to proceed with enforcement of those cases where sufficient bond value is currently retained by my Department.  However, this source of funding is simply not available for long term unadopted sites where the bonds values are now insufficient cover the cost of improvements works either due to construction price inflation or due to prior release of bonds in accordance with the previous release thresholds.Unfortunately, my Department is not currently funded to cover the shortfall between the bond value and the cost of works to bring a long term unadopted development to an adoptable standard.  Unless an alternative funding source is identified which bridges this funding gap, my Department cannot compete the works and adopt the development.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any bids made to the Public Sector Transformation Board, since March 2025.
answer_text: Since March 2025, my Department has submitted three bids to the Public Sector Transformation Board:Pathways to Work, Tackling economic inactivity through an integrated place-based approach. Community Empowerment Digital Transformation Project.Digital Transformation for Public Records Office NI/NI National Archives.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any bids made to the Public Sector Transformation Board, since March 2025.
answer_text: Departments were invited by the Public Sector Transformation Board to submit proposals they felt could deliver transformation of public services here. As part of the second call – which issued on 23 June 2025 - departments were encouraged to submit their proposals, targeting some or all the following objectives: i. Increase the financial sustainability of public services; ii. Transform the model of delivery of public services to improve effectiveness and efficiency to meet increased demands and improve outcomes for citizens; and iii. Prevention, Cost Savings, and Early Intervention. There was no bar on departments resubmitting any proposals which were not successful in the first round. The departments were encouraged, however, to review and update any previous proposal considering the new criteria for the second call.
