Responses in relation to the second call were due by 8 August 2025. In response to the second call, the secretariat to the Public Sector Transformation Board received twenty-five proposals. These are currently being considered by the Board, which will then provide recommendations to the Minister of Finance.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to outline what steps she has taken, to date, to ensure individuals charged with child sex offences cannot be permitted to use a bail address within the vicinity of a school or play park.
answer_text: The granting of bail and the setting of bail conditions are a matter for the independent judiciary and will be based on the individual circumstances of the case.  During an application for bail the defence and prosecution will make representations to the court on whether bail should be granted, and what conditions if any are necessary; this may include the matter of a suitable bail address. Where persons charged with a sexual offence are considered to pose a risk to children, and who are released on bail by the court pending trial, the court can impose, as part of bail conditions, a requirement that the defendant has no contact with any person under 18. Any breach of this bail condition will result in the arrest of the defendant who will be brought back before the court.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of her statement regarding the three students arriving from Gaza, to outline how the cost of their tuition fees are being met.
answer_text: Following my engagement with the British Government which included meetings with Minister Malhotra (then Home Office Minister), I was delighted to welcome the arrival of the first three students from Gaza.These students are in receipt of scholarships which fund the costs of tuition fees. I will continue to press the British Government for the safe passage of a fourth student.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline any engagement she has had directly with Bank of America, since its investment announcement in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My officials in Invest NI are working with Bank of America to deliver the investment announced.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the development of a pilot Stewardship Plan as outlined in the Tourism Vision and Action Plan.
answer_text: Work on the Destination Stewardship Framework is progressing well, following a period of extensive stakeholder engagement, A draft Destination Stewardship Framework has been produced, setting out the principles and proposed structures for success. The strategic approach was considered by Tourism NI’s Board in September. Work will now move to the next stage, involving further engagement with all Councils and key stakeholders to discuss the proposed approach. Tourism NI will also establish a process to select a destination for the first focused Stewardship Plan.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any further bids made to the Public Sector Transformation Board, since the first tranche of Transformation funding announced on 4 March 2025.
answer_text: The Department for the Economy submitted three bids in the Public Sector Transformation Board second call to Departments:Special Educational Needs (SEN) with a funding requirement of £7.6 million Resource DEL over four years;Transformation of Local Business Support Services, with a funding requirement of £4.6 million RDEL and £0.4 million CDEL over four years; andEstablishment of a new One Stop Shop for Energy & Carbon services, with a funding requirement of £3 million RDEL over three years.My Department received confirmation, on 24 September 2025, that the Transitions and Pathways Collaborative Project for School Leavers with SEN bid has been shortlisted to move forward to the next stage of the process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline what elements of her Tourism Vision and Action Plan have been progressed.
answer_text: The Tourism Partnership Board (TPB) last met in June to assess the implementation of the Action Plan and the next meeting is scheduled for November. While we are in the early stages of implementation of the Tourism Vision and Action Plan, progress has been noted against several actions. In 2024/25, my Department increased funding to Tourism Ireland, thereby restoring the funding ratios after many years of a disparity. This allocation of funding was baselined and has been carried forward into 2025/26. Tourism Ireland’s analysis of the return on marketing investment for last year demonstrated a return on investment of 25:1 for the top 4 markets of the US, Britain, Germany and France in 2023. The market that delivers the greatest return on investment based on total visitor spend is the US, which was 48:1, confirming it as a particularly important market for us.Tourism Ireland is rolling out Ireland Unrushed, a campaign focused on slow tourism, designed to encourage visitors to spend more time in fewer places, travel widely and slow down to experience the local culture and landscapes in a more meaningful way. The campaign is live in 13+ international markets with a consumer campaign expected to deliver 290 million impressions.Work on the Destination Stewardship Framework is progressing well, following a period of extensive stakeholder engagement. A draft Destination Stewardship Framework has been produced, setting out the principles for a destination stewardship approach and the proposed structures for success in the North.The 153rd Open in Portrush in July was a resounding success, attracting a record 278,000 attendees, and is expected to exceed the economic benefit target of £213m. Broadcast globally, the final round marked Sky’s second-largest Sunday audience for The Open.Plans are well underway another major event next year when Belfast will host An Fleadh Cheoil Na hÉireann, the world’s largest annual festival of Irish music, song and dance. The event is projected to attract over 700,000 visitors across the weeklong festivities.The DNA Museum is the first tourism project, across all the City & Growth Deals, to commence on site and will support the Tourism Vision and Action Plan by driving economic growth through tourism in the North West and by delivering a visitor experience of international significance.I joined fellow Ministers, local politicians, project partners and Derry City & Strabane District Council officials on 24th September to mark the commencement of works on site of this exciting state of the art facility which aims to open in 2027.The first meeting of the Peace Tourism Collaboration Group took place on Monday 8th September to consider the findings of a recent review and discuss collaboration moving forward. It was attended by representatives from the tourism industry, academia, community-based organisations, and Tourism NI officials.	A Progress Report on all the actions within the Tourism Vision & Action Plan was shar

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any further bids made to the Public Sector Transformation Board, since the first tranche of Transformation funding announced on 4 March 2025.
answer_text: As part of the second call for Transformation funding, departments were restricted to providing a maximum of three bids. I considered a number of high-quality transformation proposals across my Department and its ALBs and the bids as set out in the table below were submitted to the Public Sector Transformation Board. Transformation BidCapital £mResource £mEnabling Smart Infrastructure – DfI Data Hub2.250.6Climate Resilient Flood Forecasting - Leveraging Data Science14.3Transforming wastewater provision in Newry (NI Water)261.2

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether Guardian 4 Sensors can be prescribed to diabetes patients funded on the NHS, as in other parts of the United Kingdom.
answer_text: Following the endorsement of the National Institute for Health and Care Excellence TA943 as applicable to Northern Ireland, my Department has requested work to be undertaken to understand the implications.   Guardian 4 sensor is a Continuous Glucose Monitor which enables closed loop technology. Work has been completed in terms of overall provision; priority groups; system costs; workforce and communication requirements to both enable and support. Funding is required for devices in addition to upskilling and recruiting the workforce needed to offer Hybrid Closed Loop safely, effectively and equitably across the region.  I have recently supported the establishment of a clinical working group to work with relevant stakeholders across Health and Social Care to develop an implementation plan which outlines an appropriate timescale for rollout. The implementation plan is currently being drafted and will support equitable, sustainable and affordable access to this technology, which is targeted to optimise outcomes for those groups most likely to benefit.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the announcement that Bank of America intends to create new jobs in Northern Ireland.
To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department would consider creating a dedicated sensory room at the Emergency Department in the Royal Belfast Hospital for Sick Children.
answer_text: The use of a sensory room or similar amenities would be a beneficial addition to the RBHSC.  Unfortunately, Belfast Trust have advised that a sensory room in ED at the Royal Belfast Hospital for Sick Children is not feasible at this point due to very limited clinical space.  However, there are currently plans in progress to increase the clinical space and the Trust will have an opportunity to review all requirements of the department.  The Trust are also engaging with a charity to procure a mobile sensory room to locate in the vicinity of ED and they are hopeful that this will be available in the coming months.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the improvements to the junction of Shore Road and the M2 at Mount Vernon, Belfast.
answer_text: I refer the member to my answer to AQW 30447/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline any additional speed signage that can be installed in White City, Belfast, to improve road safety.
answer_text: Road safety is an extremely high priority for my Department and it is committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and to address the needs of all road users. White City, Belfast is a residential area situated within an overall 30mph zone. My Department’s current policy is that we do not provide 30mph repeater signs in street lit areas as this would lead to an over proliferation of unnecessary signage. To do so in some cases but not others would result in inconsistency, driver uncertainty and enforcement issues.Issues with driver behaviour, including speeding, should be reported to the PSNI.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline the reasons why the Chair of Northern Regional College was not reappointed.
answer_text: The Chair of Northern Regional College served for one term of four years following a public appointment competition in 2021. Reappointment to membership of Further Education College Governing Body, including to the role of Chair, is not automatic. My decision was informed by careful consideration of the skills required for role of Chair at Northern Regional College at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the future of Belfast Met's Castlereagh campus.
answer_text: Belfast Metropolitan College is currently developing a business case regarding options for the future of its Castlereagh campus, informed by a public consultation exercise undertaken as part of its consideration process. When complete, the business case will propose the recommended option and be put to me for approval. No decision has been taken in respect of the campus at this stage.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline the reasons why the auction for the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee scheme has been delayed.
answer_text: The Final Scheme Design for the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee (REPG) was published on 19 September 2025.Delivering additional renewable electricity generation is complex, demanding a careful balance between security of supply, affordability, and legislative compliance.I am confident that we are building the right foundations to ensure the scheme delivers the best outcomes for energy consumers and that local communities benefit from the transition to net zero.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when legislation on the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee scheme will be published.
answer_text: My Department is targeting to introduce the required primary legislation for the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee (REPG) to the Assembly before the end of this year. Once the primary Bill is in place, subordinate legislation will be taken forward to set out the detailed arrangements.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 31408/22-27, to detail (i) why minutes of meetings have not been taken; (ii) the last time a meeting took place; and (iii) her rationale for not calling in the bond, given the lack of progress or development by the developer.
answer_text: There are no records held of the specific dates of the discussions with my officials nor minutes taken.  These discussions are not formally established procedures, rather my officials are in regular ad-hoc contact with the developer, often by telephone rather than by meeting, requesting progress updates.The last discussion with the developer took place a few months ago.A developer is responsible for providing the streets within their development to the appropriate adoptable standard. The developer of Loughshore Manor is not in default of his obligations as he is working to secure the approval of the bridge structure, which is expected to take some time to resolve. As this work is ongoing, it is considered that it would not be appropriate to instigate enforcement action at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline the reasons why the Department for Justice will have a role in the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee scheme.
answer_text: The proposed roles and responsibilities for organisations in the delivery of the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee (REPG) is detailed in the Final Scheme Design, published on 19 September 2025. Given the scheme awards contracts of value, a dispute process is a key element of the design. As outlined in the publication the proposed pathway will be a three-tier review and appeal process. The third and final stage of that process is an appeal to the High Court which falls under the responsibility of the Department for Justice.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department will (i) increase awareness of ovarian cancer symptoms; and (ii) ensure public health information clearly details that cervical screening does not check for ovarian, womb, vulval or vaginal cancers.
answer_text: Ovarian Cancer awareness month takes place during the month of March. Each year during this period, the Public Health Agency (PHA) issue a press release and social media messaging to increase awareness of the signs and symptoms of Ovarian Cancer.  The PHA also posts regularly through social media channels to promote cancer awareness messaging. This includes information on signs and symptoms, and healthy changes people can make to help reduce the risk of developing cancer in the future.In addition, the PHA website provides information about signs and symptoms of a range of cancers, including ovarian cancer, explains what people can do if concerned, and signposts to recommended sources of support or further information.The current public information on cervical screening does not reference other forms of cancer. The PHA’s public facing information and resources for all screening programmes are kept under regular review and are updated as necessary. The PHA will consider the recent changes made to the information in England and if it is appropriate will incorporate similar messaging locally at the next review of their patient leaflets for the Northern Ireland Cervical Screening Programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail an update on the actions being taken forward by his Department from the High Street Task Force.
answer_text: ANSWERMy Department was responsible for the rating elements within the report and has undertaken actions in response to the recommendations, including-extending the Small Business Rate Relief scheme for 2024/25 and 2025/26, a measure which provides support of between 20% and 50% to some 30,000 businesses here in a taxbase of 75,000 properties.the reinstatement of the Back in Business scheme to incentivise the use of long-term vacant retail space by helping new ventures across our councils in their vital first years in business – the scheme has helped 62 new or expanding businesses since its reinstatement last year.more frequent non-domestic revaluations to ensure fairness, equity and transparency in our taxation system - Land & Property Services have now conducted non-domestic revaluations in 2015, 2020, 2023 and are due to complete the next exercise for April 2026, making us the most frequently valued jurisdiction on these islands.The High Street Task Force was a cross-cutting initiative however, with a much wider set of recommendations across Government, which were not limited to my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 30210/22-27, to detail (i) how long it is required to be advertised for; and (ii) a proposed start date for the pedestrianisation.
answer_text: The consultation period for representations to be made on the pedestrianisation of Hill Street, Belfast is 22 days.  It is anticipated that advertisement will be completed by mid October and the scheme implemented in November 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any discussions she has had with the Minister of Justice regarding the impact of vexatious judicial reviews upon major planning applications.
answer_text: To date I have not had any discussions with Minister Long on this topic.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the actions being taken forward by her Department from the High Street Task Force.
answer_text: My Department is responsible for recommendation 8 of the High Street Task Force Report: ‘Fair Work on Our High Streets’. Delivering on this recommendation aligns with my economic priority on Good Jobs. The Department carried out a public consultation in 2024, seeking views on potential updates to employment law. In April this year, I announced proposals for a comprehensive package of updates to our employment law framework, including new primary legislation in the form of an Employment Rights Bill. Drafting of the provisions of this Employment Rights Bill is underway.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 31043 22/27, to detail the (i) Departments from which the funding is being sought; and (ii) the amount of funding being sought from each Department.
answer_text: Your question to the Minister for Economy in respect of funding for late-night public transport services has been transferred to me for response, as the Minister with lead responsibility for public transport services.I am seeking support from Executive colleagues and from Belfast City Council for a collaborative funding approach to enable an extension of late-night public transport services.  This is broken down as £92.3k (or 14.2% of total cost) from Communities, Health, Justice and the First and Deputy First Ministers as well as Belfast City Council.  Myself and Minister Archibald are also committing £94.9k (or 14.6%) towards the cost of extending late night public transport services.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when (i) new signage will be installed at Global Point, Newtownabbey; and (ii) the connecting path from Mossley Train Station will be completed.
answer_text: Invest NI is working with Antrim and Newtownabbey Borough Council on ways to enhance the visibility and accessibility of Global Point Business Park. As part of ongoing site development and infrastructure improvements, plans are being progressed to develop new signage for the Park. As these proposals are at an early stage, a definitive installation date has not yet been confirmed. The timing of the installation of new signage may also be subject to securing the necessary statutory approvals. Invest NI is also currently engaging with third-party landowners to secure the legal agreement required to enable construction works on their land, which will facilitate the development of a new link into the existing path around the perimeter of the Business Park to Mossley West Train Station. Once this agreement is in place, the agency anticipates being able to move forward with the construction works.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the works Invest NI will undertake to masterplan the further development of Global Point, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: Invest NI is playing a key role in the strategic development of its Global Point Business Park in Newtownabbey. As part of the Belfast Region City Deal, the organisation is working in partnership with Queen’s University Belfast, Ulster University, and Antrim and Newtownabbey Borough Council to deliver the Advanced Manufacturing Innovation Centre (AMIC), which is due to be completed in early 2026.In addition to supporting the delivery of AMIC, Invest NI has undertaken a series of infrastructure projects aimed at enhancing connectivity and unlocking additional development land within the Park. These works will ensure that the Business Park remains well-positioned to attract high-value investment and sustained economic growth.Invest NI will continue its engagement with local stakeholders to shape the future plans for expanding the industrial and innovation capacity in and around Global Point Business Park, reinforcing its role as a strategic location for economic development.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline when the Dargan Bridge, Belfast, will be double-tracked, to build resilience and capacity into the network.
answer_text: The dualling of the bridge was not identified in the All Island Strategic Rail Review and is not under consideration by my Department at this time.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 30331/22-27, to detail the explanation provided by the Equality Commission for the delay in producing its report.
answer_text: The Equality Commission has advised that the review was carried out under the terms of a legal settlement in which the parties had agreed to a comprehensive equality review of the governance, practices and procedures applicable to the Ulster Boxing Council.The review involved engagement with the parties, meetings with a number of boxing clubs, liaison with Sport NI, and consideration of previous reports including the December 2013 Independent Working Group report. This work took time to complete. The Report was finalised in October and shared with the parties involved, Sport NI and the Minister for Communities. The Commission has advised that the Report is available upon request.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether Armour Thyroid is available for prescription when recommended for a patient by a Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: Armour Thyroid contains both thyroxine (T4) and tri-iodothyronine (T3) extracted from the thyroid gland of pigs known as desiccated pig thyroid extract (DTE). Historically, extracts of animal thyroid glands were the only way to treat thyroid underactivity, but since the 1950s pure synthetic thyroid hormones have been available in tablet form. The British Thyroid Association state that the routine use of DTE is not recommended, advising that prescribing should be reserved for use by endocrinologists prescribing in individual patients (under exceptional circumstances). DTE, also referred to as natural thyroid extract, is an unlicensed medication in the UK and the current NICE recommendation is that it should not be offered. There is not enough evidence that it offers benefits over levothyroxine, and the long-term adverse effects are uncertain. There is no obligation on GPs to prescribe any product, however in instances where a GP considers that the medication being requested is clinically appropriate, falls within their area of competence, does not breach the terms of their contract or indemnity cover, and is in keeping with the General Medical Council (GMC) guidance on Good Medical Practice, then they may choose to prescribe.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when the next phase of Your School Your Club will open.
answer_text: I have made available a budget of £3million capital in the 2025/2026 financial year to allow the continuation of the Your School Your Club scheme as I recognise the need to support sports clubs to maintain and increase participation, develop facilities and increase access to existing school based sports facilities. Sport NI will deliver the Programme and so information has also been  sought from its Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“A £3million investment has been made available for ‘Your School, Your Club’ in 2025/26.  Sport NI received applications from 27 schools, and 10 schools have successfully progressed through two assessment stages and will receive an offer of funding in October for their proposed ‘community use’ project.  All 10 schools have confirmed they are in a state of readiness to progress a project and have all necessary approvals in place.   In addition, Sport NI is currently preparing a multi-year business case for Your School, Your Club.  The business case proposes an £8million investment over three years (2026/27 to 2028/29).  Later this year, Sport NI will open a register of potential projects.  Schools with a proposal can bring forward a ‘community use’ project and will receive guidance from Sport NI on how to progress a project to a state of readiness in anticipation of funding availability.   Sport NI will use the data to identify specific investment themes across the 3 financial years”.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when works to address the dip in the Shore Road, at Greencastle Police Station, Belfast, will begin.
answer_text: My officials have had an investigatory excavation undertaken to establish the underlying ground conditions and the extent of the required remedial works. This investigation has determined that extensive works are required and will require the closure of this section of the Shore Road to complete the works. The impact on traffic movements at this location will be significant. Consideration is ongoing to determine when works can be programmed to have the least impact on the travelling public.  I have asked officials to advise you directly when a date has been agreed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 30448/22-27, why she did not provide (i) the wider duties being undertaken; and (ii) the relevant experience in Irish Language related matters.
answer_text: The work of my Department is, by its very nature, wide-ranging and varied and it would not be possible to provide an exhaustive list of the work undertaken by individual members of staff and their experience. However I can assure the member that the staff involved are well capable of developing the policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 30806/22-27, for her assessment on whether this current policy could be viewed as discriminatory.
answer_text: I do not consider the current policy supporting Graduate Entry Medicine provision in the North to be discriminatory. While only one higher education institution here, Ulster University, currently offers a designated accelerated degree programme of this kind, institutions may each determine their own course provision.Tuition fee loans are not available for five-year medical degrees undertaken as a second degree at any institution here. The review of higher education funding will consider, among other issues, the approach to the funding of second degrees.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 30556/22-27, to provide a timeframe for the issuing of the tender documents.
answer_text: The Education Authority anticipates that tenders for the traffic management scheme at Rathcoole Primary School will be issued to the market on 20 October 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any support his Department is considering in the development of Cybersecurity academies to tackle unemployment.
answer_text: My Department does not currently fund central programmes or bespoke academies focused towards cybersecurity as a means of tackling unemployment. Whilst there are no immediate plans in place for provision of this nature, my Department does providing funding that enables flexible and locally tailored employment provision through Labour Market Partnerships.  Each local Labour Market Partnership identifies concerns and potential issues, evidenced through a strategic assessment, and agrees a suite of provision to address these. Given the flexible nature of this model, there may be some potential for local LMPs to develop provision with a cybersecurity focus if this is identified as a need or a potential solution to identified issues in the local area.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to outline the amount his Department is spending on implementing the Windsor Framework.
answer_text: On 15 May 2025 the Department of Finance confirmed the HM Treasury Earmarked allocations to DAERA for the implementation of the Windsor Framework in 2025-26.  These allocations were £59.9m Resource DEL and £11.3m Capital DEL.On 16 June 2025 the Department of Finance confirmed the HM Treasury Earmarked Resource DEL allocations of £70.0m / £71.2m / £72.9m over the three years to 2028-29 and the HM Treasury Earmarked Capital DEL allocations of £4.0m / £3.3m / £3.4m / £3.7m over the four years to 2029-30.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department will consider the resurfacing of Ballyclare Road, Glengormley.
answer_text: My Department has no immediate plans to deliver resurfacing works to the A8 Ballyclare Road, Glengormley.  However, Antrim & Newtownabbey Borough Council will facilitate the delivery of resurfacing works in conjunction with their planned Public Realm scheme.  The resurfacing will extend from the Antrim Road junction to Glencairn Drive junction, with this element of work anticipated for completion by spring/summer 2027.  Following completion of these works, my Department has identified a further resurfacing scheme on the Ballyclare Road from the Glencairn Drive junction to Jubilee Road junction, for delivery subject to the availability of funding.My officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with the Department’s Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any discussions her Department has had with the US administration, regarding the introduction of United States of America pre-clearance at Belfast International Airport.
answer_text: Civil aviation is a reserved matter, therefore my Department has had no discussions with the US Administration on the matter of pre-clearance.  However, earlier this year I met with the British Secretary of State for Transport and raised the issue of pre-clearance.  I was advised that responsibility for pursuing pre-clearance facilities lies with individual airports. Airports are expected to initiate the process and secure any necessary funding to cover associated costs before approaching the Department for Transport to engage with the US authorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any discussions she has had with the Minister of Education to develop the cybersecurity curriculum.
answer_text: Officials from my department and the Department of Education have met to discuss curriculum through the Curriculum Thematic Working Group, which operates within the established 14–19 structures.Ministers will meet in the coming weeks to discuss a range of cross-cutting issues.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the development of land at Girdwood, in North Belfast, in partnership with Belfast City Council.
answer_text: My officials met with officers from Belfast City Council (BCC) on 15 September 2025 to discuss the Girdwood Mixed-Use development site.  At that meeting BCC officers indicated a willingness to work with my department to explore potential joint delivery mechanisms for the site.  BCC will provide a further update to my departmental officials following their internal discussions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the roll-out of smart meters.
answer_text: My department consulted on proposals for the Design Plan for the roll-out of smart electricity meters earlier this year. Following this, staff have been working with stakeholders from across the industry to finalise this plan.My decision paper on this will be published this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on when a biomethane policy will be published.
answer_text: As referenced in AQW 29343/22-27, my Department is currently undertaking work to analyse a range of options aimed at addressing the challenges and costs faced by biomethane producers when connecting to the gas network. A public consultation on the treatment of connection costs is planned by the end of 2025 as committed to in the Energy Strategy Action Plan.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 25187/22-27, to provide an update.
answer_text: NI Water has advised that it is continuing to investigate the issues causing the recurring flooding at the junction of Shore Road and Mill Road.  Hydraulic capacity within the sewerage network downstream of this manhole, as well as infiltration and high levels of inflow into the combined sewer network upstream of the location are all contributing to the flooding issue.NI Water has identified several sewer defects and misconnections to this sewer network during its investigations and is currently determining what maintenance funding can be allocated from within the current 2025/26 budget to address these.  However, remedying these issues will only be part of the solution with an increase in hydraulic capacity still required to provide a long-term solution.Officials from my Department will continue to work with NI Water to help find a solution to this recurring flooding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 30330/22-27, to detail (i) the dates of discussions this year between her Department and the developer; and (ii) the steps that have been taken by the developer this year to complete the development.
answer_text: There are no records held of the specific dates of the discussions with my officials. Officials are in regular ad-hoc contact with the developer requesting progress updates.The developer is responsible for providing the streets within their development to the appropriate adoptable standard. The developer of Loughshore Manor is aware of all the works required. My officials are not party to the developer’s internal processes so are not aware of the steps taken this year to complete the development.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline the work her Department is undertaking to develop a long-term cybersecurity strategy.
answer_text: Following a period of engagement with sector experts, businesses and academics my department published its dedicated Software and Cyber Sectoral Action Plan in June 2024. The Plan outlines a multi-year framework to support the growth, resilience, and innovation of the north’s internationally renowned cybersecurity ecosystem.  The plan is being delivered in collaboration with delivery partners and aligns with my economic vision, contributing to the growth of a high-productivity sector which will underpin the creation of more good jobs. 	The Plan is under regular review to ensure it remains up to date in the context of rapid technological progress.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the new build nursery unit for St Bernard's Primary School, Glengormley.
answer_text: Officials from the Department of Education and the Education Authority (EA) are continuing to consider options for the nursery unit at St Bernard’s Primary School, Glengormley. The EA is arranging a structural survey of the existing modular nursery building and are preparing costs for remedial works to the unit. Both refurbishment and replacement of the unit will be considered and the preferred option identified through the business case process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline when a grid connection policy will be developed.
answer_text: My Department consulted on options for transitioning to a more socialised grid connection charging policy in January this year. My decision will be published later this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline when an interconnection policy will be published.
answer_text: Research has been commissioned to provide evidence for the development of interconnection and storage policy here.  This research is expected to be published shortly. The output of this research will inform the development of both interconnector and energy storage policy. My Department plans to develop interconnection policy by Q4 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline the legal implications for her Department, as a result of any failure to meet the targets set in the Climate Change (Northern Ireland) Act 2022.
answer_text: All departments have a legal obligation to work together to deliver the Greenhouse Gas (GHG) emission reduction targets contained within the Climate Change Act. Achieving these targets is both a legal requirement and a moral obligation to the wellbeing of future generations, which is why decarbonisation is central to my economic plans. My Department will continue to collaborate across government and with wider stakeholders, including industry, to ensure we reduce emissions and capitalise on the economic opportunities presented by the low carbon and renewable energy economy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline what additional signage can be installed on the Ballycraigy Road, Newtownabbey, to reduce the speed of vehicles using the road.
answer_text: My officials assessed the Ballycraigy Road in February 2025 and are content with the existing signage and markings which are all in good condition.I would however like to see what can be done to improve attitudes to speeding, not only for road safety purposes, but to make places cleaner and greener.  I have therefore asked my officials to prepare a paper on the use of permanent 20mph speed limits particularly near schools, in predominantly residential areas and places such as town centres where there is a higher number of those walking, wheeling and cycling.  Road safety is an extremely high priority for my Department and it is committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and to address the needs of all road users.Any concerns about poor driver behaviour such as speeding should be directed to the PSNI who have the powers of enforcement in that regard.  They can be contacted on their non-emergency telephone number, 101.One of the best ways to ensure the safety of our roads and fulfil our long term goal of eliminating death and serious injury on roads by 2050 is by changing driver behaviour. If all of us who are drivers do not change our attitudes towards speeding and careless driving, our death toll, which has increased in the past year, will continue to increase.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy,further to her interview in agenda Ni in September 2025, to provide the evidence available of the existence of an all-island economy
answer_text: There is clear data demonstrating the extent of all-island economic integration across a range of areas, including trade, supply chains, labour mobility, and shared infrastructure.Trade Integration Official statistics from HMRC and NISRA indicate that trade flows across the island have increased substantially in recent years: since 2019, exports to the South have risen by 37% to £4.4 billion, while imports from the South have increased by 48% to £3.6 billion. The total value of all-island trade is now estimated at approximately £12.5 billion.Supply ChainsA number of sectors, most notably agri-food, advanced manufacturing, and life sciences, operate integrated supply chains spanning the island. Intermediate goods often cross the border multiple times during production, highlighting the interconnected nature of these industries.Labour MobilityLabour market integration is also evident. Research by the Centre for Cross Border Cooperation shows that cross-border commuting has increased by around 25% in the period following the COVID-19 pandemic, reflecting the fluidity of the labour market across the island. Business Operations and InfrastructureA growing number of firms operate on an all-island basis, supported by shared infrastructure such as the Single Electricity Market and aligned regulatory frameworks in key sectors. These arrangements enable businesses to trade and operate across the island. The Windsor Framework and the Common Travel Area further facilitate dual market access and workforce mobility.Independent Economic ModellingRecent modelling by the Economic and Social Research Institute and the National Institute of Economic and Social Research (January 2025) demonstrates the growing interdependence of the economies. Their analysis suggests that a 1% increase in export demand from the south of Ireland results in a 0.15% rise in the North’s GDP and a 0.3% increase in all-island output.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of additional United States of America tariffs on the Republic of Ireland, to detail the proactive steps her Department is taking to attract companies from the Republic of Ireland, to Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Invest NI is working hard to attract investment from the south (AQW 31040/22-27 refers). Recent US trade and tariff policy announcements have created significant uncertainty for exporters across the board. It is important to note that arrangements agreed by the US Administration with both the British Government and the EU are, at this stage, non-binding and subject to further negotiations. Moreover, comparing headline average tariff rates on a country-by-country basis overlooks complexities around rules of origin and so-called ‘tariff stacking’, for example – whereby it is possible that an exporter under the US-British average tariff (10%) could face a higher rate than one exporting the same product under the US-EU average tariff (15%).So, while there may be some relative benefit to exporters based in the North, in some circumstances, in some products, overall, at this stage the average tariff differential is unlikely to drive relocations of businesses from South to North at any significant scale. This assessment will be kept under review by my Tariff Working Group.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of further United States of America investment in the financial services sector, which are not bound by the Northern Ireland Protocol, for her assessment of the importance of the UK Internal Market.
answer_text: Our trading relationships both north-south and east-west are extremely important.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the Enhanced Investment Zone.
answer_text: I am pleased to confirm that the Enhanced Investment Zone has now received approval from Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government (MHCLG) for three of the five Gateway stages. Gateways 1 and 2, which set out our ambition to grow the Advanced Manufacturing sector, specifically Biotechnology and Photonics were approved in June. Gateway 3, which outlines our governance structures and project approval processes has recently been approved. My Department is progressing with Gateway 4, which details the proposed thematic areas of spend for the £150m funding. I will be bringing a paper to the Executive shortly, seeking agreement to this in advance of formal submission to MHCLG, which is anticipated to be later in November. In parallel, my officials are preparing Gateway 5 for submission. This will be the final submission and will detail the programme delivery structure and timelines. It is hoped that final approval from MHCLG to the EIZ programme will be secured in early 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the difference between all-island trade and an all-island economy.
answer_text: Whilst there are no universally agreed definitions of either term, “all-island trade” generally refers to the flow of goods and services across the island, north-to-south and vice versa, which has increased substantially in recent years to reach a total estimated value of £12.5 billion.The “all-island economy” captures trade dynamics and encompasses the integration of businesses, supply chains, and labour markets across the island.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on removing students from the Republic of Ireland from the MaSN Cap.
answer_text: As advised in my response to AQW 29042/22-27, improving student mobility remains a priority for me and I am committed to removing barriers for people from here who want to study in the South and vice versa.  Removing southern students from the number cap by increasing fees would not support this aim. Both my officials and I continue to engage with counterparts in the South to explore options for supporting improved mobility.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the amount of funding spent by the local economic partnerships to date.
answer_text: Local Economic Partnerships (LEPs) are being supported with a £45m fund over 3 years. Councils will claim funding in arrears for this work. They can do so after a letter of offer is signed on the basis of an action plan developed by the Partnership in that area.  Once these plans are submitted and agreed by the Department, letters of offer will issue to councils and councils will begin to claim, including for staff costs incurred before finalisation of the action plan.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the amount of foreign direct investment secured by the Invest NI office in Dublin.
answer_text: Table 1 shows the total number of new investments from the south over a three-year period from 2022-23 to 2024-25. 69.5% of the jobs created were outside of the Belfast Metropolitan Area. Table 1 – Invest NI New to NI Inward Investment Activity from the south (2022-23 to 2024-25)YearNumber of InvestmentsTotal Assistance £mTotal Planned Investment £mNew Jobs2022-2351.0617.131662023-2431.6719.952902024-2550.9418.10161Total133.6755.18617Notes1.  Total Assistance relates to the amount of support that Invest NI offered towards a project.2.  Total Planned Investment is the total expected cost of a supported project. This represents the amount that a business plans to invest in the local economy and includes our assistance offered.3.  New Jobs relates to the number of jobs that a business expects to create as a result of the project being fully implemented.4.  New inward investment projects refers to greenfield investment in the north by an externally owned business that has not been assisted before.5.  Invest NI revises performance data on a regular basis to ensure that it reflects implemented projects; therefore, the data above may differ to previously published information.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any work her Department has undertaken in conjunction with the Northern Ireland Executive overseas offices.
answer_text: My Department works with the Executive Office’s International Engagement Team and the overseas offices in Brussels, Washington, and Beijing to promote the north’s economic interests internationally. This includes raising our economic profile - in particular, our dual market access, building governmental connections, and supporting trade, investment, education, and tourism opportunities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether her Department has been asked to financially support the year-round late night public transport scheme.
answer_text: The Minister for Infrastructure and I have been working together alongside Belfast City Council, Translink and other interested stakeholders to respond to the increased calls for the extension of night-time public transport services.On 3 June I met with Minister Kimmins and John Walsh, Belfast City Council CEO, to discuss the Nightmover service and options for all interested parties to collaboratively contribute to funding requirements identified by Translink.Providing extended late-night weekend public transport service will provide a range of both economic and societal benefits, including:Improved connectivity to communities outside of the City Centre, facilitating people to get home safely.Improved access to cultural activities in the City Centre.Reduced potential for public order issues in the City Centre on Friday and Saturday evenings, leading to reduced policing costs and reduced pressure on health services.Reduced potential for violence against women and girls.As the extension of late night public transport services has the potential to deliver benefits for a number of Executive Departments, Minister Kimmins and I have written a joint letter to relevant Executive colleagues seeking support for a joint cross-departmental/Belfast City Council funding approach for a year-long pilot scheme.In the spirit of the Executive’s agreed Programme for Government it seeks to not only promote economic growth and provide safe transport to vulnerable groups, but also further advance our move to more sustainable travel.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for their assessment of the impact that the Army being refused a recruitment stall at an employment fair in Londonderry has on the strategic aims of Together: Building a United Community with regards to improving community relations and building a shared society.
To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline when the (i) terms of reference; and (ii) members, of the Review of Higher Education Funding will be published.
answer_text: I have recently endorsed the draft Terms of Reference for the Review of Higher Education Funding and these have been shared with key stakeholders including the Committee, local Higher Education Institutions, as well as unions.I have also endorsed the approach to the Review and my officials are now taking steps to establish appropriate governance structures.  Further information regarding these structures will be available in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the aviation strategy.
answer_text: My officials continue to engage with stakeholders across the aviation sector, local government, and tourism and investment partners. As noted in my response to AQW 26920/22-27, the consultation document is expected to be issued before year-end.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of students from the Republic of Ireland who are now enrolled at each of our university campuses, in an undergraduate course.
answer_text: My Department publishes statistics regarding higher education enrolments on its website at: https://www.economy-ni.gov.uk/articles/higher-education-enrolments The table below sets out the number of ROI domicile students enrolled on undergraduate courses at the universities here, broken down by campus, in 2022/23 (the most recent year for which data is available.Institution/campusROI EnrolmentsQUB720St Mary’s45Stranmillis15Ulster University 1,125                   UU Belfast        310                   UU Coleraine        370                   UU Magee        440Total1,905To prevent the identification of individuals, figures in the attached table are rounded to the nearest 5, with 0, 1, 2 rounded to 0.  Due to rounding, the sum of numbers in each row or column may not match the total shown.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any further clarity received from HM Treasury regarding the conditions attached to the recent Financial Transactions Capital award for Casement Park.
answer_text: ANSWERThe £50 million Financial Transactions Capital (FTC) to support the redevelopment of Casement Park has been provided to the Executive over the period 2026-27 to 2029-30, subject to sufficient finance being raised elsewhere to deliver the project. The profile of funding is also to be agreed with the British Government. The additional £50 million FTC has been provided on a ‘net’ basis, which means that it does not need to be repaid to the Treasury. There are no further conditions attached to the funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on providing Graduate Entry Medicine tuition fee funding for students at all Northern Ireland university campuses.
answer_text: A tuition fee loan is currently available to those students wishing to avail of it who attend Graduate Entry Medicine courses at higher education providers based in the North from academic year 2025/26. Ulster University offers a Graduate Entry Medicine course at its Magee campus and is currently the only provider here delivering this type of accelerated degree. My Department is undertaking a review of higher education funding, including the financial support provided to students. The review will include consideration of whether tuition fee support should be extended beyond its current coverage.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when the renewable electricity support scheme will be published.
answer_text: The introduction of the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee scheme is crucial in enabling delivery of additional renewable electricity capacity and I plan to bring primary legislation before the Assembly this year, with subordinate regulations to follow as we move towards implementation. The upcoming publication on the final scheme design, which will publish imminently, will provide a full update on the roadmap, eligibility criteria, delivery roles and responsibilities, and an outline of the contract allocation process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline how Great British Energy will be incorporated into her Department’s energy strategy.
answer_text: The Energy Strategy – The Path to Net Zero Energy, sets the long-term vision to reach net zero carbon and self-sufficiency, in affordable renewable energy, by 2050.  My officials liaised with colleagues in the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero (DESNZ) to ensure that the legislation which established Great British Energy (GBE) requires our agreement to any elements of the Statement of Strategic Priorities (SSP) that impact on our devolved energy responsibilities. The SSP was published on 17th September 2025 and confirms that GBE should work collaboratively with devolved governments to ensure alignment with our existing strategies and delivery plans. My officials are continuing to work with DESNZ as they develop GBE’s Local Power Plan for the North, which is expected to be published by the end of the year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail why he approved funding for graduate entry medicine tuition fees for the Magee Campus at Ulster University only.
answer_text: Loans for graduate entry medical students at Ulster University were introduced and funded by the Minister for the Economy, who announced in November 2024 that from academic year 2025/26 Graduate Entry Medical students at UU’s Magee campus would be able to access Government tuition fees loans to make medical education more financially accessible. My Department is not responsible for the funding underpinning this arrangement, nor for the policy decisions behind it.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline how her Department is supporting the widening of participation among working-class protestant boys in higher education.
answer_text: My Department is committed to ensuring that higher education is accessible to all, including those from under-represented backgrounds such as working-class Protestant boys. Widening participation remains a priority, and my Department continues to work closely with higher education providers to support inclusive access. As outlined in annual Widening Access and Participation Plans, Higher Education Providers here are delivering targeted outreach and engagement initiatives including those aimed at addressing barriers faced by young males from the most deprived communities. Notable programmes outlined for the 2025/26 academic year include:•	Ulster University’s “Taking Boys Seriously” programme, which seeks to understand and address the systemic and personal barriers faced by young males, including those from working-class Protestant communities.•	Northern Regional College in collaboration with Ulster University run the ‘Think HE’ outreach programme which in part focuses on young males from the most deprived areas.•	Stranmillis University College undertake a programme of engagement activities targeting young males, including ‘Males@Stran’ and ‘Look@Stran’, which involve working with schools and communities to raise aspirations and promote higher education. My Department is also progressing the development of a refreshed approach to widening participation, informed by engagement and research, with the aim of strengthening support for underrepresented learners.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline the rationale as to why her Department, or its arm's-length body, did not provide financial support for the Lewis Crocker versus Paddy Donovan boxing event at Windsor Park, Belfast.
answer_text: Neither my Department nor its arm’s-length body were approached directly by the event promoter with a request for financial support for this event.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of her commitment to regional balance, for her assessment of the decision by Derry City and Strabane District Council to stop the Army from attending its jobs fair.
answer_text: This is a matter for Derry City and Strabane District Council.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail why the Equality Commission have failed to publish their review into sectarianism in boxing.
answer_text: The Equality Commission for Northern Ireland has advised that the Commission carried out this review under the terms of a legal settlement between Daryl Clarke and the Ulster Boxing Council and the Irish Athletic Boxing Association. It was initially shared with the parties to the case, Sport NI, and the Minister for Communities.  The Commission has subsequently shared with the member directly who had requested a copy.  It was not the Commission's intention to publish the Report more widely but has advised it is available upon request.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when the 20mph speed limit signs at Abbotts Cross Primary School, Newtownabbey, will be repaired.
answer_text: The Department’s maintenance contractor has been on site and found an issue with the software within the sign. I can confirm that the sign has been repaired and is now working correctly.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she will be working with the Ministry of Defence to ensure maximum benefit to Northern Ireland from the Defence Growth Deals.
answer_text: Responsibility for the design and delivery of the Defence Growth Deals rests with the British Government.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the delivery of the proposed Traffic Management Scheme at Rathcoole Primary School, Newtownabbey, given that planning permission has been received.
answer_text: The Education Authority has received the tender documents for the traffic management project at Rathcoole Primary School. The tenders will be issued to the market in due course, dependent on available capital budget and competing priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the new build project for Seaview Primary School, Belfast.
answer_text: The feasibility study to identify viable options for the project has been completed, and the Education Authority (EA) has assigned resources to complete the Business Case.  The EA plan to engage with school stakeholders regarding the Business Case over the coming weeks and it is anticipated that the Business Case will be submitted to my Department for consideration by the end of November.Following approval of the Business Case, the preferred option will be further developed in the Concept Design stage (RIBA Stage 2).

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the most profitable date for Translink private bus hire, within each of the last three financial years, including a breakdown of the income associated with those dates.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:Translink does not determine profitability of private hire business on a day by day basis, rather  there are a number of days on which significant revenues are generated, representing a number of traditional events as well as irregular events related to tourist or sporting events for example. Over 2023, 2024 & 2025 (to date) the highest revenue events have been the 12th July, Black Saturday, The Super Cup, Belfast City Marathon, cruise ship shuttles and the 153rd Open Championship at Royal Portrush. Due to the commercial sensitivity of private hire information Translink can not provide any further financial data on these events.For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, following the announcement in July 2025, to detail any steps she has taken to ensure the operational partnership between the Home Office and food delivery companies is operating effectively in Northern Ireland, to help prevent illegal working, strengthen security checks and uphold customer safety.
answer_text: My Department has no role in relation to this partnership, which is a direct arrangement between the British Government and food delivery firms. Immigration and asylum are “excepted matters” under section 4(1) of, and Schedule 2 to, the Northern Ireland Act 1998.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer AQW 26071/22-27.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 29040 22-27, when the costing of this scheme will be completed.
answer_text: I can advise that a cost estimate is currently being undertaken and should be complete within the next month. This will allow officials to consider the viability and priority of a scheme at the location.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 29525/22-27, to detail (i) the wider duties undertaken by this member of staff; and (ii) their relevant experience in Irish Language related matters.
answer_text: As previously indicated, the staff member leading this work is doing so as part of their broader responsibilities within the Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 29443/22-27, whether she will now introduce an Ulster Scots policy for her Department.
answer_text: While there was a commitment to developing an Irish Language Policy in the 2024-25 DfE Business Plan, no such commitment was made to an Ulster Scots policy. This position will be reconsidered after the appointment of both Irish Language and Ulster Scots Language commissioners.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when the new guidelines on private street enforcement will be published.
answer_text: The new Private Streets Enforcement Guidance is planned for implementation in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when will he answer AQW 27602/22-27.
To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline why the design for the Contracts for Difference scheme was not published in June 2025.
answer_text: The introduction of the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee scheme is crucial in enabling delivery of additional renewable electricity capacity and I plan to bring primary legislation before the Assembly this year, with subordinate regulations to follow as we move towards implementation. The upcoming publication on the final scheme design, which will publish imminently, will provide a full update on the roadmap, eligibility criteria, delivery roles and responsibilities, and an outline of the contract allocation process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when year-round late-night public transport will commence.
answer_text: I am aware of the potential benefits late night public transport services provide for the nighttime economy and those working and socialising in the city.  I have been working with my Executive colleagues to support the extension of late-night public transport services, and subject to the necessary funding being in place, I would hope that these services could commence later this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to explain why Tourism NI made no mention of the Orangefest in their recent social media advertising of Belfast Festivals.
answer_text: The social media post referred to, which was issued on 11 July, was aimed at those thinking about taking a short break here, highlighting a selection of the events due to take place in future weeks. The Twelfth of July events were promoted by Tourism NI on their consumer website, www.DiscoverNorthernIreland.com

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the (i) engagement with the developer of Loughshore Manor, Newtownabbey; and (ii) efforts to have Loughshore Manor brought up to an adoptable standard.
answer_text: It is the responsibility of the developers to provide the streets within their respective developments to the appropriate adoptable standard.In this case, the developer has been and continues to be in contact with my Department regarding the completion of the development. The developer is aware of all the works required and is actively working to complete the development.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of the effectiveness of the investigation by the Equality Commission into sectarianism in amateur boxing.
answer_text: The Equality Commission has not yet published the results of its review of the Ulster Boxing Council’s governance, practices and procedures, I therefore cannot yet comment on the effectiveness of its investigation. However, I met with The Equality Commission on the 17th September and the final report is due with me shortly.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on her Department's work to introduce Harper's Law in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: ORAL QUESTION No 10 - AQO 2365/22-27		  ANSWER I thank the member for his engagement with me on this matter when we met on 1 July. Like so many elected representatives I shared the horror and revulsion of both the family and wider public at the horrific death and tragic loss of PC Harper, while he was performing his duty to the community he served in Berkshire in 2019.  In Northern Ireland a life sentence is already mandatory in any murder case, and, in circumstances considered appropriate by the Court, can be imposed where a defendant, like those in the Harper case, is found guilty of manslaughter.    Current sentencing guidance also specifically addresses the aggravating effect of attacks on public servants, where the victim is engaged in providing a service to the public, and sentences are expected to reflect that. My forthcoming Sentencing Bill will create a new offence of assaulting a person providing services to the public, performing a public duty or providing a public service. With regard to Harper’s Law, I previously committed to monitor its operation in England and Wales, and I am acutely aware of the need to maintain public confidence in sentencing policy.  Therefore, my planned review of further sentencing issues will consider the need to make provision equivalent to Harper’s Law in Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on additional funding for the Northern Ireland Children's Hospice.
answer_text: The Department of Health recognises the valuable role the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice plays in delivering specialist care to children and young people with palliative and end of life care needs across Northern Ireland.My Department has been engaging with the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice to understand the costs incurred in delivering the services that the Department procures from them. In understanding the costs incurred these are being benchmarked against similar services to ensure value for money and to ensure that the funding received by the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice is in line with the level of service provided. It is anticipated that this work will conclude within the current financial year. My Department’s direct funding to the Children’s Hospice will increase in 2025/26 (from £1.75m) to approximately £1.8m. I would note that these figures exclude an additional annual core grant fund of some £50k and also any funding provided directly by HSC Trusts to the Children’s Hospice for contracted services.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, in light of the UK Government's award of Financial Transaction Capital (FTC) funding for Casement Park, whether he will bid for FTC funding for (i) football stadium; and (ii) rugby stadium funding.
answer_text: I am actively exploring how FTC can be used for other sports in Northern Ireland.  I have said on many occasions, that all sports under the 2011 Executive agreement should be treated fairly and equitably. The decision by the HM Government to provide extra funding for one sporting body only has upset that balance and will need to be addressed. I will look at all options available including how FTC might be able to be used.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the pedestrianisation of Hill Street, Belfast.
answer_text: My officials are currently preparing all the necessary paperwork to proceed with the experimental legislation for the pedestrianisation of Hill Street, Belfast.This will be advertised in October 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance, in light of the conditional offer from the UK Government for Financial Transaction Capital (FTC) funding for the development of Casement Park, what other capital projects he is considering for the utilisation of FTC.
answer_text: It is for each individual Minister to bring forward proposals that make use of Financial Transactions Capital (FTC). As part of the information gathering exercise for the upcoming multi-year Budget, departments were asked to provide details of FTC, conventional Capital and Resource bids. This information will be analysed by my department and I intend to bring forward recommendations to the Executive for a draft Budget in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the economic impact of rugby in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Rugby is a major local sport, enjoyed by thousands of participants, spectators and volunteers. My officials recently met Ulster Rugby officials who have provided a paper reporting that in relation to the contribution made by grassroots rugby on the island of Ireland, the Irish Rugby Football Union (IRFU) found the social return on investment of the amateur game to be £447m per annum, accounting for social, economic and health inputs that can be directly attributed to participation in the sport.This includes:Social: £105m (including volunteering, crime, education and employment)Economy: £48m (Including facility value, player spending and employment)Health: £294m (Including the potential prevention of non-communicable diseases such as diabetes and CVD, cancer, mental health, ageing and subjective wellbeing)

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the resurfacing of Carolhill Road, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government.While there are many roads that would benefit from investment, due to limited budget resources, resurfacing work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need for intervention.Details of the resurfacing schemes planned for the 2025/26 financial year will be included in my Department’s Annual Report to Antrim and Newtownabbey Borough Council.Officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with my Department’s Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any consideration he is giving to amending the Houses in Multiple Occupation Act (Northern Ireland) 2016 to introduce a requirement for neighbourhood notifications to take place when a House in Multiple Occupation is applied for.
answer_text: The Houses in Multiple Occupation (Notice of Application) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2019 set out the procedures to be followed by both the applicant for a House in Multiple Occupation licence and a council. This includes the requirement to publish a notice of application in a local newspaper, the publishing of the notice on the council website and serving notice to occupiers of neighbouring land.I will be making a statement on the issue of Houses in Multiple Occupation in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of the need to update the Houses in Multiple Occupation Act (Northern Ireland) 2016.
answer_text: A review of the HMO Act was conducted in 2021. The review proposals, which were published in 2023, included a few minor legislative changes to the Act, such as amending timescales for licence transfers on the death of a licence holder and permitting fees for temporary exemption notices. I will be making an oral statement to the Assembly in due course on the matter of HMOs.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when speed ramps on (i) Gunnel Hill; and (ii) Ballyrone Hill, White City, be reinstated.
answer_text: I can advise that officials are unaware of any recent changes to the traffic calming infrastructure at both Gunnell Hill and Ballroney Hill.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on his proposed Public Health Bill.
answer_text: A public consultation on policy proposals to inform the development of a new Public Health Bill for Northern Ireland took place last year. The Department received over 8,000 submissions in response, with some concerns raised about how individuals’ human rights might be affected by the introduction of this legislation. The volume, diversity and format of consultation responses received made analysis particularly complex. A summary report has now been prepared and will be published on the Department’s website in the coming weeks. Taking into account the obvious level of public interest and the wide variety of issues highlighted in the public consultation response,  as well as the timescales required to address these adequately, I have taken the decision to instruct my officials to reengage with key stakeholders to refine and agree any policy proposals that will ensure any future legislation is fit for purpose.  As such I believe it is unlikely that any Public Health Bill will be introduced, or even drafted, in the next twelve months, but given the importance of the issue and the obvious strength of feeling in relation to it I would much rather the engagement be correct rather than hurried.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 29178/22-27, to detail her assessment of the proposal.
answer_text: In my response to AQW 29178/22-27 I stated that my Department had not undertaken an assessment of this proposal and this position has not changed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail what consideration is being given by her Department towards increasing the penalty fine amount for speeding.
answer_text: I have recently received briefing and advice from officials on a number of speeding related measures, including a review of penalty fines for speeding.  I am now considering the next steps and way forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the work carried out by NI Water to allow the development of Adam Street, Belfast, for social housing.
answer_text: This is an operational matter for NI Water; however, my officials have sought advice from NI Water to provide an update. NI Water has assumed that this question relates to a development of 12 apartments on Adam Street and advised that its wastewater infrastructure in the area is heavily constrained.NI Water has confirmed that in June 2025 it issued the developer, a solution engineering report identifying works that the developer can undertake to achieve a foul only sewer connection for this site. It is now the responsibility of the developer to develop the identified solution and progress the works.For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact NI Water directly through the Elected Representatives Hotline on 0345 300 6461 or through waterline@niwater.com. Making direct contact with NI Water will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when the Energy Efficiency Capital Grant will reopen.
answer_text: A comprehensive review of its first year is currently underway, with the outcome expected to be finalised in the coming weeks.  The review will set out recommendations for proposed changes to facilitate the re-opening of the scheme, ensure its effective delivery and better support business needs.Following the review, any changes will need to go through a formal business and approvals process, therefore I am unable to confirm a definitive reopening date at this time.
