question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any consideration he has given to an empty homes strategy.
answer_text: The Department has taken forward work to develop a new Housing Supply Strategy which was endorsed by the NI Executive on 05 December 2024 and published on 09 December 2024. The Strategy recognises the need to consider actions around the use of existing buildings to support housing supply. The Department is currently examining ‘empty homes’ approaches in other jurisdictions with a view to determining what possible policy interventions could be utilised in NI to bring vacant properties back into use.A review of the previous Empty Homes Strategy and Action Plan was completed and published on the Department’s website in 2021. The review recognised that while the strategy had limited success in addressing housing stress, the work undertaken through the strategy means that the Department is now better informed about the complexities of this, including the reasons why so many properties appear to be vacant.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 28325/22-27, whether a start date for these works has been agreed.
answer_text: My officials are continuing to engage with the Contractor regarding works to be carried out at Willowbrook.  A site meeting has already taken place to identify for the scope of the work, and a further meeting scheduled for the end of July.  It is anticipated that works will commence before the end of the Summer.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline how her Department will support companies to access lower cost energy and timely grid connections, in line with the UK Government's Industrial Strategy priorities.
answer_text: One of the biggest determinants of energy prices in the North is the price of fossil fuels in world commodity markets.  Through the Executive’s Energy Strategy, my Department seeks to break that link by promoting self-sufficiency in affordable renewable energy.  As set out in the Programme for Government, and the Executive’s Energy Strategy, we will create the right market conditions to deliver investment in our low carbon and renewable energy economy. My department works with the Utility Regulator, SONI and NIE Networks, to ensure companies can access timely and fairly priced electricity connections.  The Utility Regulator approved £2.23 billion of investment in the development and operation of the electricity network between 2025 and 2031; a stronger grid will improve the timeliness of grid connections. This network development will further support the Executive’s Programme for Government “Grow a Globally Competitive and Sustainable Economy” by delivering the right infrastructure which will create the foundations for a more productive and prosperous economy; for better rural and urban communities; and for healthier and greener lives.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the on the engagement her Department has had with the Department for Business and Trade, following the launch of the UK's Industrial Strategy.
answer_text: Since its publication on 23rd June, I have met the Secretary of State for Northern Ireland, Hilary Benn MP, and Minister of State at the Department for Business and Trade, Sarah Jones MP to discuss the strategy and what it means for the local economy and businesses.  My officials and I will continue to engage with the British government to ensure that our industrial landscape both contributes to and benefits from the wider goals of the industrial strategy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on efforts to improve access to prescriptions of (i) Megestrol and (ii) Medroxyprogesterone.
answer_text: Medicines are manufactured as part of a highly regulated, complex and global industry; therefore, medicine shortages are not uncommon and can occur for a variety of reasons.  The Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC) leads on the maintenance of medicine supply chains to the UK and works closely with the devolved administrations, and other operators within the supply chain, to manage supply disruptions.I understand that there had been a supply disruption affecting Megestrol  which was communicated by DHSC on 27 March 2025, however this was resolved on 27 June 2025. Local wholesalers are now working to expedite deliveries of stock into NI.I can also advise that Pfizer has recently released generic equivalent versions of a number of its well established brands of medicines, which includes a brand of medroxyprogesterone tablets. Consequently the branded medicine Provera (medroxyprogesterone) tablets has been discontinued. This information was communicated to GP practices and community pharmacies in NI on 1 July 2025, with advice to switch patients to generic equivalents.Any patients impacted by a supply disruption should contact their GP or clinician for advice and a review of their medication, including consideration of switching to generics or to an alternative brand.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline how her Department plans to support the industrial and manufacturing sectors to remain competitive through support for lower energy costs and incentives for new energy technologies, which are being supported through the UK Government's Industrial Strategy.
answer_text: My Department is committed to supporting local businesses to access lower energy costs and new energy technologies. My Department launched its Energy Strategy Action Plan in April 2025 which includes commitments to support businesses in accessing affordable and renewable energy, improving grid readiness through £2.23B approved by the Utility Regulator under RP7 for developing and operating the electricity network in the North, and eliminating network reinforcement connection costs By far the biggest determinant of energy prices in the North is the price of fossil fuels on world commodity markets.  Global gas prices have experienced significant volatility over the past five years due to events such as the conflict in Ukraine.  Through the Energy Strategy this department seeks to break the link with volatile world prices and transition to secure affordable renewable energy that supports economic growth and industrial competitiveness. Invest NI delivers a range of business support that contributes to investment in low carbon technologies and the green economy, thereby aligning with the British Government’s Industrial Strategy. Its Energy and Resource Efficiency Programme provides financial support and technical advice to help businesses in the North decarbonise and reduce costs. In addition, Invest NI oversee the Industrial Decarbonisation project which is working with local industry to reduce carbon emissions as well as enhancing productivity and competitiveness. The Shared Island Sustainability Capital Grant, developed by InterTradeIreland, Invest NI and Enterprise Ireland will help businesses across Ireland decarbonise their sites using novel and innovative solutions.My department and Invest NI are also developing a Net Zero Accelerator Fund. The aim of the fund is to attract and complement private sector investment in the north’s green economy whilst accelerating the scale of decarbonisation required to meet our emission reduction targets.These are part of my Department’s broader commitment to delivering a secure, affordable, and decarbonised energy system that supports economic growth and industrial competitiveness.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of the impact of the decision by the Minister of Finance to not meet all bids submitted by his Department during the June Monitoring Round.
answer_text: I am disappointed that none of my Department’s £50m Non-Ring-Fenced Resource bids have been met. I submitted an inescapable Non-Ring-Fenced Resource bid of £17.1m which largely related to increased costs for my Department and Arm’s Length Bodies following agreement of the NICS 2024-26 Pay Award and the increases in Employers National Insurance contributions. It is imperative that further resource funding is provided to meet these inescapable pressures, or the actions that my Department is required to take to live within budget may adversely impact essential front line services.With the highest economic inactivity and disability employment gap in the UK, failure to support people into work will adversely impact on young people, women, and disabled people. It is vital that my Department is sufficiently resourced in this regard and it was therefore also disappointing that my bid for employment programs was not met.In terms of Capital, the additional £9.0m for New Build Social Homes and first call of up to £2.0m in future 2025-26 monitoring rounds for New Build Social Homes is a long way off the target of 2,000 new starts in 2025-26 and only 15% of the £62m that my Department requires in 2025-26 to meet the target. The Housing Supply Strategy and Programme for Government committed the Executive to these targets, and I have requested the opportunity to meet with the Minister of Finance at the earliest opportunity to discuss how these can be met. While I understand that funding is limited and all Departments are operating within constrained budgets, it is disappointing that my Department’s other capital bids remain unfunded, especially my £3.8m bid for Affordable Warmth, a scheme addressing the effects of fuel poverty and energy efficiency for low-income households. My Department agreed to take on the responsibility of the Cladding Safety Scheme on the clear understanding that funding would be provided to cover all costs associated with the delivery of this scheme, yet no allocation was made. I will therefore have to continue to seek funding to cover the full costs of operating this scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for his assessment of the impact of the decision by the Minister of Finance to not meet all bids submitted by his Department during the June Monitoring Round.
answer_text: Education has been and remains significantly underfunded.  This is independently evidenced by not only the Institute for Fiscal Studies in its last two comparisons papers on school spending per pupil across the United Kingdom, but more recently the Independent Review of Education’s report, Investing in a Better Future.Education continues to face unavoidable cost pressures and rising service demands associated with delivering statutory and policy obligations that the current budget does not fully address.Following the outcome of June monitoring, the Department currently forecasts an overspend of c£300m in 2025-26 in respect of inescapable and pre-committed pressures only and a Capital DEL funding gap of c£80m.In addition, the Department has identified a further c£41m of other high priority / desirable pressures.  The current allocation for education means that for the third year in a row, demand-led, statutory pressures will go unfunded. Only the most limited funding will be available for Special Educational Needs, schools, schools’ maintenance, transport and school meals. This will have an extremely damaging impact on the education system, with our children with Special Education Needs facing the most significant impact.In respect of Capital funding, the absence of significant additional funding and multi-year budget indications means my Department is facing the possibility of having to make unpalatable decisions between meeting its contractual commitments and keeping schools open and safe.Whilst I welcome the additional funding that was allocated to my Department as part of June monitoring, given the scale of pressures faced by the Northern Ireland Block Grant, the Executive needs to continue to make the case to HM Treasury for additional funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the impact, following the decision by the Minister of Finance to not meet all bids submitted by her Department during the 2025-26 June monitoring round.
answer_text: In June monitoring the Department for the Economy was allocated: £7.0 million Resource DEL for Higher Education (HE) Teaching Grant – Structural Deficit;£2.8 million Resource DEL for Energy;£34.1 million ringfenced Resource DEL for depreciation; and£2.5 million Capital DEL for HE Quality Related Research Grant.This is welcomed and allows me to address pressures in those areas.  However, like every Department, my Department continues to operate under considerable financial pressure.I am seeking to make the best possible use of the resources available to my Department to deliver the priorities set out in my Economic Vision and Programme for Government.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for his assessment of the impact of the decision by the Minister of Finance to not meet all bids submitted by his Department during the June Monitoring Round.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when year-round late-night public transport will be introduced at weekends.
answer_text: I remain committed to finding a solution which will enable the extension of late-night public transport services to progress.  I recently met with the Economy Minister and representatives of Belfast City Council to explore the potential for a collaborative approach to funding this potential service. I plan to continue to build on the positive engagement that has taken place to date.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 28686/22-27, to detail the number of requests recorded by her Department from (i) staff; and (ii) the public, to have a departmental Irish Language Policy.
answer_text: The purpose of the Irish Language Policy will be to set out the ways in which the Department will fulfil its commitment to the European Charter for Regional or Minority Languages (ECRML) and those requirements stipulated within of the Identity and Language (NI) Act 2022, and not as a means to stimulate economic growth. The Department  was asked by Conradh na Gaeilge to develop an Irish Language Policy and as previously indicated, the member of staff involved in the development of this policy is doing so as part of their wider duties in my Department’s Equality Unit. DfE staff do not record time spent on specific tasks and as such the Department does not hold this information.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 28686/22-27, to outline (i) how having such a policy will grow the economy; and (ii) the evidence used to support such a claim.
answer_text: The purpose of the Irish Language Policy will be to set out the ways in which the Department will fulfil its commitment to the European Charter for Regional or Minority Languages (ECRML) and those requirements stipulated within of the Identity and Language (NI) Act 2022, and not as a means to stimulate economic growth. The Department  was asked by Conradh na Gaeilge to develop an Irish Language Policy and as previously indicated, the member of staff involved in the development of this policy is doing so as part of their wider duties in my Department’s Equality Unit. DfE staff do not record time spent on specific tasks and as such the Department does not hold this information.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 28686/22-27, to detail the number of hours spent by the staff member developing the draft policy.
answer_text: The purpose of the Irish Language Policy will be to set out the ways in which the Department will fulfil its commitment to the European Charter for Regional or Minority Languages (ECRML) and those requirements stipulated within of the Identity and Language (NI) Act 2022, and not as a means to stimulate economic growth. The Department  was asked by Conradh na Gaeilge to develop an Irish Language Policy and as previously indicated, the member of staff involved in the development of this policy is doing so as part of their wider duties in my Department’s Equality Unit. DfE staff do not record time spent on specific tasks and as such the Department does not hold this information.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when she will review her Department's grass cutting policy in urban areas.
answer_text: My Department maintains approximately 45,000km of grass verges on the road network and our approach aims to maintain the right balance between road safety and the control of grass and noxious weeds, while placing an emphasis on environmental protection enhancement.In December 2022 and to strengthen our approach, my Department issued a new policy on verge management across the road network to protect the environment. The policy remains inoperation and increases the Department’s focus on protecting wildlife and promoting biodiversity when managing roadside verges and supports the actions needed to comply with the Climate Change Act (NI) 2022.In compliance with the policy, a single swathe is cut along verges on the strategic road network, urban roads, on heavier trafficked rural roads and at sight lines and junctions twice per year for road safety purposes. On lighter trafficked roads the cutting frequency is reduced to once per year and is scheduled towards the end of the growing season. There is no planned review of the policy, but I continue to work with my officials to improve the service we deliver.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when she will appoint an independent panel to carry out the Review of Higher Education Funding.
answer_text: I want to work in partnership with the higher education institutions and relevant stakeholders to address the funding challenges experienced in the sector. My officials have engaged with the sector over the past number of months regarding the review and I am currently finalising the draft Terms of Reference. I have not yet made a final decision on the scope or approach to the review.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 28505/22-27, to outline whether it was (i) an administrative oversight; or (ii) a deliberate exclusion of the Ulster Scots tradition from her 2024-25 Business Plan.
answer_text: The 2024-25 Business Plan and the commitments therein were informed by a wide range of strategic, legal, budgetary and stakeholder considerations. There were no administrative oversights.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline the work of her Department in ensuring the maximum number of bids from Northern Ireland to His Majesty's Government's Artificial Intelligence Growth Zones.
answer_text: My Department has actively engaged with stakeholders to ensure that the North is well positioned to benefit from the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Growth Zones initiative, which forms part of the British Government’s AI Opportunities Action Plan. Since expressions of interest were sought in February, Departmental officials have liaised with interested parties, including data centre developers and local authorities, to raise awareness of the opportunity and provide guidance on the application process. While the Department does not have a formal role in nominating projects, Departmental officials continue to liaise with the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology to ensure that the interests of the North are represented.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail whether his Department will support the development proposal by Clifton Street Medical practice for the Department's land at the Girdwood site, following their expression of interest.
answer_text: My Department invited expressions of interest for the development of the Girdwood site last year.  At the closing date, five responses were received including one from Clifton Street Medical Practice.  When considering the responses, a number of development constraints were identified and my officials decided that a wider discussion with other public sector partners was required to ensure the key objectives of the site were met. All respondents were informed that if a development brief competition was to be taken forward they would be notified as an interested party.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when she will be appointing an independent panel to begin the review of further education.
answer_text: My officials have begun preparatory work on a transformation programme to review the Further Education delivery model. The programme will promote greater consistency in the provision of technical and vocational educational pathways as well as innovation support for employers in pursuit of my Economic Vision.  A team to develop options on the proposed approach will be established over the summer.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, in light of media reports, to detail (i) the total amount of the invoices submitted by the GAA to his Department between October 2024 to March 2025; (ii) the amount submitted to cover the cost of public relations engaged by the GAA; (iii) the total amount of expenditure deemed ineligible by the Department; and (iv) the reason any expenditure was deemed ineligible.
answer_text: £707,705£18,130; this element was deemed ineligible by the Department.£329,732(iv)  	The Department reserves the right, at its absolute discretion, to determine what constitutes eligible expenditure under the terms of its funding agreement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 28685/22-27, to provide a date for the publication of the consultation document.
answer_text: My Department is currently undertaking work to analyse a range of options aimed at addressing the challenges and costs faced by biomethane producers when connecting to the gas network. A public consultation on the treatment of connection costs is planned by the end of 2025 as committed to in the Energy Strategy Action Plan.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 26919/22-27, whether she will publish the legal advice referenced.
answer_text: The legal advice referred to in my previous answer is covered by legal professional privilege. My Department has no plans to publish these communications.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the funding provided by her Department and its arm's-length bodies in the 2025-26 financial year to (i) Visit Derry; and (ii) Visit Belfast.
answer_text: To support a programme of activity aimed at promoting leisure and business tourism, Tourism NI provides funding to Belfast City Council and Derry City & Strabane District Council each year to support the delivery of Service Level Agreements.The Service Level Agreements support a programme of work carried out by Visit Belfast and Visit Derry respectively.   The Q1 claims are still to be assessed, so no payments have yet been made under the current financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 28506/22-27, to detail the start date for the consultation referenced.
answer_text: As stated in my reply to AQW 28506/22-27, it is anticipated that a consultation document will be issued before the end of the year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 28508/22-27, to outline how the invitation to third parties to comment was advertised by her Department.
answer_text: The Subsidy Control Act 2022 doesn't require the Department or any other public authority to advertise the CMA's invitation to third parties to comment on Subsidies and Schemes of Particular Interest. As I pointed out in my previous answer the review process provided an opportunity for interested third parties to comment on matters relevant to the SAU’s evaluation of the assessment of compliance concerning the proposed subsidy and that no comments were received.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline what consideration is being given to installing double yellow lines at the junction of Harmin Park and the Hightown Road, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: I can confirm that the junction of Harmin Park and the Hightown Road will be surveyed this summer to assess the need for additional parking restrictions. If double yellow lines are required as an outcome of this assessment, officials will take forward this work.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 29042/22-27, for her assessment on whether, not removing students from the Irish Republic from university student cap numbers, is negatively impacting students from Northern Ireland, by limiting their opportunity to study in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Removing Southern students from the MaSN cap and associated funding arrangements would mean those students having to pay substantially higher tuition fees, unless alternative sources of funding could be found. However, recognizing the need for educational institutions to grow and thrive, my Department increased the number of funded undergraduate student places at local universities by 596 for academic year 2024/25.  This included increased places to reflect the Executive's commitment to support the expansion of Ulster University's Magee campus.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of her budget allocation, whether Tourism NI will be opening a capital grant support scheme.
answer_text: A Working Group comprising officials from my Department, Invest NI and Tourism NI has been set up to manage the transfer of capital support for development of new tourist accommodation from Invest NI to Tourism NI. This work also involves an independent assessment to consider the future provision of visitor accommodation across the North and to inform policy on future capital support and the scope of a new Tourism NI scheme. The target date for transfer of the functions to Tourism NI is April 2026.  Tourism NI will bid for capital budget for the new scheme from 2026/27 as part of the standard budget bidding processes under the Spending Review. Tourism NI will also bid for capital budget for 2026/27 and beyond to enable a new scheme for visitor attractions and amenities to be delivered in line with my Department’s 10-year Tourism Vision and Action Plan.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether she is considering financial support for Visit Belfast.
answer_text: Tourism NI provides annual funding to Visit Belfast through a Service Level Agreement with Belfast City Council to support a programme of activity aimed at promoting leisure and business tourism. A draft agreement for 2025/26, worth £440,000 has been developed by Tourism NI and is currently with Belfast City Council for review. The agreement will support a work programme across visitor information and business tourism, including the delivery of a conference support scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the impact of the climate change targets, as set out in the Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022, on economic growth.
answer_text: Meeting the targets within the climate legislation is both a legal and moral obligation. Decarbonising the economy remains one of the priorities of my Economic Vision. There, is no doubt that there are substantial opportunities in striving towards a more resource-efficient and sustainable economy that is less susceptible to economic shocks. The recent publication of the 2023 Greenhouse Gas inventory shows a continued decrease in overall emissions of 31.5% for the region. Energy related sectors are showing the steepest decline against a backdrop of significant growth of the economy since the 1990 emissions baseline. This, coupled with, the growing annual turnover of our Low Carbon and Renewable Energy Economy to £1.6bn against the Energy Strategy baseline of £1bn, are clear indicators that we are reducing emissions and growing our economy in tandem.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the proposals by Belfast City Council to extend Sunday trading for (i) the Economy, and (ii) workers' rights.
answer_text: My Department has not conducted an assessment. However, I understand that the proposal is for a pilot which will allow Belfast City Council to assess the impact.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail what additional funding his Department plans to provide to local councils in anticipation of the Dilapidation Bill becoming law.
answer_text: There are no plans to provide additional funding to district councils in anticipation of the legislation being enacted. The inclusion of robust and effective cost recovery provisions in the Bill will reduce the likelihood of councils being disadvantaged financially. Effective implementation of the new regime will require council buy-in to opt to undertake enforcement activity and it is recognised that councils will need to allocate adequate resources. Any new powers are discretionary, giving councils the flexibility to avail of them as their resources permit. Councils will also be able to retain receipts of any fixed penalties issued and may charge fees for exercising powers under clause 7 and clause 10.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the amount her Department spent on legal fees in the recent A5 Western Transport Corridor legal case.
answer_text: It is too early to establish the amount spent on legal fees for the recent A5WTC legal challenge.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline (i) what discussions she has had with His Majesty's Government regarding the UK's Industrial Strategy; and (ii) what measures announced, will include Northern Ireland.
answer_text: There has been a range of engagement at Ministerial and official level with the British government throughout the development stage of the Industrial Strategy. Since its publication on 23rd June, I have met the Secretary of State, Hilary Benn MP, and Minister of State at the Department for Business and Trade, Sarah Jones MP to discuss the strategy and what it means for the North of Ireland. The strategy encompasses a broad set of measures that will extend to the north, built around eight high-growth sectors that complement the seven sectoral action plans launched by my department in June 2024.  There is a range of discrete funding allocations and competitive funding being made available across areas such as trade support, expanding access to finance and innovation, which will be accessible to businesses in the North.  It is important to note that the strategy also includes other interventions, for example on electricity costs, grid connections and skills which do not extend to the North. My officials and I will continue to work closely with the British government to ensure that the North’s industrial landscape both contributes to and benefits from the wider goals of the industrial strategy, making the most of every opportunity it presents.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail what consideration the Arts Council has given to reopening a band uniform grant scheme.
answer_text: The Arts Council does not operate a band uniform grant scheme.  Uniforms cannot be capitalised, as they fall under day-to-day resources and would therefore not be eligible to fund with a capital grant scheme.’

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance, in light of his recent statements, what is the total cost for the proposed redevelopment of Casement Park.
answer_text: The positive allocation of £50 million to Casement Park as part of the Spending Review on 11 June marked a significant step forward for the Executive Flagship Project. The British Government has joined the Executive, the Irish Government and the GAA in the commitment of new and additional funding.This additional funding commitment brings new momentum to this vital project which will deliver a modern sporting venue for generations to come, as well as being an economic driver for the surrounding communities and region.I, and my Department, stand ready to play a full and positive role in the next steps to get Casement built. I have written to the Communities Minister seeking a meeting to discuss the delivery of Casement Park.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on when the new guidelines for enforcement on unadopted developments will be published.
answer_text: Following my consideration of the draft Private Streets Enforcement Guidance earlier this year I requested a number of clarifications which have now been addressed.  Subsequent to a final review, my Department will publish the guidance in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether the project Momentum One Zero is at risk as a result of her funding allocations.
answer_text: I remain fully committed to the Momentum One Zero project and recognise its strategic importance to Queen’s University Belfast, the Belfast Region City Deal and the wider economy in the North. 		Following constructive engagement with Queen’s University, I am pleased to confirm that a resolution has been reached which provides the necessary funding to enable the university to proceed with the Momentum One Zero project.	 	My officials continue to work closely with Queen’s University to support the project’s progression and construction is anticipated to commence in early autumn.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether he requested that the conditional funding, awarded by His Majesty's Government for Casement Park, be Financial Transactions Capital.
answer_text: The British Government had a long standing commitment to providing funding towards the redevelopment of Casement Park.  In my discussions with Treasury, in advance of the Spending review, I stressed the importance of the British Government making good on this commitment and requested that funding be provided to honour this.  I did not request that this be provided as Financial Transactions Capital.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 27992-22-27, to detail (i) the proposed length of the engagement process; and (ii) for her assessment of when a decision can be taken.
answer_text: I can advise that officials expect to complete discussions with key stakeholders in July 2025. Once completed and reviewed, a recommendation will be presented to me for consideration on the preferred option for the pedestrianisation of Hill Street, Belfast.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether her Department has any plans to remove students from the Irish Republic from the university student cap numbers, creating parity with the process for students from Great Britain.
answer_text: Improving student mobility remains a priority for me and I am committed to removing barriers for people from here who want to study in the South and vice versa.  Removing southern students from the number cap by increasing fees would not support this aim. Both my officials and I continue to engage with counterparts in the South to explore options for supporting improved mobility.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the proposals to upgrade the junction of Shore Road and the M2 at Mount Vernon, Belfast.
answer_text: A feasibility design has been undertaken for a road layout to fully control the right turn from Shore Road towards the M2 Motorway and enhanced pedestrian facilities. This has been developed to a stage that will allow the proposal to be costed, and to proceed through the Department’s approvals process.Subject to a successful outcome, the proposal will be considered for progression to detailed design, dependent on the availability of funding

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when the pedestrian steps at Deacon Street, Belfast, will be reopened.
answer_text: My Department is undertaking a detailed assessment of two alternative options for the repair or replacement of the steps at Deacon Street in Belfast. My officials are also taking forward the necessary ground investigations and structural assessment of the supporting retaining wall.  It will likely take another 12 to 18 months to conclude detailed analysis and be in a position to take forward repair works, subject to the availability of funding.During the period of closure an alternative route is available, via Gainsborough Drive, North Queen Street and Deacon Street.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether the Northern Ireland Housing Executive is considering the vesting of 2 Deacon Street, Belfast.
answer_text: I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“I can confirm that the Housing Executive is not currently considering vesting or acquisition of 2 Deacon Street, Belfast.”

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when he will answer AQW 27667/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 27667/22-27 was answered on 26 June 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when she will answer AQW 26919/22-27.
answer_text: The question was answered on 24 June 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail why a number of Assembly Written Questions tabled to his Department are not answered within the timeframe specified in Standing Orders.
answer_text: During the current Assembly session, my Department has received over 22% of all the Assembly Questions tabled.Since becoming Minister of Health on 28 May 2024, 3911 written Assembly Questions have been tabled to my Department.There are a number of reasons why a response may not issue on time, with the length of time taken to source and collate the requested information and resource issues being the most common. I am content that officials are striving to meet the deadlines set out in Standing Orders, and that they manage to do so in the majority of cases.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when the grass owned by her Department at West Crescent, Newtownabbey, will be cut.
answer_text: My Department is responsible for the maintenance of the public road network and annually cuts around 45,000 km of grass verges.  Roadside grass is cut to prevent overgrowth onto footway and carriageway surfaces and to remove obstruction from sightlines and traffic signs.In relation to West Crescent, Newtownabbey, I have been advised that grass cutting operations at this location are likely to be completed by the end of July 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any assurances he has received to ensure that departmental funding to conradh na gaeilge is not used for political purposes.
answer_text: Whilst my Department has no direct funding relationship with Conradh na Gaeilge, they do receive funding from Foras na Gaeilge as a North-South implementation body. This funding however cannot be used for political purposes, and I have sought clarification from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:	“Foras na Gaeilge provides funding totalling €867,457 annually to Conradh na Gaeilge to support it in its work as an Irish language Lead-Organisation, responsible for raising awareness of the Irish Language, in language protection and in advocacy on behalf of Irish speakers and potential speakers. Conradh na Gaeilge was selected as a lead organisation following a reorganisation process sanctioned by the North South Ministerial Council on 10 July 2013. 	This funding is allocated in line with a detailed annual business plan which is agreed with Foras na Gaeilge and implemented with regular monitoring and reporting mechanisms. 		As a North-South implementation body, Foras na Gaeilge does not provide funding to grantees, including Conradh na Gaeilge, for political purposes.”		For further clarification, you may wish to contact Foras na Gaeilge directly via email at ceisteanna@forasnagaeilge.ie.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, in light of the national inquiry into grooming gangs announced by His Majesty's Government, to detail what work she is undertaking to ensure the inquiry covers Northern Ireland.
answer_text: This inquiry has been formally announced by the UK Government to cover England and Wales, reflecting the Home Office’s responsibilities for policing and crime within those jurisdictions, and in line with the recommendation in Baroness Casey’s National Audit on Group-Based Child Sexual Exploitation and Abuse. As such, it will not currently extend to Northern Ireland. The Home Office has not yet consulted my Department on this matter: however, a commitment has been made that the Safeguarding Minister will follow up on the announcement of the inquiry with the devolved administrations. We will, therefore, consider the issues raised during that engagement and respond accordingly.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) for an update on her work with GB Energy; and (ii) to outline what funding support has been secured for Northern Ireland based projects.
answer_text: The North has been allocated £1.62 million for local delivery of GB Energy initiatives. This is for use in 2025/26 and was allocated on the basis of population. The funding is for renewable energy in public sector or community energy schemes.Constructive discussions are continuing between Department for Economy officials and DESNZ officials to identify eligible projects at a sufficient state of readiness to deliver within this financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline how she will ensure a diverse mix of renewable energy generation in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The Energy Strategy established a commitment to meet electricity consumption targets from a diverse mix of renewable sources.My Department is developing the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee (REPG) scheme with a flexible structure to tailor support towards technologies that meet the evolving needs of our electricity system, while ensuring value for money for consumers.The upcoming publication on the final scheme design will provide a full update on the criteria for the technologies eligible to participate in the first auction.My Department, in collaboration with other departments and key stakeholders, have also developed the Offshore Renewable Energy Action Plan (OREAP). The OREAP outlines the necessary policy and processes needed to enable offshore renewable energy development in the north’s marine area from 2030.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when energy support scheme legislation will be introduced.
answer_text: I plan to bring primary legislation for the Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee before the Assembly this year, with subordinate regulations to follow as we move towards implementation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the discussions she has had with the Department for Infrastructure regarding planning reform.
answer_text: I met with the Minister for Infrastructure in May 2025 to discuss planning delays, and my officials engage regularly with DfI counterparts on planning matters, particularly regarding renewable energy and telecoms. My officials have also engaged extensively with DfI counterparts on the revision of the Strategic Planning Policy Statement (SPPS) on Renewable and Low Carbon Energy Development. I understand that DfI are currently seeking Executive approval of the revised SPPS but it has not yet been tabled for discussion. On Telecoms, the Planning subgroup within the DfE-led Mobile Barrier Busting Taskforce has been tasked with looking at issues concerning planning, including possible amendments to legislation, guidance and how applications are processed. My officials are engaging with this subgroup which includes both DfI and planners from local councils as this work progresses and will provide information of the economic benefits that mobile communications can bring. This information can be used as an evidence base should DfI consider making any amendments to any of their relevant legislation and guidance. I am due to meet with the Minister of Infrastructure and members of the mobile communications industry in the autumn, at this meeting the topic of planning reform will be discussed, and I will commit to providing any support I can to aid in future considerations.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the (i) aims; (ii) objectives; and (iii) scope, of the Irish Language Policy detailed in her Department's Business Plan End Year Progress Report 2024-25.
answer_text: My Department does not currently have a Minority Language policy. My Department has, however, prepared a draft Irish Language Policy which is currently being consulted upon internally. The policy will be published in due course, setting out its aims, objectives and scope. My Department has one member of staff involved in the development of this Irish Language Policy. The member of staff has undertaken this role as part of their wider duties in my Department’s Equality Unit and therefore no additional expenditure has been incurred.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the number of staff currently working on her Department's development of the new Irish Language Policy; and (ii) the amount spent to date.
answer_text: My Department does not currently have a Minority Language policy. My Department has, however, prepared a draft Irish Language Policy which is currently being consulted upon internally. The policy will be published in due course, setting out its aims, objectives and scope. My Department has one member of staff involved in the development of this Irish Language Policy. The member of staff has undertaken this role as part of their wider duties in my Department’s Equality Unit and therefore no additional expenditure has been incurred.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to publish her Department's current minority language policy.
answer_text: My Department does not currently have a Minority Language policy. My Department has, however, prepared a draft Irish Language Policy which is currently being consulted upon internally. The policy will be published in due course, setting out its aims, objectives and scope. My Department has one member of staff involved in the development of this Irish Language Policy. The member of staff has undertaken this role as part of their wider duties in my Department’s Equality Unit and therefore no additional expenditure has been incurred.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when a consultation on the development and use of biomethane will be published.
answer_text: My Department is currently working on a number of biomethane actions as part of the 2025 Energy Strategy action plan (2025 Energy Strategy Action Plan). This includes progressing Action 6, which will support the sustainability of biomethane being utilised in the gas network in the North. Key areas of focus include:Progressing Support Mechanisms.Consulting on options for Gas Network Connection Costs.Investigating Standards/Certifications.Work is ongoing to analyse a range of options aimed at addressing the challenges and costs for biomethane producers connecting to the gas network. My Department intends to consult on the treatment of connection costs later this year. My Department will continue to collaborate with the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (DAERA), the Utility Regulator, and other key stakeholders to support the development and deployment of biomethane in the North.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline the work of her Department in supporting the development of a Hydrogen Hub.
answer_text: My Department, via the Executive’s Complementary Fund, is investing £15m into the development of a Hydrogen Hub in Ballymena which will deliver an integrated package of investment encompassing skills training, innovation and technology.   The project presents a real opportunity to develop the hydrogen economy in the North and support the transition to net zero.  Through Invest NI, my Department is supporting Mid & East Antrim Borough Council, Queen’s University and their partners to develop the business case to ensure the project maximises its potential benefits and supports a greener future for the North.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to outline the current length of contracts which are in place to cover (i) treatment; (ii) energy recovery; and (iii) disposal, of residual waste arising within the six arc21 local councils.
answer_text: My Department is not party to the contractual arrangements put in place by arc21 or individual local councils in respect of these matters.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, pursuant to AQW 11186/22-27, for an update on the analysis by the Strategic Investment Board on residual waste treatment capacity in Northern Ireland, including when such assessments may be both concluded and published.
answer_text: My Department commissioned the Strategic Investment Board (SIB), in July 2024, to undertake an analysis of Northern Ireland’s waste arisings and infrastructure requirements through to 2050, to meet a Climate Change Committee (CCC) recommendation for Northern Ireland and to better understand our waste infrastructure needs for the future.  This will include an assessment of residual waste treatment capacity here. The SIB are currently engaging with key stakeholders and collating and analysing data.I expect the SIB to make their report available to me by the end of March 2026.  Once I have had the opportunity to consider its findings, it will be published.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether she would consider authorising a revised environmental allowance for civilian staff within her Department, at the level recently agreed for PSNI civilian staff.
answer_text: Administrative staff within the Northern Ireland Prison Service currently receive a prison environmental allowance if working in a prison establishment.  A separate supplementary risk allowance is paid to operational staff.There are no plans to introduce a revised environmental allowance for other staff within my Department.  Any proposed changes to allowances would require approval by the Department of Finance.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether she will consider re-introducing the environmental allowance for forensic science staff working from PSNI stations.
answer_text: The Revised Environmental Allowance for Forensic Science NI staff was removed in 2012/13 as part of the NICS Pay and Grading review.The Department of Finance is responsible for all pay matters relating to civil servants.  The mechanism for requesting changes to pay terms and conditions is via the recognised civil service trade unions and the formal Whitley structure.   Any proposed changes to allowances would require approval by the Department of Finance.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail why her End of Year Progress Report 2024-25 gives a green status to developing an Aviation and Route Development Fund, when one has not been produced.
answer_text: The action as stated in the End of Year Progress Report is to “Maximise air connectivity to drive economic growth by developing an Aviation and Route Development Policy.” This work is ongoing. I refer you to my response of 30 May 2025 (AQW 26920/22-27) which explained that there is no funding available for an air route development fund and that my officials are working closely with the aviation and tourism sector to development an aviation policy.  It is anticipated that a consultation document will be issued before the end of the year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the rationale for not including the development of an Ulster Scots Policy in her End of Year Progress Report 2024-25.
answer_text: The End of Year Progress Report 2024-25 focuses only on the commitments made in the 2024-25 Business Plan. The development of an Ulster Scots Policy was not in the 2024-25 Business Plan.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail what discussions she has had with HM Government regarding the £30 million investment for Northern Ireland through the Local Innovation Partnerships Fund.
answer_text: I met with the Minister of State for Science, Research and Innovation, Patrick Vallance, on 5 June 2025 to discuss the Local Innovation Partnerships Fund in advance of its announcement within the Comprehensive Spending Review on 11 June. My officials and the Chief Scientific and Technology Adviser, Professor Helen McCarthy, subsequently met with officials from the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT) on 11 June to begin planning for delivery. As we move forward, it will be important to determine how an award through the Fund can best be used to advance Research and Development and Innovation aligned to the North’s sectors and technologies of strength.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the Competition and Markets Authority Subsidy Advice Unit Report on proposed subsidy to City of Derry Airport , which states the Department should have looked at the impact the award would have on the investment decisions of other Northern Ireland airports.
answer_text: The Competition and Markets Authority Subsidy Advice Unit (SAU) Report on the Assessment of Compliance, which was referred by Derry City & Strabane District Council, is positive.  It states that the Assessment demonstrates how the design features of the Subsidy contribute to minimising potential negative effects on competition.  My Department, which provided grant funding to the Council, has no role in this SAU process. I would highlight that the review process provided an opportunity for interested third parties to comment on matters relevant to the SAU’s evaluation of the assessment of compliance concerning the proposed subsidy and that no comments were received.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to explain why £8.9 million in funding for Economic Regional Growth Fund is no longer required, as outlined in her Department’s June Monitoring Round submission.
answer_text: Departmental officials engaged with council representatives on the Regional Balance Fund Project Board to discuss budget allocation for Year 1. It was agreed that 30% allocation would be more suitable, to allow time for the necessary preparatory work of the LEPs being established and to identify projects. It should be noted, however, there will be higher budget allocations for Year 2 and Year 3. Total Regional Balance Fund to support the LEPs remains £45m to 31 March 2028.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on legislation to close the Non-Domestic Renewable Heat Incentive Scheme.
answer_text: My Department is progressing both primary and subordinate legislation in relation to closure of the Non-Domestic RHI Scheme. The primary legislation will provide the powers for the Department to bring forward closure regulations, and the subordinate legislation will set out the detail of the closure arrangements. My Department aims to submit the primary legislation, the RHI (Closure of the Non-Domestic Scheme) Bill, to the Executive for approval as soon as possible, before introducing the Bill to the Assembly. Work on drafting of the subordinate legislation is being progressed alongside the development of the Bill. My Department intends to consult on the Regulations and subject to Assembly approval of the Bill, DoF and Treasury approval of the business case, and the outcome of the planned public consultation, the Department’s intention is to introduce the regulations to the Assembly with the aim of a coming into operation date of 1 April 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the role her Department is currently taking to support renewable energy.
answer_text: One of my four critical objectives for the Economy is to reduce carbon emissions; generating sufficient renewable electricity is key to facilitate this change. We must build on our previous success in renewables development and the Executive needs to take a collaborative, whole system approach to facilitate delivery of the 80% by 2030 target in the Climate Change Act. The introduction of the new Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee scheme is crucial in enabling delivery of additional renewable electricity capacity and I plan to bring primary legislation before the Assembly this year, with subordinate regulations to follow as we move towards implementation. Whilst my Department has legislative responsibility for the target, collaboration across departments and with wider stakeholders, including industry, will be essential to the successful delivery of this target.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the year-round late night public transport proposal.
answer_text: I remain committed to finding a solution which will enable the extension of late-night public transport services to progress.  I recently met with the Economy Minister and representatives of Belfast City Council to explore the potential for a collaborative approach to address funding pressures associated with potential service extension and will continue to build on the positive engagement that has taken place to date.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the projects supported by the Union Connectivity Review.
answer_text: The feasibility studies for the projects funded by the Union Connectivity are at an advanced stage and are due to be submitted to my Department shortly for consideration.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 27797-22-27, to outline why action could be taken by his Department at Willowbrook, Newtownabbey, but not at this location.
answer_text: In the case of Willowbrook, the department proceeded with enforcement action due to receiving information that the developer was defaulting on their obligation to complete the works. The developer of Loughshore has not given any indication that they are going to default, and whilst they continue to work with their appointed designers on obtaining technical approval the site is still considered current. The timeline for completion of a road to an adoptable standard remains at the developer’s discretion.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, further to her letter to Executive colleagues, how much additional funding she has requested for Higher Education in the June Monitoring Round.
answer_text: Following my letter to Executive colleagues on 20 May, my Department has requested £9.8m of Resource DEL for Higher Education institutional funding and £9m of Capital DEL for Higher Education research to be allocated through the current monitoring round.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the start date for enforcement works by his Department's contractor at Willowbrook, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: Department officials recently met with the contactor to discuss the extent of the works to bring the road up to an adoptable standard and the programming of these works. It is anticipated that work will commence before the end of the summer.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on her legislative programme.
answer_text: I have set out below an update on my legislative programme. BillType of legislationUpdatePetroleum Licensing (Repeal) Bill  Primary legislationThe Bill has reached final draft stage. It is anticipated to present the Bill to the Executive, and then Assembly, in the early Autumn period. The Utility Regulator (Support for Decarbonisation Preparation) BillPrimary legislationThe bill has been drafted, and we have received legal confirmation that the Assembly has the legislative competence to introduce the Bill. My officials are currently working through a remaining legal issue with Departmental Solicitor’s office (DSO).Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee  Primary legislationCurrently working, in collaboration with DSO, on primary instructions for the new Renewable Electricity Price Guarantee Bill and engaging with OLC. Presentation of the Bill to the Executive, and then Assembly, is expected in Autumn 2025.Offshore Renewable Energy Installation BillPrimary legislationCurrently working, in collaboration with DSO, on primary instructions for the Offshore Renewable Energy Installation Bill and engaging with OLC. Presentation of the Bill to the Executive, and then Assembly, is expected in Autumn 2025.Employment Rights BillPrimary legislationBill will modernise our employment rights framework. Statement to the Assembly on 28 April, set out the way forward for employment law. The Bill will be supported by a package of subordinate legislation, codes of practice and guidance. Written to the Executive seeking approval of the Bill and officials are working with OLC to draft the provisions. Subject to the approval of the Executive, I hope to introduce the Bill to the Assembly by January 2026, with Assembly passage being completed within this mandate. Insolvency (Amendment) BillPrimary legislationWas introduced in the Assembly on 23 June 2025. Closure of the Non-Domestic RHI SchemePrimary and Subordinate legislationMy Department would intend to progress both primary and subordinate legislation for Scheme closure, subject to Executive approval.Credit Union BillPrimary and Subordinate legislationMy Department is currently in the Public Consultation phase of the process, with the consultation ending on 10 July 2025.  Subject to approval of the Assembly, it is my intention to bring forward the Bill within the current mandate.Insolvency (Special Administration Regime for Protected Energy Companies) (SAR) RegulationsSecondary legislationCurrently in draft with DSO for review. A public consultation is planned following receipt of DSO’s advice later in 2025.Amended Guaranteed Standards of Service (GSS) RegulationsSecondary legislationGuaranteed Standards of Service (GSS) Regulations are secondary legislation that may need to be amended depending on the outcome of the Utility Regulator’s consultation.  When my officials receive the Utility Regulator’s analysis of the consultation and decision paper, they will progress any updates to the GSS Regulations as required. Ener

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the rational to refurbish, rather than to replace, the nursery modular building at St Bernard's Primary School, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: A condition survey of the nursery modular unit at St Bernard’s Primary School concluded that the unit was structurally sound and only required some refurbishment at the current time. Costs for the refurbishment remedial works are currently awaited from the Education Authority.  Given the pre-school standardisation programme, both refurbishment and replacement of the unit will be considered and the preferred option identified through the business case process. Departmental officials will keep the school updated on the scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether her Department will engage with the UK Government to ensure Northern Ireland-based (i) aerospace; (ii) defence; and (iii) security, industries will benefit from the Strategic Defence Review 2025 commitment to increase defence spending.
answer_text: My Department engages with the British Government to identify and secure funding opportunities that will enhance business growth, innovation, and competitiveness across the North.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail whether primary schools will be eligible to apply for the Your School, Your Club programme.
answer_text: Yes.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail an assessment of the disparity in funding allocated to the Ulster Scots Agency in comparison with Foras Na Gaeilge, under the Executive Office's remit for equality.
answer_text: Funding for North South Implementation Bodies is the responsibility of the relevant Sponsor Minister, alongside the Minister for Finance and their Irish Government counterparts.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the pedestrianisation of Hill Street, Belfast.
answer_text: My officials are currently finalising preparations for stakeholder engagement which will be undertaken during the month of June.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQO 2052/22-57, to provide further detail on the proposed residents' parking scheme in the area of the Ulster University campus, Belfast.
answer_text: My Department will give consideration to a scheme in the vicinity of the new Ulster University campus in Belfast in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure which schools in North Belfast will be included in the latest tranche of part-time 20mph speed limit zones.
answer_text: Road safety is a high priority for my Department and I am committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and address the needs of all road users.  A review of the recent initiative introducing part-time 20mph speed limits at schools has been undertaken and is available on my Department’s internet site https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/review-part-time-20mph-speed-limits-schools.  The review recommends the provision of a third tranche of part-time 20 mph speed limits at schools, however this will be more modest in size and is dependent on the availability of funding. The report also concludes that a more targeted approach is needed to ensure that the correct safety intervention is selected and work to update our policy document is underway.  I have asked officials to prepare the programme of schools to be treated this financial year based on the existing scored listing, but with preference given to rural schools which are located on roads where the national speed limit applies.  Funding for progressing the next tranche of part-time 20 mph speed limits at schools is included within my budget plans for this financial year and I intend to make an announcement on the next tranche in the coming weeks.I would also like to see what can be done to improve attitudes to speeding, not only for road safety purposes, but to make places cleaner and greener.  I have therefore asked my officials to prepare a paper on the use of permanent 20mph speed limits particularly near schools, in predominately residential areas and places such as town centres where there is a higher number of those walking, wheeling and cycling.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when the Renewable Electricity Support Scheme will be launched.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on work by her Department to develop additional rail haults at (i) Loughside Park, Belfast; and, (ii) the Abbeycentre, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: The All-Island Strategic Rail Review recommended that consideration be given to developing new stations in the Belfast and Derry areas.This has been identified as a medium-term intervention by the Review i.e. to be delivered between 2030 and 2040, and potential locations for the stations have not yet been identified.More work is needed to test the feasibility and affordability of the Review’s recommendations, determine an optimum sequencing for the delivery of projects, and secure the necessary funding to take projects forward. The Department is continuing to work with colleagues in the Department of Transport, Translink, Irish Rail and NTA to consider the recommendations and develop a plan for implementation. I will then need to consider which projects to prioritise as funding opportunities become available.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 27521/22-27, in light of the lack of engagement from the developer and the lack of timescales to bring the roads and footways to an acceptable standard, whether she will instruct enforcement proceedings to commence.
answer_text: Whilst the department has enforcement powers in accordance with the Private Streets (NI) Order, these powers are discretionary. It is important to note that in this case the department has not received any indication that the developer is going to default on their obligations to complete the works and therefore is not in default, so enforcement action is not appropriate. The department offers to adopt a road within a development, thereby taking over the maintenance responsibilities upon completion as per the Private Streets legislation. It is not a requirement and a road within a development can remain private, as was the original intention for Loughshore prior to the current developer obtaining the site. Therefore, the timeline for completion of the road to an adoptable standard remains at the developer’s discretion.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the extension of Park Lodge Primary School, Belfast.
answer_text: Our Lady of Lourdes Primary School (sometimes referred to as Park Lodge Primary School) was announced to advance in design under the first tranche of the School Enhancement Programme (SEP).The project is now very well advanced.  The RIBA Stage 4 (Technical Design) report has been received from the Design Team and is currently being reviewed by my Department’s technical advisors.Funding continues to be available to progress SEP projects through the design stages to pre-tender.  A decision to release projects to tender for a contractor will be taken on a case-by-case basis, dependent on budget availability at that time.I will continue to make the case for additional funding for education through the Executive.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the proposed vesting of 49 Fortwilliam Parade, Belfast.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“There are a number of derelict properties in North Belfast which are currently being assessed to determine an appropriate solution. No. 49 Fortwilliam Parade is included in this assessment, which would form a package of individual properties where the Housing Executive may consider to seek approval to vest, due to blight and dereliction. Any decision to vest would be subject to a business case which is required to be submitted to DfC for approval. We are still at early stages of gathering all required information for each of the properties”.For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Northern Ireland Housing Executive directly at  Public.Affairsunit@nihe.gov.uk Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make the best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what discussions her Department has had with utilities providers at the new housing development at Chimney Corner, Newtownabbey, to reinstate the public road to an acceptable standard, following their works.
answer_text: It is inevitable that, on occasion, works which do not meet the required Northern Ireland Road Authority and Utilities Committee (NIRAUC) Specification for the Reinstatement of Openings in Roads will occur on the public road network.  Officials contacted the contractor responsible and requested a proper permanent reinstatement, in accordance with the NIRAUC specification and I am happy to advise that this work has now been completed.I would also advise that utility works are subject to additional inspection regimes upon completion of the works, along with routine road inspections, which will ensure that any defective work can be passed onto the utility company for rectification during the appropriate warranty period.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on the forthcoming replacement of the Northern Ireland Public Sector Shared Network.
answer_text: The Public Sector Shared Network (PSSN) contract ends on 30 September 2028. Re-procurement options for the future provision of services currently provided through the PSSN contract are presently under consideration to allow sufficient time for a smooth transition to any new arrangements.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the scope of; and (ii) the start date for enforcement works for Willowbrook, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: The statutory 28 day notice period for the Enforcement Notice expired on 23 May 2025. The Department will now undertake remedial drainage works, kerbing, surfacing of footways and carriageways, and white lining to bring the development up to an adoptable standard.A start date for the commencement of the works is dependent on the availability of the Term Contractor for the Newtownabbey area.  My officials are in discussions with the contractor to programme these works.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance pursuant to AQW 26773/22-27 will these matters be tabled at the executive for approval or noting.
answer_text: ANSWERI tabled a paper at the Executive meeting on 29 May.In this paper, I recommended that the Executive note my intention to formally engage with the Secretary of State. The Executive agreed this recommendation.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to outline any consideration he has given to introducing a rural halls fund.
answer_text: Earlier this week I met Minister Lyons. We both understand the importance of sustaining community halls and have requested our officials to continue to engage on this important matter.   My Department previously delivered a pilot Rural Halls Refurbishment Scheme, which concluded in January 2024. It provided capacity building training and grants up to a maximum of £40k to rural community and voluntary sector organisations to deliver capital works to rural halls, with the aim of keeping those community facilities viable, sustainable and in many cases offering additional services.  In 2024, I established a new Rural Policy Unit tasked with developing a new rural policy for Northern Ireland.  As part of this work, previous rural policy initiatives and interventions are being reviewed and evaluated. The findings, lessons learned, and recommendations will be considered in the development of the new rural policy.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail what consideration her Department is giving to the installation of speed ramps at Gainsborough Drive, Belfast, to improve road safety.
answer_text: Road safety remains an extremely high priority for my Department and we are committed to working proactively to make our roads safer and address the needs of all road users.  While there are many roads that would benefit from investment, due to limited budget resources, traffic calming work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need for intervention.The latest assessment for Gainsborough Drive, Belfast indicated an average vehicle speed of 18mph and as such this location would not have sufficient priority to be considered for traffic calming measures at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the number of children on the ADHD assessment waiting list within the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust; (ii) the average waiting time for a child to receive an assessment appointment from the date of referral; and (iii) any update on efforts to reduce waiting times.
answer_text: Belfast Health and Social Care Trust have provided the following responses to your question; (i) There are currently 1271 children on the waiting list for an ADHD assessment.(ii) The average waiting time for a child to receive an assessment appointment from the date of referral is 5 years.(iii) The Belfast Trust continues to work with clinical teams, management colleagues and commissioning colleagues to streamline pathways to improve waiting times.  My Department has recently published the Children and Young People’s Emotional Health and Wellbeing Framework. The Framework, when finalised, will support health and social care services and practitioners to collaborate with other services, agencies and key stakeholders in order to rationalise, coordinate and integrate high-quality, evidence-based support, intervention and diagnostic care pathways that best meet the needs of children, young people and their families.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any policy interventions he will be making in light of the recently published research on the condition of listed buildings over the last ten years.
answer_text: I have more than doubled the funding available for listed building repairs this year to £500k.  I have also commissioned research on the potential to reuse vacant historic buildings for housing. This is in addition to the £240k funding available for individual communities to tackle heritage at risk in villages through our Village Catalyst partnership with DAERA, NIHE and the Architectural Heritage Fund. The research will contribute to a detailed business case to consider an appropriate level of government support for listed buildings in future years. It will also be used to inform the first Historic Environment Policy for Northern Ireland as part of the Heritage Culture and Creativity Programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department gave approval to the closing of the public road at Titanic Belfast, stopping access to the taxi rank for Class B taxi drivers.
answer_text: The public road at Titanic Belfast is not part of the adopted road network and my Department does not have any involvement with the road closure process at this location.  The road falls under the remit of the Belfast Harbour Commissioners.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer AQW 14911/22-27.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on (i) discussions with the developer of Loughshore Manor, Newtownabbey; and (ii) when the works to bring the roads and footways to an adoptable standard will be complete.
answer_text: I am advised that my officials have not had any recent discussions with the developer of Loughshore Manor, Newtownabbey.The developer and their appointed design team are aware of my Department’s position with regard to the obtaining of technical approval for the bridge.  The responsibility for bringing the roads and footways to an adoptable standard, including any associated timescales, is also a matter for the developer.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities if his Department will offer support to the Northern Ireland Federation of Sea Anglers in seeking to secure (i) recognition of Northern Ireland by Angling's International Sport Governing Body, the Confédération Internationale de la Pêche Sportive (CIPS); and (ii) membership of the CIPS.
