answer_text: I have met several times with representatives of the Northern Ireland Federation of Sea Anglers (NIFSA) and can confirm that on 20 September 2024, I wrote to Ferenc Szalay, Honorary President of CIPS, to support the ambitions of both NIFSA and the Northern Ireland Fly Fishing Federation (NIFFF) for recognition by CIPS, FIPS-Mouche, and FIPS-Mer as affiliated governing bodies for angling in Northern Ireland.However, like all sports, angling is self-governing. CIPS, as the recognised international governing body for angling, holds sole authority over international structures, including the designation of governing bodies representing each country.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any action he will take to toughen anti-social behaviour policies and powers available to the Housing Executive and other Housing Associations to remove problem tenants.
answer_text: My Department has been working extensively to develop proposals which will ensure that social landlords have appropriate powers to deal with anti-social behaviour. To this end, I will shortly be bringing forward proposals for the Executive’s consideration to amend legislation, specifically injunctions against unacceptable behaviour, grounds for possession and changes to eligibility for social housing and Full Duty homelessness status where there has been unacceptable behaviour. The proposals for a Housing Bill will be subject to the Executive’s legislative priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether there is a minimum level requirement of public-funded dental places in contractual agreements with dental practices.
answer_text: Health Service dental contracts are with individual dentists, not practices. General Dental Practitioners are self-employed, independent contractors who are free to choose the amount of Health Service care they provide.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) to detail the expected waiting time for an urgent general neurology appointment from date of referral within the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust; and (ii) for an update on efforts to reduce this waiting list.

To ask the Minister of Health whether investment he secured through the (i) Revenue Grant Scheme' and (iI) Rebuilding Support Scheme to support dentistry services has aided provision of public-funded dental places.
answer_text: The General Dental Services (GDS) Revenue Grant Schemes and Rebuilding Support Scheme were part of the Department’s COVID response to help sustain the service and mitigate against the impact of the pandemic. Neither scheme was intended to increase the number of dental registrations.Two General Dental Services (GDS) Revenue Grant Schemes were operated in 2021 (£1.5 million) and 2022 (£5million). These schemes were designed to help practices manage the Covid response and the additional requirements and restrictions that were implemented. The aim was to increase patient throughput in line with the GDS Operational Guidance, improve patient safety, improve practice efficiency and enhance the resilience of practices in order to mitigate against future transmission of aerosol based respiratory diseases, and other infectious diseases.The Rebuilding Support Scheme was established in April 2022 to support the financial viability of dental practices during the Covid-19 pandemic and to support and encourage the recovery of the service as the pandemic ended. Initially eligible practitioners could receive a 25% enhancement to the fees paid for treatment provided. As the impact of the pandemic reduced the enhancement was reduced to 10% and the scheme was phased out in June 2023.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how many NHS-funded dental places were provided in (i) 2022-23; (ii) 2023-24; and (iii) 2024-25.
answer_text: Dental practices do not have a number of Health Service places allocated to them. Under the current contract, dental practitioners have the right to register or not to register a patient as a Health Service patient.Information on the number of patients registered for Health Service dentistry in Northern Ireland by age group is routinely published on a quarterly basis. https://bso.hscni.net/directorates/operations/family-practitioner-services/directorates-operations-family-practitioner-services-information-unit/general-dental-services-statistics/In addition, financial year data on the number of registrations including trend data, is routinely published on an annual basis.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the investigation by the Equality Commission into sectarianism within boxing.
answer_text: As the Equality Commission for Northern Ireland operates as an arm’s length body of The Executive Office I am unable to provide a direct update on the progress of the review into boxing.However, I have formally requested that the Commission register my interest as Minister with responsibility for Sport and provide me a copy of the report when the review is complete.  As I outlined in my response to AQW 27010/22-27, I strongly consider that the Ulster Boxing Council and the Irish Athletic Boxing Association must act decisively on the review’s findings to promote meaningful improvements in equity and fairness within the sport.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail what action Sport NI has taken in recent months to address sectarianism within boxing.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Sport NI.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“Sport NI has discussed with the IABA the current review by the Equality Commission NI (ECNI) of governance, practices and procedures applicable to the Ulster Boxing Council. This is ongoing dialogue with IABA until the review is complete, and to consider any recommendations made. Sport NI has engaged directly with ECNI with respect to the review in order to understand its scope and anticipated timeline.  Sport NI will continue to engage with both IABA and ECNI to ensure that any recommendations or lessons learned are actioned appropriately. No timeframe has yet been confirmed for the completion of this work by the ECNI.Sport NI attaches general terms and conditions to its funding that requires all grant recipients to ensure compliance with all relevant legislation, including, but not specifically, equality legislation. As this work has progressed, Sport NI has enhanced the clarity of expectations of the roles of various organisations connected to the selection of athletes for the Commonwealth Games, through our terms and conditions. This is a direct response to the selection for the 2026 Commonwealth Games.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact Sport NI directly.  Making direct contact with Sport NI will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities what action he has taken in recent months to address sectarianism within boxing.
answer_text: I am aware of the recent issues within the sport of Boxing in Northern Ireland and the allegations of discrimination on sectarian grounds.Firstly, I would reiterate my stance that there is no room for discrimination within any sport.I have held discussions with some of those directly impacted with these negative behaviours to better understand how they are affected, especially in respect of national identity and selection for international competition.I have welcomed the Equality Commission review of the governance, practices and procedures applicable to the Ulster Boxing Council to ensure that they are compliant with the Fair Employment and Treatment (Northern Ireland) Order 1998 and any relevant Codes of Practice. I have requested a meeting with the Equality Commission to discuss this once the review is completed.I have asked my officials to seek some clarity on the progress. I also consider that it is important that the Ulster Boxing Council and the Irish Athletic Boxing Association take appropriate actions following this review to ensure a meaningful impact on equity and fairness within the sport.I have also requested that Sport NI provide assurances that any public funding that is allocated to boxing in Northern Ireland through the Ulster Council and the IABA meets the appropriate governance and statutory obligations.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any long-term frameworks her Department is putting in place to address productivity in our economy.
answer_text: Addressing our low levels of productivity is a key driver of my plans for economic development across the North and is one of my four economic priorities.  Raising the productivity of businesses here will help businesses to create more good jobs by growing their profitability, and supporting productivity growth across our economy is an important part of addressing regional economic imbalance. An effective response to the productivity challenge requires coordinated action and joined-up policymaking across government.  I am committed to working across the Executive to support this approach. The Department’s business plan sets out a range of actions that contribute to positive productivity outcomes, supported by key performance indicators for monitoring progress over time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when her Department will produce an aviation strategy.
answer_text: My officials are working closely with the aviation sector, other government departments and agencies to develop an aviation policy to give direction to the sector and establish policy rationale for any intervention which satisfy the requirements of the Subsidy Control Act.Consultation with the aviation sector is an essential part of the policymaking process. It gives us the opportunity to consider its opinion and expertise in the development of a future aviation strategy.  It is anticipated that the consultation document will be issued before the end of the year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on when the transfer of airport policy from the Department of Infrastructure to her Department will be completed.
answer_text: In order to facilitate the transfer of airport policy and funding from the Department for Infrastructure, legal advice was sought on what specific aviation powers needed to be transferred to my Department in its new policy role.  This confirmed that my Department had the requisite powers to provide funding to Derry City and Strabane District Council to ensure the long-term suitability of the City of Derry airport. My officials are continuing to explore what additional powers may be required to ensure that they have the necessary powers to take forward wider policy responsibilities .

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on her Department’s work to improve public transport connections to Belfast City Airport.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and have been advised as follows:Translink has now completed a feasibility study for a rail connection to Belfast City Airport which will inform an Outline Business Case. For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 26382/22-27, to publish a copy of the business case.
answer_text: The Business Case for funding some of the operational costs for the City of Derry Airport contains projections on the future level of financial and operational performance at the airport and analysis by Invest NI and aviation consultants on potential strategies for growth. This is commercially sensitive material which the Department does not publish.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 25977/22-27, to detail (I) what work has been undertaken to design the scheme; and (ii) the cost of the scheme.
answer_text: The land required for additional parking at Hillview Avenue is not owned by the Department. Given the need to purchase this land and reconfigure the road, it is classed as a capital improvement scheme.  However, compared to other scheme proposals in the area, this potential scheme is assessed as low priority and has not been included in the current design programme. Therefore, to date, no design work has been undertaken and there is no estimate of cost.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the progress of proposals for road safety improvements at Glenville Road, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: In 2024 my Department renewed and extended the yellow box marking at the A2 Shore Road / Glenville Road junction to improve visibility. There has been no recorded personal injury collisions since this upgrade.The detail of the proposed prohibition of the right turn movement at Glenville Road and U-turn movement at Glenavna Manor has recently been agreed with the PSNI and my Department will now proceed with the legislative process.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance if further appointments to the Public Sector Transformation Board will: (i) be subject to a public appointment exercise; and (ii) require Executive approval.
answer_text: ANSWERThe Executive agreed to my predecessor’s plan for an interim Transformation programme in May last year. This included the establishment, and appointment of members to a Public Sector Transformation Board (the Board). The Board reports to me in my role as Minister of Finance and, with the exception of the British Government representative, it is my role as Minister of Finance to appoint members to the Board. I expect to appoint new members to the Board shortly and that they will be included within the terms of reference for the substantive Board. I intend on tabling these matters at an upcoming Executive meeting.Board appointments will not be subject to a public appointments exercise. The appointments being made to the Board are for experts who will provide advice and recommendations to me as Minister of Finance, which I will then use to bring my recommendations to the Executive for decision.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 22531/22-27, was the Community Investment Fund subject to an open call for applications.
answer_text: The Community Investment Fund was established in 2005 via an open call for applications, with 34 groups initially supported. The Community Investment Fund has provided a stable basis for funding of community development activity to strengthen communities and tackle disadvantage.There have been no further open calls for new applications since the creation of the fund.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on Project Gigabit.
answer_text: The procurement for Project Gigabit commenced on 11 November 2024, with an invitation to interested suppliers to complete a selection questionnaire to demonstrate their capacity and capability to deliver such a contract. Those suppliers who met the published criteria of the selection questionnaire were invited to submit a response to the requirements of the Invitation to Tender.  The procurement is currently at the Invitation to Tender Stage and bids are expected by 22 May 2025. Evaluation of the bids will take place in June.  Subject to any necessary approvals and value for money assessments being given, my Department would hope to be able to announce a decision in July.  The procurement is seeking a supplier to address an initial scope of approximately 11,000 premises.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to provide a breakdown of the additional £4 million allocated to higher education in the 2025-26 Budget, compared with the 2025-26 Draft Budget.
answer_text: The Higher Education in 2025-26 has not increased by £4 million when compared with the DfE 2025-26 Draft Budget.  The DfE Draft Budget included a £4.4 million Executive Earmarked allocation for the Graduate Entry Management School.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 25976/22-27, for an update on enforcement proceedings to the developer of Willowbrook, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: My department issued an Article 11 Enforcement Notice on the 23rd April 2025. As per the Private Streets Order, there is a statutory 28-day notice period before the department can proceed with any enforcement works.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, in light of recent media reports, what discussion her Department has had with Belfast City Council surrounding its planning investigation into the erecting of the statue of Bobby Sands in Twinbrook.
answer_text: Planning officials in my Department have had no discussions with Belfast City Council on this matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy where Northern Ireland ranks in UK Regions for attracting foreign direct investment.
answer_text: The Financial Times (FT) fDi Markets report tracks Foreign Direct Investment projects globally.Table 1 shows the number of projects won by Britain and the North over the last financial year, from April 2024 to March 2025 and includes both new and expansion projects. This is based on public information on company investment announcements. The data from FT shows that the North ranks 6th against other areas of Britain. However, when calculated on a per capita basis, the north ranks 2nd after Greater London and South East. It should be noted that this data is for Foreign Direct Investment only and does not include Domestic Direct Investment (DDI) i.e. projects won by the North from Britain. Table 1Companies investing in Britain and the North between April 2024 and March 2025.2023-24ProjectsPopulationPer CapitaRanking per capitaGreater London & South East39918427816461851Scotland975490100565993West Midlands726085687845234North West6376001261206376East of England5164686651268377Northern Ireland351920382548682South West3958112591490079North East292711380934965Yorkshire & Humber26559412521515911East Midlands29499126517211310Wales2231644041438378		© fDi Markets, from the Financial Times Ltd 2025.  Data subject to terms and conditions of useNote:Population data estimates sourced from ONS October 2024

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any work he has undertaken to tackle poverty since taking office in February 2024.
answer_text: Many of the functions and responsibilities that sit within my Department tackle poverty both directly and indirectly. 	As you are aware, my Department has led on the development of the Executive’s draft Anti-Poverty Strategy which will be underpinned by actions that will help mitigate the impacts of, and reduce the risk of falling into poverty, as well as supporting people to exit poverty.	My Department is also developing a draft Fuel Poverty Strategy to address the root causes of fuel poverty and address how to support those living in or at risk of fuel poverty. A new Warm Healthy Homes Scheme that will replace the Affordable Warmth Scheme, providing continued support to low-income households experiencing fuel poverty, is being developed.To help mitigate against the Winter Fuel Payment policy changes announced by the UK Government for winter 2024/25, I successfully bid for and secured funding to provide 177,025 households with a Pension Age Fuel Support Payment of £100. 		My Department provides significant funding to the independent advice and debt sector who play a critical role in addressing poverty. It launched a £1.3m Integrated Advice Partnership Fund with the objective to improve the uptake of free, independent regulated debt advice through a collaborative partnership approach. My Department is working in partnership with the 11 councils across NI to deliver the fund, ensuring that debt advice is accessible in locations such as GP surgeries and food banks where people may present with debt concerns.		The ‘Make the Call’ team is available to help people claim all the benefits, supports and services they may be entitled to. Support available includes Social Fund Budgeting Loans schemes and Discretionary Support. A Universal Credit New Claims Grant is also available to people experiencing financial difficulties. More information about the financial support available can be found at www.nidirect.gov.uk/campaigns/finance-support .		My Department also works with councils to support Social Supermarket projects in each area to provide people struggling with the cost of living with access to food and wraparound support to address the underlying issues impacting their food security.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) the number of people convicted for benefit fraud; and (ii) the total value of benefit fraud convictions secured through the courts, since February 2024.
answer_text: The total number of people convicted for benefit fraud during the period 1 February 2024 to 30 April 2025 is 60.The value of overpayments relating to these 60 cases is £1.1million.All statistics provided are extracted from local operational management systems meaning that they are not quality assured to official statistics standard.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any work he has undertaken to increase housing stock since taking office in February 2024.
answer_text: Since taking office, I have been committed to increasing housing supply across all tenures. At a strategic level this has included securing the agreement and publication of an Executive Housing Supply Strategy. This is the first Executive strategy of its type for housing, which provides a 15-year framework for the policies and actions required to deliver on our supply needs.Implementation of the Strategy will involve the development of shorter-term action plans, developed across Executive Departments and focussed on addressing whole system issues impacting supply.    Shortly after I came into office, it was estimated we would only build 400 social homes in 2024/25. Because I successfully secured additional funding, we actually started over 1,500.On 20 March 2025, I also announced £153m for Co-Ownership to support 4,000 people and families to purchase a home over the next 4 years, with the aim of delivering 1,200 of these as new build homes, adding to overall housing supply.My Department also held a two-day Strategic Insights Lab in June 2024, and a Housing Roundtable meeting in January 2025, which brought together key stakeholders to share knowledge and experience on how to increase social housing delivery in the current challenging environment.  I am considering a number of recommendations which will form part of an action plan on how the outworkings of these events can be taken forward.  This will require further engagement with key stakeholders.  Our ability to increase the supply of social housing is constrained by budget and we continue to work with stakeholders to bring forward measures to maximise the Social Housing Development Programme.  We are also looking at ways in which we can make delivery timelier and bring more surety to the programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he remains committed to ensuring the Public Health Agency continues to provide nurses in special educational settings.
answer_text: The Public Health Agency do not provide nurses to special educational settings, rather this service is provided by Health and Social Care Trusts. My priority remains to ensure that all children have safe access to education, supported by the necessary clinical support. To help ensure this, my Department’s Chief Nursing Officer commissioned the Public Health Agency to work in partnership with the Education Authority and other key stakeholders to undertake a comprehensive needs assessment to scope the healthcare needs of children attending mainstream and special education schools. I anticipate that a final report will be completed for consideration by the end of May 2025.  It will allow us to identify the specific health challenges faced by this population, evaluate the complexity of their needs, and determine the support systems necessary to ensure timely, child-specific care within the school environment.There are no plans for further changes in the current provision of on-site nursing support in Special Education Needs schools until that review is completed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any work he has undertaken to promote the marching bands sector since taking office in February 2024.
answer_text: The Musical Instruments for Bands Scheme is one strand of the Musical Instruments Programme (MIP) administered by the Arts Council on behalf of my Department.  The scheme has funded 82 bands totalling £654,240.00 since I took up office.I was pleased to provide additional capital funding of £323,000 to support the overall programme in 2024/25 and to meet with some recipients of funding. A Bands Forum, convened by the Arts Council is underway.  It will bring together representatives of Bands from across Northern Ireland’s district councils.  The Forum marks the beginning of a process of engagement which will allow the Arts Council to consider the sector’s specific developmental needs.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any Ministerial Directions she, or her predecessor, has issued since the restoration of the Executive in February 2024.
answer_text: There has been 1 Ministerial Direction issued to my Department since February 2024, in relation to the Enhanced Flood Relief Scheme. Further details on all Ministerial Directions are published on the Department of Finance website, and the particular details of this Ministerial Direction can be found at the link below:Ministerial Directions 2023/24 | Department of Finance

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of recent events, whether she will be instructing her Department and its arm's-length bodies to review any relationship with the group Kneecap.
answer_text: No.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when she will publish the (i) terms of reference; and (ii) membership of her Economic Vision Engagement Forum.
answer_text: The Terms of Reference (ToR) for the Economic Vision Engagement Forum was published on the DfE website on Wednesday 7 May: Terms of Reference for the Minister’s Economic Vision Engagement Forum . The membership of the Forum is set out at Annex A of the ToR.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the capital spend by her Department across the Northern Ireland prison estate in the last two years.
answer_text: Public monies expended on capital projects across the Northern Ireland Prison Service Estate in the last two years are set out below:2024/252023/24Capital Spend£12,637,000£14,403,000Spend for the 2024/25 financial year is unaudited.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for his assessment of his Department's performance in answering Assembly Written Questions within the required deadline.
answer_text: During the current Assembly session, my Department has received over 22% of all the Assembly Questions tabled.Since becoming Minister of Health on 28 May 2024, 3911 written Assembly Questions have been tabled to my Department.There are a number of reasons why a response may not issue on time, with the length of time taken to source and collate the requested information and resource issues being the most common. I am content that officials are striving to meet the deadlines set out in Standing Orders, and that they manage to do so in the majority of cases.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the development of the RAISE programme in North Belfast.
To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the value for money exercise undertaken to approve funding for the City of Derry Airport.
answer_text: A business case was produced by departmental officials in accordance with HM Treasury’s guidance.  The case was subsequently approved through the normal departmental approvals process as well as the Department of Finance.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to list the attendees at the pre-brief undertaken with stakeholders on Monday 28 April 2025 in advance of her announcement on the Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill.
answer_text: I met with several stakeholders on Monday 28 April to discuss my announcement on the Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill. The Labour Relations Agency was invited, along with its Engagement Forum, comprising representatives of the organisations listed below: Confederation of British IndustryFederation of Small BusinessesInstitute of DirectorsNI Chamber of Commerce and IndustryManufacturing NINI Retail Consortium. Irish Congress of Trade Unions (6 representatives)In addition to the LRA Engagement Forum, the following organisations were also invited. Tiny LifeCarers NIRetail NIHospitality UlsterNI Tourism AllianceNICVASocial Enterprise NIBusiness in the Community NIWomen in Business NINI Skills Council

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the membership of the Tariff Working Group.
answer_text: I established the Tariff Working Group to assess the implications of US tariffs and any countermeasures. Details of the Working Group including its membership are available on my department’s website. The Working Group is Chaired by a Senior Official in my department, with support from Invest NI and InterTradeIreland. Membership is as follows:Stuart Anderson, NI ChamberNichola Mallon, Logistics UKStephen Kelly, Manufacturing NIClaire Sullivan, CBIThe Executive Office is also represented on the Group, while other officials and government / industry representatives are invited to attend as appropriate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any financial support his Department can provide the Amalgamation of Official Northern Ireland Supporters' Clubs to support their Fan Embassy work.
answer_text: I recently had the opportunity to meet with representatives from the Amalgamation of Official Northern Ireland Supporters’ Clubs  (AONISC) and was pleased to learn more about the valuable role played by the Fans’ Embassy. Its contribution is recognised not only by Football Supporters Europe but also by other international sporting bodies. The Embassy’s work provides vital support to Northern Ireland fans and helps to promote a positive image of Northern Ireland football and our wider community on the international stage.AONISC have recently agreed a Terms of Reference with officials from the Northern Ireland Football Fund(TNIFF). This is a funded assignment and involves the production of a specialist, fan based, report on supporter behaviour and best practice crowd control. I look forward to making an announcement on this in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the report by the Ulster University Economic Policy Centre entitled Management and Leadership Training in NI SMEs.
answer_text: The report provides a comprehensive analysis of the Management and Leadership (M&L) landscape in the North of Ireland. It contains key insights that will help inform economic and skills policies and facilitate change. Financial barriers around budget and capital for training are indicated in the report as one of the main reasons businesses have not engaged with formal M&L training.  Businesses are facing increasing costs that may have a detrimental impact on their finances. North of Ireland SMEs may be able to eligible to apply for funding towards the cost of M&L training through courses, such as Skills Focus, which are either partially or fully funded by my department. The information gap across SMEs regarding both the availability and range of management courses and practices has been highlighted as another barrier in the report. This suggests that additional efforts are required to raise awareness and access to M&L training among SMEs.  My department continues to work with various stakeholders to deliver initiatives and programmes aimed at promoting M&L. The NIBusinessinfo website may be a useful resource for information on available training.  Additionally, my department, through its delivery partner Invest NI, provide SMEs with the opportunity to develop leadership skills through its Leadership and Capability solutions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQT 1255/22-27, to provide further detail on the experimental pedestrianisation scheme of Hill Street, Belfast.
answer_text: I can advise that officials will undertake further engagement with relevant stakeholders in the coming weeks to determine a scheme that works for all. These discussions will better inform my Department on what should be included in the experimental scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the reclassification of Belfast Harbour.
answer_text: As a Trust Port, Belfast Harbour is currently classified by the Office of National Statistics (ONS) as a public corporation for accounting purposes only. A public consultation on the reclassification of all Trust Ports closed during March 2025. My Department are currently considering the outcome of the consultation and will be providing advice on policy options to me in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 25975/22-27, to detail the date on which the stakeholders' meeting is taking place.
answer_text: A meeting is currently scheduled for Monday 19th May.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the publication of her Department's Budget for 2025-26.
answer_text: As part of the final 2025-26 Budget and indicative June Monitoring allocations agreed by the Executive, on 3 April, the Department for the Economy will be allocated an additional £7.2 million Financial Transactions Capital and £13.9 million Resource DEL; £5 million for general allocation and £8.9 million earmarked for skills. Following consideration of the revised allocation to my Department I have provided revised allocations to business areas and Arm’s Length Bodies (ALBs).  In light of these changes, business areas and Arm’s Length Bodies are reviewing their original impact assessments. Following completion of the Equality Impact Assessment, my Department will publish its budget.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the amount of funding (i) her Department; and (ii) its arm's-length bodies have provided to the Nevin Economic Research Institute in the last five years.
answer_text: The amount of funding (i) the Department; and (ii) its arm's-length bodies have provided to the Nevin Economic Research Institute in the last five years is nil.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on discussions to secure a pay settlement for further education lecturers.
answer_text: I am pleased to update that I was able to facilitate a multi-year fair pay offer as a further step towards parity with teachers. The Further Education college employers have communicated this pay offer to their lecturers and are discussing with Trade Union officials as part of on-going negotiations to secure a pay settlement.		I hope this pay offer will be accepted and a pay settlement agreed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any discussions she has had with (i) His Majesty's Government; and (ii) the Utility Regulator to require transparent billing across the entire market.
answer_text: The Utility Regulator set Codes of Practice which electricity and gas supplier must comply with. Some are developed and published by suppliers in accordance with their supply licences. The Code of Practice on Energy Bills and Statements for domestic and Business Customers outlines the overarching principles and minimum requirements placed upon gas and electricity suppliers for the content and format of business bills and statements.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any steps her Department can take to remove the exemption on sheltered dwellings providing a breakdown to tenants for the purposes of gas billing.
answer_text: The Heat Networks (Metering and Billing) Regulations 2014 is the current statutory instrument regulating heat networks in Britain and the North. The Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero is responsible for administering these regulations and holds primary powers for any amendments. The Department for Energy Security and Net Zero has announced that it intends to revoke the Regulations in January 2026 for Britain as per the Energy Act 2023.  My department has now begun scoping policy for heat networks in the North. As policy development progresses, my officials will undertake stakeholder consultation and regulatory inclusion of sheltered dwellings will be reviewed.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for their assessment for the success of the North Belfast Strategic Good Relations Fund.
answer_text: The North Belfast Strategic Good Relations Programme continues to make a positive contribution to improving community relations within North Belfast.  Since its establishment, the programme has improved relationships within and between communities in an area historically affected by division and tension. It has delivered a range of positive outcomes including increased engagement across a range of different groups, the promotion of shared spaces, positive engagement at interface areas, and the strengthening of community relations within and between communities in North Belfast. An independent review of the Programme by DoF Business Consultancy Services (2022) concluded that delivery of the programme ‘largely met the objectives in relation to its alignment to T:BUC, and its assessment of measurable improvements to Good Relations in North Belfast’.The T:BUC Strategy, which provides the strategic framework for good relations work in Northern Ireland, is currently under review and the North Belfast Strategic Good Relations Programme has been considered as part of this.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when the next review of the North Belfast Strategic Good Relations Fund will be undertaken.
answer_text: The T:BUC Strategy is under review and the North Belfast Strategic Good Relations Programme has been considered as part of this.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department is engaging with the UK Government on their work towards publishing an alternative methods strategy later this year on the phasing out of animal testing.
answer_text: My officials have been engaging with colleagues in the Office For Life Sciences, Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT), regarding their upcoming strategy ‘Replacing animals in science: A strategy to support the development, validation and uptake of alternative methods’. A draft NI policy on ‘Alternatives’ is currently being considered.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on the introduction of the Dilapidation Bill.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 25638/22-27, to detail (i) how much longer her Department will allow before taking enforcement action at Lougshore Manor; (ii) the date on which the warning letter was sent to the Willowbrook developers; and (iii) the date on which the 28-day deadline will expire.
answer_text: With regards to Loughshore Manor the developer is not currently in default and the Department has received no indication that they are going to default on their obligation to complete the works. The Department offers to adopt a road within a development, thereby taking over the maintenance responsibilities upon completion as per the Private Streets legislation. The adoption of a road is not a requirement and can remain private, therefore the timeline for completion of the works to an adoptable standard remains at the control of the developer. With regard to Willowbrook, the warning letter of enforcement was issued to the developer in March 2025 and the 28-day deadline expired on 2 April 2025. Officials are currently preparing an Article 11 Enforcement Notice and it is anticipated this will be issued before the end of April.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 25259/22-27, when she expects a decision on the business case.
answer_text: I am due to meet with stakeholders in May, the position in respect of Translink’s business case for the extension of nighttime services, remains unchanged pending the outcome of that meeting.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the parking extension scheme at Hillview Avenue, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government and this has had a significant impact on its ability to deliver infrastructure schemes such the proposal at Hillview Avenue, Newtownabbey.While there are many areas that would benefit from investment, due to limited budget resources, work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need for intervention.Details of the schemes planned for the 2025/26 financial year will be included in my Department’s Annual Report to Council.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, further to her briefing to the Committee for the Economy on 9 April 2024, to provide further detail on the delay to the planned January 2026 auction.
answer_text: My officials have explored multiple avenues to implement a renewable electricity support scheme including using existing Westminster legislation. However, legal advice has said this is not possible and bespoke primary legislation needs to be made in the Assembly. I plan to bring this primary legislation before the Assembly this year, with subordinate regulations to follow as we move towards implementation. The upcoming publication on the final scheme design will provide a full update on the roadmap, eligibility criteria, delivery roles and responsibilities, and an outline of the contract allocation process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the reasons for the delay in producing a new renewable energy support scheme.
answer_text: My officials have explored multiple avenues to implement a renewable electricity support scheme including using existing Westminster legislation. However, legal advice has said this is not possible and bespoke primary legislation needs to be made in the Assembly.I plan to bring this primary legislation before the Assembly this year, with subordinate regulations to follow as we move towards implementation. The upcoming publication on the final scheme design will provide a full update on the roadmap, eligibility criteria, delivery roles and responsibilities, and an outline of the contract allocation process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 25261/22-27, (i) to define what is meant by still active and under construction; and (ii) what time frame will be able to pass before it is judged a positive response has not been received regarding Willowbrook.
answer_text: With regards to Loughshore Manor, my department has not received any indication that the developer is going to default on their obligations to complete the works and they are still working on obtaining technical approval for the structure and agreement on the adoption of the bridge therefore this site is considered to be active and under construction. In the case of Willowbrook, my Department is bound by the Private Streets (NI) Order in which the developer has 28 days to respond to the warning letter of enforcement before my Department will proceed with issuing an Article 11 Enforcement Notice.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department will consider King's Drive, Newtownabbey, for future road resurfacing.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government.While there are many roads that would benefit from investment, including King’s Drive, due to limited budget resources, resurfacing work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need for intervention.Details of the planned list of resurfacing schemes for the 2025/26 financial year will be included within my Department’s Annual Report to Councils.In the meantime, officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with my Department’s policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any further steps her Department can take to improve road safety at the Glenville Road and Shore Road junction, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: I am advised that my officials are progressing proposals for the Glenville Road junction, and once complete this will be considered for implementation alongside other priority schemes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the feasibility study to improve the Shore Road and Mount Vernon Road junction, Belfast.
answer_text: I can advise that a feasibility design has been undertaken and that a layout to fully control the right turn from Shore Road towards the M2 Motorway and enhanced pedestrian facilities has been developed to a stage that will allow the proposal to be costed, and to proceed through the Department’s approvals process.Subject to a successful outcome, the proposal will be considered for progression to detailed design, dependent on the availability of funding

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline the cost to pedestrianise Hill Street, Belfast.
answer_text: I can advise that at present a detailed cost estimate for the pedestrianisation of Hill Street has not been undertaken.However, officials anticipate the costs are likely to be in the region of £5,000 for any necessary signage.Whilst these capital costs are relatively modest, they do not include the Department staff time required to deliver the scheme.Unfortunately, my Department has a higher than ideal vacancy rate and reduced staffing levels has not allowed this work to progress as quickly as I would like.In recent months, limited technical staff resources have been focussed on other competing business priorities, including addressing city centre traffic congestion and works associated with Grand Central Station. However, I have now asked for this work to be prioritised and my officials will soon recommence engagement with stakeholders including affected businesses in the area.I have agreed that my officials commence engagement with objectors in advance of progressing the development of an experimental scheme and associated legislation for the pedestrianisation of Hill Street and the associated one way system

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 23867/22-27, to provide a breakdown by local council area.
answer_text: A breakdown by local council area of the 476 acres of land owned by Invest NI which remain available for development by both indigenous and external businesses in support of their investment projects is provided within the table below. Local Council Area Available land (Acres)Antrim and Newtownabbey Borough Council42.63Ards and North Down Borough Council19.1Armagh City, Banbridge and Craigavon Borough Council118.47Belfast City Council26.6Causeway Coast and Glens Borough Council63.4Derry City & Strabane District Council91.3Fermanagh & Omagh District Council 29.65Lisburn & Castlereagh City Council2.17Mid and East Antrim Borough Council44Mid Ulster District Council2.4Newry, Mourne and Down District Council36.50Total 476.22

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on approving and funding Translink's business case for an extension to the night-time services.
answer_text: My officials are continuing to work with Translink and other key stakeholders in respect of the extension of nighttime public transport services.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 22531/22-27, to detail what the Community Investment Fund was used to support.
answer_text: The Community Investment Fund (CIF) aims to provide a stable basis for funding community development activity to enable a strategic, longer-term approach to help strengthen communities, tackle disadvantage and ensure better publicly-funded services. In the North Belfast Assembly Constituency, Ashton Community Trust (awarded £47,318.84 for 2024/25) receives funding through CIF.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 23519/22-27, for an update on her deliberations for the new guidance to cover (i) Loughshore Manor, Newtownabbey; and (ii) Willowbrook, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: Following an initial review of the guidance, I requested additional measures be considered for inclusion.  Officials have now completed their review of these measures, and the revised guidance will be submitted for my consideration in the coming days.Officials will determine the applicability of the guidance to the developments referred to above once the guidance has been approved.  However, I can give an update on the current position of these developments.Loughshore Manor, Newtownabbey Loughshore Manor is still active and under construction, and will be offered for adoption by the developer in accordance with the provisions contained within the Private Streets (NI) Order 1980 upon completion. The developer is still engaging with the Department, and it remains their intention to achieve adoption including obtaining the necessary technical approval for the structure over the railway and the completion of the street works. Willowbrook, Newtownabbey In accordance with the Private Streets (NI) Order the Department issued a warning letter of enforcement to the developer in March 2025. If a positive response is not received, the Department will then issue an Article 11 Enforcement Notice, using the held road bond.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the amount his Department has allocated to the Arts Council in each financial year since 2016-17.
answer_text: The total amount of funding the Arts Council of Northern Ireland has received from the Department since 2016-17 is as follows:ResourceCapitalTotal2016-17£10,718,000£363,000£11,081,0002017-18£10,620,000£643,000£11,263,0002018-19£10,472,000£1,703,000£12,175,0002019-20£11,620,000£1,740,000£13,360,0002020-21£34,765,000£2,615,000£37,380,0002021-22£16,157,000£1,592,000£17,749,0002022-23£12,369,000£1,321,000£13,690,0002023-24£11,804,338£1,300,000£13,104,338							This includes all funding provided to Arts Council by my Department, including for Covid support schemes. It does not include the National Lottery money disbursed by the Arts Council.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department will consider (i) introducing new routes; or (ii) restoring previous public bus connectivity from Belfast to Carnmoney Cemetery, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: The Public Service Agreement allows Translink to amend services, but they must do so in a cost-effective manner, consistent with the required level of quality and performance, and minimise costs. At the same time, my Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government. This includes the funding available for the public transport network. I recently launched my Department’s Draft 2025-26 Budget Equality Impact Assessment consultation. I have not taken any final decisions on how the draft budget will be spent. In parallel with this consultation, Translink is developing a draft budget for 2025/26 with a focus on protecting existing services.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 20427/22-27, for an update on the investigation to seek a resolution.
answer_text: NI Water has advised that it continues to investigate the issues causing the recurring flooding at the Shore Road and Mill Road junction. Whilst the investigations have not identified any specific cause of the flooding, NI Water has identified a number of issues within the sewerage network that it is addressing, in an attempt to improve the overall network.  NI Water, along with officials from my Department will continue to work together to expediate the investigations and develop a solution to reduce the risk of flooding at this location.I have asked my officials to make contact with your office, to provide you with more detail on the ongoing investigation, issues identified and to keep you informed going forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 22061/22-27, when the next Inter Ministerial Group on Sport will take place.
answer_text: I attended the Inter Ministerial Group on Sport on Monday 24 March 2025.  A date has yet to be confirmed for the next meeting.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 24021/22-27, to detail (i) the amount of funding awarded to the Tigers Bay area; and (ii) the delivery agent for that funding.
answer_text: In North Belfast, Communities in Transition (CIT) operates across the two core areas of New Lodge and Ardoyne. CIT also supports Tigers Bay through an Area of Influence approach, which has received over £27,000 in funding. Three organisations have delivered this support: Ashton Community Trust (Health and Wellbeing); Bailie Community Training (Employability); and Northern Ireland Alternatives (Restorative Practice).

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the main steps he has made since taking office in relation to the expansion of childcare provision and addressing childcare costs for working families.
answer_text: Since taking office, I have:implemented a Northern Ireland Childcare Subsidy Scheme (NICSS) that has given eligible working parents a 15% reduction on their childcare bills.  The Scheme has provided approximately £8million in savings for these working families since September 2024;stabilised and expanded a range of existing early years and childcare programmes supporting children facing disadvantage and with additional needs, including Sure Start, Pathway Fund, Toybox and a number administered by other departments;initiated a major expansion of pre-school education so that an additional 2,500 children will get a full-time place in September 2025 with more to follow in successive years; andcommissioned the Northern Ireland Statistics and Research Agency (NISRA) to undertake a major household survey of parents’ childcare requirements, with over 5000 responses received. This is a significant programme of work which has delivered tangible benefits for children, parents and providers.  	I am currently considering what more can be done in 2025-26 with the £55m Executive budget allocation for Early Learning and Childcare and will bring forward proposals to my Executive colleagues shortly.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) for an update on efforts to expand Pharmacy First; and (ii) whether the Pharmacy First provision for urinary tract infections could be extended beyond the ages of 16-64.
answer_text: I am happy to inform the member that the Pharmacy First service for Everyday Health Conditions has been operating in Northern Ireland since March 2022, replacing the previous Minor Ailments Service which had been in place since 2010.  Referral into the Pharmacy First service includes referral from GP practices, the community pharmacist or self-referral by the patient. Pharmacy First for everyday health conditions facilitates the assessment, and treatment if appropriate, of 13 common conditions such as acne, mouth ulcers and verrucae. All community pharmacies are contracted to provide this service and 166,382 consultations took place in 2023/24. In addition, the service has expanded in recent years to include a number of additional conditions as follows:The Pharmacy First Service for Emergency Hormonal Contraception (EHC) was introduced in July 2022. The service ensures that women and young people aged 13 years and over have timely access to sexual health service and free EHC (including the provision of bridging contraception) when clinically indicated. The service is currently provided by over 400 community pharmacies. In 2023/24 there were 22,055 consultations for this service. The Pharmacy First Service for Uncomplicated Urinary Tract Infections (UTIs) in women aged 16-64 years was rolled-out in November 2023. Currently, over 450 community pharmacies provide the UTI service and in 2023/24, 8,216 patients benefited from the service.The Pharmacy First Sore Throat Test and Treat Service for management of sore throat symptoms in people aged of 5 years and above, was piloted in 43 community pharmacies in NI between November 2023 and March 2024 as part of the winter preparedness plan. 6,768 people benefited from the service during this time. Following the successful evaluation of the service by MOIC, the service was   offered to all community pharmacists in December 2024, initially for 2 months but with a recent extension until 31st May2025. The Pharmacy First Shingles Pilot was launched on 6th January 2025 in 50 pharmacies across Northern Ireland. This service for shingles will facilitate the assessment and treatment of patients aged 18 years and over in the community pharmacy, who present with symptoms of shingles. The pilot was initially introduced for 3 months but has recently been extended for a further 3 months to ensure sufficient numbers for robust evaluation.My Department launched a Strategic Plan for Community Pharmacy in May 2024; it describes the vision and strategic priorities for community pharmacy up to 2030.  One of these priorities is to expand the range of clinical services available through community pharmacies to improve access to patient care pathways for the diagnosis, treatment and prevention of illness. This will include the Pharmacy First Service, however, further development of this service in Northern Ireland is dependent on having an electronic platform and the necessary funding.There are no plans to expand the provision o

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he will seek to deliver greater equality in funding offered to the Ulster Scots Agency and Foras Na Gaeilge, due to a £9,950,000 disparity between both organisations since 2016 by his Department.
To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 23763/22-27, to detail the alternative sources of capital funding Invest NI are bidding for.
answer_text: Invest NI has applied for capital funding of £9.5m under the Shared Prosperity Fund for delivery of a range of advisory and grant supports in 2025-26. Invest NI has also bid for an additional £8.4m of Financial Transactions Capital for 2025-26.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 23108/22-27, for an update on the proposed cuts.
answer_text: All Departmental budgets are under huge pressure. Therefore, areas across my Department have been asked to assess the impact of a reduction in their baseline budget. The budget remains in draft form until conclusion of the equality impact assessment.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when AQW 23106/22-27 will be answered.
answer_text: AQW 23106/22-27 was answered on 3 April 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 20716/22-27, when Small and Micro Business Impact Tests will be published for Theme C of her proposed Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill.
answer_text: The small and micro business impact tests for Theme C will be published at the same time as the response to the consultation is published.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education when the School Crossing Patrol Service will be reinstated at Glengormley Integrated Primary School.
answer_text: The Education Authority is responsible for the operation of the School Crossing Patrol (SCP) scheme and it has advised that the SCP officer for Glengormley Integrated Primary School is currently absent from work.  The school has not requested a temporary SCP as a pedestrian crossing has recently been installed a short distance away and this crossing option is available for the pupils.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the work to implement a new lung screening programme, as per the December 2024 three-year strategic plan for health and social care.
answer_text: Implementation of a new lung screening programme will be a very significant service development encompassing new screening services and a range of additional diagnostic and treatment services. It will require input and collaboration from multiple partners.We are currently at the early stages of scoping out what the requirements of this new programme for Northern Ireland will be and will be working with colleagues across Northern Ireland and the rest of the UK to establish the resources required and the likely timeline.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any consideration given to the extension of double yellow lines at the Hightown Road to combat illegal parking.
answer_text: I am advised that the double yellow lines will be extended to the junction of Harmin Park in the week beginning 24th March 2025, subject to favourable weather conditions.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the number of applicants from the North Belfast constituency that have been successful in securing the Troubles Permanent Disablement Payment.
answer_text: The Victims’ Payments Board, which makes decisions on any entitlement to payment through the Scheme, has advised that its system does not currently allow for a breakdown of successful applications by constituency. Inclusion of post code information in applications is not mandatory and therefore a manual review of more than 10,000 applications would be required to retrieve this information. As of week commencing 24 March 2025, there have been 1,993 successful determinations resulting in over £83 million paid to eligible applicants since the Scheme opened for applications.  1,031 determinations have been unsuccessful.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how his Department is supporting Health and Social Care Trusts (i) to address Northern Ireland's higher rate of respiratory deaths in comparison to other UK and European regions; and (ii) to improve treatment of and life-expectancy for patients suffering from lung disease, including chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, asthma and pneumonia.
answer_text: Rather than launching a protracted process associated with any new health strategy, I have asked my officials and the Public Health Agency to continue their efforts in the current 4 areas of improving services for adult asthma, interstitial lung disease, children’s specialist respiratory care and diagnostics in primary care.I recognise the need to formalise these work streams into a plan for Northern Ireland, that builds on these priorities and extends into other areas such as COPD, oxygen therapy and others in 2025/26 and beyond. I have asked officials to develop this plan for discussion and presentation by April 2025 and include within that process discussion with the All-Party Group. This plan will be formalised with a reporting structure, lead officers and clinicians, timescales and milestones for transparent monitoring.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to outline their work in the United States of America to promote Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Earlier this month First Minister and deputy First Minister led a NI Chamber of Commerce delegation to North Carolina that deepened the economic and innovation partnership we have with North Carolina's highly successful research triangle.  As part of that mission, we invited the Governor to make a return visit to NI.The deputy First Ministers engagements in Washington DC included: a meeting with the President of the United States, meetings with Members of Congress; the State Department; the British Ambassador and engagement with students from Georgetown University; the Washington Forum; the Ireland Funds Gala; Taoiseach and Irish Ambassador and a Queen's University Smithsonian Institution event.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the creation of regional offices for Invest NI.
answer_text: Since its creation in 2002, Invest NI has maintained a network of regional offices in Newry, Omagh, Derry, Belfast and Ballymena. The teams in these offices work to promote local entrepreneurship and support start-ups and existing businesses to grow through product and process innovation, upskilling and exporting. They also facilitate Foreign Direct Investment (FDI). The staff in these offices are responsible for managing a significant proportion of the Invest NI client portfolio and for securing new client companies and investment / reinvestment for Invest NI.   The organisation is currently implementing a new regional approach that will see the creation of an additional regional team, the recruitment of five new senior managers, to lead each team, and a 40% increase in staffing across the regional office network. Recruitment for the new senior managers is underway, and a number of new business development roles have already been filled.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on her work to deliver the Enhanced Investment Zone in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My officials continue to work closely with the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government (MHCLG) to co-design the North’s £150m Enhanced Investment Zone programme. The development of the Programme must follow an MHCLG pre-agreed staged approval process. The target for final approval is Autumn 2025. Key to securing approval at each stage is clear demonstration that Investment Zone funding will drive productivity and leverage significant private sector funding. During March, my Department has hosted regional workshops for further engagement with the private sector and other stakeholders. As part of this engagement, we have issued a call for evidence to help inform the design of the Programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any involvement her Department has had to date in supporting Northern Ireland's role in the UK bid to host the Women's World Cup in 2035.
answer_text: My officials have had initial conversations with Department for Culture, Media and Sport, UK Sport and Department for the Communities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the closure of the Non-Domestic Renewable Heat Incentive Scheme.
answer_text: My Department is urgently progressing proposals for closure of the Non-Domestic RHI Scheme. I hope to be in a position to launch a closure consultation in the coming months, with the aim of closure, subject to necessary approvals and agreements by 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the outcome of her recent trade mission promoting Northern Ireland in the United States of America.
answer_text: In March, I travelled to the US along with officials from Invest NI where I met with senior executives from key US investors, engaged with business leaders, potential new investors and industry networks. The visit was not a trade mission but rather an opportunity to reinforce the north’s position as an attractive, competitive, and strategic location for US business investment, while also supporting our companies to succeed internationally. During my visit I addressed business leaders in New York and Boston to strengthen relationships with influential business, civic and political leaders and to further affirm our commitment to mutually beneficial transatlantic partnerships.  I also attended meeting with companies to support Invest NI discussions and also highlight the economic benefits of dual market access, our world class talent, and the opportunities for US companies to invest and expand. I attended a Tourism Ireland roundtable business breakfast and met around 20 key travel trade partners. These included tour operators and airline contacts who are promoting and selling the north as a ‘must see’ destination for American visitors to Ireland. I highlighted the potential there is for tourism here to grow, and the positive impact it can have on our local communities and wider economy”. Commercial outcomes with businesses and inward investment projects take time to come to fruition, however, I am confident that these discussions and the ongoing work of Invest NI will result in positive future outcomes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any support his Department is offering to the joint UK bid to host the 2035 Women's World Cup.
answer_text: My Department remains committed to maximising opportunities for Northern Ireland to benefit from major sporting events.  Whilst the bidding process for the 2035 FIFA Women's World Cup is at a very early stage, my Department will continue to engage with relevant partners to ensure Northern Ireland’s active participation and to capitalise on the potential benefits such events may bring.  	Policy responsibility for events, including sports events, rests in the first instance with the Department for Economy and Tourism NI.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 23517/22-27, to detail the estimated cost of the scheme.
answer_text: My Department remains committed to progressing the pedestrianisation of Hill Street by exploring different options to try to satisfy all stakeholders’ needs in order to facilitate completion of the necessary legislative process. At present a cost estimate has not been completed, however anticipated costs are likely to be in the region of £5,000. Once officials have considered all options and a solution is reached, a detailed estimate will then be compiled. The impact of over 14 years of underfunding and austerity by the British Government has left the department experiencing significant staff shortages, this has meant that work is limited and can only proceed on the basis of prioritisation. While the cost of the scheme in and of itself may be low, the wider funding challenges for the Department restricts the level of staff needed to carry out the necessary work.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the current estimated waiting times from date of referral for an urgent general neurology appointment in the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust; and (ii) any efforts being made to reduce the backlog of appointments.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the projects supported by the Union connectivity review.
answer_text: The Union Connectivity studies focused on railway improvements are nearing their conclusion, with the drafting of report documentation underway. It is anticipated that this documentation will be subject to a period of review prior to submission to the Department for Transport for their consideration.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail any community-funded posts her Department contributes to in the North Belfast constituency.
answer_text: My Department contributes towards two community-funded posts in Northern Ireland Alternatives, based within the North Belfast constituency.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the core funding provided by the Arts Council across Northern Ireland in the last five years.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Arts Council of Northern Ireland.I have, however, sought the requested information from the Arts Council who have provided the information detailed in the table below.  It should be noted that Arts Council provide core funding to arts organisations from both their Departmental and Lottery budget. Financial YearTotal Grant Amount2019-2020£12,855,831.002020-2021£12,920,993.002021-2022£13,005,025.002022-2023£14,732,194.002023-2024£12,896,523.00For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Arts Council directly. Making direct contact with the Arts Council will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail why the Communities in Transition programme in North Belfast excludes all areas with the exception of Ardoyne and New Lodge.
answer_text: Communities in Transition is part of the Executive’s Programme on Paramilitarism and Organised Crime, and since 2019 has delivered over £1.45 million of support across North Belfast in areas where there has been a significant history of paramilitary activity and coercive control.The CIT areas were identified by independent research commissioned by the Tackling Paramilitarism Programme Board in 2017. Researchers identified areas that were at particular risk from on-going paramilitary activity, highlighting New Lodge and Ardoyne as the core North Belfast CIT areas. The Tiger’s Bay area of North Belfast has received CIT support through an Area of Influence approach.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) the capital spend; and (ii) projects supported under Neighbourhood Renewal across Northern Ireland in the last five years.
answer_text: Due to the volume of detail required to answer AQW 24023/22-27, the information has been placed in the Assembly Library.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) for an update on the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust's efforts to clear the backlog of non-red flag ocular motility surgeries which were paused during the Covid-19 pandemic; and (ii) in what month the ophthalmology service resumed these non-red flag ocular motility surgeries after the Covid-19 restrictions had eased.
answer_text: Belfast Health and Social Care Trust is currently focusing efforts on the reduction of the adult and paediatric motility waiting lists, including non-red flag procedures. The Trust is current running a pilot utilising orthoptics colleagues in theatre which has helped reduce the amount of cancellations and improve the overall pathway for patients. The pilot has allowed for approximately 14 lists to proceed from October 2024 to date with approximately 20 patients operated on and a further 20 removed by orthoptic assessment (without ophthalmology visits) as surgery no longer required. The paediatric waiting list was approx. four years at the beginning of the pilot but this has since reduced to 18 months. My Department has also provided funding for an additional theatre session for paediatric squint surgery in South Eastern Trust.  This additional session commenced in February 2025.Belfast Health and Social Care Trust resumed these non-red flag ocular motility surgeries in 2022 for paediatrics, and in November 2024 for adults.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the average change of tenancy waiting times in Belfast, broken down by Housing Executive District.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows: “Please see the table below, which details the 2024/25 year-to-date average relet time for the Housing Executive’s Belfast Area Offices.Belfast Area Office Average relet time (days) North 28South and East 55West21”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Housing Executive directly. Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 22826/22-27, whether he will consider any capital funding proposals from cultural hubs of the Ulster-Scots tradition to help address the underfunding of Ulster-Scots.
answer_text: DfC provides around £2m in recurrent resource funding to the Ulster-Scots Agency annually to allow the organisation to fulfil its legislative responsibilities as one of the two agencies of the North/South Language Body.  In addition, my Department provides funding to the Ulster-Scots community through a range of other programmes such as the Musical Instruments for Bands programme and the Community Festivals Fund.My Department will consider any capital funding proposal from an Ulster-Scots cultural hub.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on efforts to improve provision of services for adults with ADHD in the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: Although there is no commissioned Adult ADHD service, Belfast Health and Social Care Trust reconfigured a small resource (0.5 Consultant Psychiatry) from within adult teams to process these referrals. Current waiting list is > 3000, with waiting times for an ADHD assessment based on current provision,  being 8 years from the point of referral.Regarding provision of service for Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD), demand for assessment and diagnosis has grown across Trusts. All Trusts are committed to work closely and collaboratively with commissioners to develop innovative, cost effective, co-produced, quality, and evidenced based services that can meet the needs of adults with ADHD within Trust areas. This work would include the development of clinical pathways.The Department is commissioning a research exercise into the current and projected demand with a view to bringing forward recommendations to inform a commissioned ADHD service, subject to future budget availability. The exercise will (amongst other things) seek to establish the prevalence of ADHD (for both children and adults) in Northern Ireland, examine best practice from other jurisdictions and identify challenges and barriers in relation to ADHD diagnosis and treatment. Importantly, the research exercise will be expected to make recommendations on what any future commissioned service might look like in terms of, for example, costs, staffing profiles and recommended care pathways.A research lead has now been identified and work on the research has commenced, with an anticipated completion date of end June 2025.Any future commissioned service will be subject to both budget availability and evidenced demand, but the research, once completed, will help provide a firm evidence base to inform a future model.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when the application submitted for the proposed VE Arch for the Shankill Road, Belfast, will be approved.
answer_text: My officials have advised that the application for the VE arch on Shankill Road, Belfast has been approved in principle, subject to the applicant’s clarification of a small number of outstanding issues. Once the outstanding information has been provided, the application will be processed and approved.
