question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the scale of undeveloped land owned by Invest NI, broken down by parliamentary constituency; and (ii) the length of time the undeveloped land had been owned by Invest NI.
answer_text: There are 476 acres of land owned by Invest NI that remain available for development by businesses to support investment projects brought forward, both indigenous and external.  A breakdown of this figure by parliamentary constituency is provided within the table below. Invest NI owns approximately 2,700 acres, most of which was acquired by predecessor bodies and transferred to the agency upon its 2002 formation.  Invest NI does not hold the records of when its predecessors acquired some of this land. question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the businesses case to award funding to the City of Derry Airport.
answer_text: The business case to enable my Department to provide financial assistance to Derry City & Strabane District Council to secure the future sustainability of the City of Derry Airport has been completed and is going through the Departmental approval process. It will then be issued to the Department for Finance for final approval.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 23321 22-27, to detail (i) where the Glider buses will terminate on the North Belfast route; and (ii) how her Department will ensure connectivity from this proposed park & ride facility to the nearest Glider bus stop.
answer_text: I refer the member to my answer to AQW 23321/22-27 with regard to the termination point for the North Belfast route.The proposed Park and Ride at O’Neill Road, Newtownabbey will be the final stop on the North Belfast route and will be served by a dedicated Metro service following Phase 1 of the Belfast Rapid Transit implementation.  This service will benefit from the bus priority measures being provided as part of the £35m funding from Belfast Region City Deal.  On full implementation of the BRT 2 project, Glider vehicles will replace the Metro service on the route and will also terminate at the Park and Ride site

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail why no funding has been made available in her Draft Budget for Invest NI's Regional Property Fund.
answer_text: I recognise the importance of delivering Invest NI’s Regional Property Programme. However, as has been well documented, the Executive’s capital budget is very challenging.  My department’s capital allocation in the draft Budget has only permitted inescapable and pre-committed bids to be met.  The department’s draft budget will be subject to public consultation before being finalised and Invest NI is currently bidding for alternative sources of capital funding for 2025-26.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when the Interface Pharmacist Network will next review the Red Amber List, in light of concerns regarding withdrawal of Shared Care Agreements for ADHD-related medication.
answer_text: The Regional Group on Specialist Medicines is responsible for the maintenance of the Red Amber List. The list is updated after each meeting of the Regional Group on Specialist Medicines. The group meets quarterly. Most HSC Shared Care Arrangements which appear on the Red Amber List are formally reviewed at least every 5 years. The next due review date for the ADHD medications on the list (atomoxetine, dexamfetamine, guanfacine, lisdexamfetamine and methylphenidate) is January 2027. There are no plans to withdraw these Shared Care Arrangements.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, further to her recent appointments to the Board of Northern Ireland Screen, to detail (i) the total number of appointable candidates she had to choose from; (ii) a breakdown of the community background of all appointable candidates; and (iii) a breakdown of the community background of the candidates she appointed.
answer_text: Twenty candidates were deemed suitable for appointment to the Board of NI Screen, of which 9 were appointed.Public appointments are based on the merit principle; community background is not considered at any stage of the appointment process.To measure the effectiveness of commitments to diversity and equality of opportunity, applicants are provided with an Equal Opportunities Monitoring form, which they may complete voluntarily. This information is used by The Executive Office to publish an annual Public Appointments Report in statistical format which includes community background. The report for 2024/25 is not yet available but will be published at a later date at: Public appointments reports | The Executive Office.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 21646/22-27, if she has not made a decision on the guidance, why does her answer only cover new developments.
answer_text: As noted in my previous response, the guidance has been drafted to significantly reduce the risk of new developments being added to the current backlog of unadopted developments.  I am considering the proposed draft guidance and how it should be applied.  My consideration will include whether the guidance should only apply to new developments or whether it could be applied to other developments.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when they will answer AQW 18186/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 18186/22-27 was answered on 5 March 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she remains committed to the delivery of the pedestrianisation of Hill Street, Belfast.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government.  Unfortunately, the current workload, competing business priorities and reduced staffing levels, has not allowed the pedestrianisation of Hill Street to progress as quickly as I would like.My Department remains committed to progressing the scheme by exploring different options to try to satisfy all stakeholders needs in order to facilitate completion of the necessary legislative process. Officials will continue to progress this matter to a conclusion when resources permit.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on his proposed Dilapidation Bill.
answer_text: I refer the Member to the answer I gave to AQW 21483/22-27.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the adoption of the Mount Gilbert development in West Belfast, following receipt of the certification from NI Water.
answer_text: Mount Gilbert is a private site under development by Platinum Assets NI Ltd. Platinum Assets NI Ltd has appointed Homesure Property Management Limited as its agent to ensure that the development is finished to a standard where it can be adopted by my Department. Homesure Property Management Limited has agreed to complete the outstanding works, including the provision of all necessary certifications.Whilst notification of certification and adoption of sewers by Northern Ireland Water has been received by my Department, there remain outstanding issues in relation to the associated technical approvals. My Department has advised Homesure Property Management Limited accordingly, and awaits a response to this outstanding issue.   My officials will continue to engage with Homesure Property Management Limited to achieve a resolution for residents, and to ensure that the developer fulfils all its obligations to allow the adoption of Mount Gilbert.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on his work to support the Northern Ireland Children's Hospice.
answer_text: I recognise the valuable role the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice plays alongside Health and Social Care Trusts and other community and voluntary sector providers in delivering specialist care to these children with palliative and end of life needs and their families from across Northern Ireland. I also recognise the extremely challenging financial context in which hospices and other charities are operating at present.To that end, my Department has been engaging with the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice since the issues of financial sustainability were raised with my predecessor in 2024. Departmental Officials initially met with the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice to understand the issues at play in relation to sustainability and it was agreed that non-recurrent funding of £420k would be made recurrent at that time. I have also recently confirmed that my Department’s direct funding to the Children’s Hospice will increase in 2025/26 (from £1.75m) to approximately £1.8m. I would note that these figures exclude an additional annual core grant fund of some £50k and also any funding provided directly by HSC Trusts to the Children’s Hospice for contracted services.My Officials continue to work with the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice to understand the operating costs of the Hospice and the costs of the services that they deliver.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department will consider delivering a small grants programme in 2026 to assist community groups to mark the close Ulster-Scots connections with the United States of America during the 250th Anniversary of the Declaration of Independence.
answer_text: •	The upcoming 250th anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence in 2026 represents a unique opportunity for us to celebrate and raise awareness of the contribution and influence that the early Ulster-Scots migrants made to the traditions and values of the United States of America.  •	The United States Northern Ireland Cultural Working Group is actively engaged in developing a programme of cultural related initiatives in the United States and in Northern Ireland and in exploring further collaborative opportunities to commemorate US250. •	The Department provides funding on an annual basis to all local councils for community festivals through the Community Festivals Fund. Community groups wishing to mark the occasion may be eligible to apply for support through their local council.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail when the impact assessments will be published for her Department's Draft Budget 2025-26.
answer_text: My department’s business areas and Arm’s Length Bodies are continuing to assess the impact of their draft budget allocations.  Once complete, officials will collate and publish an overall departmental impact assessment on the draft Budget 2025-26.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when she intends to publish the policy proposals following the consultation of the Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill.
answer_text: I anticipate that my policy proposals following the Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill consultation will be published in Spring 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the creation of an air route development fund for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I refer you to my predecessor’s response of 7 January 2025 (AQW 19735/22-27). which explained that, due to budget pressures, there is no funding available at this time for an air route development fund.However, my officials are progressing an aviation policy based on the York Aviation recommendations from their Report entitled “Air Connectivity to Support Inbound Tourism and Business Growth” published in June 2024.  Working closely with stakeholders in both the aviation and tourism sectors, my officials aim to identify innovative ways to make better use of current funding streams to enhance our air connectivity and promote route development.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether the current Police Ombudsman's performance will be reviewed.
answer_text: The Police Ombudsman is independent and appointed by Royal Warrant, as a Corporation Sole, on the recommendation of the First and deputy First Minsters, acting jointly. Neither I, as Minister, nor my Department, have any role in a performance appraisal for the Police Ombudsman.There is no provision in the Police Ombudsman legislation or Management Statement and Financial Memorandum (MSFM) for a performance appraisal for the Police Ombudsman.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, further to her oral statement on 25 February 2025, to detail the termination point of the North Belfast route.
answer_text: I can advise that the termination point of the North Belfast route is the proposed Park and Ride at O’Neill Road, Newtownabbey.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, further to her oral statement on 25 February 2025, to detail the amount her Department has spent on feasibility studies since the previous Minister's announcement in October 2022.
answer_text: My Department has spent £38,980 in relation to the feasibility studies since the previous Minister's announcement in October 2022 for BRT2.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, further to her oral statement on 25 February 2025, when her Department purchased the land at the O'Neill Road for the proposed park and ride.
answer_text: My oral statement to the Assembly on 25 February 2025 confirmed my decision on how the £35m from the Belfast Region City Deal would be utilised, alongside my commitment to £13m from my departmental budget on the proposed Park and Ride site at O’Neill Road, Newtownabbey.Following approval of the outline business case, my officials will engage with Antrim and Newtownabbey Borough Council with regard to the acquisition of the required land.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he is aware of any community concerns surrounding the test and learn pilot project in North Belfast.
answer_text: The Department are aware of some community concerns surrounding the test and learn pilot project in North Belfast and the team will continue to engage and seek feedback from the community partners to address these issues.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 21968/22-27, to detail (i) any other stakeholders that may contribute financially; (ii) any discussions she has had with stakeholders in relation to this; and (iii) when she will be in a position to approve the business case.
answer_text: As referenced in AQW 21968/22-27 officials are working with Translink to finalise the business case for the extension of nighttime services.  Once this process has been completed, I will be in a position to make a decision in this regard.  It may be appropriate for other key stakeholders to contribute to the cost of extension of night services by Translink.  I have not personally met with either organisation to discuss this matter, pending the outcome of the business case process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any invites she has received to date to attend events in the United States of America to mark St. Patrick's Day, broken down by (i) invites she has accepted; and (ii) invites she has declined, including the rationale for any decisions made.
answer_text: I have received no invitations to mark St. Patrick’s Day in the US. I will be embarking on an Invest NI trade mission the US from 8th to 13th March to promote investment in the north of Ireland. I will also be engaging with Tourism Ireland to promote the island of Ireland as a destination to US based tour operators.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether they are aware of any community concerns surrounding the test and learn pilot project in North Belfast.
answer_text: NICS Collaboration Test and Learn pilots are exploring how better place-based collaborative working between government departments and between government and community organisations can improve delivery and outcomes for local communities.     The Department are aware of some community concerns surrounding the Test and Learn pilot project in North Belfast. The team will continue to engage with the community to address these issues.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of meetings her Department has undertaken with (i) Ulster University; (ii) Queen's University Belfast; (iii) St Mary’s University College Belfast; and (iv) Stranmillis University College as part of the ongoing review into Higher Education Funding.
answer_text: The Review of Higher Education Funding remains at scoping stage.  To date, officials have undertaken one specific meeting regarding the Review of Higher Education Funding with each of (i) Ulster University; (ii) Queen's University Belfast; (iii) St Mary’s University College Belfast; and (iv) Stranmillis University College.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the proposed timeline for her review into Higher Education Funding; and (ii) whether any proposed changes will be brought to the Executive for approval.
answer_text: My predecessor agreed in principle to proceed with a Review of Higher Education Funding.  Officials have recently completed engagement with the sector as regards to the potential scope and approach to the Review and will brief me in coming weeks. The approvals process for any proposed changes emerging from the Review will be determined at the appropriate stage and will depend on whether any recommendations require Executive approval in accordance with the 1998 Act and Ministerial Code.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any consultation with Queens's University Belfast undertaken by the University of Ulster's Magee Taskforce.
answer_text: My predecessor established an independent Taskforce to develop and oversee an action plan to expand Ulster University’s Magee campus to 10,000 students. The agreed Terms of Reference for the Taskforce has been on a locally developed plan. My Department will be engaging across the sector on any aspects of the plan which could have implications for higher education more broadly.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the proposed budget cuts by her Department in the Draft Budget 2025-26 to (i) Ulster University; and (ii) Queen's University Belfast in comparison to 2024-25.
answer_text: Budget allocations in respect of financial year 2025-26 have not yet been finalised, therefore a comparison with financial year 2024-25 cannot be made.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when she will meet with the Vice-Chancellor of Queen’s University Belfast.
answer_text: I had my first formal meeting with the Vice Chancellor of Queen’s University Belfast on Wednesday 5 March 2025. This follows an informal meeting on Wednesday 19 February at the photocall for the launch of the construction of the Queen’s University iREACH Health City & Growth Deals project.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice who is the Accounting Officer for the Police Ombudsman's Office.
answer_text: The Chief Executive, Officer Hugh Hume, is the Accounting Officer for the Police Ombudsman’s Office.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for her assessment of (i) the opinion of the Retired Police Officers Association that the Police Ombudsman exceeded her legal powers, as was ruled by the High Court; and (ii) whether previous Police Ombudsman reports should be amended to remove the term collusive behaviour, which is without legal basis.
answer_text: As I have said previously, I have noted the judgement made by Justice Scoffield and will consider any implications it might have in due course; however, as the Police Ombudsman has lodged an appeal in regard to the judgement it would not be appropriate for me to make any comment at this stage.As the content of reports issued by the Police Ombudsman is an operational matter for her it would not be appropriate for me to intervene.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 19041/22-27, whether additional measures can be considered to address the recurrent flooding of the road and homes at this location, given that flooding occurred several weeks after the scheduled clean of road gullies took place.
answer_text: My Department will arrange for a drainage investigation to be undertaken on the Westland Road, in the vicinity of Cliftonville Golf Club, in the coming weeks. Works will include a further clean of the gullies and jetting of the associated drainage lines to ascertain if there are any underlying issues with the drainage system, and to establish if any remedial works are required.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail why high value items stored in garages, such as lawnmowers, are not considered under the Scheme of Emergency Financial Assistance to District Councils in the event of flooding.
answer_text: The Scheme of Emergency Financial Assistance is to enable householders to make their homes habitable as quickly as possible following flooding.    Only essential primary household utilities, defined as cooker, refrigerator, freezer and washing machines, that are stored in garages and have been damaged or are unusable as a result of flooding, will be covered by the severe inconvenience payment of £1,000.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether she has requested information from the Police Ombudsman's Office in relation to legal costs for the recent High Court action.
answer_text: The Department has not requested any information in relation to the Police Ombudsman’s legal costs for the recent high court action. Although the Office of the Police Ombudsman is funded from the Department of Justice budget, they are an independent body: how they allocate their budget is a matter for them to decide and report upon. As set out in their Management Statement Financial Memorandum (MSFM) the Ombudsman’s Office have authority to incur expenditure approved in the budget without further reference to the Department, subject to limited conditions.You may wish to contact them directly to seek this information.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the future of Abbotscoole House, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: My Department received a business case from the Housing Executive relating to Abbotscoole House in December 2024, which is now being considered by my advisory teams before any decision is made.  Each business case relating to the Tower Block Strategy is given careful examination by my officials to assess if the demolition of the towers and provision of fit for purpose affordable housing in the area provides greater value for money than undertaking the required upgrades to the towers.  As I advised the Member previously, the estimated investment requirement in improvements and planned maintenance for the Housing Executive’s portfolio of Tower Blocks is £138.8 million over the next thirty years.  These investment costs combined with projected cyclical/response maintenance and management costs amount to a total funding requirement of £308.8 million over thirty years.As you are aware, Abbotscoole House forms part of Phase 1 of the Housing Executive’s Tower Block Action plan.  Each individual business case submitted in relation to the tower block strategy sets out detailed proposals for the site in question, including a feasibility study of opportunities for new build rehousing sites in the localities and how long they would take to develop.I can assure the Member that, where tower blocks are approved for demolition, it is my priority that we have new homes there as soon as possible to meet housing needs.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail whether Physical Education clothing, including branded sportwear, will explicitly be covered under the cap proposals and remit of the School Uniforms (Guidelines and Allowances) Bill.
answer_text: Yes, the School Uniforms (Guidelines and Allowances) Bill provides the powers necessary to cover Physical Education clothing, including branded sportwear, as part of the statutory guidelines that schools will have to adhere to.Clause 3 provides a power for the Department of Education to limit – or cap – numbers of individual items of clothing or sets of clothing.  This is drafted by reference to “specific styles”, the definition of which includes clothing of particular makes or brands (or having particular logos).  This term is utilised throughout the Bill, including in relation to the definition of unfair costs aspects in clause 8 – unfair costs aspects must also be addressed in relation to school uniform policies.In addition, clause 2 provides that the guidelines can set out what is reasonable in terms of branded uniform items in schools’ requirements for either day wear or PE kits.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when she intends to launch her consultation on proposed reforms to Credit Union legislation.
answer_text: The Credit Union working group, including representatives from the Irish League and Ulster Federation of Credit Unions, will shortly finalise a co-designed consultation document for my consideration. It is my intention to launch this consultation on reforms to Credit Union legislation during Spring 2025.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the work of the Northern Ireland Executive Office in Brussels.
answer_text: The Office of the Northern Ireland Executive in Brussels provides the Brussels Report, which provides a quarterly update of its work to MLAs.  The latest version of the Brussels Report, covering 1 October to 31 December 2024 was sent to Committee Clerks for dissemination to MLAs on 28 January.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the amount of funding his Department has allocated separately to (i) Foras na Gaeilge; and (ii) the Ulster-Scots Agency, in each financial year since 2016-17.
answer_text: The amount of funding DfC has allocated to Foras na Gaeilge and the Ulster-Scots Agency, in each financial year since 2016-17 is laid out in the table below. This includes Covid support funding in 2020-21 and 2021-22:YearForas na GaeilgeUlster-Scots Agency2016-17£2,939,216£1,830,3952017-18£3,291,750£1,706,2952018-19£3,307,584£1,981,3382019-20£3,268,131£1,963,0532020-21£2,585,343£2,309,9262021-22£4,837,170£3,000,2992022-23£2,920,717£1,988,8192023-24£3,579,179£1,998,458

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the business case for Seaview Primary School’s new build site.
answer_text: A Technical Feasibility Report (TFR) was submitted to the Department for consideration on 25 November 2024. The TFR details potential options and costs to provide suitable accommodation for a fourteen-class primary school, plus double nursery unit and three class Special Educational Needs provision for Seaview Primary School. The review of the TFR is nearing completion.Following approval of the TFR, the Education Authority will draft the Business Case (BC).  It is envisaged that work on the BC will commence by the end of the first quarter of 2025 and the anticipated timescale for completion and approval of the BC is likely to be one year. Once approved this will determine the preferred option to be taken forward in design.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department will carry out full road inspections, with a view to resurfacing, following completion of utilities works along the Shore Road, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: The ongoing utility works on the Shore Road are governed by The Street Works (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 supported by a number of Codes of Practice designed to improve traffic and pedestrian safety at street works and to ensure high quality, durable reinstatement of the openings.My officials carry out statutory inspections of a random sample of road openings by utilities whilst work is in progress, within 6 months after completion, and within the 3 months before the end of the warranty period which is normally 2 years.On completion of the utility works on the Shore Road officials will undertake an assessment to determine the requirement for its inclusion on a future resurfacing programme.In the interim, my department will continue to undertake regular inspections of the Shore Road and repair defects in accordance with current maintenance policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail her Department's efforts towards more affordable student housing.
answer_text: My Department is responsible for higher education policy in relation to teaching and research. Individual institutions assess their own needs for student accommodation while the Private Rented Sector is the responsibility of the Department for Communities (DfC). In December last year the Minister for Communities announced the launch of The Housing Supply Strategy. The strategy, which was approved by the Executive, provides a 15-year framework for the development and delivery of policies and actions needed to deliver our housing supply needs. I fully support the aims of the strategy and my Department will work with DfC and other Departments to implement the strategy through membership of the  Housing Supply Strategy Project Board.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for her assessment of the success of the Young Scot cards for public transport.
answer_text: The Young Scot card is not available in the North of Ireland so I am unable to comment on it’s success.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 20517/22-27, (i) whether she has received the business case for a year round service; and (ii) when she will be in a position to make an announcement on the outcome.
answer_text: I recognise the value of late-night bus and rail services in supporting the nighttime economy and in ensuring that people have a safe and reliable means of getting home. My officials are working with Translink to consider a high level business case for an expanded late night service. It is likely that any extension of nighttime services will require additional funding. It may be appropriate that other key stakeholders contribute to the cost of rolling out any potential nighttime service.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the total allocation from the Draft Budget 2025-26 for Invest NI's Regional Property Strategy.
answer_text: Invest NI’s 10-year Regional Property Programme (the Programme) plans to increase the available stock of appropriate land and property to support business growth and stimulate economic development. As has been well documented, the Executive’s capital budget is very challenging and consequently the department’s capital allocation has only permitted inescapable and pre-committed bids to be met.  Invest NI is currently bidding for alternative sources of capital funding for 2025-26.  Should additional funding become available in year, allocations to the Programme will be considered in the context of available funding and all other competing priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer (i) AQW 20424/22-27; (ii) AQW 20425/22-27; and (iii) AQW 20426/22-27.

To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQO 7664/11-15, to detail (i) any steps his Department has taken to address the comparative underfunding of the Ulster-Scots cultural tradition; and (ii) what his Department considers to be the approximate funding ratio difference between Irish and Ulster-Scots at present.
answer_text: I recognise the importance of adequately funding the promotion and protection of the Ulster-Scots language and culture which are essential in fostering a diverse, inclusive and enriched society. While the Ulster-Scots Agency, as the primary body responsible for the promotion of Ulster-Scots language and culture in this jurisdiction, receives the majority of DfC Ulster-Scots funding, my Department does provide funding for additional Ulster-Scots programmes and activities such as the Ulster-Scots Business Development, the Musical Instruments for Bands Programme and the Community Festivals Fund.With regards to funding ratios, the proportionality of jurisdictional funding for each agency of the North/South Language Body was agreed when the Bodies were established on the basis of the assessed benefit to each jurisdiction of the activities of each agency. My department provides 75% of Ulster-Scots Agency core funding and 25% of Foras na Gaeilge core funding; the balance is provided by the Irish Government. Work has commenced on the development of the North South Language Body’s corporate plan for 2026-28, and this will include consideration of funding priorities and needs, which will need to be considered within the wider budgetary position.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on Project Gigabit.
answer_text: The procurement for Project Gigabit commenced on 11 November 2024, with an invitation to interested suppliers to complete a selection questionnaire to demonstrate their capacity and capability to deliver such a contract.  Those suppliers who meet the published criteria of the selection questionnaire will be invited to submit a response to the requirements of the Invitation to Tender for the project. The procurement will seek a supplier to address an initial scope of approximately 11,000 premises. My Department expects to award a contract for Project Gigabit in the coming months, subject to compliant bids being made, bids meeting the published criteria, and all necessary approvals being agreed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any changes she is proposing to her Department's legislative programme.
answer_text: I am proposing no changes to my legislative programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the development of a long-term energy storage policy by her Department.
answer_text: The Department has commissioned research to provide evidence for the development of interconnection and energy storage policy. This will include analysis on the effect on the cost to local consumers, security of supply and carbon emissions under specified scenarios. It is expected to be completed in April 2025. The output of this research will inform the development of both interconnector and energy storage policy. DfE is working closely with the Utility Regulator and SONI and others in the sector on this complex area.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 17862/22-27, for an update on the feasibility study.
answer_text: The feasibility and options study for Shore Road / Mount Vernon / M2 junction was commissioned on 16 April 2024 from our consultants to include surveying and modelling works at the junction.A decision on any preferred option is expected at the end of March 2025 with scheme implementation dependent on budget availability thereafter.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on work to reopen the public steps at Deacon Street, Belfast.
answer_text: Due to concerns for pedestrian safety, my officials closed the steps at Deacon Street, Belfast on 18th November 2024.A feasibility study has been prepared and two potential options for the repair or replacement of the steps have been identified.  My officials are now undertaking detailed assessment of the selected options, including the necessary ground investigations and structural assessment of the supporting retaining wall.  It is likely that this process will take between 12-18 months for completion.  Construction works could then commence, subject to the availability of funding. This timescale accounts for the ground investigations and structural assessments which need to be carried out. The division are also currently operating with a very limited staffing resource and are balancing competing demands from other schemes.During the period of closure an alternative route is available, via Gainsborough Drive, North Queen Street and Deacon Street

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail all community organisation funded posts supported by his Department in the North Belfast constituency in the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: My Department does not collect data in the format requested.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail why additional documentation is required to tax a vehicle at a Post Office in Northern Ireland compared with the rest of the United Kingdom.
answer_text: Current regulations here only allow for a legible printed copy of the electronically communicated insurance certificate to be produced at the Post Office counters when applying for vehicle excise duty, not electronic copies. This was at the specific request of Post Office management.The removal of the requirement to present vehicle insurance proof at the time of purchase in GB is a consequence of the amalgamation of insurance and car registration databases, and the GB Police National Computer, currently called ‘Tutelage’. As the body responsible for the identification and prosecution of uninsured drivers here, the PSNI participated in a week long pilot of Tutelage in October 2020 but due to budgetary constraints, the scheme could not be extended further.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 20057/22-27, for a target completion date for the works.
answer_text: I am pleased to advise the member that the road markings were completed during the week commencing 20 January 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when the pedestrian crossing at Carnmoney Road, Glengormley, will be fully operational.
answer_text: The Carnmoney Road crossing still requires an electrical connection, high friction surfacing and lining. It is currently planned to have this work completed by the 14th March 2025, at which point the crossing will be fully operational.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 21646/22-27, (i) when she will complete her consideration; (ii) whether her considerations include applying the new guidelines retrospectively; and (iii) whether she intends to make a statement in the Assembly once her considerations are completed.
answer_text: Consideration of the proposed guidance and how it is implemented will be completed in the coming weeks.  Following this consideration, I will decide if a statement to the Assembly is required.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, in light of the comments by the Minister for Communities during Question Time on 10 February 2024 that delays in progressing Belfast Rapid Transport Phase 2 are now impacting investment in public realm schemes, when she will make an announcement on the final routes for North Belfast.
answer_text: I can confirm that Minister O’Dowd had planned to make an Assembly statement on the Belfast Rapid Transit Phase 2 (BRT2) project on 28th January 2025, however this was postponed due to the severity of Storm Eowyn and its consequential impact on citizens in the north. It is my intention to provide an announcement in the near future, and I would not wish to pre-empt the contents of this statement.The Eastern Transport Plan is focused on an integrated land use and transport planning approach with collaboration from key stakeholders essential to achieving shared goals and outcomes. The Belfast City Transport Plan chapter of the Eastern Transport Plan is expected to be published for public engagement in 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail how she intends to uphold public trust in the Police Ombudsman's Office for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I will consider the High Court judgment and any implications it might have in due course, noting that the Ombudsman has lodged an appeal. The Police Ombudsman operates independently of the Minister of Justice and from the Department and is appointed by Royal Warrant on the recommendation of the First and deputy First Ministers, acting jointly.  The Ombudsman’s Office carries out a vital role in investigating complaints in regard to the conduct of police officers. I note Judge Scoffield acknowledged that the three reports were the product of detailed investigation, involving much consideration and a significant amount of hard work on the part of the Ombudsman and her officers. Judge Scoffield also noted that nothing in his judgment was intended to undermine or cast doubt upon their professionalism, dedication or bona fides in undertaking their work in relation to the relevant complaints.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the amount spent to date by the Police Ombudsman as a result of legal action taken by the Northern Ireland Retired Police Officers Association.
answer_text: This is an operational matter for the Office of Police Ombudsman for Northern Ireland. Although the Office of the Police Ombudsman is funded from the Department of Justice budget, they are an independent body and how they allocate their budget is a matter for them to decide and report upon. You may wish to contact them directly to seek this information.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice when her Department was informed by the Police Ombudsman's Office for Northern Ireland that the Police Ombudsman intended to appeal the High Court ruling that she exceeded her legal powers.
answer_text: My Department was informed of the Ombudsman’s intention to appeal the High Court judgement on the afternoon of Monday 10 February.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice when she last met the Police Ombudsman.
answer_text: I last met with the Police Ombudsman on 17 Oct 2022. As you are aware I left office at the end of October 2022 and returned on 3 February 2024 following the restoration of the Executive. The Police Ombudsman was on sick absence for three weeks in mid-May and then from June 2024 returning to the Office in January this year. I am meeting the Police Ombudsman in March 2025 as part of the normal accountability process.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department has assessed the impact of GPs revoking established prescriptions to patients because they are amber list medications recommended by private consultants.
answer_text: Medications recommended by a specialist for the treatment often fall into categories known as “Red Drugs” or ‘Amber Drugs’.  The Red-Amber list has been designed for use in Health and Social Care, and the classification for each drug is decided by the Interface Pharmacist Network for Specialist Medicines and relates to a recommendation on prescribing responsibility for the medication and whether or not the supply of a medicine should be organised through the hospital pharmacy network.  ‘Amber Drugs’ are specialist medications which have significant pharmacological complexity and/or rarity of use to make the prescribing of the medicine relatively uncommon in the community.  For these reasons, ‘amber’ medications require a written shared care arrangement between a GP, the recommending specialist and the patient, covering respective roles and responsibilities for ongoing follow up e.g. bloods, blood pressure, weight or ECG checks and regular review by the specialist.  As clinical and legal liability associated with a prescribed medication falls to the doctor who issues and signs the script, before shared care is started this should be agreed between the GP and the specialist.  The GP must be confident in the diagnosis, ongoing support and guidance given to them and the patient by the recommending specialist.  If the GP is not agreeable to take prescribing responsibility this should be explained to the specialist and patient.  Where a shared care agreement has been entered a GP may subsequently decide it is not appropriate for them to continue to prescribe the amber medication, including when the roles and responsibilities for ongoing follow up stipulated in the shared care agreement are no longer able to be fulfilled. The GP should contact the patient and the specialist to make them aware of their decision. My Department does not monitor the impact of individual clinical decisions taken by GPs.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, following the publication of the Draft Budget 2025-26, to detail any additional support he will be able to offer the Northern Ireland Children's Hospice.
answer_text: You will be aware that I have recently approved the Agenda for Change pay award for 2024/25 and this will allow SPPG to confirm the final uplift and increase to the NICH contract value for 2024/25.  This will be communicated to NICH formally through the usual channels.The Department is also working through the final stages of the consideration of interim uplifts to providers such as Hospices for 2025/26, to support the increase in costs resultant from inescapable pressures such as the increase in the National Living Wage and changes to Employer National Insurance contributions from April 2025.  When these are finalised, the NICH will be formally advised of any resultant increase to contract value.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for a breakdown of the £15 million in the Draft Budget 2025-26 allocated for the Local Economic Partnerships by local council area.
answer_text: Former Minister Murphy announced a breakdown of funding allocations on 27th January. Details of fund allocations and rationale can be found on the following webpage:  www.economy-ni.gov.uk/articles/regional-balance-fund. Minister Murphy made an allocation to the Regional Balance Fund of £5m Resource and £10m Capital within his draft budget allocations for 2025/26.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department is monitoring the levels of shared care arrangements being denied or revoked by General Practices for patients seeking attention deficit hyperactivity disorder medication.
answer_text: My Department does not monitor the impact of individual clinical decisions taken by GPs.Medications recommended by a specialist for the treatment of Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) often fall into a category known as ‘Amber Drugs’ on the Red-Amber list.  This classification is decided by the Interface Pharmacist Network for Health and Social Care (HSC) and relates to a recommendation on prescribing responsibility for the medication and whether or not the supply of a medicine should be organised through the hospital pharmacy network.  ‘Amber Drugs’ are specialist medications which have significant pharmacological complexity and/or rarity of use to make the prescribing of the medicine relatively uncommon in the community.  For these reasons, ‘amber’ medications require a written shared care arrangement between a GP, the recommending specialist and the patient, covering respective roles and responsibilities for ongoing follow up e.g. bloods, blood pressure, weight or ECG checks.  As clinical and legal liability associated with a prescribed medication falls to the doctor who issues and signs the script, a GP must be confident in the diagnosis, support and guidance given to them and the patient, by the recommending specialist.  The Red-Amber list has been designed for use in Health and Social Care only.Shared care arrangements are drawn up following local discussion and agreement by prescribing parties. A GP may choose to enter into a shared care arrangement with the HSC if the respective clinical responsibilities are fulfilled. If the GP is unwilling to prescribe on clinical grounds the specialist would be informed.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether General Practices have any responsibilities to provide a proper notification period for revoking an established prescription to a patient because they no longer wish to prescribe amber list medication from shared care arrangements.
answer_text: Medications recommended by a specialist for the treatment often fall into categories known as “Red Drugs” or ‘Amber Drugs’.  The Red-Amber list has been designed for use in Health and Social Care, and the classification for each drug is decided by the Interface Pharmacist Network for Specialist Medicines and relates to a recommendation on prescribing responsibility for the medication and whether or not the supply of a medicine should be organised through the hospital pharmacy network.  ‘Amber Drugs’ are specialist medications which have significant pharmacological complexity and/or rarity of use to make the prescribing of the medicine relatively uncommon in the community.  For these reasons, ‘amber’ medications require a written shared care arrangement between a GP, the recommending specialist and the patient, covering respective roles and responsibilities for ongoing follow up e.g. bloods, blood pressure, weight or ECG checks and regular review by the specialist.  As clinical and legal liability associated with a prescribed medication falls to the doctor who issues and signs the script, before shared care is started this should be agreed between the GP and the specialist.  The GP must be confident in the diagnosis, ongoing support and guidance given to them and the patient by the recommending specialist.  If the GP is not agreeable to take prescribing responsibility this should be explained to the specialist and patient.  Where a shared care agreement has been entered a GP may subsequently decide it is not appropriate for them to continue to prescribe the amber medication, including when the roles and responsibilities for ongoing follow up stipulated in the shared care agreement are no longer able to be fulfilled. The GP should contact the patient and the specialist to make them aware of their decision.There is no specific notification period for stopping medication including shared care medication.  The GP should take into consideration providing time for the patient or their parent/carer to discuss options with their specialist including for the medication to be supplied by the specialist or alternative prescribers. They may also reduce the strength of some medications gradually before stopping them to avoid withdrawal symptoms.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any steps he is taking to ensure funding distributed by Sport NI goes to organisations that fully comply with equality legislation.
answer_text: As Minister responsible for sport, I have met with several individuals and clubs to discuss concerns about equality within their sports, and I remain committed to listening and offering support where possible. I have directed my officials to monitor developments in these cases and provide me with relevant updates regularly.In addition, as the sponsoring Department for Sport NI, my officials ensure, on my behalf, that Sport NI allocates funding only to organisations that fully comply with the grant's terms and conditions. This includes verifying adherence to all applicable legislation, including equality laws. They oversee compliance through regular partnership meetings with Sport NI and keep me informed of significant matters.Compliance with legislation by funding recipients is an operational responsibility of Sport NI.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“Sport NI has published Promoting Fair Play in Sport at https://www.sportni.net/wp-content/uploads/2013/09/Promotingfairplayinsport.pdf, which states that public authorities are subject to the equality legislation in terms of how they deliver goods, facilities and services.  The issue of private clubs and voluntary bodies is more complex and may differ under each of the pieces of the primary legislation.“Promoting Fair Play in Sport considers the relevant legislation, the role of the Equality Commission for Northern Ireland, and directs organisations towards the appropriate codes of practice and guidance notes.“Sport NI is not a regulator or an arbitrator for the sports sector and has no powers of investigation or enforcement, beyond the specific use of any funding awarded.  The terms and conditions attached to funding requires all grant recipients to ensure compliance with all relevant legislation, including, but not specifically, equality legislation.“Sport NI is a member of the Sports Councils Equality Group (SCEG) and provides, in partnership with the other UK Sports Councils, free access for the sector to a specific ‘equality’ resource in the Moving to Inclusion hub - https://movingtoinclusion.co.uk/.  This provides resources and support for sports organisations, helping them become more diverse, inclusive and socially responsible.  It includes a self-assessment toolkit and resources to support continual improvement.”

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any proactive steps he will take to rebuild confidence in unionist communities that boxers in Northern Ireland will not face religious and political discrimination.
answer_text: I have written to Sport NI and made clear that I expect organisations that receive public funding to fully comply with their equality obligations. I will continue to use my influence to ensure that sporting organisations also comply with equality requirements which I hope will increase trust and confidence in communities that have faced discrimination.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any work he is undertaking to ensure Northern Ireland-born athletes do not face any barriers to representing the United Kingdom at international level.
answer_text: The member will be aware that I have taken a particular interest in the ability of athletes from Northern Ireland to represent the United Kingdom at international level, however this issue is an operational matter for Sport NI. I have also asked for the review of governing bodies to be included on the agenda for the next Inter Ministerial Group on Sport. I have sought further information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:‘Sport NI applies the UK Recognition Policy, in conjunction with our partners from the other UK Sports Councils.  An element of the Recognition criteria relates to international representation, specifically:‘“Under the 1998 Belfast Agreement, people from Northern Ireland have the right to identify themselves and be accepted as Irish or British or both. UK and all bodies from Ireland, applying to be recognised in the jurisdiction of Northern Ireland, are expected to provide evidence of agreement or arrangements which are in place to ensure an athlete’s right to choose their country of representation are accommodated and that the Agreement is being adhered to”.‘Evidencing the application of this criteria is a requirement for all new applications for Recognition, and as part of any Recognition review undertaken.  Sport NI, as a member of the UK Recognition Panel, provides support to governing bodies from across the UK, to ensure that the Belfast Agreement representation expectations are respected.  This is particularly relevant for Great Britain-based and all-Ireland national governing bodies, where the Belfast Agreement and its requirements are often not as well-known as with NI-based sports organisations.‘Sport NI supports sports that are Recognised per the Recognition policy. These can be NI bodies or National Governing Bodies of Sport from UK or Ireland. ‘Funding to these bodies is designed to support Northern Ireland athletes within the pathways and high-performance systems. The success of this performance funding will ultimately be measured in the number of athletes moving onto National programmes (UK or IRL) and the performance results at international level.‘One of these programmes is the Athlete Award programme, which targets athletes in the stage of their sporting career where they are committing to their sport but before they have been named on World Class funded programme (i.e. through UK Sport or Sport Ireland). In this instance we have favourably supported applications where athletes face additional travel costs to get to the UK mainland in order to join a junior or pathway programme that will ultimately contribute to their sporting journey towards Olympic and Paralympic Games.‘The Athlete Award is open to any Olympic/ Paralympic or Commonwealth sport regardless to their recognition status with SNI, and as such we have been able to target funding successfully to multiple athletes who have gone on to achieve success within GB&NI (Team GB) programmes in Olympic and Paralympic Sport.‘Sport NI are

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, in light of recent media reports, to detail any engagement his Department has had with the Equality Commission in relation to the behaviour of Boxing Governing Bodies in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I will write to the Chief Executive of the Equality Commission to register my interest as Minister with responsibility for Sport in the Comprehensive Equality Review of the governance, practices and procedures applicable to the Ulster Boxing Council which the Commission is carrying out to ensure that they are compliant with the Fair Employment and Treatment (Northern Ireland) Order 1998.In addition, the Chief Executive of the Equality Commission, an arm’s length body of my Department, has advised me as follows:-“Equality Commission NI is working with Irish Athletic Boxing Association (IABA) and Ulster Provincial Boxing Council to review issues identified in relation to the Daryl Clarke issue.  Sport NI is not dealing directly with ECNI but is working with IABA to ensure that any recommendations or lessons learned are actioned appropriately.”

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how his Department will ensure funding provided by his Department to support boxing reaches all communities in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Sport NI.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“Sport NI has engaged with the Irish Athletic Boxing Association (IABA) following the recent meeting with the Minister and Club representatives. Sport NI has highlighted the concerns of clubs to the IABA and will have further meetings to establish the full impact of its development work. Sport NI has initiated discussions on the delivery of an Inclusion Review by the IABA, the scope of this review is to be agreed.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact Sport NI directly.  Making direct contact with Sport NI will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for her assessment of Translink’s business case to introduce year-round late night public transport.
answer_text: I recognise the value of late-night bus and rail services in supporting the nighttime economy and in ensuring that people have a safe and reliable means of getting home. My officials are working with Translink to consider a high level business case for an expanded late night service. It is likely that any extension of nighttime services will require additional funding. It may be appropriate that other key stakeholders contribute to the cost of rolling out any potential nighttime service.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department will consider any measures to address excessive speeding on the Ballyhenry Road, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: Ballyhenry Road carries approximately 15,000 vehicles per day and would not be suitable for the provision of vertical traffic calming measures, such as speed humps. I am advised that the road is signed appropriately and other traffic calming measures such as traffic islands and central hatching have been provided along the route.The PSNI have designated the Ballyhenry Road a “Community Concern” site and have been taking enforcement action against drivers exceeding the speed limit as resource permits.One of the best ways to ensure safety on our roads and fulfil our long-term goal of eliminating death and serious injury by 2050, is by changing road user behaviour. As road users we all have a personal responsibility to behave in a way that keeps ourselves and others safe. The sad reality is that if we do not change our own attitudes when we are using the roads, our death toll will continue to increase.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any work his Department is undertaking to the support the return of Belfast Culture Night.
answer_text: I recognise the significant contribution that Culture Night made to the vibrancy and diversity of our night-time economy. I was pleased to meet recently with Belfast’s Night Czar, Michael Stewart, who shared his priorities for the night-time economy which included restoring Culture Night.My Department has representation on Belfast’s Purple Flag Committee, a group of stakeholders seeking to create a safe and thriving city at night for all users. Belfast City Council and the Night Czar attend the Purple Flag Committee, and my Department is engaging with them and other stakeholders to discuss issues relating to the restoration of Culture Night.Belfast City Council has launched a survey to take views on what Culture Night should look like in the future. The survey closes later this month and my Department looks forward to hearing about the findings at a future meeting of the Purple Flag Committee.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any work his Department is undertaking to the support the return of Belfast Culture Night.
answer_text: My Department has not been formally approached about the return of Belfast Culture Night however, I recognise the benefits that Culture Night provided and would be happy to engage with the City Council as they develop any proposals.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the scope; and (ii) the estimated publication date for her Department’s research into the value of the Belfast night-time economy.
answer_text: The planned research will look at the economic value of the night-time economy (focusing primarily on Belfast but also commenting on Derry, Enniskillen and the North overall). It will examine the challenges facing the night-time economy, such as transport provision and perception of safety. Finally, it will also reference best practice and aim to identify recommendations for the development of our night-time economy.		The research is expected to be published in June.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any further action his Department can take to address speeding on the Ballyhenry Road, Glengormley.
answer_text: Ballyhenry Road carries approximately 15,000 vehicles per day and would not be suitable for the provision of vertical traffic calming measures, such as speed humps. I am advised that the road is signed appropriately and other traffic calming measures such as traffic islands and central hatching have been provided along the route.The PSNI have designated the Ballyhenry Road a “Community Concern” site and have been taking enforcement action against drivers exceeding the speed limit as resource permits.One of the best ways to ensure safety on our roads and fulfil our long-term goal of eliminating death and serious injury by 2050, is by changing road user behaviour. As road users we all have a personal responsibility to behave in a way that keeps ourselves and others safe. The sad reality is that if we do not change our own attitudes when we are using the roads, our death toll will continue to increase.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on efforts to reduce the present waiting times for urgent rheumatology appointments in the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: The demand for rheumatology in all Trusts continues to exceed capacity. Regrettably patients are experiencing excessive waits for appointments and treatment which is not the service the Trusts would wish to provide. The Trusts are, however, working very hard to reduce the waiting lists and waiting times for their patients.Trusts are continuing to seek alternative ways to provide outpatient consultations to help reduce waiting times, through initiatives such as virtual or telephone appointments that provide advice and guidance. The Trusts are also seeking to implement a ‘Patient initiated follow up’ (PIFU) service and enhanced triage clinics, to further reform the outpatient service. Each of the Trusts are working with the SPPG and PHA, along with the Voluntary sector, in a regional Rheumatology Group, to help introduce new pathways, to ensure that services are available that are appropriate to patient needs.However, each Trust is also facing significant recruitment challenges.Funding of £76m has been made available in 2024/25 to reduce waiting times for assessments and treatments across a range of specialties.  This funding is being provided to address patients waiting with suspect cancer and time critical (life/limb) treatments only.If the funding bid for £135M had been approved, then part of this funding would have been assigned to address rheumatology outpatient assessment for 2,100 patients waiting over 3 years.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline any progress made on delivering the Northern Ireland Enhanced Investment Zone.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 21107/22-27, to detail (i) why the new guidelines will not be applied retrospectively; (ii) the rationale for omitting this fact when the announcement was made; and (iii) whether he will set a date that enforcement action will commence, in the absence of the developer completing the outstanding works.
answer_text: The proposed enforcement guidance has now been submitted for my consideration.  The proposed guidance will significantly reduce the risk of new developments being added to the current backlog of unadopted developments.  I am now considering the proposed draft guidance and how it should be applied.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when he expects to determine planning application LA01/2016/1328/F.
answer_text: My officials are currently finalising their assessment of the notification received from the council, in relation to application LA01/2016/1328/F. Once this is complete, I will then decide whether or not the application warrants referral to my Department for determination. I can assure you that my Department is working as quickly as possible to complete this process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when work to pedestrianise Hill Street, Belfast, will recommence.
answer_text: Unfortunately, there is no date as yet for the recommencement of work on the pedestrianisation of Hill Street. However, Officials are keeping the position under review and are committed to bringing this issue to a conclusion and as such would hope to follow up the informal consultation feedback in the coming months/ first half of 2025 as staffing permits.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when he will confirm the termination point of the North Belfast route of Belfast Rapid Transit.
answer_text: I had planned  to make a statement on the Belfast Rapid Transit Phase 2 (BRT2) project on 28th January 2025, however this was postponed due to the severity of Storm Eowyn and its consequential impact on citizens in the north.A revised date for announcement will be agreed in the near future, and I would not wish to pre-empt the contents of this statement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail why he did not inform the Assembly or the Executive of the allocation method he selected to distribute £45 million of public funds through the Local Economic Partnerships.
answer_text: The Economy Committee were notified by letter on 27th January 2025 concerning the allocation method. It was put into the public domain later that day. There was no need to inform the Assembly or Executive separately.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the other stakeholder views he considered, as referenced in his statement on 27 January 2025, when selecting the funding allocation for Local Economic Partnerships.
answer_text: DfE facilitated two workshops (15th October and 11th November 2024) where stakeholders were consulted on various funding allocation models and given the opportunity to share their views. 	Attendees at these workshops included representatives from councils, NILGA, Local Enterprise Agencies, Colleges, Universities, Third Sector and Chambers of Commerce.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail how Causeway Coast and Glens Borough Council, ranked 9th in labour productivity, has been awarded the same funding as Lisburn and Castlereagh Borough Council, which is ranked 4th in labour productivity, under the productivity funding formula he selected for Local Economic Partnerships.
answer_text: The Funding Allocation formula was announced publicly on 27th January 2025. Allocation and methodology for each council is detailed in this.  One of the three criteria is focused on productivity performance.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail why the Scheme of Emergency Financial Assistance for flooding does not include damage to items in household garages.
answer_text: The Scheme of Emergency Financial Assistance permits payment to householders as a result of flooding in garages/sheds/utility areas where essential primary utilities are installed (defined as cooker, refrigerator, freezer and washing machine only) and which have been damaged or are unusable.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department will consider the inclusion of (i) Ashgrove Primary School; and (ii) Carnmoney Primary School, Newtownabbey, in any future roll-out of the part-time 20mph speed limit at schools.
answer_text: A review of the initiative introducing part-time 20mph speed limits at schools has been completed and is available on the Department’s website.The review recommends that the Department should provide a third tranche of part-time 20 mph speed limits at schools, however this will be more modest in size and is dependent on the availability of funding.   The review report also concludes that a more targeted approach is needed to ensure that the correct safety intervention is selected and work updating our policy document is underway.  Funding for progressing the next tranche of part-time 20 mph speed limits at schools will be included within budget planning considerations for next financial year.I would also like to see what else can be done to improve attitudes to speeding, not only for road safety purposes, but to make places cleaner and greener and as part of my consideration of the recently published review I have asked my officials to prepare a paper on 20mph speed limits in general giving consideration to  schools, residential areas and places where there is a higher number of those walking, wheeling and cycling.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the implementation of a pay award for Housing Executive staff, as agreed with unions in October 2024.
answer_text: My Department has now received all of the relevant business cases from the Housing Executive. These are being considered as a matter of priority and I have been assured that, when the Department’s considerations are complete, the NIHE will progress matters as soon as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the creation of Invest NI’s Regional Property Programme.
answer_text: I approved Invest NI’s Regional Property Programme in early October 2024.  The Programme is now available on the Invest NI website; please find the link below.https://www.investni.com/sites/default/files/2024-12/invest-northern-ireland-regional-property-programme-2024-2034.pdf

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, following the publication of the Sub-Regional Economic Plan, to detail (i) the membership of each Local Economic Partnership; (ii) the funding provided to each Local Economic Partnership; and (iii) the formula used to allocate this funding.
answer_text: Membership of the Local Economic Partnerships is for to councils to determine. Proposed membership will be reviewed and approved by the Department prior to funding release from April 2025. 		I formally announced funding allocation at Newry Mourne and Down’s inaugrual Local Economic Partnership meeting on 27th January. Details of fund allocation and rationale can be found on the following webpage  Regional balance fund | Department for the Economy.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the funding awarded to the People and Places pilot project in North Belfast.
answer_text: Details of financial support for the People and Places pilot project are still being finalised.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail his Department’s involvement in the People and Places pilot scheme in North Belfast.
answer_text: My officials have been active participants of the People and Places pilot scheme in North Belfast. Officials from my Department have provided representation on the cross-departmental People and Places operational steering group and are members of the Economic Prosperity thematic working group in the North Belfast pilot.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the amount of funding to be allocated to the Local Economic Partnership in his Department's draft Budget 2025-26.
answer_text: I have allocated £5m resource and £10m capital funding to the Local Economic Partnerships in my Department’s draft Budget for 2025-26.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs what financial support his Department is providing to local councils to support an increase in the rates of household recycling.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the bids he made to provide financial support to childcare providers during the Budget 2025-26 setting process.
answer_text: The Department for Education has responsibility for the targeted business support scheme for childcare providers.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 20061-22-27, (i) why the guidelines were not produced by the end of last year as previously indicated; (ii) , given that the guidelines have not been produced, how he concludes that they will not be directly required to achieve adoption, given the passage of time since the completion of the development; and (iii) to detail any assurances he has received from the developer that has led him to conclude that it remains their intention to achieve adoption, given the passage of time since the completion of the development.
answer_text: The proposed enforcement guidance will significantly reduce the risk of new developments being added to the backlog of unadopted developments and represents a step change in how my department manages this work.  I am informed that the new guidance will be submitted to my office for consideration in the coming days.Following its approval, the revised guidance will apply to new developments that commence construction following its introduction and will not be applied retrospectively. Loughshore Manor is still active and under construction by the developer.  The Private Streets engineers dealing with this site have had no indication that the developer will default.  My officials will continue to liaise with the contractor to complete the outstanding works to allow adoption of the roads infrastructure, including obtaining the necessary technical approval for the structure over the railway.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the preschool settings in North Belfast that will become full time providers from September 2025 as a result of his recent funding announcement.
answer_text: Information on all the pre-schools transitioning to full-time provision from September 2025 is published by constituency on the Department website at Minister’s Decisions on Pre-School Settings Requesting Transition from September 2025 | Department of Education The following five pre-school settings in the Belfast North constituency have been approved to transition from September 2025 (i) Carr’s Glen Primary School Nursery Unit; (ii) Currie Primary School Nursery Unit; (iii) Mossgrove Primary School Nursery Unit; (iv) St Vincent de Paul Pre-School Playgroup; and (v) Wishing Well Family Centre Playgroup.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the bids she received from the Minister for the Economy during the Budget 2025-26 setting process to provide financial support to childcare providers.
answer_text: The Department of Education has policy responsibility for childcare. Therefore, as part of the Budget 2025-26 setting process, the Minister for the Economy did not submit any specific bids to provide financial support to childcare providers.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the amount of funding his Department has awarded to Sure Start projects in North Belfast in the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: The tables below show the 2024-25 funding allocations to each of the four Sure Start projects which provide services in the Belfast North constituency, as advised by the Department of Health (DoH) which administers the programme on behalf of my Department. Table A: Sure Start Projects - Funding AllocationsSure Start Project2024-25Abbey£1,054,180Glenbrook£618,097Shankill£760,485SMILE£890,845Total £3,323,607Table B: Sure Start Speech, Language and Communication Allocations*Health and Social Care Trust Area2024-25Belfast £422,973Northern£253,382Regional£138,101Total:£814,456*DoH advises that it is not possible to disaggregate Trust values to individual Sure Start project allocations.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 19382/22-27, and in light of the North Belfast constituency not being contained to Belfast City Council boundaries, to detail (i) completed resurfacing works; and (ii) planned resurfacing works in the North Belfast constituency in the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years. This has had a significant impact on our ability to deliver essential services such as road maintenance. The North Belfast constituency covers both Belfast North and Antrim and Newtownabbey maintenance areas.The 2024/25 resurfacing programme for the Belfast North constituency are detailed in my Department’s annual reports to Belfast City and Antrim and Newtownabbey councils which can be accessed through the following links:Annual Report to Antrim and Newtownabbey Council 2024DfI Roads Eastern Division Annual Report to Belfast City Council Autumn 2024In addition to those detailed in the Belfast City Council report, further resurfacing schemes on the New Lodge Road and Ben Madigan Park South are also planned for delivery in 2024/25.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any proposal contained in his proposed Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill that he intends to introduce through the use of (i) departmental guidance; and (ii) secondary legislation.
answer_text: My Department is working to finalise the analysis of responses to my Good Jobs Employment Rights consultation. This analysis will inform policy decisions, which will include a consideration of the most appropriate legislative vehicle.
