Where there is scope to do so, I will consider whether policy outcomes could best be delivered through primary legislation, a supporting programme of secondary legislation or guidance.  The analysis of consultation responses is nearing completion and relevant policy proposals will be brought to the Executive for agreement in due course.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail the amount of funding that will be made available to (i) Antrim and Newtownabbey Borough Council; and (ii) Belfast City Council under the Ending Violence Against Women and Girls Local Change Fund.
answer_text: Antrim and Newtownabbey Borough Council will receive total funding of £160,000. This includes allocations under both the Momentum Fund 2024/25 (£50,000) and the Local Change Fund for 2025/26 (£110,000).Belfast City Council will receive total funding of £365,000. This includes allocations under both the Momentum Fund 2024/25 (£110,000) and the Local Change Fund for 2025/26 (£255,000).

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) when the evaluation of the Building Successful Communities Pilot will be completed; and (ii) any consideration he has given to the extension of the scheme.
answer_text: The Building Successful Communities (BSC) Post Programme Review report is being finalised and I expect it to be presented to me before the end of this financial year.  The purpose of the Review is to evaluate the BSC Pilot against the original objectives and help inform any future interventions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on (i) the work of the GP Access Working Group; and (ii) the implementation of its findings to improve service provision and availability of appointments.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure why his Department was unable to bring forward a bid in the January Monitoring Round to utilise Financial Transactions Capital funding.
answer_text: Financial Transactions Capital (FTC) must be used to provide a loan to or make an equity investment in a private sector entity. To date, no opportunities have been identified within infrastructure that meet this requirement. Therefore, my Department was not able to bring forward a bid in January Monitoring to utilise FTC funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) for an update on the vesting of derelict properties and land by the Housing Executive at Fortwilliam Parade, Belfast; (ii) for an estimated date for demolition of the properties; and (iii) to detail the housing association that has been appointed to rebuild these homes.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:(i) “An update on the vesting of derelict properties and land by the Housing Executive at Fortwilliam Parade, Belfast; The properties and land at 53-57 Fortwilliam Parade were vested by the Housing Executive in August 2024.  (ii) Estimated date for demolition of the properties;The Housing Executive is currently in the process of procuring a contractor to demolish the properties. It is estimated that demolition will take place by August/September 2025.(iii) to detail the housing association that has been appointed to rebuild these homes. To date a Housing Association has not yet been appointed to redevelop the site”.  For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Northern Ireland Housing Executive directly at  Public.Affairsunit@nihe.gov.uk

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy why his Department was unable to bring forward a bid in the January Monitoring Round to utilise Financial Transactions Capital funding.
answer_text: There were no additional requirements within my Department, at January Monitoring, for Financial Transaction Capital (FTC) therefore no bid for additional FTC was submitted.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance why her Department was unable to bring forward a bid in the January Monitoring Round to utilise Financial Transactions Capital funding.
answer_text: Financial Transactions Capital (FTC) can only be used to provide loans to or equity investment in the private sector. The FTC budget is ring-fenced for this purpose and cannot be used for anything else.  Therefore there are limited opportunities within my Department to utilise Financial Transactions Capital.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when the Housing Executive's Group Repair Scheme works will be carried out at Gainsborough Drive, Belfast.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“The Housing Executive can confirm that work to undertake Group Repair Scheme based works at Gainsborough Drive, Belfast is progressing.  The necessary statutory approvals from Building Control have been requested and the contractor procurement process has commenced.  We anticipate that the contractor may commence work on site during summer 2025, however this is subject to the successful conclusion of the procurement process and budget.  Housing Executive has completed consultation apart from three households. Numerous attempts have been made in writing and by calling in person at the property.  We will continue to make contact during February.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Housing Executive directly.  Making direct contact with them will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any consideration he is giving to introducing a two-year backstop in respect of historic claims for holiday pay in his proposed Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill.
answer_text: This issue did not form part of my Good Jobs consultation. However, I am aware that it has been raised by stakeholders during our pre-consultation and consultation engagement.As such I have considered the issue and my position remains aligned with the two previous Ministers, Stephen Farry and Diane Dodds, who also considered this matter and concluded that it would not be appropriate to introduce a back stop akin to that introduced in Britain.The previous Minister wrote to Executive Colleagues in 2020 suggesting that employers should take immediate action to meet their obligation to address backdated holiday pay claims in a fair and equitable manner. That remains the advice to any employer.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when Small and Micro Business Impact Tests will be published for Theme C of his proposed Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill.
answer_text: A number of impact assessments were conducted prior to the launch of the consultation, including a Small and Micro Business Impact Test (SAMBIT) where firm policy proposals were put forward by the Department that could be tested. Theme C did not form part of this initial assessment. 		The Department invited views on the content of that impact test during the consultation, with comments particularly welcomed in respect of any potential mitigations that could be provided for small and micro businesses – for example, exemptions, guidance, training or advice.  		Further impact assessments, including small and micro business tests, will be undertaken and published once the consultation analysis has concluded and final policy decisions have been made. The policy proposals adopted for Theme C will form part of this.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when he will bring his Tourism Vision and Action Plan to the Executive.
answer_text: My Tourism Vision and Acton Plan sets out the actions that my Department, Tourism NI and Tourism Ireland will take over the next decade to deliver my strategic goal for the sector.  That being the case, I do not intend to bring the Plan to the Executive.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of the response rate to the consultation on his proposed Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill.
answer_text: My good jobs employment rights consultation prompted 192 complex and considered responses, from a range of stakeholders including individuals, businesses, trade unions, and organisations representing employers and workers, including all the key representative bodies. I would like to express my gratitude for all those who have taken the time to respond and engage with my Department on the matter so far. Although the consultation period has now formally closed, engagement nevertheless continues. I look forward to working with stakeholders on shaping any necessary updates to our employment law framework.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 20254/22/27, whether he is giving consideration to the creation of further revenue and capital funding streams to support the programme.
answer_text: The upcoming 250th anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence in 2026 represents a unique opportunity for us to celebrate and raise awareness of the contribution and influence that the early Ulster-Scots migrants made to the traditions and values of the United States of America.  The Northern Ireland Cultural Working Group is actively engaged in developing cultural related initiatives in the States and in Northern Ireland and exploring further collaborative opportunities. There are a number of exciting projects in the pipeline being developed by several organisations which my department funds including the Ulster-Scots Agency’s work in developing Presidential Homesteads and National Museums bringing forward plans for the Ulster American Folk Park. The Public Record Office is also working in partnership with key cultural organisations in the United States including the National Archives and the Library of Congress. It is envisaged that funding for new initiatives will be met from existing budget baselines. Given the significance of the anniversary to Northern Ireland I will also wish to explore the potential of securing additional resources in my Department’s budget for 2025/26 to support initiatives at local level.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the potential breaches of planning conditions his Department is currently investigating surrounding the development of Belfast Grand Central Station; and (ii) the rationale for approval being given by his Department to close Durham Street in advance of an agreed acceptable Transport Management Plan.
answer_text: (i) My department are checking for compliance with all the conditions associated with planning permission LA04/2017/1388/F. In the context of Durham Street and the Boyne Bridge, there were several conditions pertaining to commencement of works on the public highway that required submissions and approval of those submissions. These have now been approved and details are available on the Planning Portal. (ii) My department processed a Temporary Traffic Regulation to close Durham Street to permit Translink to complete the work detailed in the layout agreed in their planning permission. The Transport Management Plan includes details of diversionary routes, advanced signing and coning and was considered acceptable by my department.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the delivery of the Enhanced Investment Zone for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Departmental officials are continuing to liaise with the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government (MHCLG) to co-design the policy for the North of Ireland and to confirm the governance arrangements. Officials are working at pace towards an indicative timeline for all gates to be completed by Autumn 2025. MHCLG are working closely with our officials to  expediate the gate process to achieve this timeline.Once the gate steps are completed, officials will move into delivery of this 10 year programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, following the recent Pay & Grading Review, whether his Department will consider further re-evaluation of the pay structure for Foundation Stage Classroom Assistants whose pay award is lower than those in other similar classroom-based support roles.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) operates a well-established job evaluation process against which all job descriptions are assessed to determine their grade and associated pay points. All Classroom Assistant Job Descriptions have been evaluated under this process and graded accordingly. The EA are currently undertaking a review of the Classroom Assistant Employment model which will include an assessment of the roles required to support children in the classroom. It is anticipated that an analysis of roles, responsibilities and workload will be part of this review to ensure that children and young people are getting the best possible support.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to explain the projected overspends with his departmental budget in the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: As at December my Department forecast an overcommitment of £7.4 million in Resource DEL (RDEL) and a shortfall of £36.7 million in Ringfenced RDEL depreciation and impairment.  My Department has been working to reduce this overcommitment, and this, coupled with the £2.3 million additional RDEL allocated as part of January Monitoring will result in a balanced budget.  The bid to address the £36.7 million shortfall in Ringfenced RDEL was fully met by the Executive in January Monitoring.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) for his assessment of the success of Translink's Nightmovers service during the Christmas period; and (ii) to detail any action he is now taking to ensure this becomes a year round service.
answer_text: The data from this year’s Nightmovers service is currently being evaluated. The data will help inform an outline business case for any expansion of the service beyond the festive period.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the spend of the North and West Belfast Home Care Team based on the Shankill Road, Belfast, in each of the last five years.

To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of registered service users covered by the North and West Belfast Home Care Team based on the Shankill Road, Belfast, in each of the last five years.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 19987/22-27, when the investigations will be completed.
answer_text: NI Water has advised that the recurring flooding at the Shore Road and Mill Road junction is linked to sewerage network capacity issues during times of heavy rainfall.  Unfortunately, there are no easy solutions for addressing the complex drainage issues in this area. The detailed investigations, that are ongoing, will take time to complete, however these are needed to inform the development of a suitable long-term solution.  NI Water, along with officials from my Department will continue to work together to expedite these investigations and develop a solution that will help reduce the risk of flooding at this location. I have asked my officials to keep you updated on the progress of these investigations.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 19913/22-27, for a breakdown of how the cost of transport provided by the Education Authority has increased by over 20 per cent in the last two financial years.
answer_text: The Education Authority (EA) has advised that the costs charged to schools for its non-statutory transport hire service had remained largely unchanged for a number of years and were not reflective of the associated running costs, nor the increase in those costs in recent years. The increased running cost in the last two financial years is mainly driven by annual pay awards, fuel prices and maintenance of an aging bus fleet.I recognise the educational value of the activities this transport facilitates, and it is regrettable that the cost of transport has risen in recent years, however this service is operated by the EA on a non-profit basis to keep the costs incurred by schools to a minimum.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he is aware of concerns surrounding the performance of management of the North and West Belfast Home Care Team based on the Shankill Road, Belfast.
To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any legislative changes he is seeking to the Employment Rights Bill in advance of progressing a Legislative Consent Motion.
answer_text: I have asked that the Employment Rights Bill be amended to ensure that any future change in the remit of the Fair Work Agency that could be proposed by the Secretary of State, that involves legislation devolved to the North of Ireland, will require the consent of the relevant Department(s) in order to proceed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he has considered capping the amount of funding an organisation can receive from the (i) Irish Language Broadcast Fund; and (ii) Ulster-Scots Broadcast Fund.
answer_text: While the Ulster-Scots Broadcast Fund is administered in the North by NI Screen, broadcasting is a reserved matter under the Northern Ireland Act 1998 and policy responsibility for the Irish Language and Ulster Scots Broadcast Funds sits with the Department for Digital, Culture, Media and Sport.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the current budget allocated to the (i) Irish Language Broadcast Fund; and (ii) Ulster-Scots Broadcast Fund.
answer_text: Funding for the Irish Language and Ulster-Scots Broadcast Funds is provided by the Department for Digital, Culture, Media and Sport via the British Film Institute, and is administered by NI Screen. The Irish Language Broadcast Fund also receives funding from the Irish Government via the Department of Tourism, Culture, Arts, Gaeltacht, Sports and Media. Funding for 2024/25 is provided in the table below.	FUND	2024/25 Budget		Source	Irish Language Broadcast Fund	£3.2m	Department for Digital, Culture, Media and Sport 	Irish Language Broadcast Fund	€1.0m	Department of Tourism, Culture, Arts, Gaeltacht, Sports and Media	Ulster-Scots Broadcast Fund	£1.1m 	Department for Digital, Culture, Media and Sport

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any consideration he has given to broadening the criteria of the Ulster-Scots Broadcast Fund to include the promotion of the Ulster/British tradition.
answer_text: While the Ulster-Scots Broadcast Fund is administered in the North by NI Screen, broadcasting is a reserved matter under the Northern Ireland Act 1998 and policy responsibility for the Fund sits with the Department for Digital, Culture, Media and Sport.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any discussions he has had with Northern Ireland Screen to ensure they support projects representing all cultural and identities in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I met, most recently, with Northern Ireland Screen on 5 February to discuss the work they are doing through the important package of funding provided by my Department to support a range of cultural, education and outreach activities relating to film and the screen industry, including film festivals and cultural cinema. During this meeting I highlighted the importance of supporting projects representing all cultural and identities in Northern Ireland.I was impressed by the extent to which NI Screen focusses on ensuring that equality of opportunity is central to its work through these programmes.  It has been particularly successful in opening doors for talented people who come from disadvantaged or marginalised groups, where, for example, at least 70% of activity through the creative learning centres programme takes place in areas of disadvantage and data on those securing employment through its CINE programme shows 46% having a disability and 72% coming from what might be regarded as lower socio-economic backgrounds.While my Department does not provide funding for commercial film and television production I was pleased to hear of the work being done through its work in relation to the Irish Language Broadcast and Ulster-Scots Broadcast Funds.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how his Department intends to support the marking of the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence.
answer_text: I established the United States - Northern Ireland Cultural Working Group, to further opportunities for collaboration around the semiquincentennial (250th) anniversary. The Group comprises key agencies here and in the United States and it has been working to develop a programme of cultural engagement both in the United States and in Northern Ireland.This was a key aspect of my discussions with institutions on my recent visit to Washington DC and my department will host a series of public lectures, talks, conferences, exchanges of documents, exhibitions, and outreach in Northern Ireland and the United States to mark the 250th anniversary.PRONI houses one of the largest collections of emigrant letters and documents in the world and the programme includes the launch of a special publication by PRONI that highlights its Ulster American Treasures. This will form part of a broader programme of outreach working in partnership with key institutions including the National Archives in Washington DC.  Organisations funded by my Department continue to advance plans to mark the 250th anniversary.  These include the Ulster-Scots Agency’s work in developing Presidential Homesteads and National Museums bringing forward plans for the Ulster American Folk Park.The programme will also promote the links that Historic Royal Palace/Hillsborough Castle enjoys with Founding Father Benjamin Franklin, and there is the opportunity to loan items and documents from US institutions.  The contribution and influence that the early Ulster-Scots migrants made to the traditions and values of the United States of America is significant. The semiquincentennial anniversary provides a unique opportunity to promote that contribution and shared heritage. It was clear from the organisations that I met in Washington that they are excited to cooperate with Northern Ireland partners in celebrating this historic milestone.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail the reasons for the delay in introducing the Dilapidation Bill.
answer_text: The process of obtaining confirmation from legal advisers that the Bill is within the legislative competence of the Assembly has taken longer than had originally been anticipated.As soon as this has been received, I will bring the Bill to the Executive for approval to have it introduced to the Assembly so that we can scrutinise the legislation and see the benefits for communities of the Assembly passing it.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when his Department will recommence work on the pedestrianisation of Hill Street, Belfast.
answer_text: Unfortunately, there is no date as yet for the recommencement of work on the pedestrianisation of Hill Street. However, Officials are keeping the position under review and are committed to bringing this issue to a conclusion and as such would hope to follow up the informal consultation feedback in the coming months/ first half of 2025 as staffing permits.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the introduction of 20mph speed limit zones in residential areas.
answer_text: One of the best ways to ensure safety on our roads and fulfil our long-term goal of eliminating death and serious injury by 2050, is by changing road user behaviour. As road users we all have a personal responsibility to behave in a way that keeps ourselves and others safe. The sad reality is that if we do not change our own attitudes, particularly to speed when we are using the roads, our death toll will continue to increase. As part of my consideration of the recently published review of the part-time 20mph speed limit at schools initiative, I asked my officials to prepare me a paper on the use of 20mph speed limits. This work is in progress and includes the consideration of speed limits in residential areas.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail (i) the number of applications received to date; (ii) the number of awards made to date; and (iii) the total value of all awards made, under the Troubles Permanent Disablement Payment Scheme.
answer_text: As of 6 January 2025, there has been a total of (i) 10,534 applications received with (ii) 2,398 panel determinations made, resulting in 1,556 recommended for payment with 1,167 awards made to date and (iii) more than £73.8 million paid by the Victims’ Payments Board since the Victims’ Payments Scheme opened for applications on 31 August 2021.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any engagement his Department has had with the car insurance industry in relation to the introduction of multi-annual policies for young drivers.
answer_text: I am aware of the rising cost of vehicle insurance and the challenges this is causing for our road users, particularly young drivers. My Department works closely with the Insurance Industry, however, we have no knowledge of the intention to introduce insurance products called ‘multi-annual’ car insurance policies for young drivers.Officials have liaised with the Motor Insurers Bureau and they are also not aware of an existing insurance product called ‘multi-annual’, or the planned introduction of such a product. Multi-CAR policies exist, but they are not targeted at young drivers.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether her Department is currently investigating the level of compliance with the requirement that solicitor practices in Northern Ireland must be owned by solicitors registered in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My Department is currently considering correspondence on this issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, in light of the Chancellor’s Autumn Budget 2024, to detail any consideration she has given to increasing solicitor fees.
answer_text: I announced plans to implement an uplift in fees for publicly funded advice and representation in civil and family and criminal proceedings in my statement to the Assembly in December. Work is continuing at pace and I expect to consult on proposed fee changes in coming weeks. Subject to that consultation, and funding approval, it is anticipated fee changes will be implemented in the Spring.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the engagement his Department will have with HM Government in relation to the Motor Insurance Taskforce.
answer_text: The initial meeting of the Motor Insurance Taskforce took place during week commencing 25th November 2024. The Minister of Finance, Dr Caoimhe Archibald MLA attends this taskforce. Input to this meeting was provided by my Department and ongoing engagement will continue in the coming months.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, pursuant to AQW 19214/22-27, to detail the amount of funding being awarded to each school in the North Belfast constituency.
answer_text: The level of funding received will be dependent on pupil numbers and details of the scheme will be provided to schools and settings as soon as possible, however this must be preceded by relevant due diligence processes around use of public money.  The Department is also working alongside the Education Authority to develop guidance to ensure there is clear advice to assist schools in utilising the funding and that there are arrangements in place to measure the impact.  To ensure transparency, the allocation provided to each setting and the constituency area will be published.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 19632/22-27, to provide a cost breakdown of the total spend.
answer_text: As detailed in AQW 19632/22-27, the total cost of producing the Taskforce’s Report and Action Plan, launched on 16 December at Magee, amounted to £18,070. As this figure includes the combined costs of only two providers, to provide further detail is considered Commercial in Confidence.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 18258/22-27, (i) for an update on the publication of the guidance; and (ii) how these new guidelines will be used to assist with the outstanding works required to the roads at Loughshore Manor, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: My officials are in the final stages of drafting the Guidance on Private Streets enforcement and I expect to receive it for my consideration in the coming weeks.Loughshore Manor is under construction and will be offered for adoption by the developer in accordance with the provisions contained within the Private Streets (NI) Order 1980. It remains the responsibility and the intention of the developer to achieve adoption. In the case of the Loughshore Manor development, the new guidelines will not be directly required at present.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 19733/22-27, to detail any steps he will take to consult with stakeholders in advance of making a decision on the business case.
answer_text: Consultation with stakeholders has been undertaken by the Northern Ireland Housing Executive in preparation of their Business Case relating to Abbotscoole House. The Housing Executive’s Business Case will be given due consideration and scrutiny before any decision is made.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 19480/22-27, when consideration of the feasibility study will be completed.
answer_text: My officials have assessed the options to repair or replace the steps identified within the feasibility study and have selected two options for further consideration. Officials will now carry out a detailed assessment of the selected options, including ground investigations and a structural assessment of the supporting retaining wall.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 19482/22-27, whether a site meeting date has now been set.
answer_text: I can advise that officials carried out an assessment in the vicinity of Lowwood Primary School on the 13 January 2025 which included consultation with the Principal and School Crossing Patrol person.The assessment determined that the area would benefit from ‘School Keep Clear’ road markings at the school entrance and corner restrictions at the junction of Sheringhurst Park.Officials will now complete the necessary procedures to have the road markings installed as soon as resources permit.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) to detail the number of places available in each yearly intake for the Centre for Inclusive Learning at Belfast Metropolitan College; and (ii) whether his Department is considering increasing funding for places given the demand.
answer_text: My officials have been advised by Belfast Metropolitan College that in the current academic year 2024/25, it had 72 full time places and 156 part time places available for new applicants at its Centre for Inclusive Learning. There are currently 72 full time, and 156 part time students enrolled.For the 2025-26 academic year, the College plans to increase full time course provision for new applicants to 96 places. Part time provision will remain at 156 places.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs when he will answer AQW 18408/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 18408/22-27 was answered on 7 January 2025.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 18730/22-27, for an update on the work to find a solution.
answer_text: NI Water, along with officials from my Department are continuing to carry out detailed investigations, to fully understand the complex interactions between the different drainage systems in the area that could be contributing to the flooding.  These investigations will take time and will inform the development of a sustainable long-term solution.  Any projects that are brought forward will be subject to the necessary funding and approvals being secured.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when they will answer AQW 18186/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 18186/22-27 was answered on 5 March 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer (i) AQW 14282/22-27; (ii) AQW 14283/22-27; and (iii) AQW 14911/22-27.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department will consider adding a right-turn light to the traffic lights on the Hillview Road, Belfast, for vehicles travelling onto the Crumlin Road, to improve traffic flow.
answer_text: I can advise that officials are not aware of any difficulties or delays for right turning traffic from Hillview Road onto the Crumlin Road. Vehicles can complete their manoeuvre within one cycle of the traffic signals either in gaps in oncoming traffic or at the end of their green signal time.  An examination of the recorded collision history at this junction does not suggest it was unsafe location for vehicles to turn right.To provide an overlap (filter) facility would add delays for all users at the junction and can result in safety issues for oncoming traffic from the road opposite. As such my Department has no plans, at present, to provide an overlap facility at this junction.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education, in light of Education Authority bus hire price increases, whether his Department will consider additional financial support to primary schools for extra-curricular activities outside of school premises.
answer_text: All grant-aided schools are funded under the Common Funding Scheme (CFS).  The Common Funding Formula (CFF) is the Department’s mechanism for allocating the Aggregated Schools’ Budget (ASB) to schools funded through Local Management of Schools arrangements.  One of the five key principles of the scheme is that the formula should support schools in delivering the curriculum.My Department does not provide additional funding to schools for travel associated with extra-curricular activities.  This is a matter for individual schools to organise and fund, either via the school’s delegated budget (if this can be justified) or through voluntary contributions or fundraising events. The education sector is facing extensive unavoidable cost pressures and rising service demands associated with delivering statutory and policy obligations.  As such, my priority is to provide stability and take every action possible to protect education funding and those frontline services within my Department’s statutory remit.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for his assessment of the impact of recent price increases for Education Authority bus hire upon schools' affordability to carry out extra-curricular activities, such as travelling to swimming lessons.
answer_text: The non-statutory transport hire service provided by the Education Authority (EA) to schools is operated on a non-profit basis.  The price charged to schools is reflective of the associated running costs.  The EA has advised that transport costs have increased by 20% over the last two financial years.Schools can hire buses from private operators, in line with agreed procurement arrangements, if they wish. However, the EA has advised that its pricing model remains competitive with external providers.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) to detail the number of places currently available in each yearly intake for the Independence for Life and Work Programme at Belfast Metropolitan College; and (ii) whether his Department is considering increasing funding for places, given demand for the course.
answer_text: My officials have been advised by Belfast Metropolitan College that in the current academic year 2024/25, it had 24 places available for new applicants on two full time course cohorts for its Independence for Life and Work Entry Level programme. There are currently 24 students enrolled. For the 2025-26 academic year, the College plans to increase this provision by 50%, providing 36 places for new applicants on three full time course cohorts.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister when a letter of offer will be issued for the building of the new Westland Community Centre, North Belfast.
answer_text: Following on from the announcement that funding of £1.6 million has been allocated for the Westland Community Centre, a letter of offer for the project will issue when processes have completed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the creation of an air route development fund.
answer_text: Following confirmation of the reduced allocated 2023/24 budget for the Department, my Department took the difficult decision that work on an air route development fund should not proceed as originally planned. My Department subsequently commissioned research from York Aviation which was published in June 2024. The research identified a set of strategic routes which will help drive inbound tourism and business growth, and to find innovative ways and policy levers to enhance our air connectivity,My officials provided written and oral briefing to the Economy Committee on 6 November of the findings and recommendations of the York Aviation Report and an update on progress towards developing an aviation policy. It is anticipated that we will go out to public consultation on the policy in Spring 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he has now received a business case for the Housing Executive in relation to the future of Abbotscoole House, Rathcoole.
answer_text: My Department has now received a business case from the Housing Executive relating to Abbotscoole House for consideration.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the specific steps he is taking to deliver the Energy Strategy in a way that will not have a detrimental impact on investment in renewable energy in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The Executive’s Energy Strategy has set the pathway to achieve self-sufficiency in affordable renewable energy. To achieve this my department has taken steps to ensure investment in renewable energy remains a priority. This includes: setting up a new renewable electricity support scheme;delivering the Offshore Renewable Energy Action Plan (OREAP);facilitating a transition to green gas use; 	leading a geothermal energy demonstrator project; 	publishing a call for evidence (CfE) on the use of liquid biofuels; and, InvestNI are delivering a Net Zero Accelerator Fund (NZAF).

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) whether she is aware of support available in other parts of the UK for sports clubs impacted by recent storms; and (ii) whether she will be introducing a similar scheme in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: As Minister with responsibility for sport this question has been transferred to me for answer.I am aware that support has been provided, through Sport England, to sports clubs that have been impacted by recent storms in England. I have asked Sport NI to consider a similar approach for Northern Ireland.As this issue is an operational matter for Sport NI I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“Sport England’s Movement Fund was launched in April 2024, and offers crowdfunding pledges, grants and resources to improve physical activity opportunities for people and communities. Sport England highlighted that this fund was open to applications and that organisations experiencing storm or flood damage could apply for funding, however https://www.sportengland.org/funding-and-campaigns/our-funding/apply-funding eligibility criteria would still apply.Sport NI’s Creating Opportunities Crowdfunder small grants programme is available to local clubs, including those that have been affected by recent flooding. Clubs can apply for funding for any new and/or additional participation projects they wish to run within their club. The programme offers up to 50% match-funding support to a maximum of £5,000 from Sport NI delivered through the Crowdfunder platform. The eligible costs include (but are not exclusive to) coaching costs, venue hire, workshop costs, equipment and capital costs required to deliver their proposed participation project.A Crowdfunder participation project is a programme designed to create opportunities to ‘level the playing field’, so that accessibility and inclusion is felt and experienced by those who have been traditionally excluded from not only participating in sport but playing an active role in coaching, officiating, and leading as part of a board”. In addition, I have asked my officials to engage with the Department for Economy regarding its Enhanced Flood Support Scheme and to explore how sports clubs could be included in the eligibility criteria going forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on his engagement with the hospitality sector.
answer_text: The hospitality sector is an important part of our overall economy and my ongoing engagement reflects this. I recently met with the Northern Ireland Food-to-Go Association to understand the issues facing its members. I also met with the board of Food NI earlier this month to hear about their plans for a ‘Regenerative Year of Food and Drink’ in 2026. Along with the Minister of Finance, I will be meeting with Hospitality Ulster in January.I established a Tourism Partnership Board (TPB) to support my Department in the co-design and development of a Tourism Action Plan to lay out a vision for the sustainable and inclusive growth of our valuable tourism sector. I opened the first meeting of the newly established TPB in September this year.  To ensure a collaborative co-design approach, membership of the TPB consists of representatives from both the tourism industry and the hospitality sector, alongside Executive Departments and local government.My officials have also engaged recently with Hospitality Ulster, the Belfast Night Czar, the Music Venue Trust and have representation on Belfast’s Purple Flag Committee. My officials have also agreed to provide representation to Hospitality Ulster’s Night-Time Economy Commission.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health how he intends to work with the Minister for Communities to ensure Housing First for Youth is available in every Health and Social Care Trust area to all young people leaving care and at risk of homelessness.
answer_text: The development of Housing First has been led by a Task and Finish Group which has been chaired by the Department for Communities.  The Department of Health has also been represented on a Research Advisory Group on the feasibility study of Housing First in Northern Ireland which will help inform the Department for Communities engagement with the Department of Health and Department of Justice in any future expansion of Housing First.  The Department of Health will work with the Department for Communities as the lead department for expansion for Housing First in the future development of Housing First generally and Housing First for Youth specifically, as and when any further input is identified by the Department for Communities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he is considering the creation of a renewable energy taskforce.
answer_text: One of my four critical objectives for the Economy is to reduce carbon emissions; generating sufficient renewable electricity is key to facilitate this change.We must build on our previous success in renewables development and the Executive needs to take a collaborative, whole system approach to deliver the 80% by 2030 target.Whilst my Department has legislative responsibility for the target, collaboration across departments and with wider stakeholders, including industry, will be essential to the successful delivery of this target. 	My Department also established the Offshore Renewable Energy Action Plan (OREAP) Steering Group in April 2022. This group brings together key delivery partners to develop and deliver the action plan for offshore renewable energy in the north.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the total cost of the Ulster University Magee Taskforce report.
answer_text: Ulster University Magee Expansion Taskforce members have received no renumeration for their role on the Taskforce.  The total cost for producing the Taskforce’s Report and Action Plan, launched 16 December at Magee, was £18,070.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when he will provide an update on the proposed North Belfast route for the Belfast Rapid Transport Phase 2.
answer_text: Work on the feasibility of extending to Glengormley and Carryduff has been finalised and I commissioned work on the delivery options that could be implemented, utilising the £35m funding envelope from the Belfast Region City Deal. The Outline Business Case is being finalised in line with these feasibility reports and delivery options. When my officials have concluded this work, I will be in a position to make a decision.I welcome the £35m contribution from Belfast Region City Deal and recognise that this significant investment will bring enhancements to the city centre and wider public transport network as we seek to promote sustainable travel options and improve public transport journey time reliability.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 16908/22-27, (i) when officials carried out the site visit; and (ii) to detail any proposed actions that came from the site visit.
answer_text: I am advised that the department is not aware of any illegal parking or safety issues within close proximity to the school and that standard fixed school warning signs and appropriate traffic calming measures are in place on both approaches to Sheringhurst Park.A site visit in this area will be conducted by the end of January 2025.One of the best ways to ensure safety on our roads and fulfil our long-term goal of eliminating death and serious injury by 2050, is by changing road user behaviour. As road users we all have a personal responsibility to behave in a way that keeps ourselves and others safe.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of the creation of a Northern Ireland Boxing Association.
answer_text: I would in principle support the creation of a Northern Ireland-specific association for amateur boxing, as the Assembly did on 12 November 2012.		The Recognition Policy is operated by the five Sports Councils of the United Kingdom, and my Department has no influence over the recognition of National Governing Bodies. The policy outlines only one National Governing Body for Sport in Northern Ireland, currently this is the Ulster Provincial Boxing Council of the Irish Athletic Boxing Association for boxing in Northern Ireland.		The recognition policy is available on the Sport NI website at https://www.sportni.net/performance/governing-bodies/recognition-of-governing-bodies/. However, I can advise that the process is currently closed to new applications whilst the UK Sports Councils review the policy and process with an aim to have it completed by summer 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when the pedestrian steps closed by his Department at Deacon Street, Belfast, will be repaired and reopened.
answer_text: Due to concerns for pedestrian safety, my officials closed the steps at Deacon Street, Belfast on 18th November 2024.A feasibility study has been prepared, identifying options for repair or replacement and is currently under consideration.  A ground investigation and structural assessment will need to be conducted prior to any option being implemented. This work will be dependent on available funding.During the period of closure an alternative route is available, via Gainsborough Drive, North Queen Street and Deacon Street

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the (i) number; and (ii) value of successful applications to the the Northern Ireland Childcare Subsidy Scheme in the North Belfast constituency.
answer_text: At 15 November 2024, 429 children in the North Belfast constituency were registered and approved by their childcare provider for the Northern Ireland Childcare Subsidy Scheme (NICCS).  Since the scheme’s launch, approximately £98,400 has been claimed for children living in this constituency.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the budget allocated to the delivery of the Social Enterprise Action Plan.
answer_text: The Social Enterprise Action Plan (SEAP) aims to increase the commercial capacity of social enterprises and, in turn, strengthen and grow the sector across the North. The five objectives set out in the SEAP are Awareness of the Sector, Leadership and Development, Investment Support, Procurement and Awareness of Support. As this activity will be carried out across a range of organisations and will involve changes to existing activity to be more inclusive of social enterprises as well as new activity, it is not yet possible to detail the budget involved.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the rationale for her Department's decision not to give official recognition to K9 Search and Rescue.
answer_text: ANSWER The decision to decline the application from K9 Search and Rescue Northern Ireland for membership in the Northern Ireland Search and Rescue Practitioners’ Group was based on an assessment against the agreed criteria set out in the application process.  Based on this process and an assessment of current service provisions, it was determined that there are already established canine search and rescue services in place, and no service gap in this area was identified that would necessitate additional resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the delivery of the Enhanced Investment Zone for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Following confirmation from the British Government that the £150m for the Enhanced Investment Zone (EIZ) Programme in the North will be made available, my Department is working with the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government to agree the steps and timescales to secure approval for release of the funding. The EIZ is a 10 year programme, however it is anticipated that initial funding will be released during the 2025/26 financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance when she will present a draft Budget for 2025-26 to the Executive.
answer_text: I refer the Member to the answer provided for AQW 19519-22-27

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any discussions she has had with HM Treasury in relation to National Insurance Employers' Contributions Relief for businesses operating as part of the Enhanced Economic Zone for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My Department continues to work with officials in the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government (MHCLG) on development of a delivery plan to implement the programme. 	Development of the Enhanced Investment Zone (EIZ) is a co-design process and MHCLG will engage with HM Treasury when required. Tax breaks would be deducted from the £150m budget and would add considerably to the complexity and time involved in establishing the EIZ.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to provide a copy of his Department's resurfacing plans in the North Belfast constituency for 2024-25.
answer_text: The 2024/25 resurfacing programme for the Belfast North area was outlined in the Department’s Annual Report to Belfast City Council on 27 November 2024.  Subsequent to the meeting the Report was published on the Department’s website, issued to local MPs and MLAs in the Council area, and to the Assembly library.DfI Roads Eastern Division Annual Report to Belfast City Council Autumn 2024

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when responsibility for delivering the High Street Task Force will transfer to his Department.
answer_text: The Executive Office has written to all Ministers to ask them to take forward the actions that their Departments are responsible for in the High Street Task Force Report. I am taking forward recommendation 8 on the High Street Task Force Report: ‘Fair Work on Our High Streets’. Delivering on this recommendation aligns with my economic priority on Good Jobs.Ongoing work within my Department on the development of a new Employment Rights Bill, promotion of greater adoption of the Real Living Wage, and collaboration with the Labour Relations Agency to create a Good Employment Charter will serve to support implementation of the recommendation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of the comments made by the Federation of Small Businesses during an oral evidence to the Committee for the Economy on 4 December 2024, that the lowering of corporation tax rate in Northern Ireland would have a transformative impact on our economy.
answer_text: Tax is an important factor that companies consider when making investment decisions. However, the British Government's block grant adjustment mechanism, combined with its underfunding of public services, means that the devolution of Corporation Tax is currently unaffordable. These matters are being taking forward by the Finance Minister as part of discussions on a new Fiscal Framework.			In the meantime, it is my intention to create an attractive investment proposition for firms by improving skills and ensuring that companies can take full advantage of the dual market access opportunities that exist through the Windsor framework.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any work her Department is undertaking to support the lowering of Corporation Tax in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: I refer the Member to the answer to AQW 18801/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail what support package he is pursuing for the hospitality sector in the forthcoming budget.
answer_text: I have established a Tourism Partnership Board (TPB) whose core purpose is to develop and oversee a final Tourism Action Plan that will be co-designed and co-delivered with the industry. The TPB includes representatives from the hospitality sector to ensure hospitality issues are also addressed.		Tourism NI provides a suite of advisory supports to help tourism and hospitality businesses reduce costs, improve productivity and be more sustainable, including Leaner and Greener, and Innovate Tourism.		Tourism NI’s high profile and high impact marketing campaigns continue to drive awareness of what the north has to offer, with Tourism NI’s Spring 2024 campaign alone generating £94.74 million in visitor spend. Tourism NI also leads on significant marketing to the South, which in 2023 accounted for 1.3 million trips, 3 million nights and £268 million visitor spend, representing a significant increase from 2022. This investment in marketing drives business to many hospitality businesses, at no cost to them.		My Department has also been actively engaging with Hospitality Ulster and other stakeholders in the hospitality industry to understand the issues facing the sector and how the Department can assist. The industry has concerns around VAT and business rates and I have been liaising with the Minister of Finance on these matters. I will continue to work with the Minister of Finance to make the case to Treasury for a VAT regime which enables and supports our businesses to compete within the all-Ireland economy.		Over the past three months I have also launched both my Sub Regional Economic Plan and Social Enterprise Action Plan that specifically target supporting local business need. Each plan is built on the foundation of enabling local communities, throughout the north, to agree on their specific economic constraints and opportunities to ensure any investment my Department makes will provide the greatest impact and thus deliver best value for money in our constrained budgetary environment.		Invest NI offers financial and non-financial support to enhance the sustainability and competitiveness of local businesses in light of current economic challenges and rising operational costs.		Additional support is also available to assist companies to upskill and reskill existing staff; facilitate collaboration with partners to develop new business models, address challenges and exploit new opportunities; and enhance leadership capability.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on improving the Northern Ireland Civil Service's prompt payments.
answer_text: I am a strong advocate for the prompt payment of invoices and recognise that the delay in making payments has a particularly detrimental impact on our small and micro businesses, many of which have limited cashflow.While Central Government continues to perform well in the payment of invoices, with 97% of invoices having been paid within 30 days and 93% within 10 days, however, more needs to be done across the public and private sector to improve prompt payment performance. I wrote to Executive colleagues in October to encourage all Ministers to ensure their department and Arms Length Bodies are doing everything they can to improve prompt payment performance and have met with the Federation of Small Businesses and raised the matter at the Local Government Partnership Panel.In November, my officials hosted a prompt payment workshop to further promote best practice in prompt payment performance and the implications of the Public Procurement Act 2023 which comes into effect on 24th February 2025.I strongly believe that payment performance in the public sector will only improve if we strengthen the monitoring of prompt payment targets, and I plan to bring a refreshed Public Procurement Policy Statement to the Executive which will include a requirement for public bodies to report on their prompt payment performance. This additional transparency will improve accountability and maintain the appropriate level of scrutiny and focus on this important matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of the level of funding HM Government will make available to Northern Ireland via GB Energy.
answer_text: Officials are engaging with DESNZ to establish how GB Energy might support the delivery of our Energy Strategy. This process is still ongoing. In the Autumn budget the British Government committed £125m to establishing GB Energy however no specific allocation has been made in respect of the North.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) the number of nurseries and primary schools in North Belfast that will benefit from the announcement on funding for outdoor play equipment; and (ii) how much funding each will receive.
answer_text: I am providing £4 million of investment in play equipment and resources for outdoor learning.  Funding will be provided directly to every grant-aided pre-school, nursery and primary school to buy the equipment they need most.This will ensure every one of our youngest learners in Northern Ireland and within North Belfast will benefit from capital funding.The level of funding received will be dependent on pupil numbers and details of the scheme will be provided to schools and settings over coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any discussions he has had with HM Government in relation to the UK National Wealth Fund.
answer_text: My officials met with representatives of the UK Infrastructure Bank, now the National Wealth Fund in September. Engagement with Treasury is led by the Department of Finance (DoF). I am aware DoF officials held discussions with Treasury officials prior to the launch of the National Wealth Fund.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on his discussions with HM Government in relation to GB Energy.
answer_text: Officials in my Department have engaged with the Department of Energy Security and Net-Zero to understand how GB Energy will operate and to explore the opportunities it offers in supporting our decarbonisation goals. I have now received the necessary assurances from Minister Shanks that the organisation will operate in a way that respects devolved energy policies here.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he will instruct Sport NI to suspend and review funding to the Ulster Boxing Council and the Irish Athletic Boxing Association as a result of the discrimination case taken by Daryl Clarke.
answer_text: As part of the settlement in this discrimination case,  the Equality Commission are carrying out a Comprehensive Equality Review of the governance, practices and procedures applicable to the Ulster Boxing Council to ensure that they are compliant with the Fair Employment and Treatment (Northern Ireland) Order 1998.Once that review is complete, I will be requesting a meeting with the Chief Executive of Sport NI to discuss the matter. All Exchequer and National Lottery funding disbursed through Sport NI, regardless of recipient, must comply with all statutory requirements and other laws and regulations relating to our respective operations, including (without limitation) all relevant health, safety, data protection, public procurement, employment and equality laws and regulations.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any action he will take to ensure compliance with the Fair Employment and Treatment (Northern Ireland) Order 1998 by the Ulster Boxing Council.
answer_text: I refer the member to my answer to AQW 19164/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities why the Ulster Boxing Council has not implemented the independent working group report of 2013 to tackle religious discrimination against Protestants.
answer_text: As the Governing Bodies for Boxing in Northern Ireland, it was their responsibility to implement most of the recommendations from the Independent Working Group’s (IWG) report which examined boxing in Ulster in 2013.In 2015 the IABA established an Equality and Governance Advisory Group to take forward the recommendations set out in the IWG Report.  As a result of this work, the IABA published details on its website in 2018 of the recommendations which had been achieved along with information advising boxers on the pathways available to becoming an International boxer within Northern Ireland.At that time, Sport NI funded an IABA Operations Manager who supported the Advisory Group in this work.  Sport NI’s role outlined in the IWG report’s recommendations was subject to an internal audit carried out in 2017 and its findings confirmed that Sport NI had satisfied its responsibilities identified in the report’s recommendations.I have asked Sport NI to review the current funding conditions and to seek assurances from the IABA that it remains committed to delivering equality in boxing for Northern Ireland boxers.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of the outcome of the religious discrimination case taken by Daryl Clarke against the Ulster Boxing Council.
answer_text: There is no place for discrimination in any sport, including boxing, and I am glad that a settlement has been reached in this particular case.It is critical that as agreed under the terms of this settlement the Ulster Boxing Council engage fully with the Equality Commission as they carry out an equality review of governance, practices and procedures applicable to the Ulster Boxing Council to ensure they are compliant with the Fair Employment and Treatment (Northern Ireland) Order 1998.  Furthermore, I would expect the Irish Athletic Boxing Association ensure that any recommendations made by the Commission are implemented.  I have asked officials to keep me updated in this regard.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to explain the delays by NI Water in providing costings to Grove Community Housing Association for the development of the former Grove Primary School site.
answer_text: The management of public wastewater infrastructure is an operational matter for NI Water. However, I have sought the requested information from NI Water and have been advised as follows:NI Water has advised that the timeframe for providing costings to the Grove Community Housing Association for the development of the former Grove Primary School site is attributed to site investigations to determine flows and pipe conditions, for a proposed offsetting solution upstream of the developments on North Queen Street, Belfast. The information obtained by NI Water informed the final modelling exercise to ensure the proposed solution had no detriment to NI Water’s sewer network and enabled the company to agree the design and associated cost of the solution. NI Water has confirmed that a letter issued on 5 December 2024 to the Grove Community Housing Association advising it of the final costings for the proposed offsetting solution.For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact NI Water directly, through the Elected Representatives Hotline on 0345 300 6461 or through waterline@niwater.comMaking direct contact with NI Water will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department will consider additional measures to address recurring flooding impacting homes on Westland Road, Belfast, in the vicinity of Cliftonville Golf Club.
answer_text: Since October 2023 my Department has received and responded to a number of enquiries through the online reporting system regarding surface ponding. The issue appeared to relate to a collection of leaves in the gullies on the Westland Road, Belfast which have since been removed.The most recent scheduled clean of road gullies in Westland Road was completed on 20 September 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities (i) when letters of offer will be issued to successful applicants of the Musical Instruments for Bands Scheme; and (ii) whether he will provide additional finance to the scheme should a number of applicants score above the threshold, but the grant budget be fully allocated.
answer_text: All notifications for the Musical Instruments for Bands strand of the Programme were issued to successful applicants between 10-12 December 2024.I was pleased to provide additional funding to support all eligible applicants for the Musical Instruments Programme.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the funding for the new community centre at Westland, North Belfast.

To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) to provide the terms of reference of the political tourism audit currently being carried out by his Department; (ii) to detail the rationale for the audit; and (iii) to outline the details of the audit.
answer_text: An audit of existing peace tourism visitor experiences is currently being completed by Tourism NI. This is a desk-based exercise which aims to establish a baseline position in relation to current peace tourism provision across the north which will ultimately inform potential future work in this area. Given the nature of the exercise, a formal Terms of Reference was not required at this stage. The rationale for the audit exercise being progressed by Tourism NI is to establish a baseline of existing activity and provision in order to help scope and define a shared approach to realising the potential of Peace Tourism.  Recent consumer sentiment surveys have demonstrated that there is a growing interest in our peace story. When consumers were asked in March 2024 by Tourism NI about what they were interested in hearing about on a holiday in the north, 55% of respondents from the south and 51% from the north noted our political history as being of interest.These results, and other evidence, indicates that there is market-led opportunity for peace tourism visitor attractions and experiences to contribute to the achievement of our long-term objectives for tourism . The current audit exercise will help shape how this opportunity will be progressed.  The current audit exercise is capturing a range of information on peace tourism attractions and experiences across the north, including location, Council area, seasonality, availability, website listing, product description, booking information and current visitor numbers (if known).  It is intended that the audit exercise will, amongst other things, identify potential gaps in provision, and opportunities for product development or enhancement through collaboration and partnership.  The audit exercise will also support future decision making on how best to respond to market demand for compelling, authentic products that maximise the value of tourism to our economy.The audit exercise is scheduled to be completed by the end of January 2025.   The audit exercise is being undertaken internally by Tourism NI staff as part of ongoing work on culture and heritage experience development. There are no additional costs associated with the completion of the audit exercise.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on progress with the Department's adoption of (i) roads; (ii) footpaths; and (iii) lighting in the Mount Gilbert development, Belfast.
answer_text: Mount Gilbert is a private site under development by Platinum Assets NI Ltd. There has been a delay to the works by the private developer due to incomplete certifications for water and technical approvals. My department will continue to engage with Platinum Assets NI Ltd. to achieve a resolution for residents, whereby the developer fulfils their obligation to provide adoptable standards of road infrastructure in Mount Gilbert.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to provide further detail on his Department's bid to the Public Sector Transformation Board for transforming the planning system.
answer_text: The bid targets two critical elements of the planning system which have a system-wide impact. Specifically, planning appeal processes and statutory consultation processes. Addressing issues in these parts of the system will help to improve the reputation of the planning system and importantly assist the appropriate consideration of large-scale economic proposals, and our adaptation to and mitigation of the consequences of climate change.  The first element of the bid is to use the Department’s existing powers under the Planning Act (NI) 2011 to directly appoint suitably experienced persons to undertake ‘hear and report’ work normally undertaken by the Planning Appeals Commission, for example hearings, local public inquiries and independent examinations into councils’ local development plans. This would allow us to help address the current backlogs in such work and allow us to consider new approaches to commissioning this work in the future. The second element focusses largely on improving statutory consultations by putting in place a surge team for statutory consultation in the relevant Departmental Roads and Rivers bodies. As well as reducing backlogs it will allow a focus on assisting the submission of planning applications which are ‘right first time’, and engagement with climate change plans. The additional staff would allow the acceleration of current applications, the streamlining of processes to reduce bureaucracy, and allow the creation of improved and updated guidance for those involved in the planning porcess. I am awaiting confirmation from the Board if the bid has been successful.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any discussions his Department has had with Translink in relation to a dedicated school bus service for pupils travelling between Hazelwood Integrated College and Rathcoole, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and can confirm that Translink’s school transport network is provided under contractual arrangements with the Education Authority who assess applications for transport assistance and determine eligibility based on Departmental policy.  Any requests for additional services over and above the existing network would require prior approval from the Education Authority.  Translink inform me that they have not been asked by the Education Authority to increase resources to provide a direct school bus service from Hazelwood Integrated College to Rathcoole. For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any discussions he has had with the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust in relation to the removal of anti-Semitic graffiti and signs at the Royal Victoria Hospital, Belfast.
answer_text: Senior Officials within my Department were tasked to liaise closely with the Belfast Health and Social Care Trust (BHSCT) to expedite the removal of the anti-semantic graffiti and signs at the Royal Victoria Hospital.I am advised that the graffiti was removed by a contractor sourced by BHSCT, only for further graffiti to occur.  The second occurrence has also been removed by a contractor sourced by BHSCT.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of the comments made by the Chief Executive of the Northern Ireland Commonwealth Games Association in relation to the use of the Ulster Banner as the flag for the Northern Ireland athletes.
answer_text: I do not support any change to the use of the Ulster Banner for athletes from Northern Ireland competing at the Commonwealth Games.As witnessed at the Olympic Games in Paris, our athletes were proud to represent Northern Ireland and I believe they are proud to represent Northern Ireland on the Commonwealth stage too. Our focus should be on providing athletes with the support they need to be successful, rather than seeking to change the long standing and widely accepted status quo.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any plans his Department has to address recurring flooding issues and surface ponding on North Queen Street at Jennymount Methodist Church, Belfast.
answer_text: My Department has received and responded to four enquiries regarding surface ponding on North Queen Street at Jennymount Methodist Church, Belfast since 1 January 2023 through the online reporting system. A further enquiry regarding surface ponding at this location was raised at a meeting of the Tiger’s Bay and Mountcollyer Community Group in March 2024. The gullies in the vicinity of Jennymount Methodist Church were cleaned, tested, and found to be functioning normally at this time.I have asked my officials to follow up on this issue with you directly.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any urgent action his Department will take to prevent recurring flooding at the Shore Road and Mill Road junction, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: NI Water, along with officials from my Department have been working together to carry out investigations in the area, to identify the underlying cause of the flooding.    These ongoing investigations will help NI Water and my officials fully understand the complex interactions between the different drainage systems in the area that could be contributing to the flooding and inform the development of a possible solution.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any plans his Department has to address recurring flooding issues and surface ponding at York Road and York Street, Belfast.
answer_text: Since January 2024 my Department has received and responded to a number of enquiries through the online reporting system regarding surface ponding on York Road and York Street, Belfast. The next scheduled clean of road gullies in the York Road and York Street areas is programmed for January 2025.I have asked my officials to follow up on this issue with you directly.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on his engagement with the NI Hospice in relation to their funding requirements.
answer_text: A meeting was held with the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice (NICH) on the 20th September 2024. The meeting was attended by NICH Acting Chief Executive Officer Trevor Mc Cartney, Chief Financial Officer Brian Henderson and Head of Children Hospice Services Grace Stewart alongside Departmental officials including Finance. The meeting was arranged to explore how NICH’s estimate of their funding requirement was reached, and to further understand the costs incurred by the NICH in respect to delivering each of the individual services that the DoH commission from the NICH.Department officials are continuing to engage with the NICH in respect of their service costs identified.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the work to progress the new build for Seaview Primary School.
answer_text: A Technical Feasibility Report (TFR) was submitted to the Department on 25 November, detailing potential options and costs for the provision of suitable accommodation for a fourteen-class primary school, double nursery unit and three class SEN provision.  Further engagement with Historic Environment Division (HED) is also required due to the school’s listed status. Once the Technical Feasibility Report has been reviewed and approved, a Business Case will be completed, which will determine the preferred option to be taken forward in design.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the replacement for the boiler replacement scheme.
answer_text: I refer the member to my response to AQW 6960/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when the Social Enterprise Strategic Action Plan will be published.
answer_text: I met with the Social Enterprise Co-Design Group at the end of September when the initial actions for the Social Enterprise Action Plan were presented to me and discussed. The full action plan has since been finalised and the official launch will be on 04 December.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice, pursuant to AQW 18129/22-27, to detail (i) what is defined as a life sentence; (ii) the minimum tariff for a life sentence; and (iii) the maximum tariff for a life sentence.
answer_text: The Life Sentences (Northern Ireland) Order 2001 (‘the 2001 Order’) defines a life sentence as either a sentence of imprisonment for life, or a sentence of detention during the pleasure of the Minister in charge of the Department Justice under Article 45(1) of the Criminal Justice (Children) (Northern Ireland) Order 1998.Except in serious terrorism cases the 2001 Order makes no provision for minimum tariffs.Subject to specified exceptions, the minimum tariff in serious terrorism cases is 14 years.
