The exceptions include cases where there are exceptional circumstances justifying the specification of a shorter period.The 2001 Order provides for the court to order that the release provisions shall not apply to the offender, if it is of such an opinion due to the seriousness of the offence or the combination of one or more offences associated with it.  This is often referred to as a whole life tariff.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any funding requests he has received from the Irish Football Association since taking office in February 2024.
answer_text: My officials have confirmed that the Irish FA made a request for funding towards the provision of legacy activities on the back of hosting the UEFA European Under-19 Championships in July. Unfortunately, at that time my Department were unable to secure additional resource budget to support this request. I also met with representatives from the Irish FA in November to discuss its plans for the development of a National Football Centre. My officials from The Northern Ireland Football Fund will continue to work with the Irish FA to progress this and look forward to the receipt of a business case.Additionally, this issue is also an operational matter for Sport NI. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows: “Sport NI received a request for funding from the Irish Football Association in February 2024 under the Sports Systems Investment Governing Bodies programme for 2024-25 for £598,500. An award of £554,500 was made with £263,986 paid to date.”

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his revised Tourism Action Plan will set specific targets for Tourism Northern Ireland to attract international sporting events to Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The Tourism Partnership Board is currently developing an innovative Action Plan which will include specific actions to grow our tourism offering over the next 10 years.  Once agreed by the Board, I will consider their recommendations in line with my Economic Vision, with a view to publishing a Tourism Vision and final Action Plan before the end of the year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the membership of the Peace Tourism Working Group; and (ii) the rationale for each members' appointment.
answer_text: Tourism NI is actively progressing this area to shape our shared approach to peace tourism. An initiation workshop was held in August at which the following organisations were in attendance:National Lottery Heritage Fund, who led significant work around the Decade of Commemorations.National Museums NI, who deliver the Troubles & Beyond Exhibition and chair the Conflict Legacy Network.Eastside Partnership & Visit West Belfast who collaborate on shared tourism experiences across Belfast.Hillsborough Castle, host to many significant political negotiations.and the Museum of Free Derry who welcome over 40,000 visitors per year and is also a member of the Conflict Legacy Network. Engagement has also taken place with other tourism providers, including the Siege Museum, Linen Hall Library and Dead Centre Tours with a view to their future involvement in the work Tourism NI intend to undertake in this sector. Next steps will be the formal creation of the working group which will meet in early 2025 to progress an inclusive approach to the development and promotion of Peace Tourism.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when he hopes to publish his revised Tourism Strategy and Action Plan.
answer_text: I aim to publish a Tourism Vision and final Action Plan before the end of the year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy (i) when the restructuring of senior staff posts in his Department will be completed; and (ii) when the new senior staffing structure will be published.
answer_text: The Leadership Team within the Department has recently been restructured, with the new Department for Economy organisational structure and key functions published on the DfE Internet.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for her assessment of the current level of sickness absence in the Northern Ireland Civil Service.
answer_text: I note the increased level of sickness absence in the NICS for the 2023/2024 financial year and recognise the significant impact of ill-health and sickness absence on individuals and on the cost and quality of service delivery.  Anxiety, stress, depression and other psychiatric illnesses are the largest reason for absence. Given the prevalence of mental health issues, both in the workplace and wider society, the NICS is keen to support colleagues and offers a comprehensive range of interventions. This includes a welfare support service, access to a free, 24 hour counselling service for staff as well as wellbeing and mental health awareness training for staff and managers. The Civil Service is also placing an increased focus on our occupational health service to improve the health and wellbeing of staff.With over four fifths (82.6%) of all working days lost through long-term absence, the NICS will continue to work jointly with colleagues, Trade Unions and other partners, to support people to stay in work and return to work as soon as they are able, to provide the best possible public service.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the School Uniform Policy Consultation.
answer_text: The public consultation closed on 27 September, with just under 7,500 responses received. Analysis of the responses is being finalised, with a report on the outcome of the consultation to be published shortly.It is clear, that there is strong support for school uniform policy guidance to be made statutory. I have written to Executive colleagues to seek agreement to introduce a School Uniforms Bill to this effect as soon as possible in the coming weeks.It will then follow due process and scrutiny through the Committee and Assembly stages, and I look forward to working with Assembly colleagues to progress this legislation and make a difference for families most in need of our assistance.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of pensioner households that are expected to receive the £100 fuel payment in the North Belfast constituency.
answer_text: Based on Winter 2023 data, it is estimated that approximately 8,280 households would have received the £100 fuel payment in the North Belfast constituency had the same rules applied last year.	Source Northern Ireland Winter Fuel Payment 2023/24

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he has sought Executive approval to progress the introduction of the proposed Dilapidation Bill.
answer_text: I shall be seeking Executive approval for the introduction of the Dilapidation Bill as soon as I have obtained confirmation from the Departmental Solicitor and the Attorney General that the Bill is within the legislative competence of the Assembly.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, given that local dental practices provide a public health service, whether he will make representations to the Chancellor of the Exchequer on their behalf to seek exemption from the rise in employers National Insurance contributions.
answer_text: I acknowledge there are a number of healthcare providers that will be affected by the rise in employer National Insurance contributions announced in the Budget and I understand that as well as General Dental Practitioners, GPs, care homes and hospices have voiced concerns about the impact this will have on them. Recognising this and the valuable role played by those providers, these funding pressures will nevertheless need to be considered and prioritised alongside other priorities before I am in a position to make any commitments.It is not possible to provide a definitive assessment on our future funding position until a Budget settlement for future years has been reached. Despite the positive news about the budget it is still likely we will be facing a significant funding gap in Northern Ireland as a result of both inflationary pressures and increased demand. While I have not yet contacted the Chancellor in relation to this issue, the need to do so will be kept under consideration as the situation evolves.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for his assessment of the potential impact to dental services provision by local practices following the implementation of the rise in employers National Insurance contributions.
answer_text: I acknowledge there are a number of healthcare providers that will be affected by the rise in employer National Insurance contributions announced in the Budget and I understand that as well as General Dental Practitioners, GPs, care homes and hospices have voiced concerns about the impact this will have on them. Recognising this and the valuable role played by those providers, these funding pressures will nevertheless need to be considered and prioritised alongside other priorities before I am in a position to make any commitments.It is not possible to provide a definitive assessment on our future funding position until a Budget settlement for future years has been reached. Despite the positive news about the budget it is still likely we will be facing a significant funding gap in Northern Ireland as a result of both inflationary pressures and increased demand. While I have not yet contacted the Chancellor in relation to this issue, the need to do so will be kept under consideration as the situation evolves.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when his new guidelines on enforcement action against developers who do not bring roads and footpaths up to an adoptable standard will be published.
answer_text: My officials are working to develop guidance on enforcement action for my consideration by end of the current year.  Following my consideration, the guidelines will then be published.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure how he intends to spend the funding awarded to his Department in the October Monitoring Round.
answer_text: The additional funding awarded to my Department in the October Monitoring Round will be used towards fully funding my department’s Winter Service, and an addition £1m targeted intervention for gully emptying.In addition, almost £19m from the allocations is being used towards essential and structural road maintenance and street lighting work.NI Water is receiving an extra £31m in total, which represents half of the funding received in the October Monitoring Round. Of this, £19.5m is capital funding for targeted action to release wastewater constraintsTranslink will also receive a combined resource and capital allocation of almost £12m.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how he intends to spend the funding awarded to his Department in the October Monitoring Round.
answer_text: My Department received funding in the October Monitoring Round as follows:Non-Earmarked Resource DEL of £6.2 million for general allocation;Earmarked Resource DEL of £1.8 million for Peace Plus match funding;Earmarked Resource DEL of £0.6 million for City Deals Central Procurement Directorate costs; and Financial Transactions Capital (FTC) of £0.2 million due to reduced FTC receipts.The £6.2 million non-earmarked resource DEL will be used to partially offset pressures of £13.9 million which were identified in the In-Year Information Gathering and October Monitoring exercises.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the number of times she has met with representatives of the (I) Ulster-Scots sector; and (ii) Irish language sector.
answer_text: Since taking up office I have received three invitations to meet with representatives of the Irish language sector which I have accepted. I have not received any invitations to date to meet with the Ulster-Scots sector.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of the quality of the technology sector action plans produced by his Department.
answer_text: I am content with the quality of my Department’s Sectoral Action Plans as they have been informed and developed following extensive research and stakeholder engagement, which included a public consultation. As outlined in these action plans, these are rapidly changing sectors and horizon scanning is taking place. The Sectoral Action Plans are being continually reviewed as part of their progression with actions being added or removed in response to the needs of the sectors.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether he is considering legislation to ban the use of wild animals in circuses, as is the case in the rest of the United Kingdom and the Republic of Ireland.
answer_text: I support the introduction of a ban here; however, I am also mindful that there are now no circuses based in Northern Ireland featuring wild animals in their performances and due to the bans in place in other jurisdictions, the circuses which have travelled here in recent years have not used any wild animals in their performances.I am however meeting with stakeholders shortly to discuss my animal welfare priorities and will be happy to their views on this matter.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department is considering prescribing weight loss injections in Northern Ireland, as is currently available to patients within NHS England and Wales.
answer_text: My Department has a formal link with the National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) under which NICE Technology Appraisals are reviewed locally for their legal and policy applicability here.  Where found to be applicable, they are endorsed for implementation within Health and Social Care (HSC) organisations. In practice, this means that treatments that have been recommended by NICE for routine use in the NHS in England are also available in Northern Ireland.NICE has published TA664 (liraglutide) and TA875 (semaglutide). This advises that these treatments are recommended as an option for weight management alongside a reduced-calorie diet and increased physical activity in adults.  Treatments can only be prescribed in secondary care by a specialist multidisciplinary tier 3 weight management service.Unfortunately, there is currently no specialist multidisciplinary tier 3 weight management service available in Northern Ireland. In November last year, my Department launched a public consultation on the proposed introduction of a Regional Obesity Management Service (ROMS), which would provide a means for people living with obesity in NI to access GLP-1 medications.The consultation closed on 1 March 2024, and I am currently considering the analysis of the responses received.  At this time, I am unable to give any commitments as to the potential future establishment of this service.  Any decisions regarding future development of this service will be based on consideration of the evidence presented, availability of resources and competing priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when his Department's policy on clustering will be ready for publication.
answer_text: My department is currently exploring the benefits of clustering for our most innovative and high potential businesses across the North and will bring forward our policy on this in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any consideration he has given to updating licensing laws to support local producers, particularly in relation to the surrender principle which can hinder smaller retailers and start-ups.
answer_text: An independent review of the licensing system including surrender principle has recently been completed. The final report was received in the Department on Friday 15 November, and I will be considering its recommendations over the coming months.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister, pursuant to AQW 17348/22-27, whether the legal advice was discussed.
answer_text: At the meeting between the First Minister and Chairperson of the Committee for the Executive Office on 23 October, the receipt of legal advice by the Committee was noted, but the advice was not shared, nor was its substance discussed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the future of Abbotscoole House, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: Firstly, I note the Members’ continued interest in representing the views of his constituents regarding the future of Abbotscoole House.My Department has not received a business case from the Housing Executive relating to Abbotscoole House. I can, however, provide my assurance that if my Department receives such, it will be given due consideration and scrutiny before any decision is made.  As you are aware, Abbotscoole House forms part of Phase 1 of the Housing Executive’s Tower Block Action plan.  The estimated investment requirement in improvements and planned maintenance for the Housing Executive’s portfolio of Tower Blocks is £138.8 million over the next thirty years.  These investment costs combined with projected cyclical/response maintenance and management costs amount to a total funding requirement of £308.8 million over thirty years.In many locations the blocks are no longer meeting their communities’ needs; demolition of blocks can provide opportunities to provide the necessary mix and type of housing to better meet housing need, support sustainable communities and meet residents’ requirements.Each business case relating to the Tower Block Strategy is given substantial examination by my officials to assess if the demolition of the towers and provision of fit for purpose affordable housing in the area provides greater value for money than undertaking the required upgrades to the towers.  Each individual business case submitted in relation to the tower block strategy sets out detailed proposals for the site in question, including a feasibility study of opportunities for new build rehousing sites in the localities and how long they would take to develop.I have sought an update from the Northern Ireland Housing Executive Chief Executive on the matter and been advised that the business case is expected to be put before the NIHE Board for consideration in December 2024.  Subject to the Board’s approval, it will then be forwarded to my Department for consideration.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) whether the position of the walking and cycling Champion in his Department still exists; and (ii) to provide an update on any work they have undertaken.
answer_text: There is no single Walking and Cycling Champion within my Department. Following an internal reorganisation in 2023, responsibility for Active Travel is now under one Director, and a number of key posts have been added to the team, including a Deputy Director for Active Travel. My Department as a whole is committed to encouraging more active and sustainable travel choices.I recently launched a public consultation on a draft Active Travel Delivery Plan which sets out how my Department proposes to prioritise and deliver high quality active travel infrastructure for shorter everyday journeys. I would encourage everyone to engage with this consultation to help shape priorities for the delivery of pedestrian and cycling infrastructure in our villages, towns and cities. Once adopted, the Active Travel Delivery plan will complement the Belfast Cycling Network Delivery Plan and the Strategic Plan for Greenways to provide our teams with a firm basis for the prioritisation and delivery of high-quality active travel infrastructure across the North for the next ten years and beyond.In Belfast, design work is ongoing on the majority of the short-term schemes in the Belfast Cycling Network Delivery Plan, including a number of schemes in North Belfast. My officials are aiming to progress as many of these projects as possible in the time period up to the end of 2025, subject to staff resource, future budget availability, and the successful completion of statutory processes.Officials also continue to work with key delivery partners and stakeholders to implement active travel infrastructure and related facilities. My officials have been working with councils to identify their greenway priorities and develop an appropriate grant and support framework for its effective delivery going forward. Greenway schemes are generally council-led, with my Department funding up to 50% of the capital cost of approved projects. By working together, we can provide more people with access to high quality active travel infrastructure to encourage more walking, wheeling and cycling.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 16996/22-27, when this change in policy was agreed by the Executive.
answer_text: Upon the receipt of legal opinion on the appropriate mechanism to locate airport policy within my Department, and at the appropriate time, I will refer the matter to the Executive for approval.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 16604/22-27, when he will be requesting a meeting with the Ulster-Scots sector.
answer_text: I am content to consider any meeting request from the Ulster Scots sector that relates to the business of the department.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail any further considerations she has given to the introduction of legislation similar to Harper's Law in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My Department has continued to monitor the position in England and Wales since the introduction of Harper’s Law in 2022 and has not found any reports of it being used. In Northern Ireland a life sentence is already mandatory in any murder case, and, in circumstances considered appropriate by the Court, can be imposed where a defendant is found guilty of manslaughter.   I do not, therefore, consider any change to this position is required.I will, however, be seeking to introduce a new offence of assault on those providing services to the public, offering a public service or performing a public duty in the Sentencing Bill.  This new offence will replace existing offences of assaulting police, ambulance workers and the Fire and Rescue Service, and is intended to protect all those whose work brings them into contact with the public.  The new offence will have a higher maximum sentence in the magistrates’ courts than is currently available for the similar existing offences; and the fact that a person was providing services to the public, offering a public service or performing a public duty will become an aggravating factor in more serious assault offences.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, following his announcement of further funding for councils to provide debt advice, how he will ensure this funding is spent fairly and equitably across North Belfast.
answer_text: I announced the launch of the ‘Integrated Advice Partnership Fund’ on 5 November 2024.  On 6 November, a Memorandum of Understanding was issued to all district councils, which outlined the objective and aims of the Fund.  All councils, including Belfast City Council, have flexibility to administer the Fund within the objectives set out in the Memorandum of Understanding.District councils are public authorities and are required to have due regard to equality of opportunity and good relations in carrying out their functions, in accordance with section 75 of the Northern Ireland Act 1998.  Each council will have its own governance, approval and screening processes in place and responsibility to ensure the Fund is spent fairly and equitably in their council area.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the spending by his Department on active travel in North Belfast in the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: Monitoring of Active Travel spend within my Department is not broken down by parliamentary constituency. The current direct active travel budget allocation for the 2024-2025 financial year within the Eastern division area, which includes North Belfast, is £4.2m. This includes funding for schemes to be delivered directly by my Department and grant funding provided to councils for the delivery of Greenways and Active Travel related facilities. Budgets are monitored closely during the year, in line with sound financial management, and are subject to change. The direct active travel spend in the Eastern division to 30 September 2024 is approximately £0.6m. This figure is mid-year and therefore unaudited and subject to change.In addition to this direct allocation, my Department routinely undertake many activities for the benefit of pedestrians and cyclists that are currently not included within active travel figures. Work is currently ongoing within my Department to finalise those areas of spend for future reporting.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any bids she received from the Department of Health during the October Monitoring Round for the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice.
answer_text: I did not receive any specific bids from the Minister of Health for additional funding for the Children’s Hospice as part of the 2024-25 October monitoring round.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail each bid she submitted to the Department of Finance as part of the October Monitoring Round.
answer_text: The Department of Finance (DoF) commissioning note for the October Monitoring exercise requested bids for the following categories only: Ringfenced Resource DEL, Capital DEL and FT Capital.The following bids were submitted by my Department in the October Monitoring Round:Budget categoryBid DescriptionAmount (£k)Ringfenced Resource DELDepreciation - PSNI8,401Ringfenced Resource DELDepreciation - NICTS1,404There were no bids submitted by my Department for Capital DEL or FT Capital.The information provided by departments in the urgent in-year exercise on overcommitments and non-contractual pay costs (submitted to DoF on 30 August 2024) was used by DoF to inform consideration of the non-ringfenced Resource DEL position in October Monitoring. Departments were not required to re-submit this information.My Department reported a total non-ringfenced Resource DEL overcommitment position of £47.8m in the urgent in-year exercise.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail each bid he submitted to the Department of Finance as part of the October Monitoring Round.
answer_text: My Department submitted bids to the Department of Finance as part of the October Monitoring Round as follows:Non-Earmarked Resource DEL of £13.9 million;Earmarked Resource DEL of £1.8 million;Depreciation and Impairment of £36.7 million;Non-Earmarked Capital DEL of £19.7 million; andFinancial Transactions Capital of £0.2 million.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any bid he made to the Minister of Finance as part of the October Monitoring Round to support the extension of the Translink Nightmover service, including the value of the bid.
answer_text: I have made several bids to the Minister of Finance for additional funding to support the delivery of public transport services by Translink. I understand Translink intend providing the Nightmover service as a commercial operation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any consideration he has given to creating a taskforce to bring together representatives of the creative industries.
answer_text: The Creative Industries plays an important role in the economy of the North and support for the sector extends beyond the responsibly of any single Department. My Department seeks to support the sector through supporting a range of businesses through both Invest NI and NI Screen Industries. I have no plans to create a taskforce to bring together representatives of the creative industries at this time but will keep the issue under review.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail (i) when the last condition survey of the nursey building at St Bernard's Primary School, Glengormley, was carried out; (ii) the Department's assessment of the current condition of the nursery building; and (iii) whether the Department will request a new condition survey of the nursery building.
answer_text: A basic condition survey of the nursery building at St Bernard’s Primary School was carried out by an Education Authority (EA) maintenance officer in March 2022.Given the passage of time, the EA has been commissioned to undertake a refreshed condition survey of the mobile. This will allow the EA and the Department to assess the current condition of the building and consider remedial works or replacement of the unit.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the bid he made to the Minister of Finance during the October Monitoring Round to specifically support (i) the Northern Ireland Hospice; and (ii) the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice.
answer_text: The Department of Health reported, during October Monitoring, a total short fall of £450m.  This included £130m to fund the continuation of existing services and £320m for non-contractual pay to match the English pay offers.Recognising that the funding available was unlikely to cover existing pressures, including pay, the Department of Finance did not invite bids at October monitoring outside of these pressures.  However, given its prominence in the Programme for Government the Department did submit a bid for waiting lists. Given the severity of the financial position for both the Department and the wider Northern Ireland Block no other bids were submitted.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the work of Invest NI in promoting Northern Ireland’s creative industries.
answer_text: Invest NI promotes eligible creative industries through a wide range of support from financial and non-financial support, including advice and guidance as well as support to aid with market development, skills development, and productivity improvement. Invest NI also aims to promote the creative industries through its support of strategically important investments in the sector through the City and Growth Deal programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department is considering the commissioning of research into the economic impact and importance of the creative industries.
answer_text: The Creative Industries plays an important role in the economy of the North and I recognise the contribution the sector makes to economic growth. I am not proposing to commission any additional research in this area at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, following the publication of the Autumn Budget 2024 by HM Treasury, for an update on the Enhanced Economic Zone for Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Following a period of uncertainty, I welcome confirmation from the British Government in the Autumn Statement that funding for the North’s Enhanced Investment Zone (EIZ) programme has been secured. 	Work continues with officials in the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government on development of a delivery and resourcing plan to implement the programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when he will make a decision on the Belfast Rapid Transit Phase 2 extending to Glengormley.
answer_text: Work on the feasibility of extending Belfast Rapid Transit to Glengormley and Carryduff has been finalised.  The delivery options that could be implemented, utilising the £35m funding envelope from the Belfast Region City Deal, are being investigated.  The Outline Business Case is being finalised in line with these feasibility reports and delivery options.When my officials have concluded this work, I will be in a position to make a decision.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of the Energy Strategy Action Plan, (i) to explain the delay in completing research on interconnectors and storage; (ii) when the research will be completed; and (iii) when the research will be published.
answer_text: DfE’s Energy Strategy Action Plan point 14 states “DfE will commission research into the costs and benefits to the NI Consumer for different Interconnection scenarios, multiple Energy Storage options and the 80% Renewable Electricity target up to 2030 and beyond”. This research is on schedule to be commissioned by year end, with an estimated completion date of March 2025. The research will be published soon after.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 16993/22-27, (i) when the feasibility and options study was commissioned; and (ii) when it is due to be completed.
answer_text: The feasibility and options study for Shore Road / Mount Vernon / M2 junction was commissioned on 16 April 2024 from our consultants to include surveying and modelling works at the junction.A decision on any preferred option is expected at the end of March 2025 with scheme implementation dependent on budget availability thereafter.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the work of his Department's Credit Union division, announced on 19 March 2024.
answer_text: Officials have established a working group, partnering with the Irish League of Credit Unions (ILCU), the Ulster Federation of Credit Unions (UFCU) and other key stakeholders with whom my Department is working closely to co-design and deliver the proposed reforms outlined within the ILCU’s policy manifesto.Engagement continues with Treasury and the Financial Conduct and Prudential Regulatory authorities, to confirm the regulatory and legislative requirements needed to deliver those changes within the current mandate.I plan to launch a consultation on proposed changes early next year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the amount of funding that will be allocated to (i) Belfast City Council; and (ii) Antrim and Newtownabbey Borough Council from the Integrated Advice Partnership Fund.
answer_text: I can confirm that the Belfast City Council Area will receive £248,253.48 and Antrim and Newtownabbey Borough Council will receive £91,427.04 from the Integrated Advice Partnership Fund.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education (i) whether his Department will work to ensure every pupil diagnosed with epilepsy has an Individual Healthcare Plan accessible to the appropriate school staff with information about their condition and any support they require in school; and (ii) how his Department will improve training and awareness of epilepsy among teaching and non-teaching school staff.
answer_text: Children and young people with medical needs, including epilepsy, should have a healthcare plan in place.  The responsibility for drawing up a healthcare plan lies with the relevant Allied Healthcare Professional (AHP) involved in the healthcare of the child or young person with epilepsy.  The AHP provides a detailed plan which is agreed with the parents and school and can be used as all or part of an individual’s Medication Plan.   Schools have responsibility to make reasonable adjustments to ensure that that the needs of pupils with medical needs, including epilepsy, are met.  The department provides the following guidance to support schools “Supporting Pupils with Medication Needs” at https://www.education-ni.gov.uk/articles/support-pupils-medication-needs.  This guidance is currently undergoing review.To ensure that school staff have a basic understanding, can recognise symptoms and seek appropriate support, awareness training should be completed.  This will help ensure that the needs of pupils can be met.  Epilepsy Action provides a free training module for schools.  The training required, however, for individual children will be noted in the medication plan.  The child and their family should be known to the Paediatric Epilepsy Nursing Specialists team who can provide specific support, if required. The Education Authority (EA) Special Educational Needs (SEN) Inclusion Service Medical Needs Service operates a central point of contact for schools to support the effective inclusion in schools of children and young people with medical needs, including epilepsy.  The service can be contacted at 028 7127 2340 or EAMedicalNeedsSupport@eani.org.uk.  Information and signposting to resources and training is also available on the EA’s website Support available to schools for Medical Needs | SEND Plan.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail what consultation she carried out before approving the Civil Service Inclusive Language Guide.
answer_text: I would refer the Member to the response provided for AQW 17153/22-27.Prior to my approval, the Inclusive Language Guide was equality screened in accordance with Section 75 of the Northern Ireland Act 1998.  The screening document was shared with the Equality Consultees listed in Annex 3 of my Department’s Equality Scheme and published on the department’s website.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail how many times she has met with the Public Sector Transformation Board since taking office in February 2024.
answer_text: I met with the Head of the Civil Service, Jayne Brady, in her role as Chair of the interim Public Sector Transformation Board twice, firstly on the 31 July and again on the 22 October. I have also had regular correspondence from the Chair of the interim Board, who has written to me on three separate occasions to keep me informed of developments in relation to the interim Board’s work.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to outline when they expect the Police Ombudsman to return to work.
answer_text: There has been no indication as to when the Police Ombudsman will return to work.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance, further to her oral Ministerial statement on 4th November 2024, to detail the value of the Reserve Claim.
answer_text: I understand the Justice Minister quantified the estimated cost to her department on her 18 November statement.However, two of the issues that affect the Department of Justice will also attract costs in other departments.Since the issues are subject to ongoing legal cases, there is currently no certainty on the amount or timing of payments.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail how much funding her Department has awarded to Stonewall since taking office in February 2024.
answer_text: Since February 2024, my Department has funded Stonewall £2,875.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance when she expects to bring the outcome of the Monitoring Round to the Executive for approval.
answer_text: The October Monitoring Round was brought to the Executive and agreed on Monday 11 November. My statement to the Assembly on the same day can be viewed at https://www.finance-ni.gov.uk/publications/oral-statement-public-expenditure-2024-25-october-monitoring-11-november-2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 16077/22-27, whether his Department will confirm the number of people on zero hour contracts, before introducing legislation to ban them.
answer_text: I am content that official statistics, engagement with stakeholders and information gathered through my recent ‘Good Jobs’ Employment Rights Bill consultation are sufficient to inform decisions on this matter.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to list the resurfacing schemes he expects to go on site in the remainder of this financial year in North Belfast.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government. This has had a significant impact on our ability to deliver essential services such as road maintenance. While many roads in the North Belfast area would undoubtedly benefit from investment, due to a limited budget, resurfacing work must be taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need of intervention. The 2024/25 resurfacing programme is detailed in the Department’s annual report and is due to be delivered to Belfast City Council at its next meeting on 27th November.  Subsequent to this meeting it will be published on the Department’s website, issued to local MPs and MLAs in the Council area, and the Assembly library.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission, pursuant to AQW 16374/22-27, for an update on their investigation.
answer_text: I refer the Member to AQW 16723/22-27 on 17 October 2024 which set out the approach being taken to the examination of these matters.  This included that the matter is being examined under the authority of the Clerk/ Chief Executive as the Assembly Commission’s Accounting Officer.  Assembly Commission Members have no role in this process and therefore are not being updated with the detail of its operation.The Assembly Commission has set out that it takes matters relating to the administration of public funds extremely seriously and that it is important that these matters are dealt with in a robust way which follows due process and examines the evidence.  In AQW 16968/22-27, the Assembly Commission provided an update that the Clerk/ Chief Executive will review the initial evidence gathered by 15 November 2024 to determine the nature of any further work or action required.However, the Assembly Commission has also been clear that it will not be providing a running commentary on the detail of this process while it is continuing.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to provide an update on the outcome of his Department’s recent trade visits.
answer_text: I returned last week from a technology mission to Singapore leading an all-island visit to the world’s largest fintech trade event with seven fintech companies. Organised in partnership with Enterprise Ireland, this mission reflects my continued commitment to fostering collaboration across the island for greater economic impact.   Singapore is a global fintech leader and offered the prime location to showcase our fintech capabilities and expertise.  In October, I joined a business promotion trip to Germany.  I held meetings with a range of companies from Life & Health Sciences, Fintech and Creative to educate on the North's unique dual market access both for FDI and sourcing goods from NI companies.  It was great to support Invest NI's efforts to continue to raise the North's profile and business benefits to a European audience. In September, I travelled to the US and Canada with twelve innovative technology companies, receiving universally positive feedback. I met business leaders and politicians to strengthen transatlantic relationships and launched the Canadian Advisory Council to support the North’s trade and FDI strategy.Whilst some of the outcomes from these recent trade missions will take time to come to fruition, Invest NI is continuing to work closely with the participating companies. They will monitor progress and outcomes in the comping months and continue to support them in these key export markets.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, in light of his funding announcement for improving provision for children with disabilities, whether the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice will be eligible under the proposals.
answer_text: I announced on the 23 October 2024 that I would be providing up to £2m in year and £13.1m recurrently to support children with disabilities following the Spotlight program “I am not okay.”The funding that I have allocated will address the challenge that families are facing with respect of accessing short breaks, many of the Statutory short break’s units have had to be repurposed to provide care for children who are requiring this on a full-time basis. Many parents including those featured in the Spotlight program are struggling as the impact of this has meant that the respite they have relied on has not been able to be delivered.The funding will enable the expansion of family, therapeutic and behavioural support services on a statutory and voluntary sector basis alongside targeted investments on short breaks and residential capacity, with a view of increasing bed availability across the region.My officials are currently engaging with Trusts to understand in year spend and key priorities for investment and this is ongoing. I have asked that a proportion of the investment goes to support voluntary and community services where it can be demonstrated they can achieve the outcomes that I have mentioned above, the detail of this is currently being worked out, the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice may be considered as part of the investment proposal.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, pursuant to AQW 16372/22-27, for his assessment of whether this represents good use of public funds.
answer_text: In the absence of any other option, an assessment has been made that this expenditure is necessary to protect the flats, cover the parapet and provide safe access to the damaged roof while the Housing Executive procures a contractor to upgrade the roof.  I would, however, far rather see public funds being used to improve housing in the Member’s constituency, which he so strongly advocates for, and I look forward to working with him to deliver that.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether the First Minister, at her meeting with the Chairperson of the Committee for the Executive Office on 23 October 2024, was informed of or discussed the content of legal advice to the Committee for the Executive Office.
answer_text: The legal advice commissioned by the Committee for the Executive Office was not shared with the Department at the First Minister’s meeting with the Committee Chairperson on 23 October 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AQW 16078/22-27, when he expects the business case to be completed.
answer_text: It is anticipated that the DfE Business Case will be completed early in 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 16605/22-27, when the Section 75 screening exercise will take place.
answer_text: As indicated in my answer to AQW 16605/22-27 a Section 75 equality of opportunity screening exercise will be completed before the signs are erected and I can confirm that work on this is underway.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission to provide a list of requests by Sinn Féin to (i) cancel Assembly passes; (ii) end payroll, on 21 October 2024.
answer_text: (i)   The Assembly Commission received one request from Sinn Féin to cancel an Assembly ID pass on 21 October 2024.  This was via email to the Usher Services office at 5.07pm that day.  This request was followed up by a telephone call at 5.35pm that same day.  As both the email and telephone call were received outside normal office opening hours, the request was dealt with at 9:00am the following day, and the ID pass was cancelled accordingly.(ii)  The Assembly Commission operates a payroll bureau for parties, to process salaries for those roles that are funded by the Financial Assistance for Political Parties Scheme 2024.  One request was received from Sinn Féin on 21 October 2024 to make an individual a leaver with immediate effect from that payroll.  The Assembly Commission is obliged to take account of the privacy rights of individuals.  Therefore, it is not the practice of the Assembly Commission to provide the names and details of those employed by Members or parties, although there may be exceptions – for example, where the name of an individual is already in the public domain.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the number of occasions; and (ii) the dates he has met with the Vice Chancellor of (a) Ulster University; and (b) Queen's University Belfast since taking office.
answer_text: I have met with the Vice Chancellors on the following occasions:Vice ChancellorTotal number of occasionsDatesProfessor Paul Bartholomew, Ulster University414 February 20246 March 202422 March 202411 September 2024Professor Sir Ian Greer, Queen’s University Belfast421 February 20244 June 202429 August 20245 September 2024 This data can also be accessed using the Open Data Portal. The link for the Portal is available here: https://www.opendatani.gov.uk/. The data for the most recent quarter, June to date, will be available shortly.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when the cross-cutting decision to transfer £3 million of his departmental budget to the Department for Infrastructure for the City for Derry Airport was approved by the Executive.
answer_text: On 10 June the Minister of Infrastructure and I agreed that aviation policy should sit with my department. This would it make it much clearer for stakeholders in the aviation industry to understand the overarching governance of this industry and also end the confusion caused by the division of funding responsibilities for airports between DfE, DfI and DoF. Budget Cover Transfer is used when one department agrees to transfer cover to another department to cover costs incurred because of a change in policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he is aware of the concerns raised directly to his Department by Queen's University Belfast in relation to the proposed changes to tuition fee loan support for Four Year Graduate Entry Medicine Programmes.
answer_text: Queen’s University and other stakeholders provided written views on the proposed changes to tuition fee loan support in response to a request from the Department.  The views received were taken into account when coming to a decision to implement the proposed changes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the study to improve road safety at the Shore Road, Mount Vernon and Fortwilliam roundabout junction, North Belfast.
answer_text: On review of the most recent PSNI collision statistics, I can advise that my department has commissioned a feasibility and options study for the Shore Road / Mount Vernon/ M2 junction which will consider if an improvement scheme is required.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department will (i) assess the findings of the UK Blood Cancer Action Plan 2024; and (ii) consider implementing the 17 policy recommendations for services.
answer_text: Whilst my Department acknowledges the helpful 17 recommendations outlined within the UK Blood Cancer Action Plan, it should be stated that it was not consulted or involved in the development of this Action Plan. However, the recommendations around the themes of workforce, diagnosis, care, treatment, and access to clinical trials and data, are all areas we acknowledge require work, and we have been working to address these via the NI Cancer Strategy 2022 to 2032. Blood cancer /haemato-oncology services in NI have been under growing pressures for some time amidst serious workforce concerns for haematological services UK wide.  In order to address the severe regional pressures within haematology services, in 2023 my NI Cancer Policy and Projects Team initiated a project for the redesign of Haematology Treatment Pathways across NI to include the three main haematological pathways: Myeloma, Lymphoma and Leukaemia. Three reports with recommendations were completed in March 2024.The Strategic Planning and Performance Group (SPPG) of my Department were asked to implement the recommended actions of these reports in April 2024. SPPG have engaged with Trusts on the reports’ recommendations.  Some progress has been made on early stage actions including, baseline assessment and identification of nursing workforce gaps, maximising virtual clinics, identifying appropriate time and process for holistic needs assessment, weekly multi-disciplinary Team (MDT) meetings, adoption of NICE guidance and development of a haematology cancer nurse career pathway. Many actions across all 3 reports are dependent upon reform and greater skill mix within haematology services. While some recommendations are cost neutral, others require investment, and resource to implement.  Progression of some key actions is dependent on consultant recruitment and retention as well as additional investment in greater skill mix for the nursing workforce to include more Advanced Nurse Practitioners.  This will help create capacity to take forward a number of the recommended actions.   Action 16 of NI Cancer Strategy 2022 - 2032 is to develop a specialist integrated haematological diagnostics service (SIHMDS) for Northern Ireland. This will provide more timely, co-ordinated and equitable diagnosis for people presenting with blood cancers and will be taken forward when funding becomes available.Clinical trial uptake is low in Northern Ireland and this is acknowledged in the NI cancer strategy under Actions 55 and 56. Work will be undertaken to address these actions over the lifetime of the NI Cancer Strategy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer (i) AQW 14911/22-27; (ii) AQW 14282/22-27; and (iii) AQW 14283/22-27.

To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs (i) when he was informed by his former Special Adviser that she was leaving her role to take up an appointment with Ulster University; (ii) when he informed his Permanent Secretary of that decision; (iii) whether he reminded his Special Adviser of her requirement to comply with the Northern Ireland Civil Service Code of Ethics when taking up that appointment; and (iv) for his assessment of whether those requirements were followed by his former Special Adviser.
answer_text: My former Special Adviser informed me of her resignation on 04 August 2024 and I informed my Permanent Secretary on 05 August 2024.  She did not inform me that she was leaving her role to take up an appointment with Ulster University. Annex 4 of the Standards of Conduct policy sets out the procedure to be followed in relation to the acceptance of outside business appointments, employment or self-employment by Civil Servants (including Special Advisers) after leaving the NI Civil Service.  Civil servants (including Special Advisers) are reminded of this requirement in the confirmation of resignation letter.  As the policy explains, it is for the former civil servant to determine whether the requirements for submitting an application are met and, if they consider they are, to submit an application.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of the remarks by the Chief Executive of Invest NI at the meeting of the Committee for the Economy on 16 October 2024, that there has been no evidence yet of foreign direct investment secured in Northern Ireland as a result of dual market access.
answer_text: EU Exit and its outworkings created an extended period of uncertainty and instability for businesses and investors across these islands and beyond. The Windsor Framework, agreed last year, provides a welcome degree of stability by confirming our unique dual market access position whereby traders in the North of Ireland have access to the EU and British markets for goods without border checks, tariffs or customs declarations.   Combined, these markets have well over 500 million people and produce around £17.5 trillion in GDP (2023).Investment decisions take time, typically years, and require a degree of certainty which, thankfully, we now enjoy to a much greater extent. There is also a job of work to raise awareness and understanding of our trading position, both at home and further afield. That is why I have put dual market access at the front and centre of our international value proposition, working alongside Invest NI to take that message directly to buyers and potential investors. It is also important to point out that a key benefit of dual market access is the potential to grow domestic manufacturing exports.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities when he will answer (i) AQW 14910/22-27; and (ii) AQW 15088/22-27.
answer_text: AQW 14910/22-27 was answered on 11 December 2024.AQW 15088/22-27 was answered on 28 October 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance when will appointments to the Public Sector Transformation Board be publicly advertised.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department will consider improving road markings and signage at Lowwood Primary School, North Belfast, to address illegal parking and improve road safety.
answer_text: Lowwood Primary School is accessed from Sheringhurst Park which is a No Through Road. I can advise that standard school warning signs and traffic calming features (speed ramps) are in place on Lowood Park on both approaches to Sheringhurst Park. Officials are not aware of any illegal parking or road safety issues within close proximity to Lowood Primary School. However, I will arrange for them to visit the area in due course and assess if there is a need to implement any further engineering measures.One of the best ways to ensure safety on our roads and fulfil our long-term goal of eliminating death and serious injury by 2050, is by changing road user behaviour. As road users we all have a personal responsibility to behave in a way that keeps ourselves and others safe. The sad reality is that if we do not change our own attitudes when we are using the roads, our death toll will continue to increase.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the present waiting time for early years autism assessments from the date of referral, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: Autism referrals and assessments are provided as a routine service and referrals are managed in chronological order of acceptance in line with Integrated Elective Access Protocols (IEAP) guidelines. HSC Trusts do not maintain an Early Years waiting list for autism referrals and therefore this information is not readily available as this cohort cannot be managed differently from anyone else on a waiting list. The Department through existing reporting mechanisms collects and monitors information in relation to children and young people waiting on an ASD diagnostic pathway from all HSC Trusts. This information is recorded by length of wait but does not report the length of time or age of any individual child or young person on that waiting list.Each HSC Trust maintains individual information on all referrals accepted onto their waiting lists. Information on individual children referred for an autism assessment will be available from their local HSC Trust.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer AQW 9710/22-27.
To ask the Minister for the Economy, pursuant to AWQ 16078/22-27, to detail when and how he referred this cross-cutting issue to the Executive.
answer_text: My Department is in the process of undertaking an economic analysis of the City of Derry Airport’s performance to inform its business case and a value for money assessment. This is with the objective of assuming responsibility for working with Derry City and Strabane District Council on the future sustainability of the airport from 1 April 2025. The appropriate approvals and decision making process will be informed by this activity, including referral to the Executive for the requisite approvals.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the strategies to reduce waiting times for autism spectrum disorder diagnosis in the (i) Belfast Health and Social Care Trust; and (ii) Northern Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: My Department has recently published the cross-departmental Autism Strategy 2023-2028 including a delivery plan for 2023-2025. This includes an action to consult on and finalise a Children and Young People’s Emotional Health and Wellbeing Framework which will guide future development of services. The Framework embraces a move towards supporting Autism services within a broader neurodevelopmental approach and incorporate regionally consistent single points of referral and diagnostic assessment pathways.Following a regional audit of paediatric autism referral and assessment pathways my officials are working with colleagues from Public Health Agency to develop a regionally consistent pathway that is compliant with NICE guidelines and taking into consideration best practice, emerging evidence and the recognition that workforce planning is critical to meeting the needs of children and young people in a timely manner. This work will inform appropriate investment across all HSC Trusts to provide equitable pathways. A Review of Adult Autism Services across the region has been undertaken and the recommendations were presented to the Permanent Secretary.  One of the recommendations is the appointment of a Lead for Adult Autism Services. This post has been appointed and it is anticipated they will take up post in January 2025. This role will be responsible for taking forward the recommendations emerging from the review; this will include the management of waiting lists for adult autism assessment waiting lists.Each HSC Trust has developed their care pathways to best meet their local population needs and to reduce duplication of effort in completing autism assessments. In relation to your question BHSCT and NHSCT are implementing a number of local service improvements; BHSCT Within both Children’s and Adult ASD Services, Belfast Health and Social Care Trust continues to recruit and train staff to meet funded assessment capacity.  Belfast Trust also uses Independent Sector providers on a non-recurrent basis to supplement its own staffing capacity and increase the number of assessments available. Belfast Trust continually engages in audit and service improvement initiatives to ensure the highest level of quality, efficiency and effectiveness regarding the delivery of ASD assessments and interventions. NHSCT     Community Paediatric Medical Service (CPMS) have a care pathway for children under 4 who have been referred to CPMS and have been assessed by a medic as requiring an ASD assessment. In these cases, the assessment process will have been partially completed requiring less input from ASD clinicians. Established a similar pathway with the Looked After Children’s (LAC) Social Work team. Training has been provided to the LAC team to complete the initial developmental history and inform ASD clinicians about each child. The LAC team can also provide ongoing post diagnostic support to LAC diagnosed with ASD. A similar pathway with the Multi-Disciplinary Team (MD

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the official in-person engagements she carried in her Ministerial capacity between 10 October 2024 and 15 October 2024.
answer_text: I did not undertake any in-person engagements between Thursday, 10 October 2024 and Tuesday, 15 October 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of students currently studying at the Ulster University Coleraine campus.
answer_text: The Department publishes a range of statistics regarding enrolments at Northern Ireland’s higher education institutions. Statistics for the most recent year for which data is available (2021/22) can be found at the following link:https://www.economy-ni.gov.uk/publications/enrolments-uk-higher-education-institutions-northern-ireland-analysis-202122  Table 9c sets out enrolments at Ulster University broken down by campus. It shows that 3,055 students were enrolled at the Coleraine campus.Statistics for 2022/23 are expected to be published later this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when the Ulster University will transfer its sporting excellence programme from its Jordanstown site to its Coleraine campus.
answer_text: Ulster University is an autonomous institution responsible for its own course provision. The University is committed to campus balance and keeps provision at all campuses under review.Ulster University has already extended its School of Sport onto its Coleraine campus with the Adventure Sport programme, and has recently announced a new Masters programme in Golf Management that will be delivered at the Coleraine campus from academic year 2025/26.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how his Department is supporting the future growth of student places at the Ulster University Coleraine campus.
answer_text: My Department maintains a dialogue with Ulster University on the full range of issues affecting its sustainability and growth across all of its campuses. I fully support and recognise the benefits of the University’s three-campus model in providing accessible higher education to students from all communities.I am open to engagement on how my Department can best continue to support the higher education sector as a whole across the North and I look forward to receiving views as part of the review of higher education funding that my Department will shortly begin.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will answer (i) AQW 14282/22-27; and (ii) AQW 14283/22-27.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, in light of his announcement to introduce bi-lingual traffic signs, using both Irish and English, in West Belfast, (i) to detail the total cost of this scheme; and (ii) to provide a copy of the Equality Impact Assessment for the scheme.
answer_text: On 10 October 2024, I announced the bi-lingual traffic sign pilot for the Gaeltacht Quarter in West Belfast.  The pilot will install bi-lingual traffic signs and road markings on the Falls Road between the Springfield Road and Whiterock Road.  i) the current estimate for this pilot is £50k.ii) A Section 75 equality of opportunity screening exercise will be completed before the signs are erected.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of times he has met with representatives of (i) the Irish language sector; (ii) the Ulster Scots sector; and (iii) other minority languages, since taking up office.
answer_text: I have met with representatives from the Irish language Sector on three occasions since taking up office.  I have received no meeting requests from any other language sectors.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the Urban Villages Initiative in North Belfast.
To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his proposal to allocate £3 million to the City of Derry Airport will require a Ministerial Direction to be issued, in relation to value for money.
answer_text: My Department is in the process of undertaking an economic analysis of the City of Derry Airport’s performance to inform its business case and a value for money assessment. The appropriate approvals and decision making process will be informed by this activity.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he will be proposing any changes to the allocation of the teaching grant provided to higher education providers.
answer_text: I have given approval for a Review of Higher Education Funding, to include consideration of the allocation of teaching grant provided to Higher Education Institutions.  The scope of the review is currently being drafted.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when his review into the funding model for higher education will be completed.
answer_text: The teaching funding model will be reviewed as part of the Review of Higher Education Funding.  I intend to launch the Review in early 2025. Currently, it is expected the Review will take at least 12 months to complete.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of his proposal to increase departmental funding to the City of Derry Airport, to detail the funding he is proposing to award to (i) Belfast International Airport; and (ii) George Best Belfast City Airport.
answer_text: Belfast International Airport and Belfast City Airport are privately owned and operated. I welcome the commitment given in the Draft Programme for Government 2024-2027 ‘Our Plan: Doing What Matters Most’to look at how we can develop new routes and offer further support to our three airports to support tourism and deliver economic growth.My officials have met with the management teams at both airports in recent weeks to discuss potential policy levers and opportunities for route development following the publication of research commissioned by my Department:  “Air Connectivity to Support Inbound Tourism and Business Growth”.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the provisions his Department has in place to support the night-time economy.
answer_text: My Department is exploring the night-time economy and how it can be best supported. Officials have been engaging with relevant stakeholders to hear their views and understand the issues affecting their organisations and relevant sectors more broadly. Further data is needed on our night-time economy and the policies that would allow it to reach its significant potential. With that in mind, my Department along with Tourism NI and the three Business Improvement Districts of Belfast, are working to commission research into the value of the night-time economy. This work will provide much needed insight into how we can support the development of a safe, diverse and thriving night economy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice when she intends to introduce her sentencing Bill to the Assembly.
answer_text: I have been planning on the basis that a Sentencing Bill would be introduced later in 2025, though how quickly this Bill is able to progress will be dependent on the progress of earlier Assembly business.  The Sentencing Bill is intended to be the second DoJ Bill in this mandate. This is expected to be a large and complex Bill, given the variety of matters to be included.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission to detail any powers it has to require MLAs to repay public funds that have been received in breach of Part 4 of the Assembly Members (Salaries and Expenses) Determination.
answer_text: Paragraph 43 of the Assembly Members (Salaries and Expenses) Determination (Northern Ireland) 2016, as amended (‘the Determination’), provides for the recoupment of payments made under the Determination. If the Assembly Commission determines that a breach of the provisions in Part 4 has occurred, full recovery of any monies paid during a period of non-compliance is sought.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission, further to recent media reports, is it investigating whether there has been a misappropriation of public funds in relation to the employment of Michael McMonagle.
answer_text: The Assembly Commission takes matters relating to the administration of public funds seriously.  The Assembly Commission has responsibility for the administration of the Assembly Members (Salaries and Expenses) Determination (Northern Ireland) (‘the Determination’), and as the Speaker informed the Assembly on 7 October 2024, officials are actively looking at issues that have arisen in relation to Michael McMonagle.  A detailed examination is underway by officials to ensure that there has been compliance with the relevant Determination.  Should any issues of non-compliance be identified, these will be dealt with in accordance with the provisions of the Determination for the recovery of funds.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the amount spent to date by the Housing Executive on scaffolding at Green End, Rathcoole.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“To date, scaffolding costs are £238,847.73. This includes erection of the scaffolding and the hire costs.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Housing Executive directly.  Making direct contact with them will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any plans he has to create a night-time economy taskforce.
answer_text: I believe it is important that we have a greater insight into the value of the night-time economy across the north. For this reason, my Department is currently working with three of the Belfast Business Improvement Districts and with Tourism NI to commission research to better understand the issues affecting the value of the night-time economy.This research, along with continued stakeholder engagement with relevant Departments and external organisations, will help inform any future work to support the night-time economy including any potential formation of a night-time economy taskforce.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the Housing Executive's Roof Replacement at Green End, Rathcoole, which has been outstanding since October 2022.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“The Green End roof replacement and external cyclical maintenance scheme was originally tendered in October 2023 but there were no responses.  The project was subsequently re-tendered in November 2023 and three tender submissions were received.  However, the lowest tenderer subsequently withdrew, and the other two tenders were not considered value for money, as a result of which that tender exercise has been stood down at that time and the options reassessed.  The Housing Executive can advise that the project has been retendered with submissions received and it is proposed to bring the contract to the Housing Executive Board by the end of October for consideration.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Housing Executive directly.  Making direct contact with them will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission, from June 2022, to detail (i) the number of times the Assembly pass issued to Michael McMonagle, as a result of his employment with Sinn Féin, was used to access Parliament Buildings; (ii) the dates this Assembly pass was used to access Parliament Buildings; and (iii) the events held in Parliament Buildings that were attended by people under the age of 18 on any day which the pass issued to Michael McMonagle was used.
answer_text: Data relative to the use of Assembly ID passes is only retained on the Assembly’s Visitor Management System for a period of 90 days after use.  The pass issued to Michael McMonagle was cancelled on 1 October 2024, and there is no record of it being used in the preceding 90 days, which extend back until 4 July 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department is aware of any ongoing legal action against the Ulster Boxing Council for religious discrimination.
answer_text: My Department have recently been made aware of legal proceedings against the Ulster Boxing Council for religious discrimination. It is understood that a settlement has been reached with the plaintiff.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the amount of funding his Department will provide the Ulster Boxing Council in the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Sport NI. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:		“Sport NI has not provided any funding to the Ulster Boxing Council in the 2024-25 financial year and has no planned funding due to be provided in the 2024-25 financial year.		Sport NI has provided two awards to the Irish Athletic Boxing Association (IABA):	£474,000 awarded, with £222,573 paid to date and £251,427 planned	£36,000 Athlete Award, with £18,000 paid to date and £18,000 planned.		Funding award 2024-25 breakdown is:	Retention & Growth£177,900.00Medals and More£296,100.00Total£474,000.00”		For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact Sport NI directly.  Making direct contact with Sport NI will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when his Department will publish the call for evidence on potential alternatives to fossil fuels for home heating, focused particularly on hydrotreated vegetable oil and biofuels.
answer_text: In the coming weeks, my Department will be issuing a call for evidence to examine how biofuels such as HVO and BioLPG can contribute to the energy transition by offering sustainable heating alternatives in situations where low-carbon technologies, such as heat pumps and heat networks, are not yet suitable or feasible.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, given the precedent set by the size of projects clearing in the three recent Republic of Ireland Renewable Energy Support Scheme (RESS) auctions, for his assessment of the Renewable NI and KPMG report on Accelerating Renewables in Northern Ireland, in which the industry group requested the entry point be lowered to >1MW for projects bidding into the planned Northern Ireland RESS auctions between 2025 and 2027, so as to maximise opportunities for new renewable generation and participation ahead of the 80 per cent by 2030 deadline.
answer_text: My Department published a High-Level Design (HLD) for a Renewable Electricity Support Scheme in April 2024.As part of the stakeholder engagement within the design process, industry stakeholders suggested a minimum capacity requirement for eligibility of between 0.5MW and 5MW. Based on this feedback, the HLD included the proposed 5MW eligibility requirement but noted that further analysis would be considered before confirming the minimum eligibility for the final scheme design.My officials continue to engage with RenewableNI as a key stakeholder and this report is a useful addition to the evidence base for scheme design.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on (i) when his Department aims to finalise the design of the new Northern Ireland Renewable Energy Support Scheme (RESS); and (ii) when the subsequent RESS auctions will take place.
answer_text: Action 17 in my Department’s recently published business plan has a target date of March 2025 to publish the final design of a renewable electricity support scheme for the north.		The first auction will be in early 2026 with another auction anticipated in late 2027.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on his work to create a management authority to support the controlled schools’ sector.

To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail all additional incentives that will be offered by Invest NI to foreign direct investment clients to operate outside of the Belfast Metropolitan Area Plan (BMAP), compared to within the BMAP, as outlined in his Sub-Regional Economic Plan.
answer_text: Invest NI is focussed on attracting investors to the North as a whole.
