Once that interest is secured, the investor will consider and assess locations across the region specific to needs in relation to skills, infrastructure, transport etc. Partnership working between Invest NI, the councils and regional stakeholders will be essential to ensure that the strengths of locations across the North are identified and showcased.Where the proposition of a region aligns to an investor’s needs, the use of incentives will be an important instrument in encouraging investors to establish operations in locations across the North of Ireland.If an investor is location-agnostic, Invest NI will seek to incentivise them to locate in regions across the North, helping to distribute FDI opportunities across the region. Two thirds of our investors go onto re-invest. Invest NI will also lever incentives to encourage expansion into to other parts of the North,.In all instances, the use of incentives to secure FDI opportunities for all parts of the North will be balanced and ensure value for money is delivered.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how need will be defined when determining the funding formula for the Local Economic Partnerships in his Sub-Regional Economic Plan.
answer_text: Departmental officials are developing funding formula options, which will be brought to stakeholders over the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of people currently on zero hours contracts.
answer_text: Evidence from the Labour Force Survey (LFS) from 2023 suggests that there were 11,000 employees on zero hours contracts.  The LFS is a household survey. Individuals provide responses on their personal circumstances and work i.e. information is self-reported. In the case of zero hours contracts, answering positively relies on individuals being aware of the term and knowing their type of contract.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether the Regional Property Strategy will (i) have a dedicated budget; and (ii) cover the entirety of Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The Regional Property Programme is a ten-year programme and as such will have multi-year budget requirements.  Given we operate in an annual budget cycle it is not possible to commit a budget over the life of the Programme.  Discussions are ongoing between my officials and Invest NI around prioritising this budget requirement in future budget planning exercises with the Department of Finance.Invest NI’s Regional Property Programme is aligned to my Department’s Sub Regional Economic Plan and will act as a major enabler to regional balance.  Regional Property Executives, based in Invest NI’s Regional Offices, will work closely with local stakeholders, across the entire region, including Councils, Enterprise Agencies and the private sector to develop partnership-based property solutions tailored to each area.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy, in light of his Sub-Regional Economic Plan, to detail (i) the additional funding his Department will provide to the City of Derry Airport; (ii) the percentage of overall public funding his Department’s contribution will represent; (iii) when a business case to support this funding was (a) developed; and (b) approved by his Department’s Accounting Officer; (iv) the management role his Department will have in the operation of the airport; and (v) whether a cap on the funding amount awarded by his Department to the airport will be put in place.
answer_text: My Department has agreed to assume responsibility for providing financial assistance to Derry City and Strabane District Council (DCSDC) for the City of Derry Airport to safeguard air connectivity and the associated economic benefits to the North West Region; Initial funding of £3m funding for 2024/25 will be administered via DfI. My Department will assume funding responsibility from 1 April 2025; We understand the total cost of running the airport is c£4.5m per annum including £1.3m in loan charges/capita funding. The £3m proposed contribution for this financial year will therefore represent 66.6% of the Council’s funding for the airport;(a) The original business case was developed by DCSDC in March 2021, with an Addendum provided by DCSDC in March 2024. It covers the period up to 31 March 2029 and is now being subjected to a commercial appraisal by my Department which will assist in the development of the DfE business case in advance of the next financial year. (b) The DfE business case has therefore not yet been approved by my Department’s Accounting Officer;My Department will have no management role in the operation of the airport; and My Department’s commercial appraisal of the business case will inform whether a cap on the funding amount awarded by DfE to DCSDC will be put in place.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when he will seek Executive approval for his Sub-Regional Economic Plan.
answer_text: The Sub-Regional Economic Plan focuses on matters within the remit of the Department for the Economy. I therefore will not be seeking Executive approval for the Plan.A fundamental building block of the Plan is the significantly enhanced role of Invest NI, an Arms Length Body of the Department. This is a direct response to the findings and recommendations arising from the Independent Review of Invest NI (Lyons Review) in relation to their role at a sub-regional level. I welcome increased collaboration from other departments, but I am not requiring them to take action at this point.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the rationale for selecting the Belfast Metropolitan Area Plan as the basis upon which he will judge regional balance in his Sub-Regional Economic Plan.
answer_text: A vibrant Belfast Metropolitan Area has always been and will continue to be vital to the North’s economy. Cities and city regions drive growth, and we will continue to support councils in this area to tackle their own sub-regional imbalances. 		However, the Independent Review of Invest NI made it clear that more needs done outside the Belfast area and the draft Programme for Government recognises the need for greater regional economic balance too. 58% of the population is outside the Belfast Metropolitan Area yet it is just 48% of the economy and makes up only 56% of Invest NI investments. 		We need to ensure those council areas perform better and so we will increase this to 65%.		Invest NI will continue to support and grow economic activity in the Belfast Metropolitan Area, but I am giving them a target to do more outside this area also.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the number of staff that will be based in his Regional Balance Unit; (ii) the business unit these members of staff will be redeployed from; and (iii) the cost associated with the creation of this unit.
answer_text: Internal departmental restructuring of the Economic Policy Group is ongoing including the establishment of this unit. Due to ongoing consultation with staff it would not be appropriate to comment on the detail of staff movement within the Department. At this point it is anticipated that this will involve movement of staff within the Department rather than adding to the overall headcount of the Department. Therefore I do not envisage any additional cost to the Department of this restructure.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the engagements and consultations he held with (i) trade unions; (ii) departmental staff; (iii) the general public on his proposed restructuring of the Department for the Economy, as outlined in his Sub-Regional Economic Plan.
answer_text: All appropriate consultations required for staffing changes have been completed for the first stage of this restructure. This includes consultation with trade unions and staff. My officials have engaged with Councils and a range of other stakeholders with an interest in sub-regional economic development on this and other issues related to it.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the stakeholders who will co-design the funding allocation model for the Local Economic Partnerships.
answer_text: Departmental officials are currently exploring a range of funding allocation models. Officials will shortly be meeting a number of key stakeholders including SOLACE, NILGA , Enterprise NI and NI  Chamber of Commerce.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how he is encouraging collaboration between the further education sector.
answer_text: Collaboration across our further education sector is essential to providing the best vocational and technical educational pathways for students and innovation opportunities for local businesses.I can advise that there is a number of forums which facilitate productive collaboration across the six colleges including Principals’ Group, Finance Officers Network, Human Resources Working Group and a Curriculum Directors’ Group. My officials also regularly meet with Chairs collectively as well as with all Principals to encourage a collaborative approach on sector-wide issues.Effective collaboration is key to my economic vision; I have tasked my officials and their colleagues in the Further Education sector to consider how these arrangements could be optimised and improved.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the role Invest NI could play in encouraging its clients to offer apprenticeships.
answer_text: Invest NI works with businesses to ensure they have access to a range of tailored interventions designed to address their skills needs. Where appropriate this routinely includes signposting to relevant Apprenticeship schemes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department holds data on the number of apprenticeships offered in each of the last three years.
answer_text: My Department’s ApprenticeshipsNI and Higher Level Apprenticeship programmes are demand-led, with apprentices recruited and retained by employers in line with business needs. The tables below provide occupancy/participant figures for both programmes for the past three years:ApprenticeshipsNI occupancy for the last three yearsApril 2021April 2022April 2023April 20248,67210,2269,65210,433	Higher Level Apprenticeships - at Further Education colleges including the College of Agriculture, Food and Rural Enterprise 	2020/212021/222022/239901,1111,530		Higher Level Apprenticeships - at north of Ireland higher education institutions	2019/202020/212021/22290405650

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when his Department will publish its three-year Apprenticeship Action Plan.
answer_text: The purpose of the new Apprenticeship Action Plan is to clearly outline the key activities and outputs for the 2024-2027 period that will support the delivery and development of our apprenticeship provision. Officials are developing the draft Action Plan which will be presented for Ministerial Approval in the coming months.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the action his Department is taking to increase the number of employers offering apprenticeships.
answer_text: Apprenticeships provide significant benefits to participants, employers and our economy. The Department is working across a range of approaches to grow and increase awareness of apprenticeships. To ensure our apprenticeship provision aligns with the changing needs of our economy, the Department has established 16 sectoral partnerships. My Department delivers a wide range of activities to promote awareness of apprenticeships.  There is an annual media campaign over TV, radio and social media, part of which is aimed specifically at employers looking to offer apprenticeship opportunities. In addition, my Department works closely with a wide range of regional partners to deliver the NI Apprenticeship Week. This is an annual event which aims to bring all the key partners within the development and delivery of apprenticeships together to raise the profile of apprenticeships across the North. My officials also attend Job, Employment and Careers Fairs to promote the profile of DfE Apprenticeships and to engage with employers.I have asked officials to develop an Apprenticeship Inclusion Challenge Fund to facilitate collaborative projects across employers, education and community sectors to test innovative approaches aimed at increasing future apprenticeship participation from underrepresented inclusion cohorts.In addition, work is ongoing within my Department to develop supporting business cases to ensure continuation of the ApprenticeshipsNI and Higher Level Apprenticeship programmes in 2025 and beyond.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of jobs supported by Invest NI that were located (i) in Belfast; and (ii) outside Belfast, in the last ten years.
answer_text: Invest NI reports its performance on a financial year basis with the year ending 31st March 2024 being the most recent available.  Over the ten-year period 1st April 2014 to the 31st March 2024, Invest NI supported projects that will create 50,544 new jobs.A breakdown of this total figure by Belfast District Council Area (DCA) and Outside Belfast DCA is detailed in the table below.Table I – 	Invest NI New Jobs Supported within Belfast DCA and Outside Belfast DCA (2014-15 to 2023-24)AreaNew JobsBelfast DCA21,480Outside Belfast DCA & north of Ireland Wide28,853Total50,333Not Yet Determined/External Delivery Organisations /Universities212NOTES: Outside Belfast and north of Ireland Wide refers to projects that are located outside of Belfast or to those that have the potential to impact multiple locations throughout the North of Ireland.Not yet determined refers to projects that cannot be allocated at this level.The beneficiaries of support offered to External Delivery Organisations (EDO) /Universities are not restricted to the DCA in which the EDO or University is located.Totals may not add due to rounding.For the Member’s future reference, Invest NI proactively publishes a full breakdown of financial support offered to every business over the past 5 years on the NI Open Data online portal, which can be accessed from the following link: Invest NI Open Data.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the number of businesses supported by Invest NI that were located (i) in Belfast; and (ii) outside Belfast, in the last ten years.
answer_text: Invest NI reports its performance on a financial year basis with the year ending 31st March 2024 being the most recent available.  Over the ten-year period 1st April 2014 to the 31st March 2024 Invest NI provided support to 8,115 businesses.A breakdown of this total figure by Belfast District Council Area (DCA) and Outside Belfast DCA is detailed in the table below.Table I – 	Invest NI Number of Businesses Supported within Belfast DCA and Outside Belfast DCA (2014-15 to 2023-24)AreaNumber of BusinessesBelfast DCA2,114Outside Belfast DCA & NI Wide5,966Total8,080Not Yet Determined35NOTES: Outside Belfast DCA and NI Wide refers to projects that are located outside of Belfast DCA or to those that have the potential to impact multiple locations throughout the North of Ireland.Not yet determined refers to projects that cannot be allocated at this level.Totals may not add due to rounding.	For the Member’s future reference, Invest NI proactively publishes a full breakdown of financial support offered to every business over the past 5 years on the NI Open Data online portal, which can be accessed from the following link: Invest NI Open Data.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the percentage of overall financial support offered by Invest NI was to small and medium-sized enterprises, in the last ten years.
answer_text: Invest NI reports its performance on a financial year basis with the year ending 31st March 2024 being the most recent available.  Over the ten-year period 1st April 2014 to the 31st March 2024, Invest NI provided support to businesses totalling £1,034m, 59% of this support was to small and medium-sized enterprises. Invest NI also offered £99m to External Delivery Organisations (EDO’s) or Universities to support projects or initiatives that will benefit businesses of all sizes. In addition to financial support Invest NI also provides wider support to all businesses through advice, assistance or guidance from its staff and through nibusinessinfo.co.uk, our online business advice portal.For the Member’s future reference, Invest NI proactively publishes a full breakdown of financial support offered to every business over the past 5 years on the NI Open Data online portal which can be accessed from the following link: Invest NI Open Data.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the Electricity Connection Policy Framework.
answer_text: Policy proposals for the Review of the Connections Policy Framework here continue to be refined with the Utility Regulator. My Department and the Utility Regulator intend to publish a joint consultation on this in the next month.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of the economic outlook of the manufacturing sector as a result of industrial derating.
answer_text: Question 8 – AQO 936/22-27Industrial Derating awards a 70% reduction to the normal rate liability for property deemed to be used for manufacturing purposes.  The relief is awarded to around 4,500 manufacturing properties, half of which are located in four council areas: Armagh City, Banbridge and Craigavon, Belfast, Mid Ulster and Newry, Mourne & Down. In the context of rising input costs, Industrial Derating is a valuable support, helping to keep manufacturing businesses competitive in the global market.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the work his Department has undertaken to develop post-19 special educational needs legislation.
answer_text: In June, I commissioned a review from officials on the existing skills and training provision for young people with special educational needs, including legislative protections, with a view to providing advice and recommendations. The work is complex and involves many different stakeholders and issues so it will take time. My officials are currently working through the planning and scoping activity and liaising with stakeholders to get their early insights and thoughts to help with shaping the work.  In terms of overall timeframe, I have indicated my desire to see improvements during this Mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the applications his Department made to the Public Services Transformation Board that were unsuccessful; and (ii) any feedback that has been received on those proposals.
answer_text: One DfE application was unsuccessful during the initial application process and will not proceed at this time.The proposal, “Public Sector Apprenticeship Programme”, aimed to assist public services to address their recruitment and retention challenges by providing the ecosystem to develop existing public sector staff through apprenticeship programmes. This bid presented two options, a small programme of £3.4 million over five years from 2024-25, focussed on support and facilitation for existing bodies engaged in this space, or a larger programme of £23.4 million, over the same period, which would seek to fund the training provision for public sector apprenticeships.I understand the intention is for the Public Sector Transformation Board to provide feedback on unsuccessful applications, at a later date.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when the second annual monitoring report on the Skills for a 10x Economy Strategy will be published.
answer_text: The second annual monitoring report on the Skills for a 10x Economy Strategy will not be published. I am firmly focused on working across government to recalibrate our approach to skills in support of the Executive’s agreed economic and social priorities, as set out in the draft Programme for Government 2024 - 27. Future progress on skills will be measured against the indicators set out in my Department’s Business Plan, published on the 9th September. I can confirm, however, that the evidence and objectives included in the Skills Strategy continue to inform my skills agenda. I have announced the expansion of places in education to address skills imbalances across the north, invested in lifelong learning and a more inclusive approach to education and work through the ring-fenced skills fund and recently launched my Department’s Digital Skills Action Plan.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the engagement he has had with the liquefied petroleum gas sector in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: My officials have engaged with the sector in recent years on consumer issues and developments in liquid biofuels, and in relation to the Department’s forthcoming Call for Evidence on the use of biofuels as a means to decarbonise heating.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the appointments he has made to the Tourism Partnership Board.
answer_text: Details of the appointments to the Tourism Partnership Board have been published and are available on the departmental website via the following link: Tourism Partnership Board.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the budget for Invest NI's new Hub and event space in Dublin.
answer_text: Annual budget for the operating of the Dublin Hub is £268,000 including rent, rates, service charge, staff costs and operating costs (staff costs, electricity, cleaning, insurance and general operating costs).

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy how his Department differentiates between cross-border trade and the all-island economy in the production of their statistical information.
answer_text: NISRA, ONS and HMRC are responsible for the production of statistical information relating to the performance of the economy in the North of Ireland. The Central Statistics Office (CSO) produces equivalent information for the economy in the South.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the membership of the Canadian Advisory Council.
answer_text: The table below lists the members of the newly established Canadian Advisory Council. It is comprised of C-suite level executives from sectors including Agri-Tech, Advanced Manufacturing & Engineering, and Fintech & financial services. The Council members are engaged in a voluntary capacity based on their connection to the North of Ireland and their willingness to support the growth of the North’s Economy.Rodney HillDermot MuirTerence DobbinGary McDowellJohn SpeersJenny O'DonnellJohn LaughlinStephen McCulloughMairead LaveryJohn GalwayBrenda GardinerIan CampbellKevin Magee	The Advisory Council will support Invest Northern Ireland’s trade and FDI strategy. The Advisory Council will help local businesses expand their presence in the Canadian market and seek to open a channel for Canadian companies interested in investing in the north of Ireland.	The Advisory Council will operate for an initial period of 2 years.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether his Department has received updated guidance from the National Institute for Health and Care Excellence on the use of trastuzumab deruxtecan (Enhertu) for treating HER2-low metastatic or unresectable breast cancer.
answer_text: My Department on 20 August 2024 endorsed ‘NICE Technology Appraisal TA992’ in which trastuzumab deruxtecan (Enhertu®), is not recommended for the treatment of HER2-low metastatic or unresectable breast cancer in adults.NICE have been unable to recommend Enhertu® for treating HER2-low metastatic or unresectable breast cancer within the NHS, as the most likely cost-effectiveness estimates based on the price offered by the manufacturer are above the upper end of the range NICE considers an acceptable use of NHS resources. NICE have indicated publicly that should the manufacturer of Enhertu® decide to reduce the price of treatment to a level that makes Enhertu® good value for money for the taxpayer, then NICE will consider its position under its rapid review process.Therefore, given the Department's formal link with NICE and in the absence of a positive determination, trastuzumab deruxtecan (Enhertu®) is currently not offered as a routinely commissioned treatment for HER2-low metastatic or unresectable breast cancer in Northern Ireland.However, the individual funding request (IFR) process provides a mechanism to consider requests from clinical consultants for treatments for individual patients that fall outside the range of services routinely commissioned. Clinical decisions such as these are a matter for the patient’s specialist consultant, in discussion with the patient or carer. Further information can be found online at Individual Funding Requests (IFRs) - HSC (hscni.net)

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the rationale for the proposed changes to tuition fee loan support for Four Year Graduate Entry Medicine Programmes.
answer_text: The policy change I am proposing will deliver greater consistency with arrangements supporting graduate entry medical schools elsewhere in these islands, where some form of fee support is in place.  Currently local students studying a graduate entry medical programme here cannot access a government tuition fee loan.  I want to change that.Making this change will support the NDNA and draft PfG commitments to grow Ulster University’s Derry~Londonderry campus to 10,000 students, as well as the Executive’s decision to establish a Graduate Entry Medical School in the North West.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy why his proposed changes to tuition fee loan support for Graduate Entry Medicine Programmes does not extend to five year graduate entry.
answer_text: Graduate Entry Medical (GEM) programmes can only be accessed by those holding an undergraduate degree.  Tuition fee loans are not currently available to local GEM students as second degrees do not generally qualify for this support.  Students therefore must fund their own tuition, making GEM programmes less accessible, particularly to students from disadvantaged backgrounds.  Undergraduate medical programmes are accredited for those completing their first degree and most students can therefore access the full range of financial support.  Extending tuition fee loan support to GEM students will deliver greater consistency with arrangements elsewhere in these islands, and support the NDNA and draft PfG commitments to grow Ulster University’s Derry~Londonderry campus to 10,000 students, as well as the Executive’s decision to establish a Graduate Entry Medical School in the North West.  Support for second degrees more broadly will be considered as part of the review of higher education funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail who he consulted with in advance of his proposed changes to tuition fee loan support for Four Year Graduate Entry Medicine Programmes.
answer_text: The NDNA agreement and the draft Programme for Government both commit us to growing Ulster University’s Derry~Londonderry campus (Magee) to 10,000 students. Ulster University has repeatedly cited the absence of tuition fee loans as a barrier to attracting students to the Graduate Entry Medical School at Magee.I have secured the agreement of the Minister of Health to move forward with the policy proposal.My officials are now seeking views from the higher education institutions in the North as well as NUS-USI to inform the way ahead.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he has made an assessment of the risk of a legal challenge on his proposed changes to tuition fee loan support for Four Year Graduate Entry Medicine Programmes.
answer_text: I will be taking all relevant factors into account when reaching a final position on this issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy why his proposed changes to tuition fee loan support for Four Year Graduate Entry Medicine Programmes will only benefit one university institution.
answer_text: Those studying medicine for a first undergraduate degree at any higher education institution offering such a course in the North can already avail of a tuition fee loan.Extending that facility to students entering an accredited graduate entry medical programme will impact on Ulster University alone because that institution is the only one in the North to operate an accredited graduate entry medical programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail her discussions with HM Government to increase funding for the PSNI.
answer_text: I have consistently raised the issue of the underfunding of the Justice system, including the PSNI, with successive Secretaries of State and Prime Ministers.  I have also raised the need for more investment in policing with the current Secretary of State and Prime Minister, including at meetings in July and August, and most recently on Thursday 26th September.  I will continue to do so, along with my Executive colleagues, to ensure that Northern Ireland public services are funded appropriately.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether he will be implementing the recommendations of the Northern Ireland Audit Office report, Developing the skills for Northern Ireland’s future.
answer_text: Officials are working through the Northern Ireland Audit Office’s findings and will bring forward advice on the recommendations contained in the Report. There is a need to recalibrate the approach to skills to deliver the economic vision reflected in the draft Programme for Government. I have commissioned officials to develop a refreshed action plan for skills which will reflect the recommendations made by the NIAO.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of the Northern Ireland Audit Office report, Developing the skills for Northern Ireland’s future.
answer_text: I welcome the Northern Ireland Audit Office (NIAO) Report. Officials are working through the NIAO’s findings and will bring forward advice on the recommendations contained in the report.  There is a need to recalibrate our approach to skills to deliver the economic vision reflected in the draft Programme for Government. I have commissioned officials to develop a refreshed skills action plan which will reflect the recommendations made by the NIAO.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail any discussions she has had with the Irish Government since taking up office in relation to allegations of Irish state collusion during the Troubles.
answer_text: I have not had any formal discussions with the Irish Government in my capacity as Justice Minister since taking up office, in relation to allegations of Irish state collusion during the Troubles. Matters relating to such legacy issues are not devolved and, as such, any engagement on these issues has been in a party political capacity.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the York Street Interchange scheme.
To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on when he will receive the business case from the IFA for the development of a National Training Centre for Football.
answer_text: The Irish FA have indicated that it plans to submit a business case to my Department detailing its proposals for a National Training Centre, in due course. All business cases are subject to the usual approvals process to consider whether it is appropriate to provide funding and in the context of available budgets.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether she has received any indication as to when the Police Ombudsman will return to work.
answer_text: Matters relating to the employment of the Ombudsman are for the Executive Office to consider.  I have received no indication of when the Police Ombudsman will return to work.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether her Department has received any additional funding requests from the Police Ombudsman's Office.
answer_text: The Department has received an additional funding request from the Police Ombudsman’s Office for £62,000 of capital funding in relation to 2024-25. This is currently being considered as part of the October Monitoring round.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether she is aware of any current investigations into the Police Ombudsman's Office.
answer_text: I am not aware of any current investigations into the Police Ombudsman’s Office.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether she is being kept updated on the investigation by West Midlands Police in relation to the Police Ombudsman.
answer_text: As this is an on-going police investigation I am unable to comment.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for her assessment of the impact of the absence of the Police Ombudsman.
answer_text: The Ombudsman, prior to her absence from the Office, delegated her powers to senior officials in the organisation as permitted under the Police (Northern Ireland) Act 1998, to ensure the functions of the Office can continue to be carried out.I met recently with the Chief Executive and the Senior Director of Investigations and I am assured that the work of the organisation continues in line with its statutory duties.The Ombudsman is appointed by Royal Warrant on the recommendation of First Minister and deputy First Minister, and any matters relating to her employment are for the Executive Office to consider.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on his proposals for an Enhanced Investment Zone.
answer_text: In February 2024 I announced in my Economic Vision statement that the North would utilise Enhanced Investment Zone funding to support green technologies.Investment Zones are a British government policy and the new Labour government has yet to confirm its position on the Enhanced Investment Zone (EIZ) for the North. A decision on this is expected in the October Budget announcement, at the earliest, however this may realise into the Spring announcement.My officials remain in contact with officials from the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government on the EIZ programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any mitigations that have been considered to prevent school support staff, who are in receipt of means-tested benefits, from being disproportionately impacted when receiving the lump sum payment as per the pay and grading agreement.
answer_text: While I am very sympathetic towards those staff in receipt of benefits whose payments may be affected, unfortunately, as is normally the case with government pay settlements, it is not possible to arrange for payments to be staggered.  Officials in the Department and the Education Authority (EA) have investigated this issue.  The EA does not hold information that would identify the employees who claim mean-tested benefits.  In addition, benefits such as Universal Credit, apply in different ways to different individuals. There is, therefore, no way to know what adjustment to pay would be required for anyone affected by this.The UK NJC National Employers also examined this issue as part of their last pay settlement and concluded that it would not be feasible for employers to resolve this through phased payments. Their advice is that any employees affected by this issue should speak to their Universal Credit Work Coach via their online account at Universal Credit Service Centre | nidirect.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the number of regionally significant planning applications with his Department for decision; and (ii) the length of time each application has been with his Department for decision.
answer_text: There are No 2 regionally significant planning applications with my Department:SPD/2023/0951/F	Carnbuck Windfarm has been in the planning system for 82 weeks.SPD/2023/0981/F	Owenreagh/Craignapple Windfarm has in the planning system for 52 weeks. There are also No 4 regionally significant planning applications which have been referred to the Planning Appeals Commission (PAC):P/2015/0218/F 	Gruggandoo Wind Farm has been in the planning system for 496 weeks, 129 of those weeks with the PAC;  LA02/2020/0458/F	 Ballygilbert Wind Farm has been in the planning system for 220 weeks, 99 of those weeks with the PAC;LA02/2021/1205/F	Unshinagh Wind Farm has been in the planning system for 143 weeks, 35 of those weeks with the PAC;LA10/2017/1249/F 	Dalradian underground mine has been in the planning system for 355 weeks, 157 of those weeks with the PAC.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when he intends to bring his proposals to the Executive for changes to employment law.
answer_text: I estimate that I will be in a position to bring proposals to change employment law to the Executive in the early part of 2025. I aim to seek Executive agreement to introduce the Good Jobs Employment Rights Bill to the Assembly in early 2026, or earlier if there is scope to do so. Once introduced, progress of the bill will be determined by Assembly processes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to provide details on the proposed Apprenticeship Inclusion Challenge Fund.
answer_text: The Apprenticeship Inclusion Challenge Fund will facilitate collaborative projects across education, business and community sectors to develop and test innovative solutions to increase apprenticeship participation from underrepresented groups.  The Fund will promote apprenticeship uptake from women, people with disabilities and people living in under-represented geographical areas.Subject to approvals, the Fund will open for applications during autumn 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the trade missions he will be undertaking in the remainder of the 2024-25 financial year; (ii) the UK government departments that will be attending; (iii) the non-UK government departments that will be attending; and (iv) the rationale for the inclusion of non-UK government departments.
answer_text: In November I will lead an Invest NI technology sector trade mission to Singapore. I will be travelling with a delegation of technology and fintech companies from the North to support them to develop export opportunities. This trade mission will be delivered in collaboration with Enterprise Ireland and will include a joint presence at the Singapore Fintech Festival, one of the largest events of its kind in the world.Like Invest NI, Enterprise Ireland will bring a delegation of companies from the South which will be led by Minister of Enterprise, Trade and Employment, Peter Burke. During this trade mission I will also meet with the British High Commission and the Department of Business and Trade (DBT) in Singapore.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any representations they will make to HM Government in relation to the decision to pause funding for the Shore Road Skills Centre.
answer_text: We are disappointed that the UK Government recently decided to pause its funding commitments in a number of areas.  The impact on the Shore Road Skills Centre epitomises the inevitable outcome of this decision on many similar organisations within local communities.We, along with Executive colleagues, will continue to engage with the Secretary of State and UKG Ministers directly to argue for a fair and sustainable financial settlement.  These discussions have included engagement on the design and delivery of EU Replacement Funding.     We understand that both the Minister for Communities and the Minister of Finance have raised this specific project with the Minister for Democracy and Local Growth, Alex Norris MP, since the pause in funding was announced.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any representations he will make to HM Government in relation to the decision to pause funding for the Shore Road Skills Centre.
answer_text: It is disappointing that the UK Government has paused funding for the Shore Road Skills Centre. This matter was raised when I met with the Minister for Democracy and Local Growth, Alex Norris MP, on Tuesday 18th September 2024.  I will continue to push for clarity and resolution of this and the other paused spending commitments as soon as possible. My Department is continuing to work with the Department of Finance and other Executive Departments in engaging with the UK Government on the design and delivery of EU Replacement Funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any representations she will make to HM Government in relation to the decision to pause funding for the Shore Road Skills Centre.
answer_text: It is disappointing that the British Government has paused funding for the Shore Road Skills Centre. This matter was raised when I met with the Minister for Democracy and Local Growth, Alex Norris MP, on Tuesday 18th September 2024.  I will continue to push for clarity and resolution of this matter and the other paused spending commitments as soon as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how he will ensure a legacy for local football emanating from the 2028 UEFA European Football Championship.
answer_text: Following the UK Government announcement, I remain committed to securing a role for Northern Ireland in the EURO 2028 tournament and I will engage with the UK Government and other partners to ensure that there is a lasting legacy for football here, including through the progression of the Northern Ireland Football Fund.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the projected overspend in his departmental spending in the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: As Minister for Infrastructure, I have made considerable efforts to live within my Department’s budget allocation. I have applied reductions of £117.1m to my Department’s original resource requirements, which is a cut of 17.3%.However, even with these actions, given the real and serious impacts, my Department continues to face pressures across infrastructure, including resource pressures of £40.8m. My officials continue to review these pressures to identify actions that could be taken to manage within the budget allocation. There is also a capital overplanning commitment of £13.7m and it is expected that this will be addressed through proactive management before the end of the financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the projected overspend in her departmental spending in the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: My Department is currently facing stabilisation pressures of £48m. This overcommitment was reported to the Department of Finance as part of the recent in-year exercise. Further to this, my Department is also facing exceptional pressures of £227m in relation to legal claims for McCloud Injury to Feelings, Data Breach and Holiday Pay. These costs are not affordable within my Department’s budget under any circumstance.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether she remains committed to maintaining the Industrial derating rates relief.
answer_text: The consultation directed by the then Secretary of State was limited in nature looking simply at whether support should be removed or retained. Having met with ratepayers and business organisations over recent months I understand these are complex issues.I very much recognise the role that industrial derating has played in supporting local manufacturers. We have a strong manufacturing sector that supports an awful lot of jobs, and anything that we do needs to align with what we are trying to achieve with our economic vision of creating good jobs, improving productivity, decarbonisation and regional balance.I will be bringing forward short, medium and long-term rating policy proposals including a more strategic longer-term policy approach in the next couple of weeks.I will be engaging with Executive colleagues on the next steps and will continue my engagement with business bodies, organisations and wider society to work together to build a progressive rates system which grows our tax base and stimulates our economy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the projected overspend in his departmental spending in the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: The current projected overcommitment is £13.9 million Resource DEL (RDEL).

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the projected overspend in her departmental spending in the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: The extremely challenging opening budget resulted in decisions taken to identify savings of £15.7m in Non-ringfenced Resource across my Department.  While spend continues to be closely monitored my Department is not currently projecting an overspend in either Resource or Capital.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for his assessment of the availability of NHS dentistry places for those living in North Belfast.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the steps his Department can take to improve road safety for cars exiting the junction of Church Road and Prince Charles Way, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: My Department prioritises provision of safety engineering measures by taking into account a number of factors: collision records for the site; the speed, volume and type of traffic, and; the characteristics of the site. Collision records at the junction of Church Road and Prince Charles Way include one minor injury collision in the past three years. Given the need for my Department to target its constrained funding where it will be most effective at providing road safety benefits, there are no immediate plans for any remedial work at this site.Meanwhile concerns about driver behaviour such as speeding or other inconsiderate behaviour should be directed to the PSNI on their non-emergency 101 telephone number.One of the best ways to ensure safety on our roads and fulfil our long-term goal of eliminating death and serious injury by 2050, is by changing road user behaviour. As road users we all have a personal responsibility to behave in a way that keeps ourselves and others safe.  The sad reality is that if we do not change our own attitudes when we are using the roads, our death toll will continue to increase.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether she is considering the removal of the early payment discount for domestic rates.
answer_text: The consultation directed by the then Secretary of State was limited in nature looking simply at whether support should be removed or retained. Having met with ratepayers and business organisations over recent months I understand these are complex issues.I will be bringing forward short, medium and long-term rating policy proposals including a more strategic longer-term policy approach in the next couple of weeks.I will be engaging with Executive colleagues on the next steps and will continue my engagement with business bodies, organisations and wider society to work together to build a progressive rates system which grows our tax base and stimulates our economy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance when she will present her proposals for changes to the rates system.
answer_text: I will be bringing forward short, medium and long-term rating policy proposals including a more strategic longer-term policy approach to the Executive for discussion in the next couple of weeks.It is my intention to make a statement to the Assembly on this issue after the Executive has agreed the way forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when the Dalriada Urgent Care out of hours service will be reinstated at Whiteabbey Hospital, Newtownabbey.
To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any representations he has made to HM Government to increase the income threshold for Pension Credit entitlement.
answer_text: The income thresholds for entitlement to Pension Credit are set by the UK Government and maintained here in accordance with Section 87 of the Northern Ireland Act 1998. I, along with all Executive Ministers, have written to the Prime Minister, Sir Keir Starmer, to convey our collective concern at the Government’s changes to the Winter Fuel Payment Scheme and urged an immediate review of the Pension Credit threshold. This must ensure that more low-income pensioner households, who are not financially secure and will narrowly miss out on a Winter Fuel Payment this year, can meet the eligibility criteria for Pension Credit. I also raised this matter directly with Sir Stephen Timms MP, Minister of State for Social Security and Disability, when I met with him on 3 September. I am committed to doing all I can to support pensioners who are on a low income, and I will continue to urge the UK Government to review the financial support available through the social security system. This includes exploring, along with Executive colleagues, the option of issuing a one-off hardship payment to those in receipt of state pension who will lose out on the Winter Fuel Payment this winter. My officials are currently working with DWP counterparts to consider the legislative and operational implications of this approach. I will keep the Assembly informed about how we can best help in this regard.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether she has received any representations from the Minister of Health for additional funding for the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice since his appointment on 28 May 2024.
answer_text: I have not received any specific bids from the Minister of Health for additional funding for the Children’s Hospice since his appointment on 28 May 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice whether the letter from her Permanent Secretary to the Chief Constable of the PSNI in week commencing 12 August 2024 in relation to budgetary issues was sent with her prior approval.
answer_text: The Permanent Secretary of the Department of Justice is the Principal Accounting Officer and, as such, is accountable to the Northern Ireland Assembly for the issue of grant-in-aid paid to the PSNI.  The correspondence to which you refer is a private matter and is regrettable that it was leaked.  However, I can confirm that I was not sighted on the letter to the Chief Constable, as this was issued in his role as Principal Accounting Officer.  The responsibilities of a Departmental Accounting Officer are set out in more detail in Chapter 3 of Managing Public Money Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he remains committed to including Glengormley in Belfast Rapid Transit Phase 2.
answer_text: Work on the feasibility of extending to Glengormley and Carryduff has been finalised.  I have asked officials in my department to consider the delivery options that could be implemented utilising the £35m funding envelope from the Belfast Region City Deal.  The Outline Business Case is being finalised in line with these feasibility reports and delivery options, and an announcement will be made in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the Belfast Rapid Transit Phase 2.
answer_text: The estimated cost of the North Belfast to South Belfast Glider route is currently in the range of £142m to £148m. £35m of funding has been secured through Belfast Region City Deal.  I will continue to work with Executive colleagues and the Belfast Region City Deal Partners to ensure that the appropriate level of investment is made available to deliver the project.Work on the feasibility of extending to Glengormley and Carryduff has been finalised.  The delivery options that could be implemented, utilising the £35m funding envelope from the Belfast Region City Deal, are being investigated.  The Outline Business Case is being finalised in line with these feasibility reports and delivery options.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure (i) for an update on the pedestrianisation of Hill Street, Belfast; and (ii) to detail the amount spent on this project to date.
answer_text: I can advise that my officials are currently working on several competing work priorities and consequently development work on the proposal to pedestrianise Hill Street / Gordon Street, Belfast has temporarily been put on hold. Officials will keep the position under review and recommence work as soon as resources are available. Regarding costs associated with the scheme, I can advise that the development work for this proposal has been carried out by officials in Eastern Division. While staffing costs would have been associated with the scheme, these are not recorded. There has been no recent capital expenditure.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on proposals to ensure victims of domestic abuse have access to paid safe leave from work.
answer_text: On 5 July 2024 I launched a consultation on the introduction of paid safe leave from work for victims of domestic abuse.  This consultation invites views on the issues that need to be included in the regulations that will implement the provisions of the Domestic Abuse (Safe Leave) Act (NI) 2022.  		The consultation closes on 27 September 2024.  I encourage everyone with an interest in this important area to respond to the consultation and share their views.  The responses to the consultation will inform next steps and the detail of the regulations.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will publish his plan for hospital reconfiguration.
To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the People and Place review.
answer_text: The Review of the Executive’s People and Place strategy has progressed well with collaborative structures in place to facilitate engagement with a wide range of key stakeholders and other focus groups from across Northern Ireland.This work aims to produce a sound evidence-based approach that could be used to identify areas experiencing deprivation and on which a future funding programme to tackle deprivation could be based.  The Development of an outline Strategic Framework, informed by the views expressed through the stakeholder and sectoral engagement exercise has broadly concluded with a successful Strategic Insight Lab event in June.Subject to my consideration and that of my Executive colleagues, the Strategic Framework will be subject to a full public consultation exercise. Following a public consultation exercise, the planning assumption is that the new Strategic Framework will be completed during 2025, followed by a transition phase.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the delivery of the National Training Centre for football.
answer_text: The development of a National Training Centre is a key commitment of The Northern Ireland Football Fund and officials are working with the Irish FA and other key partners to progress its development. Officials are currently awaiting receipt of a Business Case from the Irish FA where the site of any proposed development will be confirmed.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any consideration that is being given to the extension of the Building Successful Communities Pilot programme.
answer_text: The Building Successful Communities (BSC) Programme is now coming to an end and a Post Programme Review report will be finalised and presented to me in the coming months. This report will include consideration of any potential extension.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to outline his ongoing work to tackle educational underachievement among working-class Protestant boys.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail the reasons for the delay in the opening of the new maternity hospital at the Royal Victoria Hospital.
answer_text: During construction of the New Maternity Hospital at the Royal Victoria Hospital, as part of routine monitoring, the contractor reported levels of pseudomonas aeruginosa (PSA) within the domestic water systems, and action was taken to remediate this. The contractor continued to test the water and provided a set of samples immediately prior to handover.  The results of these samples were reviewed, and they met contractual obligations. The building was handed over in March 2024.  Following handover, the Trust put the water systems within the building into ‘operational mode’ and ran a period of ‘steady state’.  During this period, the Trust took sampling from all water outlets within the building and PSA was detected in a significant number of outlets. This current issue involves the detection of PSA within the domestic water systems.  The Trust want to provide assurance to member of the public that it takes steps to prevent pseudomonas colonising in their plumbing network to protect the vulnerable patients in their care, and that includes regularly testing their water system. The Trust is currently commissioning an independent review of the Maternity Hospital water systems.  This review will be undertaken by leading industry experts and will inform next steps for the Trust under the relevant design and construction contracts. As was reported on 25th July 2024, Maternity Hospital Briefing Note Status – 25 July 2024 | Belfast Health & Social Care Trust website (hscni.net) - in tandem with the independent review, and subject to its final outcome, the Trust is developing remediation options for the water systems.   Belfast Trust staff continue to provide excellent care to women and babies in the Royal Jubilee Maternity Hospital and service delivery has not been impacted as a result of the delay.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the number of applications made to the Energy Efficiency Capital Grant; (ii) the number of applications approved; and (iii) the value of the approved applications.
answer_text: My Department has worked in partnership with Invest NI to deliver the new flagship Energy Efficiency Capital Grant Scheme, which launched in May 2024. The scheme has received a significant number of inquiries to date, across a wide range of businesses and project types. A total of 43 inquiries have progressed to final application stage following support from the Invest NI team and 22 of these applications have been approved with businesses having received official offers, at a total grant value of £1,079,628.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail (i) the number of public appointments he has made since taking office; and (ii) the religious breakdown of the appointments.
answer_text: As Economy Minister I have made public appointments to the following bodies:Health & Safety Executive NI Consumer Council NISouth Eastern Regional CollegeLabour Relations AgencyFuller details of all appointments are available via press releases at:News | Department for the EconomyPublic appointments are based on the merit principle. Community background is not considered at any stage of the process.   To measure the effectiveness of commitments to diversity and equality of opportunity, The Executive Office publishes Public Appointments Reports and Community Background is included in statistical format.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 14235/22-27, given the passage of time since the occupation of the developments, to detail when the pursuing of enforcement action will be considered by his Department.
answer_text: The enforcement powers which are available to my Department through Article 11 of the Private Streets (Northern Ireland) Order are discretionary and do not stipulate a time for enforcement action to be taken.Enforcement has been considered regarding Willowbrook and Loughshore Manor but as stated in my previous reply to AQW 14235/22-27, it is not considered appropriate to pursue this course of action at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the Studio Ulster project.
answer_text: The Studio Ulster project is progressing well and will become operational in early 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail what will replace the Non-domestic Renewable Heat Incentive Scheme.
answer_text: New Decade New Approach states that “RHI will be closed down and replaced by a scheme that effectively cuts carbon emissions.” I am seeking the Executive’s agreement to honour this commitment. At present, the replacement for RHI has not yet been finalised.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the proposed Traffic Management Scheme at Rathcoole Primary School, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: The Traffic Management scheme at Rathcoole Primary School has received planning approval, however delivery is currently paused due to the extremely constrained capital budget position. As you will appreciate, decisions have necessarily been taken to direct available investment to keep schools open, pupils safe and provide education access for the most vulnerable.I will continue to make the case for additional capital funding for education to ensure all our schools are fit for purpose.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail his Department’s resurfacing programme for the 2024-25 financial year in the North Belfast constituency.
answer_text: Resurfacing schemes are funded through my Department’s Structural Maintenance Programme; regrettably, for some time the available investment has not kept pace with what is needed to maintain all of our roads in a reasonable condition.  While there are many roads that would benefit from investment, due to limited budget resources resurfacing work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need of intervention.Details of the list of planned resurfacing schemes for the 2024/25 financial year will be included within the Department’s Annual Report to Belfast City Council in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy when Tourism NI will publish its 2022-23 annual accounts.
answer_text: The Northern Ireland Audit Office’s (NIAO) audit of Tourism NI’s 2022-23 Annual Report and Accounts is not complete. The accounts will be published as soon as the audit concludes and NIAO certification obtained.Tourism NI and the NIAO are still working through a number of issues identified during the audit process. My Department continues to work with NIAO to reach a conclusion of the audit at the earliest juncture.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the steps his Department can take to improve parking in Waveney Avenue, Belfast; and (ii) whether his Department will consider the painting of parking bays to improve parking in Waveney Avenue, Belfast.
answer_text: My Officials have carried out site inspections of the location. This review has confirmed that parking was available and found that any issues were caused by inconsiderate parking. If a motorist parks a vehicle in such a manner which is causing an obstruction, or blocking access or egress, they are committing an offence which can only be enforced by the PSNI.My department does not provide individual parking bays in a residential area, as formalising parking generally tends to reduce the number of parking spaces available to residents. The ad hoc nature of parking within Waveney Avenue, provides the most flexibility for residents and helps to control traffic speeds.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the work to bring forward a development proposal for a new build for Seaview Primary School, Belfast.
answer_text: Seaview Primary School was announced under the Major Capital Works Programme in March 2016.  Progress on the project has been delayed due to a lack of available suitable sites for a new school.  However, an Integrated Consultant Team was appointed for the project in December 2023 and a Technical Feasibility Report, which will detail potential options and costs for the provision of suitable accommodation for a fourteen class primary school with double nursery unit, is anticipated to be lodged with the Department for consideration within the coming weeks.  Once the Technical Feasibility Report has been approved by the Department a Business Case will be completed by the Education Authority, and once approved this will determine the preferred option which will be taken forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs when he intends to introduce the Dilapidation Bill.
answer_text: Question 8 - Phillip BrettTo ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs when he intends to introduce the Dilapidation Bill.AQO 758/22-27Answer:	It is my intention to introduce a Dilapidation bill to the Assembly in the autumn term of this current sitting, subject of course to the agreement of my Executive colleagues. 		The proposed Bill would provide councils with a modern and fit-for-purpose regime, including clear powers to tackle dilapidated buildings and neglected sites. Powers would be similar to those available to local authorities in other parts of the UK.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) any plans his Department has to reduce waiting times for percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy insertion surgery at the Royal Hospital for Sick Children; and (ii) whether his Department can refer patients for this surgery to hospitals in other parts of the United Kingdom.
answer_text: The issue of access to percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy (PEG) insertion for children and young people is an important issue as is the range of all surgical procedures that face children and young people in Northern Ireland.  This procedure is a complex procedure that requires specialised clinical and nursing input prior to, during and post procedure.  My officials within SPPG have now carried out some initial scoping on the question that you have raised with senior officials in BHSCT and have been informed of the following.There are 93 children currently on the waiting list with the longest wait just over three years.  Each case is constantly assessed by the specialist team at the RBHSC and dealt with as quickly as possible based on clinical priority and chronological order.A Paediatric Surgeon is currently going through an urgent validation of Peg Tube waiting lists.  This process will identity which children are suitable to transfer to Alderhey Hospital in Liverpool for the procedure.  Clinical criteria, agreed by the specialist team, will be in place to determine this.Belfast Trust are also hoping to provide a waiting list initiative in October, to carry out additional sessions on Fridays, with patients being nursed in Knox ward in RBHSC over the weekend. Further discussions are underway with South Eastern Trust to provide the Belfast Trust paediatric surgeon a gastrostomy surgery list in the Ulster Hospital.  Belfast Trust Gastrostomy Specialist Nurses will provide training to staff in South Eastern Trust to help facilitate this.A further review of surgical work performed in RBHSC is underway and any work that can be redirected to District General Hospitals will be transferred.  This may free up capacity in RBHSC for Paediatric Surgeons to perform more gastrostomy surgery and other more complex surgery that can only be carried out in RBHSC.The Trust are also examining partnership working with Private Sector to use RBHSC capacity over the weekend to deliver Waiting List Initiatives in the Children’s Hospital and will be dependent on unscheduled care pressures. My Officials will continue to meet with BHSCT to discuss these potential solutions to examine their progress and success.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for a breakdown of funding allocated to individual organisations in North Belfast under the Communities in Transition since the programme began.
answer_text: Communities in Transition (CIT) operates across eight designated areas. The North Belfast CIT area covers New Lodge and Ardoyne. To date £1.45 million has been allocated to projects in the North Belfast CIT area. CIT delivery partners have also supported some residents in Tigers Bay through the ‘Area of influence’ approach. The breakdown is as follows: Ashton Community Trust£965,536Community Restorative Justice Ireland (CRJI)£306,079BCT NI CIC£184,996In addition to projects specific to North Belfast, the area has received support from three of CIT’s regional projects; Ex-Prisoner Employability, Ex-Prisoner Support and Advocacy, and Restorative Justice Practice.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for a list of all resurfacing schemes to be completed in North Belfast and Newtownabbey between September 2024 and the end of the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: Resurfacing schemes are funded through my Department’s Structural Maintenance Programme; regrettably, for some time the available investment has not kept pace with what is needed to maintain all of our roads in a reasonable condition.  While there are many roads that would benefit from investment, due to limited budget resources resurfacing work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need of intervention.Details of the planned list of resurfacing schemes will be included within the Department’s 2024/25 Annual Reports to Belfast City Council and Antrim & Newtownabbey Borough Council in due course.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any schemes being delivered by the Housing Executive in the area of the North Belfast District Office, broken down by (i) areas covered by each scheme; (ii) scheme type; (iii) scheme cost; and (iv) total number of properties in the scheme, from September 2022 until the end of the 2024-25 financial year.
answer_text: Due to the volume of detail required to answer AQW 14284/22-27, the information has been placed in the Assembly Library.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the current estimated waiting time for percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy insertion surgery at the Royal Hospital for Sick Children from the initial date of referral; and (ii) the number of patients currently on this waiting list.
answer_text: The issue of access to percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy (PEG) insertion for children and young people is an important issue as is the range of all surgical procedures that face children and young people in Northern Ireland.  This procedure is a complex procedure that requires specialised clinical and nursing input prior to, during and post procedure.  My officials within SPPG have now carried out some initial scoping on the question that you have raised with senior officials in BHSCT and have been informed of the following.There are 93 children currently on the waiting list with the longest wait just over three years.  Each case is constantly assessed by the specialist team at the RBHSC and dealt with as quickly as possible based on clinical priority and chronological order.A Paediatric Surgeon is currently going through an urgent validation of Peg Tube waiting lists.  This process will identity which children are suitable to transfer to Alderhey Hospital in Liverpool for the procedure.  Clinical criteria, agreed by the specialist team, will be in place to determine this.Belfast Trust are also hoping to provide a waiting list initiative in October, to carry out additional sessions on Fridays, with patients being nursed in Knox ward in RBHSC over the weekend. Further discussions are underway with South Eastern Trust to provide the Belfast Trust paediatric surgeon a gastrostomy surgery list in the Ulster Hospital.  Belfast Trust Gastrostomy Specialist Nurses will provide training to staff in South Eastern Trust to help facilitate this.A further review of surgical work performed in RBHSC is underway and any work that can be redirected to District General Hospitals will be transferred.  This may free up capacity in RBHSC for Paediatric Surgeons to perform more gastrostomy surgery and other more complex surgery that can only be carried out in RBHSC.The Trust are also examining partnership working with Private Sector to use RBHSC capacity over the weekend to deliver Waiting List Initiatives in the Children’s Hospital and will be dependent on unscheduled care pressures. My Officials will continue to meet with BHSCT to discuss these potential solutions to examine their progress and success.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 12989/22-27, why the grass verges remain uncut.
answer_text: Grass cutting operations in the North Belfast area commenced on 1 July 2024 and are ongoing. The contractor for the work has had issues with equipment and the need for additional temporary traffic management arrangements. My Department is working with the contractor to resolve these issues with the view to completing the grass cutting operations as quickly as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what actions his Department can take to require the developers of (i) Willowbank; and (ii) Loughshore Manor, Newtownabbey, to bring the roads and footways up to an adoptable standard.
answer_text: Departmental records do not include a development named Willowbank in the Newtownabbey area, however there is one named Willowbrook. This response refers to the roads and footways of Willowbrook and Loughshore Manor.The Private Streets Order (NI) 1980 allows my Department to exercise powers throughout the process of planning, construction and adoption of new developments.The Order provides my Department with discretionary powers to commence enforcement action using a bond provided by the developer, to fund the completion of the roads and footways. Enforcement action can often be a protracted process, and liaison with developers to achieve completion of infrastructure works to an adoptable standard is the preferred approach.The developers of both Willowbrook and Loughshore Manor are liaising with my Department to achieve completion and adoption of their respective developments. It would therefore be inappropriate to pursue enforcement action at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on his Department’s efforts to have the roads adopted at (i) Willowbank; and (ii) Loughshore Manor, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: Departmental records do not include a development named Willowbank in the Newtownabbey area, however there is one named Willowbrook.  This response relates to the roads within Willowbrook and Loughshore Manor developments.It is the responsibility of the developers to provide the streets within their respective developments to the appropriate adoptable standard.In these cases, the developers have been, and continue to be in contact with my department regarding the completion of their developments. The developers are aware of all the works required and are actively working to complete their respective developments.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health when he will visit the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice.
To ask the Minister for Communities whether he has received a business case from the Northern Ireland Housing Executive on the future of Abbotscoole House, Newtownabbey.
answer_text: My Department has not received a business case from the Housing Executive relating to Abbotscoole House.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance when she expects the June Monitoring Round to be completed.
answer_text: The Executive agreed the June Monitoring round on 01 July 2024, and I made a statement to the Assembly on the same day. A copy of my statement can be viewed on the Department’s website at https://www.finance-ni.gov.uk/publications/ministerial-statement-2023-24-provisional-outturn-and-2024-25-june-monitoring

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on his work to secure additional funding for the Northern Ireland Children's Hospice.
answer_text: The Department of Health recognises the valuable role of hospices in delivering specialist care to people with palliative and end of life care needs across Northern Ireland.My Department has provided core funding of £1.8m in 2023/24 directly to the Northern Ireland Children’s Hospice (NICH) for a range of services for children with life limiting conditions and their families, including respite care and end of life care. In June 2023 the NICH was advised that an additional non-recurrent allocation of £0.17m would no longer be available from September 2023, this was in relation to a short-term respite contract. In February 2024 in light of the financial challenges faced by the Hospice my department extended this support until the end of March 2024 and in addition £420,000 of non-recurrent funding was made recurrent to provide additional financial security.My Department provides funding of c30% of the NICH contract at present.  The NICH advised they require additional funding in the region of c£860k per annum to ensure financial stability alongside additional financial support to keep in line with AFC uplifts. You will appreciate that the Department of Health faces a number of hugely significant financial challenges and competing priorities at this time. This position has been exacerbated by this Houses’ recent regrettable approval of the 2024/25 budget. The NICH and NI Hospice parent body will in association need to develop longer-term sustainable financial solutions, to which I am sure Government will want to be supportive. I am also very conscious that other hospice organisations, and many other health and care providers, are also grappling with growing budget pressures and will be seeking further statutory financial support.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the current waiting time for an endometriosis diagnosis; and (ii) how his Department will reduce this.
answer_text: (i) Please see below the response provided by each Trust in relation to the current waiting time for an endometriosis diagnosis;Trust ResponseBelfastEndometriosis is a complex condition that affects patients to differing degrees.  A diagnosis is determined based on the individual’s presenting symptoms and associated medical and surgical history, as well as the results obtained from a wide range of clinical tests and diagnostic procedures.  Belfast Trust waiting times to be seen at an Endometriosis Outpatient Clinic are currently:Urgent consultation:	  6 monthsRoutine consultation:	10 monthsSouthern Eastern South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust gynaecology waiting times for an outpatient appointment are as follows:Urgent – 146 weeks (approx.)Routine - 347weeks (approx.)The service is focusing on prioritising the red flag and cancer appointments and this has had an impact on the waiting times for urgent and routine appointments. The current longest wait for a red flag gynaecology appointment is 12 weeks. The trust has seen a 12% increase in gynaecology red flag referrals and this increase in demand has put a further pressure on the service.NorthernEndometriosis patients referred with suspected endometriosis are included in the Waiting Time for the General Gynae List.
